Genesis1:1 God created the sky and the earth. At first
Genesis1:2 the earth was completely empty. There was nothing on the earth. Darkness covered the ocean
Genesis1:3 Then God said
Genesis1:4 He saw the light
Genesis1:5 God named the light day
Genesis1:6 Then God said
Genesis1:7 So God made the space and separated the water. Some of the water was above it
Genesis1:8 God named that space sky. There was evening
Genesis1:9 Then God said
Genesis1:10 God named the dry land earth
Genesis1:11 Then God said
Genesis1:12 The earth grew grass and plants that made grain. And it grew trees that made fruit with seeds in it. Every plant made its own kind of seeds. And God saw that this was good.
Genesis1:13 There was evening
Genesis1:14 Then God said
Genesis1:15 They will be in the sky to shine light on the earth. And it happened.
Genesis1:16 So God made the two large lights. He made the larger light to rule during the day and the smaller light to rule during the night. He also made the stars.
Genesis1:17 God put these lights in the sky to shine on the earth.
Genesis1:18 He put them in the sky to rule over the day and over the night. They separated the light from the darkness. And God saw that this was good.
Genesis1:19 There was evening
Genesis1:20 Then God said
Genesis1:21 So God created the large sea animals. He created all the many living things in the sea and every kind of bird that flies in the air. And God saw that this was good.
Genesis1:22 God blessed all the living things in the sea and told them to have many babies and fill the seas. And he blessed the birds on land and told them to have many more babies.
Genesis1:23 There was evening
Genesis1:24 Then God said
Genesis1:25 So God made every kind of animal. He made the wild animals
Genesis1:26 Then God said
Genesis1:27 So God created humans in his own image. He created them to be like himself. He created them male and female.
Genesis1:28 God blessed them and said to them
Genesis1:29 God said
Genesis1:30 And I am giving all the green plants to the animals. These green plants will be their food. Every animal on earth
Genesis1:31 God looked at everything he had made. And he saw that everything was very good. There was evening
Genesis2:1 So the earth
Genesis2:2 God finished the work he was doing
Genesis2:3 God blessed the seventh day and made it a holy day. He made it special because on that day he rested from all the work he did while creating the world.
Genesis2:4 This is the story about the creation of the sky and the earth. This is what happened when the Lord God made the earth and the sky.
Genesis2:5 This was before there were plants on the earth. Nothing was growing in the fields because the Lord God had not yet made it rain on the earth
Genesis2:6 So water came up from the earth and spread over the ground.
Genesis2:7 Then the Lord God took dust from the ground and made a man. He breathed the breath of life into the man's nose
Genesis2:8 Then the Lord God planted a garden in the East
Genesis2:9 Then the Lord God caused all the beautiful trees that were good for food to grow in the garden. In the middle of the garden
Genesis2:10 A river flowed from Eden and watered the garden. The river then separated and became four smaller rivers.
Genesis2:11 The name of the first river was Pishon. This river flowed around the entire country of Havilah.
Genesis2:12 (There is gold in that country
Genesis2:13 The name of the second river was Gihon. This river flowed around the entire country of Cush.
Genesis2:14 The name of the third river was Tigris. This river flowed east of Assyria. The fourth river was the Euphrates.
Genesis2:15 The Lord God put the man in the Garden of Eden to work the soil and take care of the garden.
Genesis2:16 The Lord God gave him this command: You may eat from any tree in the garden.
Genesis2:17 But you must not eat from the tree that gives knowledge about good and evil. If you eat fruit from that tree
Genesis2:18 Then the Lord God said
Genesis2:19 The Lord God used dust from the ground and made every animal in the fields and every bird in the air. He brought all these animals to the man
Genesis2:20 The man gave names to all the tame animals
Genesis2:21 So the Lord God caused the man to sleep very deeply. While he was asleep
Genesis2:22 The Lord God used the rib from the man to make a woman. Then he brought the woman to the man.
Genesis2:23 And the man said
Genesis2:24 That is why a man leaves his father and mother and is joined to his wife. In this way two people become one.

Genesis4:20 Adah gave birth to Jabal. Jabal was the father of people who live in tents and earn their living by keeping cattle.

Genesis8:2 Rain stopped falling from the sky
Genesis8:3 The water that covered the earth began to go down. After 150 days the water was low enough that the boat touched land again. The boat stopped on one of the mountains of Ararat. This was the 17 day of the seventh month.
Genesis8:4 ***
Genesis8:5 The water continued to go down
Genesis8:6 Forty days later Noah opened the window he had made in the boat.
Genesis8:7 Then he sent out a raven. The raven flew from place to place until the ground was dry and the water was gone.
Genesis8:8 Noah also sent out a dove. He wanted it to find dry ground. He wanted to know if water still covered the earth.
Genesis8:9 The dove could not find a place to rest because water still covered the earth
Genesis8:10 After seven days Noah again sent out the dove.
Genesis8:11 And that afternoon the dove came back to Noah. The dove had a fresh olive leaf in its mouth. This was a sign to show Noah that there was dry ground on the earth.
Genesis8:12 Seven days later Noah sent the dove out again. But this time the dove didn't come back.
Genesis8:13 After that Noah opened the door of the boat. He looked and saw that the ground was dry. This was the first day of the first month of the year. He was 601 years old.

Exodus20:1 Then God said
Exodus20:2 I am the Lord your God. I led you out of the land of Egypt where you were slaves. These are the commands I give you:
Exodus20:3 You must not worship any other gods except me.
Exodus20:4 You must not make any idols. Don't make any statues or pictures of anything up in the sky or of anything on the earth or of anything down in the water.
Exodus20:5 Don't worship or serve idols of any kind
Exodus20:6 But I will be very kind to people who love me and obey my commands. I will be kind to their families for thousands of generations.
Exodus20:7 You must not use the name of the Lord your God to make empty promises. If you do
Exodus20:8 You must remember to keep the Sabbath a special day.
Exodus20:9 You may work six days a week to do your job.
Exodus20:10 But the seventh day is a day of rest in honor of the Lord your God. So on that day no one should work—not you
Exodus20:11 That is because the Lord worked six days and made the sky
Exodus20:12 You must honor and respect your father and your mother. Do this so that you will have a full life in the land that the Lord your God gives you.
Exodus20:13 You must not murder anyone.
Exodus20:14 You must not commit adultery.
Exodus20:15 You must not steal anything.
Exodus20:16 You must not tell lies about other people.
Exodus20:17 You must not want to take your neighbor's house. You must not want his wife. And you must not want his men and women servants or his cattle or donkeys. You must not want to take anything that belongs to another person.
Exodus20:18 During all this time
Exodus20:19 Then the people said to Moses
Exodus20:20 Then Moses said to them
Exodus20:21 The people stood far away from the mountain while Moses went to the dark cloud where God was.
Exodus20:22 Then the Lord told Moses to say this to the Israelites: You people have seen that I talked with you from heaven.
Exodus20:23 So you must not make idols using gold or silver to compete with me. You must not make these false gods.
Exodus20:24 Make a special altar for me. You should use dirt to make this altar. Offer sheep and cattle as burnt offerings and fellowship offerings on this altar. Do this in every place where I tell you to remember me. Then I will come and bless you.
Exodus20:25 But if you use stones to make an altar
Exodus20:26 And you must not make steps leading up to the altar. If there are steps
Exodus21:1 Then God said to Moses
Exodus21:2 If you buy a Hebrew slave
Exodus21:3 If he is not married when he becomes your slave
Exodus21:4 If the slave is not married
Exodus21:5 But if the slave decides that he wants to stay with the master
Exodus21:6 If this happens
Exodus21:7 A man might decide to sell his daughter as a slave. If this happens
Exodus21:8 If the master who chose her for himself is not pleased with her
Exodus21:9 If the master promised to let the slave woman marry his son
Exodus21:10 If the master marries another woman
Exodus21:11 The man must do these three things for her. If he does not
Exodus21:12 Whoever hits and kills someone must be killed too.
Exodus21:13 But if an accident happens
Exodus21:14 But whoever plans to kill someone out of anger or hatred must be punished. Take them away from my altar and kill them.
Exodus21:15 Whoever hits their father or their mother must be killed.
Exodus21:16 Whoever steals someone to sell them as a slave or to keep them for their own slave must be killed.
Exodus21:17 Whoever curses their father or mother must be killed.
Exodus21:18 Two men might argue and one might hit the other with a rock or with his fist. If the man who was hurt is not killed
Exodus21:19 If the man was hurt and must stay in bed for some time
Exodus21:20 Sometimes people beat their slaves. If the slave dies after being beaten
Exodus21:21 But if the slave gets up after a few days
Exodus21:22 Two men might be fighting and hurt a pregnant woman. This might make the woman give birth to her baby before its time. If the woman was not hurt badly
Exodus21:23 But if the woman was hurt badly
Exodus21:24 You must trade an eye for an eye
Exodus21:25 a burn for a burn
Exodus21:26 If a man hits a slave in the eye
Exodus21:27 If a master hits his slave in the mouth
Exodus21:28 If a man's bull kills a man or woman
Exodus21:29 But if the bull had hurt people in the past
Exodus21:30 But the family of the dead man may accept money. If they accept money
Exodus21:31 This same law must be followed if the bull kills someone's son or daughter.
Exodus21:32 But if the bull kills a slave
Exodus21:33 A man might take a cover off a well or dig a hole and not cover it. If another man's animal comes and falls into that hole
Exodus21:34 The man who owns the hole must pay for the animal. But after he pays for the animal
Exodus21:35 If one man's bull kills another man's bull
Exodus21:36 But if a man's bull has hurt other animals in the past
Exodus22:1 How should you punish a man who steals a bull or a sheep? If the man kills the animal or sells it
Exodus22:2 If he owns nothing

Exodus22:4 ***

Exodus22:6 A man might start a fire to burn thornbushes on his field. But if the fire grows and burns his neighbor's crops or the grain growing on the neighbor's field
Exodus22:7 Someone might give some money or tools to a neighbor for safekeeping. What should you do if someone steals those things from the neighbor's house? If you find the one who stole them
Exodus22:8 If you don't find the thief
Exodus22:9 What should you do if two men disagree about a bull or a donkey or sheep or clothing or something that is lost? One man says
Exodus22:10 A man might ask his neighbor to take care of an animal for a short time. It might be a donkey
Exodus22:11 That neighbor must explain that he did not steal the animal. If this is true
Exodus22:12 But if the neighbor stole the animal
Exodus22:13 If wild animals killed the animal
Exodus22:14 If a man borrows an animal from his neighbor
Exodus22:15 But if the owner was there
Exodus22:16 If a man has sexual relations with a virgin who he is not engaged to
Exodus22:17 If the father refuses to allow his daughter to marry him
Exodus22:18 You must not allow any woman to do evil magic. If she does magic
Exodus22:19 You must not allow anyone to have sexual relations with an animal. If this happens
Exodus22:20 Whoever makes a sacrifice to a false god should be destroyed. The Lord is the only one you should make sacrifices to.
Exodus22:21 Remember
Exodus22:22 You must never do anything bad to women whose husbands are dead or to orphans.
Exodus22:23 If you do anything wrong to these widows or orphans
Exodus22:24 And I will be very angry. I will kill you with a sword. Then your wives will become widows
Exodus22:25 If you loan money to any of my people
Exodus22:26 You might take their cloak to make sure they pay the money back
Exodus22:27 That cloak might be their only protection against the cold when they lie down to sleep. If they call to me for help
Exodus22:28 You must not curse God or the leaders of your people.
Exodus22:29 At harvest time you should give me the first grain and the first juice from your fruit. Don't wait until late in the year. Give me your firstborn sons.
Exodus22:30 Also
Exodus22:31 You are my special people. So don't eat the meat from something that was killed by wild animals. Let the dogs eat that dead animal.

Exodus23:2 Don't do something just because everyone else is doing it. If a group of people are doing wrong
Exodus23:3 In court
Exodus23:4 If you see a lost bull or donkey
Exodus23:5 If you see an animal that cannot walk because it has too much to carry
Exodus23:6 In court
Exodus23:7 Be very careful if you say that someone is guilty of something. Don't make false charges against a person. Never allow innocent people to be killed as punishment for something they did not do. Whoever kills an innocent person is evil
Exodus23:8 If someone tries to pay you to agree with them when they are wrong
Exodus23:9 You must never do wrong things to a foreigner. Remember
Exodus23:10 Plant seeds
Exodus23:11 But the seventh year must be a special time of rest for the land. Don't plant anything in your fields. If any crops grow there
Exodus23:12 Work for six days
Exodus23:13 Be sure that you obey all these laws. Don't worship false gods. You should not even speak their names!
Exodus23:14 You will have three special festivals each year. You must come to my special place to worship me during these festivals.
Exodus23:15 The first festival is the Festival of Unleavened Bread. This is as I commanded you. During this time you will eat bread that is made without yeast. This will continue for seven days. You will do this during the month of Abib
Exodus23:16 The second festival will be the Festival of Weeks. This festival will be during the early summer when you begin harvesting the first crops that you planted in your fields. The third festival will be the Festival of Shelters. This will be in the fall
Exodus23:17 So three times each year all the men will come to the special place to be with the Lord God.
Exodus23:18 When you kill an animal and offer its blood as a sacrifice
Exodus23:19 When you gather your crops at harvest time
Exodus23:20 I am sending an angel before you to protect you along the way and to lead you to the place that I have prepared for you.
Exodus23:21 Obey the angel and follow him. Be careful in his presence
Exodus23:22 If you listen to what he says and do everything I tell you
Exodus23:23 My angel will lead you through the land. He will lead you against many different people—the Amorites
Exodus23:24 Don't worship their gods. Don't ever bow down to those gods. You must never live the way those people live. You must destroy their idols. And you must break the stones that help them remember their gods.
Exodus23:25 You must serve the Lord your God. If you do this
Exodus23:26 Your women will all be able to have babies. None of their babies will die at birth. And I will allow you to live long lives.
Exodus23:27 When you fight against your enemies
Exodus23:28 I will send the hornet in front of you. He will force your enemies to leave. The Hivites
Exodus23:29 But I will not force all of them out of your land quickly. I will not do this in only one year. The land will be empty if I force the people out too fast. Then all the wild animals would increase and control the land. And they would be much trouble for you.
Exodus23:30 So I will force them out of your land very slowly. I will do this as you grow and eventually take the land to be your own.
Exodus23:31 I will give you all the land from the Red Sea to the Euphrates River. The western border will be the Philistine Sea
Exodus23:32 You must not make any agreements with any of those people or their gods.
Exodus23:33 Don't let them stay in your country. If you let them stay

Exodus25:10 Use acacia wood and build a special box. This Holy Box must be 2?cubits long

Exodus25:15 These poles should always stay in the rings of the Box. Don't take the poles out.

Exodus25:24 Cover the table with pure gold and put gold trim around it.
Exodus25:25 Then make a frame 1 handbreadth wide around the table. And put gold trim on the frame.
Exodus25:26 Then make four gold rings and put them on the four corners of the table
Exodus25:27 Put the rings close to the frame around the top of the table. These rings will hold the poles used to carry the table.
Exodus25:28 Use acacia wood to make the poles
Exodus25:29 Make the plates
Exodus25:30 Put the special bread before me on the table. It must always be there in front of me.
Exodus25:31 Then you must make a lampstand. Use pure gold and hammer it to make the base and the shaft. Make flowers
Exodus25:32 The lampstand must have six branches—three branches on one side
Exodus25:33 Each branch must have three flowers. Make these flowers like almond flowers with buds and petals.
Exodus25:34 Make four more flowers for the lampstand. These flowers must be made like almond flowers with buds and petals.
Exodus25:35 There will be six branches on the lampstand—three branches coming out from each side of the shaft. Make a flower with buds and petals below each of the three places where the branches join the shaft.
Exodus25:36 The whole lampstand with the flowers and branches must be made from pure gold. All this gold must be hammered and joined together into one piece.
Exodus25:37 Then make seven lamps to go on the lampstand. These lamps will give light to the area in front of the lampstand.
Exodus25:38 Use pure gold to make the wick trimmers and the trays.
Exodus25:39 Use 75 pounds of pure gold to make the lampstand and the things to be used with it.
Exodus25:40 Be very careful to make everything exactly the way I showed you on the mountain.
Exodus26:1 The Holy Tent should be made from ten curtains. These curtains must be made from fine linen and blue
Exodus26:2 Make each curtain the same size. Each curtain should be 28 cubits long and 4 cubits wide.
Exodus26:3 Join the curtains together into two groups. Join five curtains together to make one group and join five curtains together to make the other group.
Exodus26:4 Use blue cloth to make loops along the edge of the end curtain in one group. Do the same on the end curtain in the other group.
Exodus26:5 There must be 50 loops on the end curtain of the first group. And there must be 50 loops on the end curtain of the other group. These loops must be opposite each other.
Exodus26:6 Then make 50 gold rings to join the curtains together. This will join the Holy Tent together into one piece.
Exodus26:7 Make another tent that will cover the Holy Tent. Use eleven curtains to make this tent. Make these curtains from goat hair.
Exodus26:8 All these curtains must be the same size. They must be 30 cubits long and 4 cubits wide.
Exodus26:9 Join five of the curtains together into one group. Then join the other six curtains together into another group. Fold back half of the sixth curtain at the front of the Tent.
Exodus26:10 Make 50 loops down the edge of the end curtain of one group. Do the same for the end curtain of the other group.
Exodus26:11 Then make 50 bronze rings to join the curtains together. This will join the tent together into one piece.
Exodus26:12 Half of the end curtain of this tent will hang down below the back edge of the Holy Tent.
Exodus26:13 On the sides
Exodus26:14 Make two coverings to go over the outer tent. One covering should be made from ram skins dyed red. The other covering should be made from fine leather.
Exodus26:15 Use acacia wood to make frames to support the Holy Tent.
Exodus26:16 The frames should be 10 cubits high and 1?cubits wide.
Exodus26:17 Two side poles should be joined together with cross pieces to make each frame. All the frames for the Holy Tent must be the same.
Exodus26:18 Make 20 frames for the south side of the Holy Tent.
Exodus26:19 And make 40 silver bases for the frames. Each frame should have two silver bases to go under it—one base for each side pole.
Exodus26:20 Make 20 more frames for the other side (the north side) of the Holy Tent.
Exodus26:21 And make 40 silver bases for these frames— two bases under each frame.
Exodus26:22 Make six more frames for the back (the west side) of the Holy Tent.
Exodus26:23 Make two frames for the corners at the back of the Holy Tent.
Exodus26:24 The frames at the corners should be joined together at the bottom. At the top a ring will hold the frames together. Do the same for both corners.
Exodus26:25 There will be a total of eight frames for the west end of the Tent. There will be 16 silver bases—two bases under each frame.
Exodus26:26 Use acacia wood and make braces for the frames of the Holy Tent. There should be five braces for the first side of the Holy Tent.
Exodus26:27 And there should be five braces for the frames on the other side of the Holy Tent. And there should be five braces for the frames at the back (the west side) of the Holy Tent.
Exodus26:28 The middle brace should pass through the frames from one end to the other.
Exodus26:29 Cover the frames with gold. And make rings for the frames to hold the braces. Make these rings from gold. Also cover the braces with gold.
Exodus26:30 Build the Holy Tent the way I showed you on the mountain.
Exodus26:31 Use fine linen and make a special curtain for the inside of the Holy Tent. Use blue
Exodus26:32 Make four posts from acacia wood
Exodus26:33 Put the curtain under the gold rings. Then put the Box of the Agreement behind the curtain. This curtain will separate the Holy Place from the Most Holy Place.
Exodus26:34 Put the mercy-cover on the Box of the Agreement in the Most Holy Place.
Exodus26:35 Put the special table you made outside of the curtain. The table should be on the north side of the Holy Tent. Then put the lampstand on the south side
Exodus26:36 Then make a curtain to cover the entrance to the Holy Tent. Use blue
Exodus26:37 Make gold hooks for this curtain. Make five posts using acacia wood covered with gold and make five bronze bases for the five posts.
Exodus27:1 Use acacia wood and build an altar. The altar should be square. It must be 5 cubits long
Exodus27:2 Make a horn for each of the four corners of the altar. Join each horn to its corner so that everything is one piece. Then cover the altar with bronze.
Exodus27:3 Use bronze to make all the tools and dishes that will be used on the altar. Make pots
Exodus27:4 Make a grating for the altar. This grating will be shaped like a net. And make a bronze ring at each of the four corners of the grating.
Exodus27:5 Put the grating under the ledge at the bottom of the altar. The grating will go halfway up into the altar from below.
Exodus27:6 Use acacia wood to make poles for the altar
Exodus27:7 Put the poles through the rings on both sides of the altar. Use these poles for carrying the altar.
Exodus27:8 Make the altar like an empty box with the sides made from boards. Make the altar just as I showed you on the mountain.
Exodus27:9 Make a courtyard for the Holy Tent. The south side should have a wall of curtains 100 cubits long. These curtains must be made from fine linen.
Exodus27:10 Use 20 posts and 20 bronze bases under the posts. The hooks for the posts and the curtain rods should be made from silver.
Exodus27:11 The north side must also have a wall of curtains 100 cubits long. It must have 20 posts and 20 bronze bases. The hooks for the posts and the curtain rods must be made from silver.
Exodus27:12 On the west side of the courtyard there must be a wall of curtains 50 cubits long. There must be ten posts and ten bases.
Exodus27:13 The east side of the courtyard must also be 50 cubits long.
Exodus27:14 Here at entrance to the courtyard
Exodus27:15 The other side of the entrance must also have curtains 15 cubits long. There must be three posts and three bases on that side.
Exodus27:16 Make a curtain 20 cubits long to cover the entrance to the courtyard. Make that curtain from fine linen and blue
Exodus27:17 All the posts around the courtyard must be joined with silver curtain rods. The hooks on the posts must be made from silver
Exodus27:18 The courtyard should be 100 cubits long and 50 cubits wide. The wall of curtains around the courtyard should be 5 cubits high. The curtains must be made from fine linen. The bases under the posts must be bronze.
Exodus27:19 All the tools
Exodus27:20 Command the Israelites to bring their best olive oil for the lamp that must be lit each evening.
Exodus27:21 This lamp is in the first room of the Meeting Tent
Exodus28:1 Tell your brother Aaron and his sons
Exodus28:2 Make special clothes for your brother Aaron. These clothes will give him honor and respect.
Exodus28:3 I have given special wisdom to some of the skilled men. Tell them to make the clothes for Aaron. These clothes will show that he serves me in a special way. Then he can serve me as a priest.
Exodus28:4 These are the clothes the men should make: the judgment pouch
Exodus28:5 Tell the men to use gold thread
Exodus28:6 Use gold thread
Exodus28:7 At each shoulder of the ephod
Exodus28:8 The men will very carefully weave a cloth belt for the ephod. This belt must be made the same way as the ephod—use gold threads
Exodus28:9 Take two onyx stones. Write the names of the twelve sons of Israel on these jewels.
Exodus28:10 Write six names on one jewel and six names on the other jewel. Write the names in order
Exodus28:11 Cut the names of the sons of Israel on these stones. Do this the way a worker makes a seal. Put the jewels in gold settings.
Exodus28:12 Then put these two jewels on the shoulder pieces of the ephod. Aaron will wear this special coat when he stands before the Lord. And the two stones with the names of the sons of Israel will be on the ephod. These jewels will cause the Lord to remember the Israelites.
Exodus28:13 Use fine gold to hold the stones on the ephod.
Exodus28:14 Twist chains of pure gold together like a rope. Make two of these gold chains and fasten them to the gold settings.
Exodus28:15 Make the judgment pouch for the high priest. Skilled workers should make this pouch just as they made the ephod. They must use gold threads
Exodus28:16 The judgment pouch should be folded double to make a square pocket. It should be 1 span long and 1 span wide.
Exodus28:17 Put four rows of beautiful jewels on the judgment pouch. The first row of jewels should have a ruby
Exodus28:18 The second row should have a turquoise
Exodus28:19 The third row should have a jacinth
Exodus28:20 The fourth row should have a chrysolite
Exodus28:21 There will be twelve jewels on the judgment pouch—one jewel for each of the sons of Israel. Write the name of one of the sons of Israel on each of the stones. Cut these names into each stone like a worker makes a seal.
Exodus28:22 Make chains of pure gold for the judgment pouch. These chains must be braided like a rope.
Exodus28:23 Make two gold rings and put them on two corners of the judgment pouch.
Exodus28:24 Put the two golden chains through the two rings at the corners of the judgment pouch.
Exodus28:25 Fasten the other ends of the gold chains to the two settings. This will fasten them to the two shoulder pieces of the ephod on the front.
Exodus28:26 Make two more gold rings and put them on the other two corners of the judgment pouch. This will be on the inside edge of the judgment pouch next to the ephod.
Exodus28:27 Make two more gold rings and put them on the bottom of the shoulder pieces on the front of the ephod. Put the gold rings above the cloth belt of the ephod.
Exodus28:28 Use blue ribbon to tie the rings of the judgment pouch to the rings of the ephod. In this way the judgment pouch will rest close to the cloth belt and will be held against the ephod.
Exodus28:29 When Aaron enters the Holy Place
Exodus28:30 Put the Urim and Thummim inside the judgment pouch. They will be over Aaron's heart when he goes before the Lord. So Aaron will always carry with him a way of judging for the Israelites when he is before the Lord.
Exodus28:31 Make a blue robe for the ephod.
Exodus28:32 Make a hole in the center for the head. And sew a piece of cloth around the edge of this hole. This cloth will be like a collar that keeps the hole from tearing.
Exodus28:33 Use blue
Exodus28:34 So around the bottom edge of the robe there should be bells and pomegranates. There should be a bell following each pomegranate.
Exodus28:35 Aaron will wear this robe when he serves as a priest. The bells will ring as Aaron goes into the Holy Place to stand before the Lord
Exodus28:36 Mrie a strip of pure gold and carve words into the gold the way people make a seal. Write these words: holy to the lord.
Exodus28:37 Fasten the gold strip to a blue ribbon. Tie the blue ribbon around the turban. The gold strip should be on the front of the turban.
Exodus28:38 Aaron will wear this on his head. In this way he will remove the guilt if anything is wrong with the gifts that the Israelites give to God. Aaron will always wear this on his head so that the Lord will accept the gifts of the people.
Exodus28:39 Use fine linen to make the white woven robe and the turban. The cloth belt should have designs sewn into it.
Exodus28:40 Also make coats
Exodus28:41 Put the clothes on your brother Aaron and his sons. Then pour the special oil on them to make them priests. This will make them holy
Exodus28:42 Use linen to make underclothes for the priests. These underclothes will cover them from the waist to the thighs.
Exodus28:43 Aaron and his sons must wear these clothes anytime they enter the Meeting Tent. They must wear these clothes when they come near to the altar to serve as priests in the Holy Place. If they don't wear these clothes
Exodus29:1 Now I will tell you what you must do to show that Aaron and his sons serve me in a special way as priests. Find one young bull and two rams that have nothing wrong with them.
Exodus29:2 Then use fine wheat flour without yeast to make bread. And use the same things to make cakes mixed with olive oil. Make small thin cakes spread with oil.
Exodus29:3 Put this bread and the cakes in a basket. Then give the basket to Aaron and his sons. At the same time
Exodus29:4 Then bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the Meeting Tent. Wash them with water.
Exodus29:5 Put the special clothes on Aaron. Put on him the white woven robe and the blue robe that is worn with the ephod. Put the ephod and the judgment pouch on him. Then tie the beautiful cloth belt on him.
Exodus29:6 Put the turban on his head and the special crown around the turban.
Exodus29:7 Take the anointing oil and pour it on Aaron's head. This will show that he is chosen for this work.
Exodus29:8 Then bring Aaron's sons to that place. Put the white woven robes on them.
Exodus29:9 Then tie cloth belts around their waists. Give them the special hats to wear. At that time they will begin to be priests. They will be priests because of the special law that will continue forever. This is the way you will make Aaron and his sons priests.
Exodus29:10 Then bring the bull to that place at the front of the Meeting Tent. Aaron and his sons must put their hands on the bull's head.
Exodus29:11 Then kill the bull there in the Lord's presence at the entrance to the Meeting Tent.
Exodus29:12 Then take some of the bull's blood and go to the altar. Use your finger to put some blood on the horns of the altar. Pour all the blood that is left at the bottom of the altar.
Exodus29:13 Then take all the fat from inside the bull
Exodus29:14 Then take the bull's meat
Exodus29:15 Then tell Aaron and his sons to put their hands on the head of one of the rams.
Exodus29:16 Kill that ram and save the blood. Throw the blood against the altar on all four sides.
Exodus29:17 Then cut the ram into several pieces. Wash all the parts from inside the ram and the legs. Put these things with the head and the other pieces of the ram.
Exodus29:18 Then burn everything on the altar. It is a burnt offering to the Lord. It is a sweet-smelling gift to the Lord.
Exodus29:19 Tell Aaron and his sons to put their hands on the other ram.
Exodus29:20 Kill that ram and save some of its blood. Put that blood on the right ear lobes of Aaron and his sons. Also put some of the blood on the thumbs of their right hands and on the big toes of their right feet. Then throw blood against all four sides of the altar.
Exodus29:21 Then take some of the blood from the altar. Mix it with the special oil and sprinkle it on Aaron and his clothes. And sprinkle it on his sons and their clothes. This will show that Aaron and his sons serve me in a special way. And it will show that their clothes are used only at special times.
Exodus29:22 Then take the fat from the ram. (This is the ram that will be used in the ceremony to make Aaron the high priest.) Take the fat from around the tail and the fat that covers the organs inside the body. Then take the fat that covers the liver
Exodus29:23 Then take the basket of bread that you made without yeast. This is the basket you put before the Lord. Take these things out of the basket: one loaf of bread
Exodus29:24 Give them to Aaron and his sons. Tell them to hold these things in their hands before the Lord. This will be a special offering to the Lord.
Exodus29:25 Then take them from Aaron and his sons and put them on the altar with the ram. Then burn everything on the altar. It is a burnt offering to the Lord. It is a sweetsmelling gift to the Lord.
Exodus29:26 Then take the breast from the ram. (This is the ram that will be used in the ceremony to make Aaron the high priest.) Hold the breast of the ram before the Lord as a special offering. Then take it back and keep it. This part of the animal will be for you.
Exodus29:27 Take the breast and the leg of the ram that was used to make Aaron the high priest and make these parts holy. Then give these special parts to Aaron and his sons.
Exodus29:28 The Israelites will always give Aaron and his sons these parts. These parts will always belong to the priests when the Israelites make an offering to the Lord. When they give these parts to the priest
Exodus29:29 Save these special clothes that were made for Aaron and his descendants. They will wear these clothes when they are chosen to be priests.
Exodus29:30 Aaron's son will become the next high priest after him. That son will wear these clothes seven days when he comes to the Meeting Tent to serve in the Holy Place.
Exodus29:31 Cook the meat from the ram that was used to make Aaron the high priest. Cook that meat in a holy place.
Exodus29:32 Then Aaron and his sons must eat the meat at the front door of the Meeting Tent. And they must also eat the bread that is in the basket.
Exodus29:33 These offerings were used to take away their sins when they were made priests. Now they should eat these offerings.
Exodus29:34 If any of the meat from that ram or any of the bread is left the next morning
Exodus29:35 You must do all these things for Aaron and his sons. You must do them exactly as I told you. The ceremony for appointing them to be priests must continue for seven days.
Exodus29:36 You must kill one bull every day for seven days. This will be an offering for the sins of Aaron and his sons. You will use these sacrifices to make the altar pure
Exodus29:37 You will make the altar pure and holy for seven days. At that time the altar will be most holy. Anything that touches the altar will also be holy.
Exodus29:38 Every day you must make an offering on the altar. You must kill two lambs that are one year old.
Exodus29:39 Offer one lamb in the morning and the other in the evening.
Exodus29:40 When you kill the first lamb
Exodus29:41 ***
Exodus29:42 You must burn these things as an offering to the Lord every day. Do this at the entrance of the Meeting Tent before the Lord. Continue to do this for all time. When you make the offering
Exodus29:43 I will meet with the Israelites at that place
Exodus29:44 So I will make the Meeting Tent and the altar holy. I will also make Aaron and his sons holy so that they can serve me as priests.
Exodus29:45 I will live with the Israelites. I will be their God.
Exodus29:46 The people will know that I am the Lord
Exodus30:1 Make an altar from acacia wood. You will use this altar for burning incense.
Exodus30:2 You must make the altar square—1 cubit long and 1 cubit wide. It must be 2 cubits high. There will be horns at the four corners. These horns must be made as one piece with the altar.
Exodus30:3 Cover the top
Exodus30:4 Below this trim there should be two gold rings. There should be two gold rings on opposite sides of the altar. These gold rings will be used with poles to carry the altar.
Exodus30:5 Make the poles from acacia wood and cover them with gold.
Exodus30:6 Put the altar just outside the special curtain that hangs in front of the Box of the Agreement. So the altar will be in front of the mercy-cover that is above the Agreement. This is the place where I will meet with you.
Exodus30:7 Aaron must burn sweet-smelling incense on the altar every morning. He will do this when he comes to care for the lamps.
Exodus30:8 He must burn incense again when he checks the lamps in the evenings so that incense will be burned before the Lord every day forever.
Exodus30:9 Don't use this altar for offering any other kind of incense or burnt offering or for any kind of grain offering or drink offering.
Exodus30:10 Once a year Aaron must make a special sacrifice. He will use the blood of the sin offering to erase the sins of the people. He will do this at the horns of this altar. This day will be called the Day of Atonement
Exodus30:11 The Lord said to Moses
Exodus30:12 Count the Israelites so that you will know how many people there are. Every time this is done
Exodus30:13 Each man who is counted must pay?shekel of silver. (That is?shekel by the official measure. This shekel weighs 20 gerahs. ) This half shekel is an offering to the Lord.
Exodus30:14 Every man who is at least 20 years old must be counted. And every man who is counted must give the Lord this offering.
Exodus30:15 The rich must not give more than?shekel
Exodus30:16 Gather this money from the Israelites. Use the money for the service in the Meeting Tent. This payment will be a way for the Lord to remember his people. They will be paying for their own lives.
Exodus30:17 The Lord said to Moses
Exodus30:18 Make a bronze bowl and put it on a bronze base. You will use this for washing. Put the bowl between the Meeting Tent and the altar. Fill the bowl with water.
Exodus30:19 Aaron and his sons must wash their hands and feet with the water from this bowl.
Exodus30:20 Every time they enter the Meeting Tent
Exodus30:21 They must wash their hands and their feet so that they will not die. This will be a law that continues forever for Aaron and his people who will live in the future.
Exodus30:22 Then the Lord said to Moses
Exodus30:23 Find the finest spices. Get 12 pounds of liquid myrrh
Exodus30:24 and 12 pounds of cassia. Use the official measure to measure all these things. Also get 1 gallon of olive oil.
Exodus30:25 Mix all these things to make a special sweet-smelling anointing oil.
Exodus30:26 Pour this oil on the Meeting Tent and on the Box of the Agreement. This will show that these things have a special purpose.
Exodus30:27 Pour the oil on the table and on all the dishes on the table. And pour this oil on the lamp and on all its tools. Pour the oil on the incense altar.
Exodus30:28 Also
Exodus30:29 You will make all these things holy. They will be very special to the Lord. Anything that touches these things will also become holy.
Exodus30:30 Pour the oil on Aaron and his sons to show that they are separated from the rest of the people to serve as priests.
Exodus30:31 Tell the Israelites that the anointing oil is holy—it must always be used only for me.
Exodus30:32 This oil is holy and you must treat it as something special. Don't use the same formula for making perfume and don't let people use this oil like an ordinary perfume.
Exodus30:33 Whoever makes a perfume like that and puts it on anyone except a priest must be separated from the people.
Exodus30:34 Then the Lord said to Moses
Exodus30:35 Mix the spices together to make a sweet-smelling incense. Do this the same as a perfume maker would do it. Also mix salt with this incense. This will make it pure and special.
Exodus30:36 Grind some of the incense until it becomes a fine powder. Put the powder in front of the Holy Box that holds the Agreement in the Meeting Tent. This is the place where I meet with you. You must use this incense powder only for its very special purpose.
Exodus30:37 You must use it only in this special way for the Lord. You must also make it in a special way. Don't make any other incense in this way.
Exodus30:38 There may be people who will want to make some of this incense for themselves so that they can enjoy the smell. But whoever does this must be separated from their people.

Exodus31:15 There are six other days in the week for working. But the seventh day is a very special day of rest. That is the special day to honor the Lord. Anyone who works during the Sabbath must be killed.
Exodus31:16 The Israelites must remember the Sabbath and make it a special day. They must continue to do this forever. It is an agreement between them and me that will continue forever.

Exodus34:1 Then the Lord said to Moses
Exodus34:2 Be ready tomorrow morning and come up on Mount Sinai. Stand before me there on the top of the mountain.
Exodus34:3 No one will be allowed to come with you. No one should even be seen anywhere on the mountain. Even your herds of animals or flocks of sheep will not be allowed to eat grass at the bottom of the mountain.
Exodus34:4 So Moses made two more stone tablets like the first ones. Early the next morning Moses went up Mount Sinai
Exodus34:5 Then the Lord came down to him in a cloud
Exodus34:6 That is
Exodus34:7 He shows his faithful love for thousands of generations. The Lord forgives people for the wrong things they do

Exodus34:9 Lord
Exodus34:10 Then the Lord said
Exodus34:11 Obey what I command you today
Exodus34:12 Be careful! Don't make any agreement with the people who live in the land where you are going. If you make an agreement with them
Exodus34:13 So destroy their altars
Exodus34:14 Don't worship any other god. I am Yahweh Kanah—the jealous Lord. That is my name. I hate for my people to worship other gods.
Exodus34:15 Be careful not to make any agreements with the people who live in that land. If you do this
Exodus34:16 You might choose some of their daughters as wives for your sons. Those daughters serve false gods. They might lead your sons to do the same thing.
Exodus34:17 Don't make idols.
Exodus34:18 Celebrate the Festival of Unleavened Bread. For seven days eat the bread made without yeast as I commanded you before. Do this during the month I have chosen
Exodus34:19 A woman's first baby always belongs to me. Even the first animals that are born from your cattle or sheep belong to me.
Exodus34:20 If you want to keep a donkey that is the first born
Exodus34:21 You will work for six days
Exodus34:22 Celebrate the Festival of Weeks. Use the first grain from the wheat harvest for this festival. And in the fall celebrate the Festival of Harvest.

Exodus34:24 When you go into your land
Exodus34:25 When you kill an animal and offer its blood as a sacrifice

Exodus34:27 Then the Lord said to Moses
Exodus34:28 Moses stayed there with the Lord for 40 days and 40 nights. Moses did not eat any food or drink any water. And he wrote the words of the agreement (the Ten Commandments) on the two stone tablets.
