Exodus35:15 the altar for burning incense and its poles; the anointing oil and the sweet-smelling incense; the curtain that covers the door at the entrance to the Holy Tent;
Exodus35:16 the altar for burning offerings and its bronze grating
Exodus35:17 the curtains around the yard
Exodus35:18 the pegs used to support the Tent and the wall of curtains around the courtyard
Exodus35:19 and the special woven clothes for the priest to wear in the Holy Place. These are the special clothes for Aaron the priest and his sons to wear when they serve as priests.
Exodus35:20 Then all the Israelites went away from Moses.
Exodus35:21 All the people who wanted to give came and brought a gift to the Lord. These gifts were used for making the Meeting Tent
Exodus35:22 All the men and women who wanted to give brought gold jewelry of all kinds. They brought pins
Exodus35:23 Everyone who had fine linen and blue
Exodus35:24 Everyone who wanted to give silver or bronze brought that as a gift to the Lord. Everyone who had acacia wood came and gave it to the Lord.
Exodus35:25 Every skilled woman made fine linen and blue
Exodus35:26 And all the women who were skilled and wanted to help made cloth from the goat hair.
Exodus35:27 The leaders brought onyx stones and other jewels. These stones and jewels were put on the ephod and judgment pouch of the priest.
Exodus35:28 The people also brought spices and olive oil. These things were used for the sweet-smelling incense
Exodus35:29 All the Israelites who wanted to help brought gifts to the Lord. They gave these gifts freely
Exodus35:30 Then Moses said to the Israelites
Exodus35:31 The Lord filled Bezalel with the Spirit of God—he gave Bezalel special skill and knowledge to do all kinds of things.
Exodus35:32 He can design and make things with gold
Exodus35:33 He can cut and set stones and jewels. He can work with wood and make all kinds of things.
Exodus35:34 The Lord has given Bezalel and Oholiab special skills to teach other people. (Oholiab was the son of Ahisamach from the tribe of Dan.)
Exodus35:35 The Lord has given both of these men special skill to do all kinds of work. They are able to do the work of carpenters and metal workers. They can weave cloth with designs in it from the blue
Exodus36:1 So Bezalel
Exodus36:2 Then Moses called Bezalel
Exodus36:3 Moses gave them everything the Israelites had brought as gifts
Exodus36:4 Finally
Exodus36:5 The people have brought too much. We have more than we need to finish the work the Lord told us to do.
Exodus36:6 Then Moses sent this message throughout the camp: No man or woman should make anything else as a gift for the holy place. So the people were forced to stop giving more.
Exodus36:7 The people had brought more than enough things to finish the work of building God's holy place.
Exodus36:8 Then the skilled workers began making the Holy Tent. They made the ten curtains from fine linen and blue
Exodus36:9 Each curtain was the same size—28 cubits long and 4 cubits wide.
Exodus36:10 The workers joined the curtains together into two groups of curtains. They joined five curtains together to make one group and five curtains together to make the other group.
Exodus36:11 Then they used blue cloth to make loops along the edge of the end curtain of one group. And they did the same on the end curtain in the other group.
Exodus36:12 There were 50 loops on the end curtain in one group
Exodus36:13 Then they made 50 gold rings to join the two curtains together. So the Holy Tent was joined together into one piece.
Exodus36:14 Then the workers made another tent to cover the Holy Tent. They used goat hair to make eleven curtains.
Exodus36:15 All the curtains were the same size—30 cubits long and 4 cubits wide.
Exodus36:16 The workers joined five curtains together into one group and six curtains together into another group.
Exodus36:17 They put 50 loops along the edge of the end curtain of one group. And they did the same on the end curtain of the other group.
Exodus36:18 The workers made 50 bronze rings to join the two groups of curtains together to form one tent.
Exodus36:19 Then they made two more coverings for the Holy Tent. One covering was made from ram skins dyed red. The other covering was made from fine leather.
Exodus36:20 Then the workers made frames from acacia wood to support the Holy Tent.
Exodus36:21 Each frame was 10 cubits long and 1?cubits wide.
Exodus36:22 There were two side poles joined together with cross pieces to make each frame. Every frame for the Holy Tent was made the same.
Exodus36:23 They made 20 frames for the south side of the Holy Tent.
Exodus36:24 Then they made 40 silver bases for the frames. There were two bases for each frame—one base for each side pole.
Exodus36:25 They also made 20 frames for the other side (the north side) of the Holy Tent.
Exodus36:26 They made 40 silver bases for the frames—two bases for each frame.
Exodus36:27 They made six more frames for the back (the west side) of the Holy Tent.
Exodus36:28 They also made two frames for the corners at the back of the Holy Tent.
Exodus36:29 These frames were joined together at the bottom. And at the top a ring held the corner frames together. They did the same for both corners.
Exodus36:30 There was a total of eight frames for the west side of the Holy Tent. And there were 16 silver bases—two bases for each frame.
Exodus36:31 Then the workers used acacia wood to make the braces for the frames—five braces for the first side of the Holy Tent
Exodus36:32 five braces for the other side
Exodus36:33 They made the middle brace so that it passed through the frames from one end to the other.
Exodus36:34 They covered these frames with gold. Then they used gold to make the rings to hold the braces
Exodus36:35 They used fine linen and blue
Exodus36:36 They made four posts using acacia wood
Exodus36:37 Then they made the curtain to cover the entrance to the Tent. They used blue
Exodus36:38 Then they made the five posts and the hooks for this curtain over the entrance. They covered the tops of the posts and the curtain rods with gold. And they made the five bronze bases for the posts.
Exodus37:1 Bezalel made the Holy Box from acacia wood. The Box was 2?cubits long
Exodus37:2 He covered the inside and outside of the Box with pure gold. Then he put gold trim around the Box.
Exodus37:3 He made four rings of gold and put them on the four corners. These rings were used for carrying the Box. There were two rings on each side.
Exodus37:4 Then he made the poles for carrying the Box. He used acacia wood and covered the poles with pure gold.
Exodus37:5 He put the poles through the rings on each side of the Box.
Exodus37:6 Then he made the mercy-cover from pure gold. It was 2?cubits long and 1?cubits wide.
Exodus37:7 Then Bezalel hammered gold to make two Cherub angels. He put the Cherub angels on each end of the mercy-cover.
Exodus37:8 He put one angel on one end and the other angel on the other end. The angels were joined together with the mercy-cover to make one piece.
Exodus37:9 The wings of the angels were spread up toward the sky. The angels covered the Box with their wings and faced each other
Exodus37:10 Then he made the table from acacia wood. The table was 2 cubits long
Exodus37:11 He covered it with pure gold and put gold trim around it.
Exodus37:12 Then he made a frame 1 handbreadth wide around the table. He put gold trim on the frame.
Exodus37:13 Then he made four gold rings and put them at the four corners of the table
Exodus37:14 He put the rings close to the frame. The rings were to hold the poles used to carry the table.
Exodus37:15 Then he used acacia wood to make the poles for carrying the table. He covered the poles with pure gold.
Exodus37:16 Then he made everything that was used on the table. He made the plates
Exodus37:17 Then he made the lampstand. He used pure gold and hammered it to make the base and the shaft. Then he made flowers
Exodus37:18 The lampstand had six branches—three branches on one side and three branches on the other side.
Exodus37:19 Each branch had three flowers on it. These flowers were made like almond flowers with buds and petals.
Exodus37:20 The shaft of the lampstand had four more flowers. They were also made like almond flowers with buds and petals.
Exodus37:21 There were six branches—three branches coming out from each side of the shaft. And there was a flower with buds and petals below each of the three places where the branches joined the shaft.
Exodus37:22 The whole lampstand
Exodus37:23 He made seven lamps for this lampstand. Then he made wick trimmers and trays from pure gold.
Exodus37:24 He used 75 pounds of pure gold to make the lampstand and the things used with it.
Exodus37:25 He made the altar for burning incense from acacia wood. The altar was square. It was 1 cubit long
Exodus37:26 He covered the top
Exodus37:27 He made two gold rings for the altar. He put the gold rings below the trim on each side of the altar. These gold rings held the poles for carrying the altar.
Exodus37:28 He made the poles from acacia wood and covered them with gold.
Exodus37:29 Then he made the holy anointing oil. He also made the pure
Exodus38:1 Then Bezalel used acacia wood to build the altar. This was the altar used for burning offerings. The altar was square. It was 5 cubits long
Exodus38:2 He made a horn for each of the four corners of the altar. He joined each horn to its corner so that everything was one piece. He covered the altar with bronze.
Exodus38:3 Then he used bronze to make all the tools to be used on the altar. He made the pots
Exodus38:4 Then he made a bronze grating for the altar. This grating was shaped like a net. The grating was put under the ledge at the bottom of the altar. It went halfway up into the altar from below.
Exodus38:5 Then he made bronze rings to hold the poles for carrying the altar. He put the rings at the four corners of the grating.
Exodus38:6 Then he used acacia wood to make the poles and covered them with bronze.
Exodus38:7 He put the poles through the rings on the sides of the altar to carry it. He used boards to make the sides of the altar. It was hollow
Exodus38:8 He made the bowl and its base with bronze. He used the bronze mirrors that the women gave. These were the women who served at the entrance to the Meeting Tent.
Exodus38:9 Then he made a wall of curtains around the courtyard. On the south side
Exodus38:10 The curtains on the south side were supported by 20 posts. The posts were on 20 bronze bases. The hooks for the posts and the curtain rods were made from silver.
Exodus38:11 The north side of the courtyard also had a wall of curtains 100 cubits long. There were 20 posts with 20 bronze bases. The hooks for the posts and the curtain rods were made from silver.
Exodus38:12 On the west side of the courtyard the wall of curtains was 50 cubits long. There were 10 posts and 10 bases. The hooks for the posts and the curtain rods were made from silver.
Exodus38:13 The east side of the courtyard was 50 cubits wide. The entrance to the courtyard was on this side.
Exodus38:14 On one side of the entrance the wall of curtains was 15 cubits long. There were three posts and three bases on this side.
Exodus38:15 The wall of curtains on the other side of the entrance was also 15 cubits long. There were three posts and three bases on that side.
Exodus38:16 All the curtains around the courtyard were made from fine linen.
Exodus38:17 The bases for the posts were made from bronze. The hooks and the curtain rods were made from silver. The tops of the posts were covered with silver also. All the posts in the courtyard had silver curtain rods.
Exodus38:18 The curtain for the entrance of the courtyard was made from fine linen and blue
Exodus38:19 The curtain was supported by four posts and four bronze bases. The hooks on the posts were made from silver. The tops on the posts were covered with silver
Exodus38:20 All the tent pegs for the Holy Tent and for the curtains around the courtyard were made from bronze.
Exodus38:21 Moses commanded the Levites to write down everything that was used to make the Holy Tent
Exodus38:22 Bezalel son of Uri
Exodus38:23 Also Oholiab son of Ahisamach
Exodus38:24 The people gave over a ton of gold as an offering to the Lord for his holy place. (This was weighed using the official measure. )
Exodus38:25 All the men 20 years old or older were counted. Each man had to pay a tax of 1 beka of silver. (Using the official measure
Exodus38:26 ***
Exodus38:27 They used 7550 pounds of that silver to make 100 bases for the holy place and its curtains. They used 75 pounds of silver for each base.
Exodus38:28 They used the remaining 44 pounds of silver to make the hooks
Exodus38:29 They gave more than 2?tons of bronze to the Lord.
Exodus38:30 That bronze was used to make the bases at the entrance of the Meeting Tent. They also used the bronze to make the altar and the bronze grating. And the bronze was used to make all the tools and dishes for the altar.
Exodus38:31 It was also used to make the bases for the curtains around the courtyard and the bases for the curtains at the entrance. And the bronze was used to make the tent pegs for the Holy Tent and for the curtains around the courtyard.
Exodus39:1 The workers used the blue
Exodus39:2 They made the ephod from gold thread
Exodus39:3 (They hammered the gold into thin strips and cut the gold into long threads. They wove the gold into the blue
Exodus39:4 They made the shoulder pieces for the ephod. They tied these shoulder pieces to the two corners of the ephod.
Exodus39:5 They wove the cloth belt and fastened it to the ephod. It was made the same way as the ephod— they used gold thread
Exodus39:6 The workers put the onyx stones for the ephod in gold settings. They wrote the names of the sons of Israel on these stones.
Exodus39:7 Then they put these jewels on the shoulder pieces of the ephod. These jewels were to help God to remember the Israelites. This was done as the Lord commanded Moses.
Exodus39:8 Then they made the judgment pouch. It was the work of a skilled person
Exodus39:9 The judgment pouch was folded in half to make a square pocket. It was 1 span long and 1 span wide.
Exodus39:10 Then the workers put four rows of beautiful jewels on the judgment pouch. The first row had a ruby
Exodus39:11 The second row had a turquoise
Exodus39:12 The third row had a jacinth
Exodus39:13 The fourth row had a chrysolite
Exodus39:14 There were twelve jewels on the judgment pouch—one jewel for each of the sons of Israel. Each stone had the name of one of the sons of Israel carved onto it
Exodus39:15 The workers made two chains from pure gold for the judgment pouch. The chains were braided like a rope.
Exodus39:16 The workers made two gold rings and fastened them to two corners of the judgment pouch. Then they made two gold settings for the shoulder pieces.
Exodus39:17 They fastened the gold chains to the rings at the corners of the judgment pouch.
Exodus39:18 They fastened the other ends of the gold chains to the settings on the shoulder pieces. They fastened these to the front of the ephod.
Exodus39:19 Then they made two more gold rings and put them on the other two corners of the judgment pouch. This was on the inside edge of the judgment pouch next to the ephod.
Exodus39:20 They also put two gold rings on the bottom of the shoulder pieces on the front of the ephod. These rings were near the fastener
Exodus39:21 Then they used a blue ribbon and tied the rings of the judgment pouch to the rings of the ephod. In this way the judgment pouch would rest close to the cloth belt and would be held tight against the ephod. They did everything just as the Lord commanded.
Exodus39:22 Then they made the robe for the ephod from blue cloth. It was woven by a skilled worker.
Exodus39:23 They made a hole in the center of the robe and sewed a piece of cloth around the edge of this hole. This cloth kept the hole from tearing.
Exodus39:24 Then they used fine linen and blue
Exodus39:25 Then they made bells from pure gold. They hung these bells around the bottom edge of the robe between the pomegranates.
Exodus39:26 Around the bottom edge of the robe there were bells and pomegranates. There was a bell following each pomegranate. This robe was for the priest to wear when he served the Lord. It was made just as the Lord commanded Moses.
Exodus39:27 Skilled workers wove shirts for Aaron and his sons. These shirts were made from fine linen.
Exodus39:28 And the workers made a turban from fine linen. They also used fine linen to make head bands and underclothes.
Exodus39:29 Then they made the cloth belt from fine linen and blue
Exodus39:30 Then they made the strip of gold for the holy crown. They made it from pure gold. They wrote words into the gold. They wrote these words: holy to the lord.
Exodus39:31 They fastened the gold strip to a blue ribbon. Then they tied the blue ribbon around the turban like the Lord had commanded Moses.
Exodus39:32 So all the work on the Holy Tent
Exodus39:33 Then they showed the Holy Tent and everything in it to Moses. They showed him the rings
Exodus39:34 They showed him the covering of the Tent that was made from ram skins dyed red and the covering that was made from fine leather. And they showed him the curtain that covered the entrance to the Most Holy Place.
Exodus39:35 They showed Moses the Box of the Agreement
Exodus39:36 They showed him the table with everything on it and the special bread.
Exodus39:37 They showed him the pure gold lampstand and the lamps on it
Exodus39:38 They showed Moses the golden altar
Exodus39:39 They showed him the bronze altar
Exodus39:40 They showed Moses the wall of curtains around the courtyard with the posts and bases. They showed him the curtain that covered the entrance to the courtyard. They showed him the ropes and the tent pegs. They showed him everything in the Holy Tent
Exodus39:41 Then they showed Moses the clothes that were made for the priests serving in the holy area. They showed him the special clothes for Aaron the priest and his sons to wear when they served as priests.
Exodus39:42 The Israelites did all this work exactly as the Lord had commanded Moses.
Exodus39:43 Moses looked closely at all the work and saw that it was done exactly as the Lord had commanded. So Moses blessed them.
Exodus40:1 Then the Lord said to Moses
Exodus40:2 On the first day of the first month
Exodus40:3 Put the Box of the Agreement in the Holy Tent. Cover the Box with the curtain.
Exodus40:4 Then bring in the table. Put the things on the table that should be there. Then put the lampstand in the Tent. Put the lamps on the lampstand in the right places.
Exodus40:5 Put the golden altar for offering incense in the Tent in front of the Box of the Agreement. Then put the curtain at the entrance to the Holy Tent.
Exodus40:6 Put the altar for burning offerings in front of the entrance of the Holy Tent
Exodus40:7 Put the bowl between the Meeting Tent and the altar. Put water in the bowl.
Exodus40:8 Set up the wall of curtains around the courtyard. Then put the curtain at the entrance to the courtyard.
Exodus40:9 Use the anointing oil and anoint the Holy Tent and everything in it. When you put the oil on these things
Exodus40:10 Anoint the altar for burning offerings. Anoint everything on the altar. You will make the altar holy. It will be very holy.
Exodus40:11 Then anoint the bowl and the base under it to make these things holy.
Exodus40:12 Bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the Meeting Tent. Wash them with water.
Exodus40:13 Then put the special clothes on Aaron. Anoint him with the oil and make him holy. Then he can serve me as a priest.
Exodus40:14 Then put the clothes on his sons.
Exodus40:15 Anoint the sons in the same way that you anointed their father. Then they can also serve me as priests. When you anoint them
Exodus40:16 Moses obeyed the Lord. He did everything that the Lord commanded him.
Exodus40:17 So the Holy Tent was set up at the right time. It was the first day of the first month during the second year from the time they left Egypt.
Exodus40:18 Moses set up the Holy Tent just as the Lord had said. He put the bases down first. Then he put the frames on the bases. Then he put the braces on and set up the posts.
Exodus40:19 After that
Exodus40:20 Moses took the Agreement and put it in the Holy Box. He put the poles on the Box and put the mercy-cover on it.
Exodus40:21 Then Moses put the Holy Box into the Holy Tent. He hung the curtain in the right place to protect it. In this way he protected the Box of the Agreement behind the curtain just as the Lord had commanded him.
Exodus40:22 Then Moses put the table in the Meeting Tent on the north side of the Holy Tent. He put it in the Holy Place
Exodus40:23 Then he put the bread on the table before the Lord. He did this just as the Lord had commanded him.
Exodus40:24 Then Moses put the lampstand in the Meeting Tent on the south side of the Tent
Exodus40:25 Then Moses put the lamps on the lampstand before the Lord. He did this just as the Lord had commanded him.
Exodus40:26 Then Moses put the golden altar in the Meeting Tent
Exodus40:27 Then he burned sweet-smelling incense on the altar. He did this as the Lord had commanded him.
Exodus40:28 Then Moses put the curtain at the entrance to the Holy Tent.
Exodus40:29 Moses put the altar for burning offerings at the entrance to the Holy Tent
Exodus40:30 Then Moses put the bowl between the Meeting Tent and the altar. He put water in the bowl for washing.
Exodus40:31 Moses
Exodus40:32 They washed themselves every time they entered the Meeting Tent. They also washed themselves every time they went near the altar. They did these things just as the Lord commanded Moses.
Exodus40:33 Then Moses set up the curtains around the courtyard of the Holy Tent. He put the altar in the courtyard. Then he put the curtain at the entrance to the courtyard. So he finished all the work.
Exodus40:34 Then the cloud covered the Meeting Tent and the Glory of the Lord filled the Holy Tent.
Exodus40:35 Moses could not go into the Meeting Tent because the cloud had settled on it
Exodus40:36 When the cloud rose from the Holy Tent
Exodus40:37 But when the cloud stayed on the Holy Tent
Exodus40:38 So the cloud of the Lord was over the Holy Tent during the day
Leviticus1:1 So the Lord called out to Moses from inside the Meeting Tent and said
Leviticus1:2 Tell the Israelites: When you bring an offering to the Lord
Leviticus1:3 If you offer one of your cattle as a burnt offering
Leviticus1:4 You must put your hand on the animal's head while it is being killed. So the Lord will accept it as your burnt offering to make you pure.
Leviticus1:5 You must kill the young bull in front of the Lord. Then Aaron's sons
Leviticus1:6 You must remove the skin from that animal and then cut the animal into pieces.
Leviticus1:7 Aaron's sons
Leviticus1:8 They will lay the pieces of the animal
Leviticus1:9 You must wash the legs and inner parts of the animal with water. Then the priest will bring all of these parts to the altar to be offered as a burnt offering
Leviticus1:10 If you offer a sheep or a goat as a burnt offering
Leviticus1:11 You must kill the animal on the north side of the altar in front of the Lord. Then Aaron's sons
Leviticus1:12 You must cut the animal into pieces and remove the head and the fat. The priest will then lay them on the wood that is on the fire of the altar.
Leviticus1:13 You must wash the legs and inner parts of the animal with water. Then the priest will bring all these parts to the altar to be offered as a burnt offering
Leviticus1:14 If you offer a bird as a burnt offering to the Lord
Leviticus1:15 The priest will bring the offering to the altar. There the priest will remove the bird's head
Leviticus1:16 He will remove the bird's tail and inner parts and throw them onto the pile of ashes east of the altar.
Leviticus1:17 Then the priest will tear the bird open by its wings
Leviticus2:1 When you give a grain offering to the Lord
Leviticus2:2 Then you must bring it to Aaron's sons
Leviticus2:3 The rest of that grain offering will belong to Aaron and his sons. This gift to the Lord is very holy.
Leviticus2:4 If you give a grain offering that was baked in the oven
Leviticus2:5 If you bring a grain offering cooked in a baking pan
Leviticus2:6 You must break it into pieces and pour oil over it. It is a grain offering.
Leviticus2:7 If you bring a grain offering cooked in a frying pan
Leviticus2:8 When you bring grain offerings made from these things to the Lord
Leviticus2:9 Then the priest will take part of the grain offering and lift it up as a memorial offering. He will bring it to the altar to be burned up as a sweetsmelling gift to the Lord.
Leviticus2:10 The rest of that grain offering will belong to Aaron and his sons. This gift to the Lord is very holy.
Leviticus2:11 You must not give any grain offering to the Lord that has yeast in it. You must not burn yeast or honey as a gift to the Lord.
Leviticus2:12 You may bring yeast and honey to the Lord as an offering from the first harvest
Leviticus2:13 Also
Leviticus2:14 If you bring a grain offering from the first harvest to the Lord
Leviticus2:15 You must put oil and frankincense on it. It is a grain offering.
Leviticus2:16 The priest must burn part of the crushed grain
Leviticus3:1 If you offer one of your cattle as a fellowship offering
Leviticus3:2 You must put your hand on the animal's head and kill the animal at the entrance of the Meeting Tent. Then Aaron's sons
Leviticus3:3 The priest will take a part of that fellowship offering as a gift to the Lord. He will take the fat that is over and around the inner parts.
Leviticus3:4 He will take the two kidneys and the fat covering them near the lower back muscle. He will also remove the fat part of the liver that is near the kidneys.
Leviticus3:5 Then Aaron's sons will bring the fat to the altar and put it on the burnt offering that is on the wood on the fire. It is a sweet-smelling gift to the Lord.
Leviticus3:6 If you offer a sheep or a goat as a fellowship offering to the Lord
Leviticus3:7 If you bring a lamb as an offering to the Lord
Leviticus3:8 you must put your hand on the animal's head and kill it in front of the Meeting Tent. Then Aaron's sons will splash the animal's blood on all four sides of the altar.
Leviticus3:9 The priest will take part of the fellowship offering to the altar as a gift to the Lord. The priest must cut off the tail close to the backbone. Then he will offer the whole fat tail and the fat that is over and around the animal's inner parts.
Leviticus3:10 He will also offer the two kidneys and the fat covering them near the lower back muscles. He will also offer the fat part of the liver. He must remove it with the kidneys.
Leviticus3:11 Then the priest will take that part to the altar to be burned up as food
Leviticus3:12 If the offering is a goat
Leviticus3:13 You must put your hand on the goat's head and kill it in front of the Meeting Tent. Then Aaron's sons
Leviticus3:14 The priest will give part of the fellowship offering as a gift to the Lord. He will offer the fat that is over and around the animal's inner parts.
Leviticus3:15 He will offer the two kidneys and the fat covering them near the lower back muscle. He will also offer the fat part of the liver. He will remove it with the kidneys.
Leviticus3:16 Then the priest will bring that food as a sweetsmelling gift to the Lord. The fat belongs to the Lord.
Leviticus3:17 This rule will continue forever through all your generations. Wherever you live
Leviticus4:1 The Lord spoke to Moses and said
Leviticus4:2 Tell the Israelites this: A person might sin without meaning to and do something that the Lord commanded should not be done. For example:
Leviticus4:3 If the anointed priest makes a mistake that leaves the people guilty for their sin
Leviticus4:4 The anointed priest must bring the bull to the entrance of the Meeting Tent in front of the Lord. He must put his hand on the bull's head and kill the bull in front of the Lord.
Leviticus4:5 Then the anointed priest must get some of the blood from the bull and take it into the Meeting Tent.
Leviticus4:6 He must put his finger in the blood and sprinkle the blood seven times before the Lord in front of the curtain of the Most Holy Place.
Leviticus4:7 The priest must put some of the blood on the corners of the incense altar. (This altar is in the Meeting Tent
Leviticus4:8 Then the priest must take all the fat from the bull of the sin offering. He must take the fat that is on and around the inner parts.
Leviticus4:9 He must take the two kidneys and the fat covering them near the lower back muscle. He must also take the fat part of the liver. He must remove it with the kidneys.
Leviticus4:10 The priest must lift up these things just as they do for the fellowship offering and burn them on the altar for burnt offerings.
Leviticus4:11 But the priest must carry out the bull's skin
Leviticus4:12 ***
Leviticus4:13 The whole nation of Israel might sin without knowing it. They might break one of the commands of the Lord and become guilty of doing something he said must not be done.
Leviticus4:14 When they learn about that sin
Leviticus4:15 The elders of the people must put their hands on the bull's head in front of the Lord. Then they must kill the bull in front of the Lord.
Leviticus4:16 The anointed priest must get some of the bull's blood and take it into the Meeting Tent.
Leviticus4:17 He must put his finger in the blood and sprinkle it seven times in front of the curtain before the Lord.
Leviticus4:18 He must put some of the blood on the corners of the altar. (This altar is inside the Meeting Tent
Leviticus4:19 Then he must take all the fat from the animal and bring it to the altar.
Leviticus4:20 He must offer these parts just as he offered the bull of the sin offering. In this way the priest will make the people pure
Leviticus4:21 The priest must carry this bull outside the camp and burn it
Leviticus4:22 A ruler might sin without meaning to and break one of the commands of the Lord. He might do something that his God said must not be done.
Leviticus4:23 If he learns about his sin
Leviticus4:24 The ruler must put his hand on the goat's head and kill the goat at the place where they kill the burnt offering before the Lord. The goat is a sin offering.
Leviticus4:25 The priest will take some of the blood of the sin offering on his finger and put it on the corners of the altar of burnt offering. He will pour out the rest of the blood at the base of the altar.
Leviticus4:26 The priest will burn all the goat's fat on the altar just as they do for the fellowship offerings. In this way the priest will make the ruler pure
Leviticus4:27 Finally
Leviticus4:28 If you learn about that sin
Leviticus4:29 You must put your hand on the animal's head and kill it at the place for the burnt offering.
Leviticus4:30 Then the priest will take some of the goat's blood on his finger and put it on the corners of the altar of burnt offering. He will pour out the rest of its blood at the base of the altar.
Leviticus4:31 The priest will then remove all of its fat
Leviticus4:32 If you bring a lamb as your sin offering
Leviticus4:33 You must put your hand on the animal's head and kill it as a sin offering in the place where people kill the burnt offering.
Leviticus4:34 The priest will take some of the blood from the sin offering on his finger and put it on the corners of the altar of burnt offering. He will pour out the rest of the lamb's blood at the base of the altar.
Leviticus4:35 He will then remove all the lamb's fat just as is done for the fellowship offerings. Then he will bring it to the altar as a gift to the Lord. The priest will do this to make you pure
Leviticus5:1 You might be called as a witness and take an oath to tell the truth. If you saw something or knew something but did not tell it
Leviticus5:2 You might touch something unclean. It might be the dead body of any kind of animal. You might not know that you touched these things
Leviticus5:3 You might touch any of the many things that can make a person unclean. You might touch something unclean
Leviticus5:4 You might make a quick promise to do something—it makes no difference if it is bad or good. People make many kinds of quick promises. You might make such a promise and forget it. When you remember your promise
Leviticus5:5 If you are guilty of any of these things
Leviticus5:6 Then you must bring your guilt offering to the Lord for the sin you did. You must bring a female lamb or a female goat as a sin offering. The priest will do this to make you pure from your sin.
Leviticus5:7 If you cannot afford a lamb
Leviticus5:8 Take them to the priest. First
Leviticus5:9 The priest will sprinkle the blood from the sin offering on the side of the altar. Then he will pour out the rest of the blood at the base of the altar. It is a sin offering.
Leviticus5:10 Then he will offer the second bird according to the rules for a burnt offering. The priest will do this to make you pure from the sin you did
Leviticus5:11 If you cannot afford two doves or two pigeons
Leviticus5:12 You must bring the flour to the priest. The priest will take a handful of the flour as the memorial offering and bring it to the altar as a gift to the Lord. It is a sin offering.
Leviticus5:13 The priest will do this to make you pure
Leviticus5:14 The Lord gave this command to Moses {for the people}:
Leviticus5:15 You might promise to give something to the Lord. You might sin against me without meaning to by not giving what you promised. If you do that
Leviticus5:16 You must give what you promised and add one-fifth of that amount as a fine. Give it to the priest and he will use the ram to make you pure
Leviticus5:17 If you sin and break any of the commands that the Lord said must not be done
Leviticus5:18 You must bring a ram that has nothing wrong with it (or the same amount in silver) to the priest. The priest will offer the ram
Leviticus5:19 You are guilty
Leviticus6:1 The Lord gave this command to Moses {for the people}
Leviticus6:2 You might sin against the Lord by doing one of these sins: You might lie about what happened to something you were taking care of for someone else; you might lie about a deposit you received; you might steal something; you might cheat someone;
Leviticus6:3 you might find something that was lost and then lie about it; you might promise to do something and then not do what you promised
Leviticus6:4 If you do any of these things
Leviticus6:5 whatever you made a false promise about. You must pay the full price and then add one-fifth of that amount as a fine and give it all to the true owner.
Leviticus6:6 You must also bring a ram to the Lord that has nothing wrong with it (or the same amount in silver) to the priest.
Leviticus6:7 Then the priest will go to the Lord to make you pure
Leviticus6:8 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus6:9 Give this command to Aaron and his sons: This is the law of the burnt offering. The burnt offering must stay on the hearth of the altar all night until morning. The altar's fire must be kept burning.
Leviticus6:10 The priest must change clothes and put on the special linen underwear and linen robe. Then he must gather up the ashes from the fire and burnt offerings and set them down by the altar.
Leviticus6:11 Then he must take off the special clothes and put on the other clothes and carry the ashes outside the camp to a special place that is pure.
Leviticus6:12 The fire that was started on the altar must never be allowed to stop burning. Every morning the priests must put wood on the altar. They must arrange the burnt offerings on the wood
Leviticus6:13 That fire must always be kept burning on the altar. It must never be allowed to stop burning.
Leviticus6:14 This is the law for the grain offering: The sons of Aaron will bring it to the front of the altar as an offering to the Lord.
Leviticus6:15 There must be some oil and frankincense on the grain offering. The priest will take a handful of fine flour from the grain offering and burn it on the altar as a sweet-smelling memorial offering to the Lord.
Leviticus6:16 Aaron and his sons will use the rest of that grain to make bread without yeast. This must be eaten in a holy place—in the courtyard around the Meeting Tent.
Leviticus6:17 I have given this part of the grain offering as the priests' share of the gifts offered to me. Like the sin offering and the guilt offering
Leviticus6:18 Any male descendant of Aaron may eat from these gifts to the Lord. This is their share forever throughout your generations. Whatever touches these offerings will be made holy.
Leviticus6:19 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus6:20 This is the offering that Aaron and his sons must bring to the Lord when Aaron is anointed to be the high priest. They must bring 8 cups of fine flour for a grain offering. This will be offered at the times of the daily offering—half of it in the morning and half of it in the evening.
Leviticus6:21 The fine flour must be mixed with oil and baked on a pan. After it is cooked
Leviticus6:22 In the future
Leviticus6:23 Every grain offering that a priest gives must be completely burned. It must not be eaten.
Leviticus6:24 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus6:25 Tell Aaron and his sons: This is the law of the sin offering. The sin offering must be killed in the place where the burnt offering is killed before the Lord. It is most holy.
Leviticus6:26 The priest who offers the sin offering must eat it. But he must eat it in a holy place
Leviticus6:27 Touching the meat of the sin offering makes a person or a thing holy. If any of the sprinkled blood falls on a person's clothes
Leviticus6:28 If the sin offering was boiled in a clay pot
Leviticus6:29 Any male in a priest's family may eat the sin offering. It is very holy.
Leviticus6:30 But if the blood of the sin offering was taken into the Meeting Tent and used in the Holy Place to make people pure
Leviticus7:1 These are the rules for the guilt offering
Leviticus7:2 A priest must kill the guilt offering in the same place where they kill the burnt offerings. Then he must sprinkle the blood from the guilt offering around the altar.
Leviticus7:3 The priest must offer all the fat from the guilt offering. He must offer the fat tail and the fat that covers the inner parts.
Leviticus7:4 He must offer the two kidneys and the fat covering them at the lower back muscle. He must also offer the fat part of the liver. He must remove it with the kidneys.
Leviticus7:5 He must bring these things to the altar as a gift to the Lord. It is a guilt offering.
Leviticus7:6 Any male in a priest's family may eat the guilt offering. It is very holy
Leviticus7:7 The guilt offering is like the sin offering. The same rules are for both offerings. The priest who does the sacrificing will get the meat for food.
Leviticus7:8 He will also get the skin from the burnt offering.
Leviticus7:9 Every grain offering belongs to the priest who offers it. That priest will get the grain offerings that were baked in an oven
Leviticus7:10 The grain offerings will belong to Aaron's sons. It doesn't make any difference if the grain offerings are dry or mixed with oil. The sons of Aaron will all share this food.
Leviticus7:11 This is the law of the sacrifice of fellowship offerings that you bring to the Lord:
Leviticus7:12 People can bring fellowship offerings to show their thanks to God. If you bring your sacrifice to give thanks
Leviticus7:13 You must also bring loaves of bread made with yeast to go with your fellowship offering.
Leviticus7:14 One of these loaves of bread will belong to the priest who sprinkles the blood of the fellowship offerings.
Leviticus7:15 The meat of the fellowship offering must be eaten on the same day it is offered as a way of showing thanks to God. None of the meat should remain until the next morning.
Leviticus7:16 If you bring a fellowship offering simply because you want to give a gift to God or because it is part of a special promise you made to him
Leviticus7:17 If any meat from this sacrifice is still left over on the third day
Leviticus7:18 The Lord will not accept the offering from anyone who eats any of that meat on the third day. The Lord will not count the sacrifice for you—he will treat it like rotten meat! Whoever eats that meat will be responsible for their own sin.
Leviticus7:19 People must not eat any of the meat that touches anything unclean. They must burn this meat in the fire. Whoever is clean may eat the meat from the fellowship offering.
Leviticus7:20 But anyone who is unclean and eats the meat from the fellowship offerings that was offered to the Lord must be separated from their people.
Leviticus7:21 If you touch something that is unclean
Leviticus7:22 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus7:23 Tell the Israelites: You must not eat any fat from your cattle
Leviticus7:24 You may use the fat from any animal that has died by itself or was torn by other animals
Leviticus7:25 Whoever eats the fat from an animal that was offered as a gift to the Lord must be separated from their people.
Leviticus7:26 No matter where you live
Leviticus7:27 Anyone who eats blood must be separated from their people.
Leviticus7:28 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus7:29 Tell the Israelites: If you bring a fellowship offering to the Lord
Leviticus7:30 You must bring the fat and the breast of the animal to the priest. Then he will lift up the breast in front of the Lord to show it was presented to God.
Leviticus7:31 The priest will burn the fat on the altar
Leviticus7:32 You must also give the right thigh from the fellowship offering as a gift to the priest.
Leviticus7:33 That part of the fellowship offering will belong to the priest who carries the blood and fat to the altar.
Leviticus7:34 I will accept the breast that was lifted up and the gift of the right thigh from the Israelites. Then I will give these things to Aaron and his sons. This is their share from the fellowship offerings of the Israelites forever.
Leviticus7:35 Those parts from the gifts offered to the Lord were given to Aaron and his sons. Whenever Aaron and his sons serve as the Lord's priests
Leviticus7:36 The Lord commanded the Israelites to give those parts to the priests once they have been anointed. That will be their share from the Israelites forever.
Leviticus7:37 These are the laws about burnt offerings
Leviticus7:38 The Lord gave these laws to Moses on Mount Sinai when he commanded the Israelites to bring their offerings to the Lord in the Sinai desert.
Leviticus8:1 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus8:2 Take Aaron and his sons with him and the clothes
Leviticus8:3 Then gather the people together at the entrance of the Meeting Tent.
Leviticus8:4 Moses did what the Lord commanded him. The people met together at the entrance of the Meeting Tent.
Leviticus8:5 Then Moses said to them
Leviticus8:6 Moses brought Aaron and his sons forward and washed them with water.
Leviticus8:7 Moses put the woven shirt on Aaron and tied the cloth belt around him. Then Moses put the robe and the ephod on Aaron and tied the beautiful cloth belt around him.
Leviticus8:8 Moses put the judgment pouch on Aaron and put the Urim and Thummim inside its pocket.
Leviticus8:9 He also put the turban on Aaron's head. He put the strip of gold on the front of the turban. This strip of gold is the holy crown. Moses did this just as the Lord had commanded.
Leviticus8:10 Then Moses took the anointing oil and sprinkled it on the Holy Tent and on everything in it. In this way he made them holy.
Leviticus8:11 He sprinkled some of the anointing oil on the altar seven times. He sprinkled the oil on the altar
Leviticus8:12 He poured some of the anointing oil on Aaron's head to make him holy.
Leviticus8:13 Then Moses brought Aaron's sons forward. He put the woven shirts on them
Leviticus8:14 Then Moses brought out the bull of the sin offering. Aaron and his sons put their hands on the bull's head.
Leviticus8:15 Then Moses killed the bull and collected its blood. He used his finger to put some of the blood on all the corners of the altar. In this way he made the altar ready for sacrifices. Then he poured out the blood at the base of the altar to make the altar ready for sacrifices to make the people pure.
Leviticus8:16 Moses took all the fat from the inner parts of the bull. He took the fat part of the liver with the two kidneys and the fat on them. Then he burned them on the altar.
Leviticus8:17 Moses took the bull's skin
Leviticus8:18 Then Moses brought the ram of the burnt offering. Aaron and his sons put their hands on the ram's head.
Leviticus8:19 Then Moses killed the ram. He sprinkled the blood around on the altar.
Leviticus8:20 He cut the ram into pieces. He washed the inner parts and legs with water. Then he burned the whole ram on the altar. He burned the head
Leviticus8:21 ***
Leviticus8:22 Then Moses brought the other ram. This ram was used for appointing Aaron and his sons to become priests. Aaron and his sons put their hands on the ram's head.
Leviticus8:23 Then Moses killed the ram. He put some of its blood on the tip of Aaron's ear
Leviticus8:24 Then Moses brought Aaron's sons close to the altar. He put some of the blood on the tip of their right ears
Leviticus8:25 He took the fat
Leviticus8:26 A basket of unleavened bread is put before the Lord each day. Moses took one of those loaves of bread
Leviticus8:27 Then he put all of it in the hands of Aaron and his sons. Moses lifted these pieces to show he was offering them before the Lord.
Leviticus8:28 Then Moses took these things from the hands of Aaron and his sons and burned them on the altar on top of the burnt offering. So this was the offering for appointing Aaron and his sons as priests. It was a sweet-smelling gift to the Lord.
Leviticus8:29 Moses took the breast
Leviticus8:30 Moses took some of the anointing oil and some of the blood that was on the altar. He sprinkled some on Aaron and on Aaron's clothes. He sprinkled some on Aaron's sons who were with Aaron and on their clothes. In this way Moses made Aaron
Leviticus8:31 Then Moses said to Aaron and his sons
Leviticus8:32 If any of the meat or bread is left
Leviticus8:33 The ceremony for appointing the priests will last for seven days. You must not leave the entrance of the Meeting Tent until that time is finished.
Leviticus8:34 Everything we did today was what the Lord commanded us to do in order to make you pure.
Leviticus8:35 You must stay at the entrance of the Meeting Tent day and night for seven days. If you don't obey the Lord's commands
Leviticus8:36 So Aaron and his sons did everything that the Lord had commanded Moses.

Leviticus9:3 Tell the Israelites

Leviticus9:5 So all the people came to the Meeting Tent. They all brought the things that Moses had commanded. All the people stood before the Lord.
Leviticus9:6 Moses said
Leviticus9:7 Then Moses told Aaron: Go do what the Lord commanded. Go to the altar and offer sin offerings and burnt offerings. Do what will make you and the people pure. Take the people's sacrifices and make them pure.
Leviticus9:8 So Aaron went to the altar. He killed the bull for the sin offering. This sin offering was for himself.
Leviticus9:9 Then the sons of Aaron brought the blood to Aaron. Aaron put his finger in the blood and put it on the corners of the altar. Then he poured out the blood at the base of the altar.
Leviticus9:10 He took the fat
Leviticus9:11 Then Aaron burned the meat and skin on a fire outside the camp.
Leviticus9:12 Next

Leviticus9:14 He also washed the inner parts and the legs of the burnt offering and burned them on the altar.

Leviticus9:16 He brought the burnt offering and offered it
Leviticus9:17 He brought the grain offering to the altar. He took a handful of the grain and put it on the altar beside that morning's daily sacrifice.

Leviticus9:19 Aaron's sons also brought him the fat of the bull and the ram. They brought the fat tail

Leviticus10:1 Then Aaron's sons Nadab and Abihu made a mistake. They took their incense dishes and put some fire and incense in them. But they did not use the fire that was on the altar—they took fire from some other place and brought it to the Lord. This was not what he had commanded.
Leviticus10:2 So fire came from the Lord and destroyed Nadab and Abihu
Leviticus10:3 Then Moses said to Aaron
Leviticus10:4 Aaron's uncle Uzziel had two sons. They were Mishael and Elzaphan. Moses said to these sons
Leviticus10:5 So Mishael and Elzaphan obeyed Moses. They carried the bodies of Nadab and Abihu outside the camp. Nadab and Abihu were still wearing their special woven shirts.

Leviticus10:7 But you must not even leave the entrance of the Meeting Tent. If you leave

Leviticus10:10 You must be able to clearly tell the difference between what is holy and what is not holy
Leviticus10:11 And you must teach the people about all the laws that the Lord gave them through Moses.
Leviticus10:12 Aaron had two sons who were still alive
Leviticus10:13 The portion of food for you and your sons will come from the special gifts to the Lord
Leviticus10:14 Also you

Leviticus10:17 You were supposed to eat that goat in the holy area! It is very holy! Why did you not eat it in front of the Lord? The Lord gave it to you to carry away the guilt of the people—to make the people pure.
Leviticus10:18 That goat's blood was not brought into the Holy Place. So you should have eaten the meat in the holy area

Leviticus11:1 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus11:2 Tell the Israelites: These are the animals you can eat:
Leviticus11:3 If an animal has hooves that are split into two parts
Leviticus11:4 Some animals chew the cud
Leviticus11:5 ***
Leviticus11:6 ***
Leviticus11:7 Other animals have hooves that are split into two parts
Leviticus11:8 Don't eat the meat from these animals. Don't even touch their dead bodies! They are unclean for you.
Leviticus11:9 If an animal lives in the sea or in a river and it has fins and scales
Leviticus11:10 But if an animal lives in the sea or in a river and does not have fins and scales
Leviticus11:11 ***
Leviticus11:12 You must treat any animal in the water that does not have fins and scales as one of the animals that God says is wrong to eat.
Leviticus11:13 You must also treat these birds as animals God says are wrong to eat. Don't eat any of these birds: eagles
Leviticus11:14 kites
Leviticus11:15 all kinds of black birds
Leviticus11:16 ostriches
Leviticus11:17 owls
Leviticus11:18 water hens
Leviticus11:19 storks
Leviticus11:20 If insects have wings and crawl
Leviticus11:21 But you may eat insects if they have legs with joints above their feet so that they can jump.
Leviticus11:22 You may also eat all kinds of locusts
Leviticus11:23 But all the other insects that have wings and crawl are those the Lord says you must not eat.
Leviticus11:24 They will make you unclean. If you touch the dead bodies of these insects you will become unclean until evening.
Leviticus11:25 If you pick up one of these dead insects
Leviticus11:26 Some animals have split hooves
Leviticus11:27 ***
Leviticus11:28 If you pick up the dead bodies of these unclean animals
Leviticus11:29 These small animals are unclean for you: moles
Leviticus11:30 geckos
Leviticus11:31 Whoever touches their dead bodies will be unclean until evening.
Leviticus11:32 If any of these unclean animals dies and falls on something
Leviticus11:33 If any of these unclean animals dies and falls into a clay dish
Leviticus11:34 If water from the unclean clay dish touches any food
Leviticus11:35 If any part of a dead
Leviticus11:36 A spring or a well that collects water will remain clean
Leviticus11:37 If any part of a dead
Leviticus11:38 But if you put water on some seed and if any part of a dead
Leviticus11:39 Also
Leviticus11:40 If you eat meat from this animal's body
Leviticus11:41 You must treat all the crawling animals that live on the dirt as disgusting things that you must not eat.
Leviticus11:42 You must not eat any of the reptiles that crawl on their bellies or that walk on all four feet
Leviticus11:43 Don't let them make you filthy. You must not become unclean
Leviticus11:44 because I am the Lord your God. I am holy
Leviticus11:45 I brought you out of Egypt so that you could be my special people and I could be your God. I am holy
Leviticus11:46 These are the rules about all of the tame animals
Leviticus11:47 These rules will help the people know which animals are unclean and which animals they are allowed to eat and which ones they cannot eat.
Leviticus12:1 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus12:2 Tell the Israelites: When a woman gives birth
Leviticus12:3 The baby boy must be circumcised on the eighth day.
Leviticus12:4 Because of the blood from childbirth
Leviticus12:5 But if she gives birth to a girl
Leviticus12:6 After the time of her purification is finished
Leviticus12:7 If the woman cannot afford a lamb
Leviticus13:1 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus13:2 Someone might have a swelling on their skin
Leviticus13:3 The priest must look at the sore on the person's skin. If the hair in the sore has become white
Leviticus13:4 Sometimes there is a white spot on a person's skin that does not seem deeper than the skin. If that is true
Leviticus13:5 On the seventh day the priest must look at the person again. If the priest sees that the sore has not changed and has not spread on the skin
Leviticus13:6 Seven days later the priest must look at the person again. If the sore has faded and has not spread on the skin
Leviticus13:7 But if the rash spreads over the skin after the person has shown himself to the priest to be made clean again
Leviticus13:8 The priest must look
Leviticus13:9 Whoever has leprosy must be brought to the priest.
Leviticus13:10 He must look at that person. If there is a white swelling on the skin
Leviticus13:11 it is leprosy that has been there for a long time. The priest must announce that the person is unclean. He does not have to wait until after a period of separation
Leviticus13:12 Sometimes a skin disease will spread all over a person's body
Leviticus13:13 If the priest sees that the skin disease covers the whole body and that it has turned all of the skin white
Leviticus13:14 But if the skin is raw
Leviticus13:15 When the priest sees the raw skin
Leviticus13:16 If the raw skin changes and becomes white
Leviticus13:17 The priest must look at the person. If the skin has become white
Leviticus13:18 Someone might get a boil on their skin that heals over.
Leviticus13:19 Then that boil might become a white swelling or a bright
Leviticus13:20 The priest must look at it. If the swelling is deeper than the skin
Leviticus13:21 But if the priest looks at the spot
Leviticus13:22 If the spot spreads on the skin
Leviticus13:23 But if the bright spot stays in its place and does not spread
Leviticus13:24 Someone might get a burn on the skin. If the raw skin becomes a white spot or a white spot with red streaks in it
Leviticus13:25 ***
Leviticus13:26 But if the priest looks at the spot
Leviticus13:27 On the seventh day
Leviticus13:28 But if the bright spot has not spread on the skin but has faded
Leviticus13:29 Someone might get an infection on the scalp or beard.
Leviticus13:30 A priest must look at the infection. If the infection seems to be deeper than the skin
Leviticus13:31 If the disease does not seem deeper than the skin
Leviticus13:32 On the seventh day
Leviticus13:33 the person must shave. But the diseased area should not be shaved. The priest must separate that person for seven more days.
Leviticus13:34 On the seventh day
Leviticus13:35 But if the disease spreads on the skin after the person has become clean
Leviticus13:36 then the priest must look at the person again. If the disease has spread
Leviticus13:37 But if the priest thinks that the disease has stopped
Leviticus13:38 If anyone has white spots on the skin
Leviticus13:39 a priest must look at them. If the spots on that person's skin are dull white
Leviticus13:40 A man might begin to lose the hair on his head. It is only baldness
Leviticus13:41 A man might lose hair from the sides of his head. He is clean. It is only another kind of baldness.
Leviticus13:42 But if there is a red and white infection on his scalp
Leviticus13:43 A priest must look at him. If the swelling of the infection is red and white and looks like the leprosy on other parts of his body
Leviticus13:44 then he has leprosy on his scalp. The person is unclean. The priest must announce that he is unclean.
Leviticus13:45 People with leprosy must warn other people. They must shout
Leviticus13:46 They are unclean the whole time that they have the infection. They are unclean and must live outside the camp.
Leviticus13:47 Some clothing might have mildew on it. The cloth could be linen or wool
Leviticus13:48 ***
Leviticus13:49 If the mildew is green or red
Leviticus13:50 The priest must look at it and put it in a separate place for seven days.
Leviticus13:51 On the seventh day
Leviticus13:52 ***
Leviticus13:53 If the priest sees that the mildew did not spread on the object
Leviticus13:54 He must order the people to wash it. Then he must separate the clothing for seven more days.
Leviticus13:55 After that time
Leviticus13:56 But if the priest looks at that piece of leather or cloth
Leviticus13:57 But the mildew might come back to that piece of leather or cloth. If that happens
Leviticus13:58 But if the mildew did not come back after washing
Leviticus13:59 These are the rules for mildew on pieces of leather or cloth
Leviticus14:1 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus14:2 These are the rules for people who have had a skin disease and have been made well. These rules are for making them clean. A priest must look at those who had the skin disease.
Leviticus14:3 The priest must go to them outside the camp and look to see if the skin disease is healed.
Leviticus14:4 If they are healthy
Leviticus14:5 Then the priest must order one bird to be killed in a clay bowl over running water.
Leviticus14:6 He must take the other bird that is still alive and the piece of cedar wood
Leviticus14:7 He must sprinkle the blood seven times on those who had the skin disease. Then he must announce that they are clean. After that the priest must go to an open field and let the living bird go free.
Leviticus14:8 The people going through this purification ceremony must wash their clothes
Leviticus14:9 On the seventh day
Leviticus14:10 On the eighth day
Leviticus14:11 The priest must bring that person and those sacrifices before the Lord at the entrance of the Meeting Tent. (This must be the same priest who announced that the person is clean.)
Leviticus14:12 The priest will take one of the lambs and the oil as a guilt offering. He will lift them in front of the Lord to show they were presented to God.
Leviticus14:13 Then the priest will kill the male lamb in the holy place where they kill the sin offering and the burnt offering. Like the sin offering
Leviticus14:14 The priest will take some of the blood of the guilt offering. He will put some of this blood on the tip of the right ear of the person to be made clean. The priest will put some of this blood on the thumb of the right hand and on the big toe of the right foot of that person.
Leviticus14:15 He will also take some of the oil and pour it into his left palm.
Leviticus14:16 Then the priest will dip the finger of his right hand into the oil that is in his left palm. He will use his finger to sprinkle some of the oil seven times before the Lord.
Leviticus14:17 Then he will put some of the oil that is in his palm on the person to be made clean. He will put that oil on the same places he put the blood of the guilt offering. The priest will put some of the oil on the tip of the person's right ear
Leviticus14:18 He will put the oil that is left in his palm on the head of the person to be made clean. In this way he will make that person pure before the Lord.
Leviticus14:19 Then the priest must offer the sin offering to make that person pure. After that he will kill the animal for the burnt offering.
Leviticus14:20 He will then offer up the burnt offering and the grain offering on the altar. In this way the priest will make that person pure
Leviticus14:21 A poor person might not be able to afford all these offerings. So that poor person can use one male lamb as a guilt offering. It will be presented to God so that the priest can make that person pure. The poor person must take 8 cups of fine flour mixed with oil. This flour will be used for a grain offering. The poor person must also take?of a pint of olive oil
Leviticus14:22 and two doves or two young pigeons. Even poor people can afford these things. One bird will be a sin offering

Leviticus14:25 Then he will kill the lamb of the guilt offering
Leviticus14:26 He will also pour some of this oil into his own left palm.
Leviticus14:27 He will use the finger of his right hand to sprinkle some of the oil that is in his left palm seven times before the Lord.
Leviticus14:28 Then he will put some of the oil that is in his palm on the same places he put the blood from the guilt offering. He will put some of the oil on the tip of the right ear of the person to be made clean. The priest will put some of the oil on the thumb of the right hand and on the big toe of the person's right foot.
Leviticus14:29 He will put the oil that is left in his palm on the head of the person to be made clean. In this way he will make that person pure before the Lord.
Leviticus14:30 Then the priest must offer one of the doves or young pigeons. (He must offer whichever the person can afford.)
Leviticus14:31 He must offer one of these birds as a sin offering and the other bird as a burnt offering. He must offer the birds with the grain offering. In this way the priest will make that person pure before the Lord
Leviticus14:32 These are the rules for making people clean after they become well from a skin disease. These are the rules for those who cannot afford the regular sacrifices for becoming clean.
Leviticus14:33 The Lord also said to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus14:34 I am giving the land of Canaan to your people. Your people will enter that land. At that time I might cause mildew to grow in someone's house.
Leviticus14:35 The person who owns that house must come and tell the priest
Leviticus14:36 Then the priest must order the people to take everything out of the house before he goes in to look at the mildew. Then the priest will not have to say everything in the house is unclean. After the people have taken everything out of the house
Leviticus14:37 He will look at the mildew. If the mildew on the walls of the house has holes that are a green or red color
Leviticus14:38 he must go out of the house and lock the house for seven days.
Leviticus14:39 On the seventh day the priest must come back and check the house. If the mildew has spread on the walls of the house
Leviticus14:40 then he must order the people to tear out the stones with the mildew on them and throw them away. They must put these stones at a special unclean place outside the city.
Leviticus14:41 Then the priest must have the entire house scraped inside. The people must throw away the plaster that was scraped off the walls. They must put that plaster at a special unclean place outside the city.
Leviticus14:42 Then new stones must be put in the walls
Leviticus14:43 Maybe someone took away the old stones and plaster and put in new stones and plaster. And maybe mildew again appears in that house.
Leviticus14:44 Then the priest must come in and check the house. If the infection has spread in the house
Leviticus14:45 The house must be torn down. All the stones
Leviticus14:46 Anyone who goes into that house will be unclean until evening.
Leviticus14:47 Anyone who eats in that house or lies down in there must wash their clothes.
Leviticus14:48 After new stones and plaster are put in a house
Leviticus14:49 Then
Leviticus14:50 He will kill one bird in a clay bowl over running water.
Leviticus14:51 Then he will take the cedar wood
Leviticus14:52 In this way he will use these things to make the house clean.
Leviticus14:53 He will go to an open field outside the city and let the living bird go free. In this way the priest will make the house pure. The house will be clean.
Leviticus14:54 These are the rules for any infection of leprosy
Leviticus14:55 for mildew on pieces of cloth or in a house.

Leviticus15:2 Say to the Israelites: Whoever has a genital discharge is unclean.
Leviticus15:3 The person is unclean whether the discharge continues to flow or whether it stops.
Leviticus15:4 If a man with a discharge lies on a bed
Leviticus15:5 If you touch that bed
Leviticus15:6 If you sit on anything that he sat on
Leviticus15:7 If you touch him
Leviticus15:8 If he spits on you
Leviticus15:9 If that man sits on a saddle
Leviticus15:10 If you touch or carry anything that was under him
Leviticus15:11 If he touches you
Leviticus15:12 If a man with a discharge touches a clay bowl

Leviticus15:16 If a man has a flow of semen
Leviticus15:17 If the semen is on any clothing or leather
Leviticus15:18 If a woman has sexual relations with a man
Leviticus15:19 If a woman has a discharge from her monthly time of bleeding
Leviticus15:20 Everything she lies on during her monthly time of bleeding will be unclean. And everything she sits on during that time will be unclean.
Leviticus15:21 Whoever touches her bed must wash their clothes and bathe in water. They will be unclean until evening.
Leviticus15:22 Whoever touches anything she has sat on must wash their clothes and bathe in water. They will be unclean until evening.
Leviticus15:23 It doesn't matter if they touched the woman's bed or if they touched something she sat on
Leviticus15:24 If a man has sexual relations with a woman during her monthly time of bleeding
Leviticus15:25 If a woman has a discharge of blood for many days
Leviticus15:26 Any bed she lies on during the time of her discharge will be like her bed during the time of her monthly bleeding. Everything she sits on will be unclean
Leviticus15:27 Whoever touches these things will be unclean until evening. They must wash their own clothes and bathe in water.
Leviticus15:28 After the woman's discharge stops
Leviticus15:29 Then on the eighth day she must bring two doves or two young pigeons to the priest at the entrance of the Meeting Tent.
Leviticus15:30 Then the priest must offer one bird for a sin offering and the other bird for a burnt offering. In this way the priest will make her pure before the Lord.
Leviticus15:31 So you must warn the Israelites about being unclean. If you don't warn the people

Leviticus15:33 And these rules are for women who become unclean from their monthly time of bleeding. And these are the rules for anyone who becomes unclean by sleeping with another person who is unclean.
Leviticus16:1 Two of Aaron's sons died while offering incense to the Lord. After that time
Leviticus16:2 The Lord said
Leviticus16:3 Before Aaron enters the Most Holy Place
Leviticus16:4 Aaron will wash his whole body with water and put on the special clothes. He will put on the linen underwear next to his body
Leviticus16:5 From the whole community of Israel
Leviticus16:6 Then he will offer the bull for the sin offering. This sin offering is for himself. He will do this to purify himself and his family.
Leviticus16:7 Then Aaron will take the two goats and bring them before the Lord at the doorway of the Meeting Tent.
Leviticus16:8 Aaron will throw lots for the two goats. One lot will be for the Lord. The other lot will be for Azazel.
Leviticus16:9 Then Aaron will offer the goat chosen by the lot for the Lord. Aaron will make this goat a sin offering.
Leviticus16:10 But the goat chosen by the lot for Azazel will be brought alive before the Lord. Then this goat will be sent out to Azazel in the desert. This is to make the people pure.
Leviticus16:11 Then Aaron will offer the bull as a sin offering for himself. He will purify himself and his family. He will kill the bull for the sin offering for himself.
Leviticus16:12 Then he will take a firepan full of coals of fire from the altar before the Lord. Aaron will take two handfuls of sweet incense that has been ground into powder and take it into the room behind the curtain.
Leviticus16:13 He will put the incense on the fire before the Lord. Then the cloud of incense will hide the mercy-cover that is over the Box that holds the Agreement. This way Aaron will not die.
Leviticus16:14 Aaron will dip his finger into the bull's blood and sprinkle it on the front of the Holy Box. Then he will sprinkle the blood seven times onto the front of the mercy-cover.
Leviticus16:15 Then Aaron will kill the goat of the sin offering for the people. He will bring this goat's blood into the room behind the curtain. He will do with the goat's blood as he did with the bull's blood. He will sprinkle the goat's blood on the mercycover and in front of it.
Leviticus16:16 In this way Aaron will purify the Most Holy Place from all the uncleanness and sins of the Israelites. He will also purify the Meeting Tent
Leviticus16:17 No one must be in the Meeting Tent when Aaron goes in to purify the Most Holy Place. No one is to go in there until Aaron comes out after purifying himself
Leviticus16:18 Then Aaron will go out to the altar that is before the Lord. Aaron will make the altar pure. He will take some of the blood from the bull and from the goat and put it on the corners of the altar on all four sides.
Leviticus16:19 Then he will dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle it on the altar seven times. In this way Aaron will make the altar holy and clean from all the sins of the Israelites.
Leviticus16:20 So Aaron will make the Most Holy Place
Leviticus16:21 He will put both his hands on the head of the living goat. Then he will confess the sins and crimes of the Israelites over the goat. In this way Aaron will lay the people's sins on the goat's head. Then he will send the goat away into the desert. A man will be standing by
Leviticus16:22 So the goat will carry all the people's sins on itself into the empty desert. The man who leads the goat will let it loose in the desert.
Leviticus16:23 Then Aaron will enter the Meeting Tent. He will take off the linen clothes that he put on when he went into the Holy Place. He will leave these clothes there.
Leviticus16:24 He will wash his whole body with water in a holy place. Then he will put on his clothes. He will come out and offer his burnt offering and the people's burnt offering. He will make himself and the people pure.
Leviticus16:25 Then he will burn the fat of the sin offering on the altar.
Leviticus16:26 The man who led the goat to Azazel must wash his clothes and his whole body with water. After that he may come into the camp.
Leviticus16:27 The bull and the goat for the sin offerings will be taken outside the camp. (The blood from these animals was brought into the Holy Place to make {the holy things} pure.) The skins
Leviticus16:28 Then the man who burns them must wash his clothes and bathe his whole body with water. After that he may come into the camp.
Leviticus16:29 This law will always continue for you: On the tenth day of the seventh month
Leviticus16:30 Because on this day
Leviticus16:31 You must humble yourselves because this day is a very important day of rest for you. This law will continue forever.
Leviticus16:32 In the future
Leviticus16:33 and make the Most Holy Place
Leviticus16:34 That law will continue forever. Once every year you will purify the Israelites from all their sins. So they did everything that the Lord had commanded Moses.

Leviticus17:2 Speak to Aaron and to his sons

Leviticus17:4 It doesn't matter; you must bring that animal to the entrance of the Meeting Tent. You must give a part of that animal as a gift to the Lord. You spilled blood
Leviticus17:5 This rule is so that you will bring your fellowship offering to the Lord. You must bring any animal that you kill in the field to the Lord at the entrance of the Meeting Tent. Bring those animals to the priest.
Leviticus17:6 Then the priest will throw their blood onto the Lord's altar near the entrance of the Meeting Tent. And the priest will burn the fat from those animals on the altar as a sweet-smelling gift to the Lord.
Leviticus17:7 In this way you will stop being unfaithful to me by offering sacrifices to your 'goat gods.' This law will continue forever.

Leviticus17:9 They must take the sacrifice to the entrance of the Meeting Tent and offer it to the Lord. Whoever does not do this will be separated from their people.

Leviticus17:11 This is because the life of the body is in the blood. I have told you that you must pour the blood on the altar to purify yourselves. Blood is what makes a person pure.
Leviticus17:12 That is why I am telling you Israelites and the foreigners living among you that you must not eat blood.

Leviticus18:1 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus18:2 Tell the Israelites: I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus18:3 You must not follow the customs of Egypt where you lived
Leviticus18:4 You must obey my rules and follow my laws. Be sure to follow my rules because I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus18:5 You must obey my rules and my laws
Leviticus18:6 You must never have sexual relations with your close relatives. I am the Lord.
Leviticus18:7 You must never have sexual relations with your father or mother. She is your mother
Leviticus18:8 You must not have sexual relations with your father's wife
Leviticus18:9 You must not have sexual relations with your sister. It doesn't matter if she is the daughter of your father or your mother. And it doesn't matter if your sister was born in your house or at some other place.
Leviticus18:10 You must not have sexual relations with your granddaughter. It doesn't matter whether she is the daughter of your son or the daughter of your daughter—they are all a part of you!
Leviticus18:11 If your father and his wife have a daughter
Leviticus18:12 You must not have sexual relations with your father's sister. She is your father's close relative.
Leviticus18:13 You must not have sexual relations with your mother's sister. She is your mother's close relative.
Leviticus18:14 You must not have sexual relations with the wife of your father's brother. You must not go near your uncle's wife for sexual relations. She is your aunt.
Leviticus18:15 You must not have sexual relations with your daughter-in-law. She is your son's wife
Leviticus18:16 You must not have sexual relations with your brother's wife. That would be like having sexual relations with your brother.
Leviticus18:17 You must not have sexual relations with a mother and her daughter or her granddaughter. It doesn't matter if this granddaughter is the daughter of this woman's son or daughter. Her granddaughters are her close relatives. It is wrong to do this.
Leviticus18:18 While your wife is still living
Leviticus18:19 You must not go near a woman to have sexual relations with her during her monthly time of bleeding. She is unclean during this time.
Leviticus18:20 You must not have sexual relations with your neighbor's wife. This will only make you filthy.
Leviticus18:21 You must not give any of your children through the fire to Molech. If you do this
