Leviticus18:23 Men
Leviticus18:24 Don't make yourself unclean with anything wrong like that! I am throwing nations off their land and giving it to you because they did those terrible sins.
Leviticus18:25 They made the land filthy. Now the land is sick of those things
Leviticus18:26 So you must obey my laws and rules. You must not do any of these terrible sins. These rules are for the citizens of Israel and the people living among you.
Leviticus18:27 Those who lived in the land before you have done all these terrible things. So the land became filthy.
Leviticus18:28 If you do these things
Leviticus18:29 Whoever does any of these terrible sins must be separated from their people!
Leviticus18:30 Other people have done these terrible sins
Leviticus19:1 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus19:2 Tell all the Israelites: I am the Lord your God. I am holy
Leviticus19:3 Each of you must honor your mother and father and keep my special days of rest. I am the Lord your God!
Leviticus19:4 Do not worship idols. Do not make statues of gods for yourselves. I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus19:5 When you offer a sacrifice of fellowship offerings to the Lord
Leviticus19:6 You may eat it the same day you offer it and on the next day. But if any of that sacrifice is left on the third day
Leviticus19:7 You must not eat any of that sacrifice on the third day. It will be unclean
Leviticus19:8 You will be guilty of sin if you do that because you did not respect the holy things that belong to the Lord. If you do that you will be separated from your people.
Leviticus19:9 When you cut your crops at harvest time
Leviticus19:10 Don't pick all the grapes in your vineyards or pick up the grapes that fall to the ground. You must leave those things for your poor people and for people traveling through your country. I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus19:11 You must not steal. You must not cheat people. You must not lie to each other.
Leviticus19:12 You must not use my name to make false promises. If you do that
Leviticus19:13 You must not cheat or rob your neighbor. You must not hold a hired worker's salary overnight until morning.
Leviticus19:14 You must not curse anyone who is deaf. You must not do anything to make a blind person fall. But you must respect your God. I am the Lord.
Leviticus19:15 You must be fair in judgment. You must not show special favor to the poor. And you must not show special favor to important people. You must be fair when you judge your neighbor.
Leviticus19:16 You must not go around spreading false stories against other people. Don't do anything that would put your neighbor's life in danger. I am the Lord.
Leviticus19:17 You must not hate your people in your heart. If your neighbors do something wrong
Leviticus19:18 Forget about the wrong things people do to you. Don't try to get even. Love your neighbor as yourself. I am the Lord.
Leviticus19:19 You must obey my laws. You must not let your animals mate with animals of a different kind. You must not sow your field with two kinds of seed. You must not wear clothing made from two kinds of material mixed together.
Leviticus19:20 It may happen that a man has sexual relations with a woman who is the slave of another man. But this slave woman has not been bought or given her freedom. If this happens
Leviticus19:21 The man must bring his guilt offering to the Lord at the entrance of the Meeting Tent. He must bring a ram for a guilt offering.
Leviticus19:22 The priest will make him pure. The priest will offer the ram as a guilt offering before the Lord. It will be for the sins the man did. Then the man will be forgiven for his sins.
Leviticus19:23 In the future
Leviticus19:24 In the fourth year
Leviticus19:25 Then
Leviticus19:26 You must not eat any meat with blood still in it. You must not try to use different kinds of magic to tell the future.
Leviticus19:27 You must not round off the hair that grows on the side of your face. You must not cut your beard that grows on the side of your face.
Leviticus19:28 You must not cut your body as a way to remember the dead. You must not make any tattoo marks on yourselves. I am the Lord.
Leviticus19:29 Do not dishonor your daughters by making them become prostitutes. If you do that
Leviticus19:30 You must not work on my special days of rest. You must honor my holy place. I am the Lord.
Leviticus19:31 Do not go to mediums or wizards for advice—they will only make you unclean. I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus19:32 Show honor to old people. Stand up when they come into the room. And show respect to your leaders. I am the Lord.
Leviticus19:33 Do not do bad things to foreigners living in your country.
Leviticus19:34 You must treat them the same as you treat your own citizens. Love them as you love yourselves. Remember
Leviticus19:35 You must be fair when you judge people
Leviticus19:36 Your baskets should be the right size. Your jars should hold the right amount of liquids. Your weights and balances should weigh things correctly. I am the Lord your God. I brought you out of the land of Egypt.
Leviticus19:37 You must remember all my laws and rules. And you must obey them. I am the Lord.

Leviticus20:6 I will be against anyone who goes to mediums and wizards for advice. Whoever does this is being unfaithful to me. So I will separate them from their people.
Leviticus20:7 Be special. Make yourselves holy
Leviticus20:8 Remember and obey my laws. I am the Lord. And I have made you my special people.

Leviticus20:11 If a man has sexual relations with his father's wife

Leviticus20:13 If a man has sexual relations with another man as with a woman

Leviticus20:16 If a woman has sexual relations with an animal

Leviticus20:18 If a man has sexual relations with a woman during her monthly time of bleeding

Leviticus20:21 It is wrong for a man to take his brother's wife. It would be like having sexual relations with his brother! They will have no children.

Leviticus20:23 I am forcing other people to leave that country because they committed all those sins. I hate those sins! So don't live the way those people lived.
Leviticus20:24 But I have told you that you will get their land. I will give their land to you. It will be your land! It is a land filled with many good things. I am the Lord your God. I have treated you differently from other people.
Leviticus20:25 So you must treat clean animals differently from unclean animals. You must also treat clean birds differently from unclean birds. Don't eat any of these unclean birds or animals or things that crawl on the ground. I have made these things unclean.
Leviticus20:26 I have made you my special people. So you must be holy because I am the Lord
Leviticus20:27 A man or a woman who is a medium or a wizard must be put to death. The people must kill them with stones. They are responsible for their own death.

Leviticus22:25 You must not take animals from foreigners as sacrifices to the Lord. The animals might have been hurt in some way. They might have something wrong with them
Leviticus22:26 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus22:27 When a calf
Leviticus22:28 But you must not kill the animal and its mother on the same day. This rule is the same for cattle and sheep.
Leviticus22:29 If you want to offer some special offering of thanks to the Lord
Leviticus22:30 You must eat the whole animal that day. You must not leave any of the meat for the next morning. I am the Lord.
Leviticus22:31 Remember my commands
Leviticus22:32 Show respect for my holy name. I must be very special to the Israelites. I
Leviticus22:33 I brought you out of Egypt in order to be your God. I am the Lord.
Leviticus23:1 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus23:2 Tell the Israelites: You will announce the Lord's chosen festivals as holy meetings. These are my special festivals.
Leviticus23:3 Work for six days
Leviticus23:4 These are the Lord's chosen festivals. You will announce the holy meetings at the times chosen for them.
Leviticus23:5 The Lord's Passover is on the 14 day of the first month just before dark.
Leviticus23:6 The Festival of Unleavened Bread is on the 15 day of the same month. You will eat unleavened bread for seven days.
Leviticus23:7 On the first day of this festival
Leviticus23:8 For seven days
Leviticus23:9 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus23:10 Tell the Israelites: You will enter the land that I will give you and reap its harvest. At that time you must bring in the first sheaf of your harvest to the priest.
Leviticus23:11 The priest will lift the sheaf to show it was offered before the Lord. Then you will be accepted. The priest will present the sheaf on Sunday morning.
Leviticus23:12 On the day when you present the sheaf
Leviticus23:13 You must also offer a grain offering of 16 cups of fine flour mixed with olive oil. You must also offer 1 quart of wine. The smell of that offering will please the Lord.
Leviticus23:14 You must not eat any of the new grain
Leviticus23:15 From that Sunday morning (the day you bring the sheaf to be presented to God)
Leviticus23:16 On the Sunday following the seventh week (that is
Leviticus23:17 On that day
Leviticus23:18 One bull
Leviticus23:19 You will also offer one male goat for a sin offering and two one-year-old male lambs as a fellowship offering.
Leviticus23:20 The priest will lift them up with the bread from the first harvest to show they were offered with the two lambs before the Lord. They are holy to the Lord. They will belong to the priest.
Leviticus23:21 On that same day
Leviticus23:22 Also
Leviticus23:23 Again the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus23:24 Tell the Israelites: On the first day of the seventh month
Leviticus23:25 You must not do any work. You must bring an offering as a gift to the Lord.
Leviticus23:26 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus23:27 The Day of Atonement will be on the tenth day of the seventh month. There will be a holy meeting. You must not eat food
Leviticus23:28 You must not do any work on that day
Leviticus23:29 Anyone who refuses to fast on this day must be separated from their people.
Leviticus23:30 If anyone does any work on this day
Leviticus23:31 You must not do any work at all. This is a law that continues forever for you
Leviticus23:32 It will be a special day of rest for you. You must not eat food. You will start this special day of rest on the evening following the ninth day of the month. This special day of rest continues from that evening until the next evening.
Leviticus23:33 Again the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus23:34 Tell the Israelites: On the 15 day of the seventh month is the Festival of Shelters. This festival to the Lord will continue for seven days.
Leviticus23:35 There will be a holy meeting on the first day. You must not do any work.
Leviticus23:36 You will bring offerings as gifts to the Lord for seven days. On the eighth day you will have another holy meeting. This will be a holy meeting. You must not do any work. You will bring an offering as a gift to the Lord.
Leviticus23:37 These are the Lord's special festivals. There will be holy meetings during these festivals. You will bring offerings as gifts to the Lord—burnt offerings
Leviticus23:38 You will celebrate these festivals in addition to remembering the Lord's Sabbath days. You will offer these gifts in addition to your other gifts to the Lord. You will offer these things in addition to any offerings you give as payment for your special promises. They will be in addition to any special offerings you want to give to the Lord.
Leviticus23:39 On the 15 day of the seventh month
Leviticus23:40 On the first day you will take good fruit from fruit trees. And you will take branches from palm trees
Leviticus23:41 You will celebrate this festival to the Lord for seven days each year. This law will continue forever. You will celebrate this festival in the seventh month.
Leviticus23:42 You will live in temporary shelters for seven days. All the people born in Israel will live in them.
Leviticus23:43 Why? So all your descendants will know that I made the Israelites live in temporary shelters during the time I brought them out of Egypt. I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus23:44 So Moses told the Israelites about all of the special meetings to honor the Lord.
Leviticus24:1 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus24:2 Command the Israelites to bring to you pure oil from crushed olives. That oil is for the lamps that must burn without stopping.
Leviticus24:3 Aaron will keep the light burning in the Meeting Tent before the Lord from evening until morning. This light will be outside the curtain that hangs in front of the Box that holds the Agreement. This law will continue forever.
Leviticus24:4 Aaron must always keep the lamps burning on the lampstand of pure gold before the Lord.
Leviticus24:5 Take fine flour and bake twelve loaves with it. Use 16 cups of flour for each loaf.
Leviticus24:6 Put them in two rows on the golden table before the Lord. Six loaves will be in each row.
Leviticus24:7 Put pure frankincense on each row. This will help the Lord remember the gift.
Leviticus24:8 Every Sabbath day Aaron will put the bread in order before the Lord. This must be done forever. This agreement with the Israelites will continue forever.
Leviticus24:9 That bread will belong to Aaron and his sons. They will eat the bread in a holy place
Leviticus24:10 There was a son of an Israelite woman and an Egyptian father. He was walking among the Israelites
Leviticus24:11 The Israelite woman's son began cursing and saying bad things about the Lord's name
Leviticus24:12 The people held him as a prisoner and waited for the Lord's command to be made clear to them.
Leviticus24:13 Then the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus24:14 Bring the man who cursed to a place outside the camp. Then bring together everyone who heard him curse. They will put their hands on his head. And then all the people must throw stones at him and kill him.
Leviticus24:15 You must tell the Israelites: Anyone who curses their God must be punished.
Leviticus24:16 Anyone who speaks against the name of the Lord must be put to death. All the people must stone him. Foreigners must be punished just as the person who was born in Israel. Anyone who curses the Lord's name must be put to death.
Leviticus24:17 And whoever kills another person must be put to death.
Leviticus24:18 Whoever kills an animal that belongs to another person must give another animal to take its place.
Leviticus24:19 And whoever causes an injury to their neighbor must be given the same kind of injury:
Leviticus24:20 A broken bone for a broken bone
Leviticus24:21 Whoever kills an animal must pay for the animal. But whoever kills another person must be put to death.
Leviticus24:22 The law will be the same for foreigners and for people from your own country. This is because I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus24:23 Then Moses spoke to the Israelites
Leviticus25:1 The Lord spoke to Moses at Mount Sinai. He said
Leviticus25:2 Tell the Israelites: When you enter the land that I am giving to you
Leviticus25:3 You will plant seed in your field for six years. You will trim your vineyards for six years and bring in its fruits.
Leviticus25:4 But during the seventh year
Leviticus25:5 You must not cut the crops that grow by themselves after your harvest. You must not gather the grapes from your vines that are not trimmed. The land will have a year of rest.
Leviticus25:6 The land will have a year of rest
Leviticus25:7 And there will be enough food for your cattle and other animals to eat.
Leviticus25:8 You will also count seven groups of seven years. This will be 49 years. During that time there will be seven years of rest for the land.
Leviticus25:9 On the Day of Atonement
Leviticus25:10 You will make the 50 year a special year. You will announce freedom for everyone living in your country. This time will be called 'Jubilee.' Each of you will go back to your own property. And each of you will go back to your own family.
Leviticus25:11 The 50 year will be a special celebration for you. Don't plant seeds
Leviticus25:12 That year is Jubilee. It will be a holy time for you. You will eat the crops that come from the field.
Leviticus25:13 In the year of Jubilee
Leviticus25:14 Don't cheat your neighbors when you sell your land to them. Don't cheat one another when you buy or sell land.
Leviticus25:15 If you want to buy your neighbor's land
Leviticus25:16 If there are many years {before the next Jubilee}
Leviticus25:17 ***
Leviticus25:18 Remember my laws and rules. Obey them and you will live safely in your country.
Leviticus25:19 And the land will produce good crops for you. Then you will have plenty of food
Leviticus25:20 But maybe you will say
Leviticus25:21 I will order my blessing to come to you during the sixth year. The land will continue growing crops for three years.
Leviticus25:22 When you plant in the eighth year
Leviticus25:23 The land really belongs to me
Leviticus25:24 People might sell their land
Leviticus25:25 If someone in your country becomes very poor and must sell their property
Leviticus25:26 If there is not a close relative to buy back the land
Leviticus25:27 Then the years must be counted since the land was sold. That number must be used to decide how much to pay for the land. The person must then buy back the land
Leviticus25:28 But if this first owner cannot find enough money to buy the land back
Leviticus25:29 Anyone who sells a home in a walled city still has the right to get it back until a full year after it was sold. Their right to get the house back will continue one year.
Leviticus25:30 But if the owner does not buy back the house before a full year is finished
Leviticus25:31 Towns without walls around them will be treated like open fields. So houses built in these small towns will go back to the first owners at the time of Jubilee.
Leviticus25:32 But about the cities of the Levites: The Levites can buy back at any time their houses in the cities that belong to them.
Leviticus25:33 If someone buys a house from a Levite
Leviticus25:34 Also
Leviticus25:35 If anyone from your own country becomes too poor to support themselves
Leviticus25:36 Don't charge them any interest on money you might loan to them. Respect your God and let those from your own country live with you.
Leviticus25:37 Don't charge them interest on any money you lend them. And don't try to make a profit from the food you sell them.
Leviticus25:38 I am the Lord your God. I brought you out of the land of Egypt to give the land of Canaan to you and to become your God.
Leviticus25:39 If anyone from your own country becomes so poor that they must sell themselves to you
Leviticus25:40 They will be like hired workers and visitors with you until the year of Jubilee.
Leviticus25:41 Then they can leave you
Leviticus25:42 because they are my servants. I brought them out of slavery in Egypt. They must not become slaves again.
Leviticus25:43 You must not be a cruel master to them. You must respect your God.
Leviticus25:44 About your men and women slaves: You may get men and women slaves from the other nations around you.
Leviticus25:45 Also
Leviticus25:46 You may even pass these foreign slaves on to your children after you die so that they will belong to them. They will be your slaves forever. You may make slaves of these foreigners. But you must not be a cruel master over your own brothers
Leviticus25:47 Maybe a foreigner or visitor among you becomes rich. Or maybe someone from your own country becomes so poor that they sell themselves as slaves to a foreigner living among you or to a member of a foreigner's family.
Leviticus25:48 These people have the right to be bought back and become free. Someone from their own country can buy them back.
Leviticus25:49 Or their uncle
Leviticus25:50 You must count the years from the time they sold themselves to the foreigner up to the next year of Jubilee. Use that number to decide the price
Leviticus25:51 If there are still many years before the year of Jubilee
Leviticus25:52 If only a few years are left until the year of Jubilee
Leviticus25:53 But that person will live like a hired worker with the foreigner every year. Don't let the foreigner be a cruel master over that person.
Leviticus25:54 Those who sold themselves will become free
Leviticus25:55 This is because the Israelites are my servants. They are the servants who I brought out of slavery in Egypt. I am the Lord your God!
Leviticus26:1 Don't make idols for yourselves. Don't set up statues or memorial stones in your land to bow down to
Leviticus26:2 Remember my special days of rest and honor my holy place. I am the Lord.
Leviticus26:3 Remember my laws and commands
Leviticus26:4 If you do these things
Leviticus26:5 Your threshing will continue until it is time to gather grapes. And your grape gathering will continue until it is time to plant. Then you will have plenty to eat. And you will live safely in your land.
Leviticus26:6 I will give peace to your country. You will lie down in peace. No one will come to make you afraid. I will keep harmful animals out of your country. And armies will not come through your country.
Leviticus26:7 You will chase your enemies and defeat them. You will kill them with your sword.
Leviticus26:8 Five of you will chase 100 men
Leviticus26:9 Then I will turn to you. I will let you have many children. I will keep my agreement with you.
Leviticus26:10 You will have enough crops to last for more than a year. You will harvest the new crops. But then you will have to throw out the old crops to make room for the new crops.
Leviticus26:11 Also
Leviticus26:12 I will walk with you and be your God. And you will be my people.
Leviticus26:13 I am the Lord your God. You were slaves in Egypt
Leviticus26:14 But if you don't obey me and all my commands
Leviticus26:15 If you refuse to obey my laws and commands
Leviticus26:16 If you do that
Leviticus26:17 I will be against you
Leviticus26:18 After these things
Leviticus26:19 And I will also destroy the great cities that make you proud. The skies will not give rain
Leviticus26:20 You will work hard
Leviticus26:21 If you still turn against me and refuse to obey me
Leviticus26:22 I will send wild animals against you. They will take your children away from you. They will destroy your animals. They will kill many of your people. The roads will all be empty.
Leviticus26:23 If you don't learn your lesson after all this
Leviticus26:24 then I will also turn against you. Yes
Leviticus26:25 You will have broken my agreement
Leviticus26:26 I will give you a share of the grain left in that city. But there will be very little food to eat. Ten women will be able to cook all their bread in one oven. They will measure each piece of bread. You will eat
Leviticus26:27 If you still refuse to listen to me
Leviticus26:28 then I will really show my anger! Yes
Leviticus26:29 {You will become so hungry that} you will eat the bodies of your sons and daughters.
Leviticus26:30 I will destroy your high places. I will cut down your incense altars. I will put your dead bodies on the dead bodies of your idols. You will be very disgusting to me.
Leviticus26:31 I will destroy your cities. I will make your holy places empty. I will stop smelling your offerings.
Leviticus26:32 I will make your land empty. And your enemies who come to live there will be shocked at it.
Leviticus26:33 I will scatter you among the nations. I will pull out my sword and destroy you. Your land will become empty
Leviticus26:34 You will be taken to your enemy's country. Your country will be empty. So your land will finally get its rest. The land will enjoy its time of rest.
Leviticus26:35 During the time that the land is empty
Leviticus26:36 The survivors will lose their courage in the land of their enemies. They will be scared of everything. They will run around like a leaf being blown by the wind. They will run as if someone is chasing them with a sword. They will fall even when no one is chasing them.
Leviticus26:37 They will run as if someone is chasing them with a sword. They will fall over each other—even when no one is chasing them. You will not be strong enough to stand up against your enemies.
Leviticus26:38 You will be lost in other nations. You will disappear in the land of your enemies.
Leviticus26:39 So the survivors will rot away in their sin in their enemies' countries. They will rot away in their sins just as their ancestors did.
Leviticus26:40 But maybe the people will confess their sins. And maybe they will confess the sins of their ancestors. Maybe they will admit that they turned against me. Maybe they will admit that they sinned against me.
Leviticus26:41 Maybe they will admit that I turned against them and brought them into the land of their enemies. These people will be like strangers to me. But maybe they will become humble and accept the punishment for their sin.
Leviticus26:42 If they do
Leviticus26:43 The land will be empty. The land will enjoy its time of rest. Then the survivors will accept the punishment for their sins. They will learn that they were punished because they hated my laws and refused to obey my rules.
Leviticus26:44 They have sinned. {But if they come to me for help
Leviticus26:45 For them
Leviticus26:46 These are the laws
Leviticus27:1 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus27:2 Tell the Israelites: You might promise to give someone to the Lord as a servant. The priest must set a price for that person.
Leviticus27:3 The price for a man from 20 to 60 years old is 50 shekels of silver. (You must use the official measure for the silver.)
Leviticus27:4 The price for a woman who is 20 to 60 years old is 30 shekels.
Leviticus27:5 The price for a man from 5 to 20 years old is 20 shekels. The price for a woman {from 5 to 20 years old} is 10 shekels.
Leviticus27:6 The price for a boy from one month to five years old is 5 shekels. For a girl
Leviticus27:7 The price for a man who is 60 years old or older is 15 shekels. The price for a woman is 10 shekels.
Leviticus27:8 If anyone is too poor to pay the price
Leviticus27:9 Some animals can be used as sacrifices to the Lord. If you bring one of these animals
Leviticus27:10 You must not try to put another animal in its place. You must not try to change it for something else. You must not try to change a good animal for a bad animal. You must not change a bad animal for a good animal. If you do try to change animals
Leviticus27:11 Some animals cannot be offered as sacrifices to the Lord. If you bring one of these unclean animals to the Lord
Leviticus27:12 The priest will decide a price for that animal. It doesn't make any difference if the animal is good or bad. If the priest decides on a price
Leviticus27:13 If you want to buy back the animal
Leviticus27:14 If you dedicate your house as holy to the Lord
Leviticus27:15 But if you want to get the house back
Leviticus27:16 You might dedicate a field to God. The value of this field will depend on how much seed is needed to plant it. It will be 50 shekels of silver for each homer of barley seed.
Leviticus27:17 If you give your field to God during the year of Jubilee
Leviticus27:18 But if you give your field after the Jubilee
Leviticus27:19 If you want to buy the field back
Leviticus27:20 If you don't buy the field back and the land is sold to someone else
Leviticus27:21 If you don't buy the land back by the year of Jubilee
Leviticus27:22 If you dedicate a field to the Lord that you had bought
Leviticus27:23 then the priest must count the years to the year of Jubilee and decide the price for the land. Then that land will belong to the Lord.
Leviticus27:24 At the year of Jubilee
Leviticus27:25 You must use the official measure in paying these prices. The shekel by that measure weighs 20 gerahs.
Leviticus27:26 You can give cattle and sheep as special gifts to the Lord. But if the animal is the firstborn
Leviticus27:27 If the firstborn animal is an unclean animal
Leviticus27:28 There is a special kind of gift that people give to the Lord. It belongs only to him
Leviticus27:29 If this gift is a person
Leviticus27:30 A tenth of all crops belong to the Lord. This means the crops from fields and the fruit from trees—a tenth belongs to the Lord.
Leviticus27:31 So if you want to get back your tenth
Leviticus27:32 The priests will take every tenth animal from a person's cattle or sheep. Every tenth animal will belong to the Lord.
Leviticus27:33 The owner should not worry if the chosen animal is good or bad or change the animal for another animal. If this happens
Leviticus27:34 These are the commands that the Lord gave Moses at Mount Sinai for the Israelites.
Numbers1:1 The Lord spoke to Moses in the Meeting Tent. This was in the Sinai Desert. It was on the first day of the second month of the second year after the Israelites left Egypt. He said to Moses:
Numbers1:2 Count all the Israelites. List the name of each man with his family and his family group.
Numbers1:3 You and Aaron must count the men of Israel who are 20 years old or older. (These are all the men who are able to serve in the army of Israel.) List them by their divisions.
Numbers1:4 One man from each tribe will help you. This man will be the leader of his tribe.
Numbers1:5 These are the names of the men who will stand with you and help you: from the tribe of Reuben—Elizur son of Shedeur;
Numbers1:6 from the tribe of Simeon—Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai;
Numbers1:7 from the tribe of Judah—Nahshon son of Amminadab;
Numbers1:8 from the tribe of Issachar—Nethanel son of Zuar;
Numbers1:9 from the tribe of Zebulun—Eliab son of Helon;
Numbers1:10 from the descendants of Joseph: from the tribe of Ephraim—Elishama son of Ammihud; from the tribe of Manasseh—Gamaliel son of Pedahzur;
Numbers1:11 from the tribe of Benjamin—Abidan son of Gideoni;
Numbers1:12 from the tribe of Dan—Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai;
Numbers1:13 from the tribe of Asher—Pagiel son of Ocran;
Numbers1:14 from the tribe of Gad—Eliasaph son of Deuel;
Numbers1:15 from the tribe of Naphtali—Ahira son of Enan.
Numbers1:16 All these men were the leaders of their families. The people also chose them to be leaders of their tribes.
Numbers1:17 Moses and Aaron took the men who had been chosen to be leaders
Numbers1:18 and called all the Israelites together on the first day of the second month. Then the people were listed by their families and their family groups. All the men who were 20 years old or older were listed.
Numbers1:19 Moses did exactly what the Lord commanded—he counted the people while they were in the Sinai desert.
Numbers1:20 The tribe of Reuben was counted. (Reuben was the firstborn son of Israel.) The names of all the men who were 20 years old or older and able to serve in the army were listed. They were listed with their families and family groups.
Numbers1:21 The total number of men counted from the tribe of Reuben was 46
Numbers1:22 The tribe of Simeon was counted. The names of all the men who were 20 years old or older and able to serve in the army were listed. They were listed with their families and family groups.
Numbers1:23 The total number of men counted from the tribe of Simeon was 59
Numbers1:24 The tribe of Gad was counted. The names of all the men who were 20 years old or older and able to serve in the army were listed. They were listed with their families and family groups.
Numbers1:25 The total number of men counted from the tribe of Gad was 45
Numbers1:26 The tribe of Judah was counted. The names of all the men who were 20 years old or older and able to serve in the army were listed. They were listed with their families and family groups.
Numbers1:27 The total number of men counted from the tribe of Judah was 74
Numbers1:28 The tribe of Issachar was counted. The names of all the men who were 20 years old or older and able to serve in the army were listed. They were listed with their families and family groups.
Numbers1:29 The total number of men counted from the tribe of Issachar was 54
Numbers1:30 The tribe of Zebulun was counted. The names of all the men who were 20 years old or older and able to serve in the army were listed. They were listed with their families and family groups.
Numbers1:31 The total number of men counted from the tribe of Zebulun was 57
Numbers1:32 The tribe of Ephraim was counted. (Ephraim was Joseph's son.) The names of all the men who were 20 years old or older and able to serve in the army were listed. They were listed with their families and family groups.
Numbers1:33 The total number of men counted from the tribe of Ephraim was 40
Numbers1:34 The tribe of Manasseh was counted. (Manasseh was also Joseph's son.) The names of all the men who were 20 years old or older and able to serve in the army were listed. They were listed with their families and family groups.
Numbers1:35 The total number of men counted from the tribe of Manasseh was 32
Numbers1:36 The tribe of Benjamin was counted. The names of all the men who were 20 years old or older and able to serve in the army were listed. They were listed with their families and family groups.
Numbers1:37 The total number of men counted from the tribe of Benjamin was 35
Numbers1:38 The tribe of Dan was counted. The names of all the men who were 20 years old or older and able to serve in the army were listed. They were listed with their families and family groups.
Numbers1:39 The total number of men counted from the tribe of Dan was 62
Numbers1:40 The tribe of Asher was counted. The names of all the men who were 20 years old or older and able to serve in the army were listed. They were listed with their families and family groups.
Numbers1:41 The total number of men counted from the tribe of Asher was 41
Numbers1:42 The tribe of Naphtali was counted. The names of all the men who were 20 years old or older and able to serve in the army were listed. They were listed by name with their families and family groups.
Numbers1:43 The total number of men counted from the tribe of Naphtali was 53
Numbers1:44 Moses
Numbers1:45 They counted every man who was 20 years old or older and able to serve in the army. Each man was listed with his family.
Numbers1:46 The total number of men counted was 603
Numbers1:47 The families from the tribe of Levi were not counted with the other Israelites.
Numbers1:48 The Lord had told Moses:
Numbers1:49 Don't count the men from the tribe of Levi or include them with the other Israelites.
Numbers1:50 Tell the Levites that they are responsible for the Tent of the Agreement. They must take care of that tent and everything that is with it. They must carry the Holy Tent and everything in it. They must make their camp around it and take care of it.
Numbers1:51 Whenever the Holy Tent is moved
Numbers1:52 The Israelites will make their camps in separate divisions. Everyone must camp near their family flag.
Numbers1:53 The Levites will set up their tents so that they surround the Tent of the Agreement. Then they will serve as guards to keep people from doing anything to the Tent that will make the Lord angry. This will protect their people.
Numbers1:54 The Lord had given these commandments to Moses. So the Israelites did everything he commanded.

Numbers2:27 The people from the tribe of Asher will camp next to the tribe of Dan. The leader of the tribe of Asher is Pagiel son of Ocran.
Numbers2:28 There are 41
Numbers2:29 The tribe of Naphtali will also camp next to the tribe of Dan. The leader of the tribe of Naphtali is Ahira son of Enan.
Numbers2:30 There are 53
Numbers2:31 There are 157
Numbers2:32 So these were the Israelites. They were counted by families. The total number of Israelite men in the camps
Numbers2:33 Moses obeyed the Lord and did not count the Levites with the other Israelites.
Numbers2:34 So the Israelites did everything the Lord told Moses. Each group camped under its own flag. And everyone stayed with their own family and family group.
Numbers3:1 This is the family history of Aaron and Moses at the time the Lord talked to Moses on Mount Sinai.
Numbers3:2 Aaron had four sons. Nadab was the firstborn son. Then there were Abihu
Numbers3:3 These sons were the chosen priests. They were given the special work of serving the Lord as priests.
Numbers3:4 But Nadab and Abihu died while serving the Lord. They used fire that the Lord did not allow when they made an offering to him. So Nadab and Abihu died there
Numbers3:5 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers3:6 Bring all the men from the tribe of Levi. Bring them to Aaron the priest. These men will be his helpers.
Numbers3:7 The Levites will help him when he serves at the Meeting Tent. And they will help all the Israelites when they come to worship at the Holy Tent.
Numbers3:8 The Israelites should protect everything in the Meeting Tent; it is their duty. But the Levites will serve the Israelites by caring for these things. This will be their way of serving at the Holy Tent.
Numbers3:9 Give the Levites to Aaron and his sons. The Levites were chosen from all the Israelites to help Aaron and his sons.
Numbers3:10 Appoint Aaron and his sons to be priests. They must do their duty and serve as priests. Anyone else who tries to come near the holy things must be killed.
Numbers3:11 The Lord also said to Moses
Numbers3:12 I destroyed all the firstborn in Egypt. At that time I chose all the firstborn from every family in Israel to be mine in a special way. That included all the firstborn men and animals. But now I am choosing the Levites to take their place. Now they will be my special servants. I
Numbers3:13 ***
Numbers3:14 The Lord again said to Moses in the Sinai desert
Numbers3:15 Count all the families and family groups in the tribe of Levi. Count every man or boy who is one month old or older.
Numbers3:16 So Moses obeyed the Lord. He counted them all.
Numbers3:17 Levi had three sons. Their names were Gershon
Numbers3:18 Each son was the leader of several family groups. The Gershon family groups were Libni and Shimei.
Numbers3:19 The Kohath family groups were Amram
Numbers3:20 The Merari family groups were Mahli and Mushi. These are the families that belonged to Levi's family group.
Numbers3:21 The families of Libni and Shimei belonged to the family of Gershon. They were the Gershonite family groups.
Numbers3:22 There were 7500 men and boys over one month old in these two family groups.
Numbers3:23 The Gershonite family groups were told to camp in the west. They made their camp behind the Holy Tent.
Numbers3:24 The leader of the family groups of the Gershonites was Eliasaph son of Lael.
Numbers3:25 In the Meeting Tent
Numbers3:26 They cared for the curtain in the courtyard. And they cared for the curtain at the entrance of the courtyard. This courtyard was around the Holy Tent and the altar. And they cared for the ropes and for everything that was used with the curtains.
Numbers3:27 The families of Amram
Numbers3:28 In this family group there were 8300 men and boys a month old or over. The Kohathites were given the job of taking care of the things in the Holy Place.
Numbers3:29 The Kohathite family groups were given the area to the south of the Holy Tent. This was the area where they camped.
Numbers3:30 The leader of the Kohathite family groups was Elizaphan son of Uzziel.
Numbers3:31 Their job was to take care of the Holy Box
Numbers3:32 The leader over the leaders of the Levites was Eleazar son of Aaron the priest. Eleazar was in charge of everyone who took care of the holy things.
Numbers3:33 The family groups of Mahli and Mushi belonged to the Merari family. There were 6200 men and boys who were one month old or older in the Mahli family group.
Numbers3:34 ***
Numbers3:35 The leader of the Merari family group was Zuriel son of Abihail. This family group was given the area to the north of the Holy Tent. This is the area where they camped.
Numbers3:36 The people from the Merari family were given the job of caring for the frames of the Holy Tent. They cared for all the braces
Numbers3:37 They also cared for all the posts in the courtyard around the Holy Tent. This included all the bases
Numbers3:38 Moses
Numbers3:39 The Lord commanded Moses and Aaron to count all the men and boys one month old or older in Levi's family group. The total number was 22
Numbers3:40 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers3:41 I will take the Levites instead of all the firstborn men and boys of Israel. I will also take the animals from the Levites instead of taking all the firstborn animals from the other people in Israel.
Numbers3:42 So Moses did what the Lord commanded. He counted all the firstborn children of the Israelites.
Numbers3:43 He listed all the firstborn men and boys who were one month old or older. There were 22
Numbers3:44 The Lord also said to Moses
Numbers3:45 I
Numbers3:46 There are 22
Numbers3:47 Using the official measure
Numbers3:48 Give the silver to Aaron and his sons as payment for the 273 Israelites.'
Numbers3:49 There were not enough Levites to take the place of all of the men from the other family groups
Numbers3:50 Moses collected the silver from the firstborn men of the Israelites. He collected 1365 shekels of silver
Numbers3:51 Moses obeyed the Lord's command and gave the silver to Aaron and his sons.
Numbers4:1 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Numbers4:2 Count the men in the families of the Kohath family group. (The Kohath family group is a part of Levi's family group.)
Numbers4:3 Count all the men from 30 to 50 years old who come to serve. These men will work in the Meeting Tent.
Numbers4:4 Their job is to take care of the most holy things in the Meeting Tent.
Numbers4:5 When the Israelites travel to a new place
Numbers4:6 Then they must cover all of this with covering made from fine leather. Then they must spread the solid blue cloth over the leather and put the poles in the rings on the Holy Box.
Numbers4:7 Then they must spread a blue cloth over the holy table. Then they must put the plates
Numbers4:8 Then you must put a red cloth over all of these things
Numbers4:9 Then they must cover the lampstand and its lamps with a blue cloth. They must also cover all the things used to keep the lamps burning and all the jars of oil that are used in the lamps.
Numbers4:10 Then wrap everything in fine leather. Then they must put all these things on poles used for carrying them.
Numbers4:11 They must spread a blue cloth over the golden altar. They must cover that with fine leather. Then they must put the poles for carrying it in the rings on the altar.
Numbers4:12 Then they must gather together all the special things that are used for worship in the Holy Place. They must gather them together and wrap them in a blue cloth. Then they must cover that with fine leather. They must put these things on a frame for carrying them.
Numbers4:13 They must clean the ashes out of the bronze altar and spread a purple cloth over it.
Numbers4:14 Then they must gather together all the things that are used for worship at the altar. These are the fire pans
Numbers4:15 Aaron and his sons must finish covering all the holy things in the Holy Place. Then the men from the Kohath family can go in and begin carrying these things. In this way they will not touch the holy things and die.
Numbers4:16 Eleazar son of Aaron the priest will be responsible for the Holy Tent. He will be responsible for everything in it
Numbers4:17 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Numbers4:18 Be careful! Don't let these Kohathite men be destroyed.
Numbers4:19 You must do these things so that the Kohathite men can go near the most holy things and not die. Aaron and his sons must go in and show each Kohathite man what to do and what to carry.
Numbers4:20 If you don't do this
Numbers4:21 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers4:22 Count all the people of the Gershon family. List them by family and family group.
Numbers4:23 Count all the men who are from 30 to 50 years old who come to serve. These men will have the job of caring for the Meeting Tent.
Numbers4:24 This is what the Gershonite family must do and the things they must carry:
Numbers4:25 They must carry the curtains of the Holy Tent
Numbers4:26 They must carry the curtains of the courtyard that are around the Holy Tent and the altar. And they must carry the curtain for the entrance of the courtyard. They must also carry all of the ropes and all the things that are used with the curtains. The Gershonite men will be responsible for anything that needs to be done with these things.
Numbers4:27 Aaron and his sons will watch all the work that is done. Everything the Gershonites carry and the other work they do will be watched by Aaron and his sons. You must tell them what they are responsible for carrying.
Numbers4:28 This is the work that the men of the Gershonite family group must do for the Meeting Tent. Ithamar son of Aaron the priest will be responsible for their work.
Numbers4:29 Count all of the men in the families and family groups in the Merari family group.
Numbers4:30 Count all the men who are from 30 to 50 years old and come to serve. These men will do a special work for the Meeting Tent.
Numbers4:31 When you travel
Numbers4:32 They must also carry the posts that are around the courtyard. They must carry the bases
Numbers4:33 This is what the people from the Merari family will do to serve in the work for the Meeting Tent. Ithamar son of Aaron the priest will be responsible for their work.
Numbers4:34 Moses
Numbers4:35 They counted all the men from 30 to 50 years old who were able to serve. These men were given special work to do for the Meeting Tent.
Numbers4:36 There were 2750 men in the Kohath family group who were qualified to do this work.
Numbers4:37 So these men from the Kohath family group were given their special work to do for the Meeting Tent. Moses and Aaron did this the way the Lord had told Moses to do.
Numbers4:38 Also
Numbers4:39 All the men from 30 to 50 years old who qualified to serve were counted. These men were given their special work to do for the Meeting Tent.
Numbers4:40 There were 2630 men in the families of the Gershon family group who were qualified.
Numbers4:41 So these men from the Gershon family group were given their special work to do for the Meeting Tent. Moses and Aaron did this the way the Lord had told Moses to do.
Numbers4:42 Also
Numbers4:43 All the men from 30 to 50 years old who qualified to serve were counted. These men were given their special work to do for the Meeting Tent.
Numbers4:44 There were 3200 men in the families of the Merari family group who were qualified.
Numbers4:45 So these men from the Merari family group were given their special work. Moses and Aaron did this the way the Lord told Moses to do.
Numbers4:46 So Moses
Numbers4:47 All the men between the ages of 30 and 50 who qualified to serve were counted. These men were given a special work to do for the Meeting Tent. They did the work of carrying the Meeting Tent when they traveled.
Numbers4:48 The total number was 8580.
Numbers4:49 Each man was counted just as the Lord commanded Moses. Each man was given his own work and told what he must carry just as the Lord commanded.
Numbers5:1 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers5:2 Tell the people to send away from the camp anyone who is unclean
Numbers5:3 Whether they are a man or a woman
Numbers5:4 So the Israelites obeyed God's command. They sent those people outside the camp. They did what the Lord had commanded Moses.
Numbers5:5 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers5:6 Tell this to the Israelites: You might do something wrong to another person. When you do that
Numbers5:7 You must confess that sin. Then you must fully pay for that wrong thing you did. You must add one-fifth to the payment and give it all to the person you had done wrong to.
Numbers5:8 But maybe the person is dead and does not have any close relatives to accept the payment. In that case
Numbers5:9 If any of you Israelites gives a special gift to God
Numbers5:10 You don't have to give these special gifts
Numbers5:11 Then the Lord said to Moses
Numbers5:12 Tell the Israelites this: A man's wife might be unfaithful to him.
Numbers5:13 She might have sexual relations with another man and hide this from her husband. And there might not be anyone to tell him that his wife committed this sin. Her husband might never know about the wrong thing she did
Numbers5:14 But the husband might begin to think that his wife sinned against him
Numbers5:15 If that happens
Numbers5:16 The priest will take the woman before the Lord and make her stand there.
Numbers5:17 Then he will take some special water and put it in a clay jar. He will put some dirt from the floor of the Holy Tent into the water.
Numbers5:18 He will force the woman to stand before the Lord. Then he will loosen her hair and put the grain offering in her hand. This is the barley flour that her husband gave because he was jealous. At the same time
Numbers5:19 Then the priest will make the woman promise to tell the truth and say to her: 'If you have not slept with another man
Numbers5:20 But if you have sinned against your husband—if you had sexual relations with a man who is not your husband—then you are not pure.
Numbers5:21 If that is true
Numbers5:22 The priest must say
Numbers5:23 The priest should write these warnings on a scroll. Then he should wash the words off into the water.
Numbers5:24 Then the woman must drink the water that brings trouble. This water will enter her and
Numbers5:25 Then the priest will take the grain offering from her (the offering for jealousy) and raise it before the Lord. Then he will carry it to the altar.
Numbers5:26 The priest will fill his hands with some of the grain and put it on the altar and let it burn there. After that he will tell the woman to drink the water.
Numbers5:27 If the woman has sinned against her husband
Numbers5:28 But if the woman has not sinned against her husband and she is pure
Numbers5:29 So this is the law about jealousy. This is what you should do when a woman sins against her husband while she is married to him.
Numbers5:30 Or if the man becomes jealous and thinks his wife has sinned against him
Numbers5:31 The husband will not be guilty of doing anything wrong
Numbers6:1 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers6:2 Tell the Israelites this: If there are people
Numbers6:3 During this time
Numbers6:4 They must not eat anything that comes from grapes during that special time of separation. They must not even eat the seeds or the skins from grapes.
Numbers6:5 They must not cut their hair during that special time of dedication. They must be holy until the time that their dedication is ended. They must let their hair grow long. Their hair is a special part of their promise to God
Numbers6:6 If you have taken the Nazirite vow
Numbers6:7 so you must not let yourself become unclean like this—even for your own father or mother
Numbers6:8 It is holy because you have given yourself fully to the Lord for the full time of that dedication.
Numbers6:9 So if you are with someone when they suddenly die
Numbers6:10 Then on the eighth day
Numbers6:11 Then the priest will offer one bird as a sin offering and the other one as a burnt offering. The burnt offering will be a payment for the sin of touching a dead body during your special time of dedication. Then you must again promise to give the hair on your head as a gift to God.
Numbers6:12 This means you must again give yourself to the Lord for another time of dedication. You must bring a one-year-old male lamb to offer as a guilt offering. You must start over from the beginning with a new time of dedication because you touched a dead body during your first time of dedication.
Numbers6:13 After their time of dedication is ended
Numbers6:14 and give their offering to the Lord. Their offering must be: A one-year-old male lamb that has nothing wrong with it for a burnt offering; A one-year-old female lamb that has nothing wrong with it for a sin offering; One ram that has nothing wrong with it for a fellowship offering;
Numbers6:15 A basket of bread made without yeast (cakes made with fine flour mixed with oil). Oil must be spread on these cakes. The grain offerings and drink offerings that are a part of these gifts.
Numbers6:16 The priest will give these things to the Lord
Numbers6:17 He will give the basket of bread without yeast to the Lord. Then he will kill the ram as a fellowship offering to the Lord. He will give it to the Lord with the grain offering and the drink offering.
Numbers6:18 The Nazirites must go to the entrance of the Meeting Tent. There they must shave off their hair that they grew for the Lord. That hair will be put in the fire that is burning under the sacrifice of the fellowship offering.
Numbers6:19 After the Nazirites have cut off their hair
Numbers6:20 Then the priest will lift these things up to show they were presented before the Lord. These things are holy and belong to the priest. Also
Numbers6:21 These are the rules for someone who decides to make the Nazirite vow. They must give all of these gifts to the Lord. But they might be able to give much more to the Lord. If they promise to do more
Numbers6:22 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers6:23 Tell Aaron and his sons that this is the way they should bless the Israelites. They should say:
Numbers6:24 May the Lord bless you and keep you.
Numbers6:25 May the Lord smile down on you and show you his kindness.
Numbers6:26 May the Lord answer your prayers and give you peace.
Numbers6:27 Then the Lord said
Numbers7:1 Moses finished setting up the Holy Tent. On that day he dedicated it to the Lord. Moses anointed the Tent and everything in it. He also anointed the altar and all the things used with it. This showed that these things should be used only for worshiping the Lord.
Numbers7:2 Then the leaders of Israel gave offerings to the Lord. These men were the heads of their families and leaders of their tribes. These were the same men who were in charge of counting the people.
Numbers7:3 These leaders brought gifts to the Lord. They brought six covered wagons and twelve oxen for pulling the wagons. (One ox was given by each leader. Each leader joined with another leader to give one wagon.) The leaders gave these things to the Lord at the Holy Tent.
Numbers7:4 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers7:5 Accept these gifts from the leaders. These gifts can be used in the work of the Meeting Tent. Give them to the Levites to help them do their work.
Numbers7:6 So Moses accepted the wagons and the oxen and gave them to the Levites.
Numbers7:7 He gave two wagons and four oxen to the men in Gershon's group. They needed the wagons and the oxen for their work.
Numbers7:8 Then Moses gave four wagons and eight oxen to the men in Merari's group. They needed the wagons and oxen for their work. Ithamar son of Aaron the priest was responsible for the work of all these men.
Numbers7:9 Moses did not give any oxen or wagons to the men in Kohath's group
Numbers7:10 Moses anointed the altar. That same day the leaders brought their offerings for dedicating the altar. They gave their offerings to the Lord at the altar.

Both of these gifts were weighed by the official measure. The bowl and the plate were each filled with fine flour mixed with oil. This was to be used as a grain offering. Each leader also brought a large gold spoon that weighed about 4 ounces. The spoon was filled with incense. Each leader also brought 1 young bull

Numbers7:89 When Moses went into the Meeting Tent to speak to the Lord
Numbers8:1 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers8:2 Tell Aaron to put the seven lamps in the place I showed you. These lamps will light the area in front of the lampstand.
Numbers8:3 Aaron did this. He put the lamps in the right place so that they lighted the area in front of the lampstand. He obeyed the command that the Lord gave Moses.
Numbers8:4 This is how the lampstand was made: It was made from hammered gold
Numbers8:5 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers8:6 Separate the Levites from the other Israelites. Make these Levites clean.
Numbers8:7 This is what you should do to make them clean: Sprinkle the special water from the sin offering on them. This water will make them clean. Then they must shave their bodies and wash their clothes. This will make their bodies clean.
Numbers8:8 They must take a young bull and the grain offering that must be offered with it. This grain offering will be flour mixed with oil. Then take another young bull for a sin offering.
Numbers8:9 Bring the Levites to the area in front of the Meeting Tent. Then bring all the Israelites together at that place.
Numbers8:10 Bring the Levites before the Lord. The Israelites will put their hands on them.
Numbers8:11 Then Aaron will give the Levites to the Lord— they will be like an offering to God. In this way the Levites will be ready to do their special work for the Lord.
Numbers8:12 Tell the Levites to put their hands on the heads of the bulls. One bull will be a sin offering to the Lord. The other bull will be used as a burnt offering to the Lord. These offerings will make the Levites pure.
Numbers8:13 Tell the Levites to stand in front of Aaron and his sons. Then give the Levites to the Lord. They will be like an offering.
Numbers8:14 This will make the Levites holy. They will be different from the other Israelites. The Levites will belong to me.
Numbers8:15 So make the Levites pure and give them to the Lord as a special offering. After you do this
Numbers8:16 The Israelites will give me the Levites. They will belong to me. In the past I told every Israelite family to give me their firstborn son. But now I am taking the Levites in place of these firstborn sons from the other families in Israel.
Numbers8:17 Every firstborn in Israel—man or animal—is mine
Numbers8:18 But now I will take the Levites in their place. I will take the Levites in place of all the firstborn sons from the other families in Israel.
Numbers8:19 I chose the Levites from among all the Israelites. And I give them as gifts to Aaron and his sons. I want them to do the work at the Meeting Tent. They will serve for all the Israelites. They will help make the sacrifices that make the Israelites pure. Then no great sickness or trouble will come to the Israelites when they come near the holy place.
Numbers8:20 So Moses
Numbers8:21 The Levites washed themselves and their clothes. Then Aaron gave them to the Lord as special offerings. Aaron gave the offerings that covered their sins and made them pure.
Numbers8:22 After that the Levites came to the Meeting Tent to do their work. Aaron and his sons watched them. They were responsible for the work of the Levites. Aaron and his sons did what the Lord commanded Moses.
Numbers8:23 Then the Lord said to Moses
Numbers8:24 This is a special command for the Levites: Every Levite man who is 25 years old or older must come and share in the work at the Meeting Tent.
Numbers8:25 But when a man is 50 years old
Numbers8:26 Men who are at least 50 years old will be on duty to help their brothers
Numbers9:1 The Lord spoke to Moses in the Sinai desert. This was during the first month of the second year after the Israelites came out of Egypt. He said to Moses
Numbers9:2 Tell the Israelites to celebrate Passover at the chosen time.
Numbers9:3 They must eat the Passover meal just before dark on the 14 day of this month. They must do this at the chosen time
Numbers9:4 So Moses told the Israelites to celebrate Passover.
Numbers9:5 The people did this in the Sinai desert just before dark on the 14 day of the first month. The Israelites did everything just as the Lord commanded Moses.
Numbers9:6 But some of the people could not celebrate Passover that day. They were unclean because they had touched a dead body. So they went to Moses and Aaron that day
Numbers9:7 and said to Moses
Numbers9:8 Moses said to them
Numbers9:9 Then the Lord said to Moses
Numbers9:10 Tell the Israelites this: This rule will be for you and your descendants. Maybe someone is not able to celebrate Passover at the right time. Maybe they are unclean because they touched a dead body. Or maybe they were away on a trip.
Numbers9:11 They will still be able to celebrate Passover at another time. They must celebrate Passover just before dark on the 14 day of the second month. At that time they must eat the lamb
Numbers9:12 They must not leave any of that food until the next morning. And they must not break any of the bones of the lamb. They must follow all the rules about Passover.
Numbers9:13 But anyone who is able must celebrate Passover at the right time. If they are clean and they are not away on a trip
Numbers9:14 A foreigner living among you might want to share in the Lord's Passover with you. This is allowed
Numbers9:15 On the day the Holy Tent
Numbers9:16 The cloud stayed over the Holy Tent all the time. And at night the cloud looked like fire.
Numbers9:17 When the cloud moved from its place over the Holy Tent
Numbers9:18 This was the way the Lord showed the Israelites when to move and when to stop and set up camp. While the cloud stayed over the Holy Tent
Numbers9:19 Sometimes the cloud would stay over the Holy Tent for a long time. The Israelites obeyed the Lord and did not move.
Numbers9:20 Sometimes the cloud was over the Holy Tent for only a few days. So the people obeyed the Lord's command—they followed the cloud when it moved.
Numbers9:21 Sometimes the cloud stayed only during the night—the next morning the cloud moved. So the people gathered their things and followed it. If the cloud moved
Numbers9:22 If the cloud stayed over the Holy Tent for two days
Numbers9:23 So the people obeyed the Lord's commands. They camped when the Lord told them to
Numbers10:1 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers10:2 Make two trumpets. Use silver and hammer it to make the trumpets. These trumpets will be for calling the people together and for telling them when it is time to move the camp.
Numbers10:3 If you blow long blasts on both trumpets
Numbers10:4 But if you blow long blasts on only one trumpet
Numbers10:5 Short blasts on the trumpets will be the way to tell the people to move the camp. The first time you blow a short blast on the trumpets
Numbers10:6 The second time you blow a short blast on the trumpets
Numbers10:7 But if you want to gather the people together for a special meeting
Numbers10:8 Only Aaron's sons
Numbers10:9 If you are fighting an enemy in your own land
Numbers10:10 Also blow these trumpets for your special meetings
Numbers10:11 On the 20 day of the second month of the second year after the Israelites left Egypt
Numbers10:12 So the Israelites began their journey. They left the Sinai desert and traveled until the cloud stopped in the desert of Paran.
Numbers10:13 This was the first time the people moved their camp. They moved it the way the Lord commanded Moses.
Numbers10:14 The three divisions from Judah's camp went first. They traveled under their flag. The first group was the tribe of Judah. Nahshon son of Amminadab was the commander of that group.
Numbers10:15 Next came the tribe of Issachar. Nethanel son of Zuar was the commander of that group.
Numbers10:16 And then came the tribe of Zebulun. Eliab son of Helon was the commander of that group.
Numbers10:17 Then the Holy Tent was taken down. And the men from the Gershon and the Merari families carried the Holy Tent. So the people from these families were next in line.
Numbers10:18 Then came the three divisions from Reuben's camp. They traveled under their flag. The first group was the tribe of Reuben. Elizur son of Shedeur was the commander of that group.
Numbers10:19 Next came the tribe of Simeon. Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai was the commander of that group.
Numbers10:20 And then came the tribe of Gad. Eliasaph son of Deuel was the commander of that group.
Numbers10:21 Then came the Kohath family. They carried the holy things from inside the Holy Tent. These people came at this time so that the other people could set up the Holy Tent and make it ready at the new camp before these people arrived.
Numbers10:22 Next came the three groups from Ephraim's camp. They traveled under their flag. The first group was the tribe of Ephraim. Elishama son of Ammihud was the commander of that group.
Numbers10:23 Next came the tribe of Manasseh. Gamaliel son of Pedahzur was the commander of that group.
Numbers10:24 Then came the tribe of Benjamin. Abidan son of Gideoni was the commander of that group.
Numbers10:25 The last three tribes in the line were the rear guard for all the other tribes. These were the groups from Dan's camp. They traveled under their flag. The first group was the tribe of Dan. Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai was their commander.
Numbers10:26 Next came the tribe of Asher. Pagiel son of Ocran was the commander of that group.
Numbers10:27 Then came the tribe of Naphtali. Ahira son of Enan was the commander of that group.

Numbers11:1 The people started complaining about their troubles. The Lord heard their complaints. He heard these things and became angry. Fire from the Lord burned among the people. The fire burned some of the areas at the edge of the camp.
Numbers11:2 So the people cried to Moses for help. He prayed to the Lord and the fire stopped burning.
Numbers11:3 So that place was called Taberah. The people gave the place that name because the Lord caused a fire to burn in their camp.
Numbers11:4 The foreigners who had joined the Israelites began wanting other things to eat. Soon all the Israelites began complaining again. The people said
Numbers11:5 We remember the fish we ate in Egypt. That fish cost us nothing. We also had good vegetables like cucumbers
Numbers11:6 But now we have lost our strength. We never eat anything—only this manna!
Numbers11:7 (The manna was like small coriander seeds
Numbers11:8 The people gathered the manna. Then they used rocks to crush it and cooked it in a pot. Or they ground it into flour and made thin cakes with it. The cakes tasted like sweet cakes cooked with olive oil.
Numbers11:9 The manna fell on the ground each night when the ground became wet with dew.)
Numbers11:10 Moses heard the people complaining. People from every family were sitting by their tents and complaining. The Lord became very angry
Numbers11:11 He asked the Lord
Numbers11:12 You know that I am not the father of all these people. You know that I did not give birth to them. But I must take care of them
Numbers11:13 I don't have enough meat for all these people! And they continue complaining to me. They say
Numbers11:14 I cannot take care of all these people alone. The burden is too heavy for me.
Numbers11:15 If you plan to continue giving me their troubles
Numbers11:16 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers11:17 Then I will come down and speak with you there. The Spirit is on you now. But I will also give some of that Spirit to them. Then they will help you take care of the people. In this way you will not have to be responsible for these people alone.
Numbers11:18 Tell the people this: Make yourselves ready for tomorrow. Tomorrow you will eat meat. The Lord heard you when you cried. The Lord heard your words when you said
Numbers11:19 You will eat it for more than one
Numbers11:20 You will eat that meat for a whole month until you are sick of it. This will happen to you because you complained against the Lord. He lives among you and knows what you need
Numbers11:21 Moses said
Numbers11:22 If we were to kill all of the sheep and cattle
Numbers11:23 But the Lord said to Moses
Numbers11:24 So Moses went out to speak with the people. He told them what the Lord said. Then he gathered 70 of the elders together and told them to stand around the Tent.
Numbers11:25 Then the Lord came down in the cloud and spoke to Moses. The Spirit was on Moses. The Lord put that same Spirit on the 70 elders. After the Spirit came down on them
Numbers11:26 Two of the elders

Numbers11:28 Joshua son of Nun said to Moses
Numbers11:29 But Moses answered

Numbers11:32 They went out and gathered quail all that day and all that night. And they gathered quail all the next day too! The smallest amount anyone gathered was 60 bushels. Then the people spread the quail meat all around the camp to dry in the sun.
Numbers11:33 People began to eat the meat
Numbers11:34 So the people named that place Kibroth Hattaavah
Numbers11:35 From Kibroth Hattaavah the people traveled to Hazeroth and stayed there.

Numbers13:19 Learn about the land that they live in. Is it good land or bad land? What kind of towns do they live in? Do the towns have walls protecting them? Are the towns strongly defended?
Numbers13:20 And learn other things about the land. Is the dirt good for growing things
Numbers13:21 So they went to explore the country. They explored the area from the Zin desert to Rehob and Lebo Hamath.

Numbers14:1 That night all the people in the camp began shouting loudly.
Numbers14:2 The Israelites complained against Moses and Aaron. All the people came together and said to Moses and Aaron

Numbers14:4 Then the people said to each other
Numbers14:5 Moses and Aaron bowed low to the ground in front of all the people gathered there.
Numbers14:6 Joshua and Caleb became very upset. (Joshua son of Nun and Caleb son of Jephunneh were two of the men who explored the land.)
Numbers14:7 These two men said to all of the Israelites gathered there
Numbers14:8 It is a land filled with many good things. If the Lord is pleased with us
Numbers14:9 So don't turn against the Lord! Don't be afraid of the people in that land. We can defeat them. They have no protection

Those people know about the special cloud. They know you use the cloud to lead your people during the day. And they know the cloud becomes a fire to lead your people at night.
Numbers14:15 So you must not kill these people now. If you kill them
Numbers14:16 'The Lord was not able to bring them into the land he promised them. So he killed them in the desert.'

Numbers14:18 You said
Numbers14:19 Now
Numbers14:20 The Lord answered
Numbers14:21 But
Numbers14:22 None of the people I led out of Egypt will ever see the land of Canaan. They saw my glory and the great signs that I did in Egypt and in the desert. But they disobeyed me and tested me ten times.
Numbers14:23 I promised their ancestors that I would give them that land. But none of those people who turned against me will ever enter that land!
Numbers14:24 But my servant Caleb was different. He follows me completely. So I will bring him into the land that he has already seen
Numbers14:25 The Amalekites and the Canaanites are living in the valley. So tomorrow you must leave this place. Go back to the desert on the road to the Red Sea.
Numbers14:26 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Numbers14:27 How long will these evil people continue to complain against me? I have heard their complaints and their griping.
Numbers14:28 So tell them
Numbers14:29 You will die in this desert. Every person who is 20 years old or older and was counted as one of my people will die. You complained against me
Numbers14:30 So none of you will ever enter and live in the land that I promised to give you. Only Caleb son of Jephunneh and Joshua son of Nun will enter that land.
Numbers14:31 You were afraid and complained that your enemies in that new land would take your children away from you. But I tell you that I will bring them into the land. They will enjoy what you refused to accept.
Numbers14:32 As for you people
Numbers14:33 'Your children will wander around like shepherds here in the desert for 40 years. They will suffer because you were not faithful to me. They must suffer until all of you lie dead in the desert.
Numbers14:34 For 40 years you will suffer for your sins. (That is one year for each of the 40 days that the men explored the land.) You will know that it is a terrible thing for me to be against you.'
Numbers14:35 I am the Lord
Numbers14:36 The men Moses sent to explore the new land were the ones who came back complaining about him to all the Israelites. They said that the people were not strong enough to enter that land.
Numbers14:37 The men were responsible for spreading the trouble among the Israelites. So the Lord caused a sickness to kill all those men.
Numbers14:38 But Joshua son of Nun and Caleb son of Jephunneh were among the men who were sent out to explore the land
Numbers14:39 When Moses told the Israelites this

Numbers14:43 The Amalekites and Canaanites will fight against you there. You have turned away from the Lord
Numbers14:44 But the people did not believe Moses. They went toward the high hill country. But Moses and the Box of the Lord's Agreement did not go with the people.
Numbers14:45 The Amalekites and the Canaanites living in the hill country came down and attacked the Israelites and easily defeated them and chased them all the way to Hormah.
Numbers15:1 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers15:2 Speak to the Israelites and say to them: I am giving you a land to be your home. When you enter that land
Numbers15:3 you must give special gifts to the Lord. Their smell will please the Lord. You will use your cattle
Numbers15:4 At the time someone brings their offering
Numbers15:5 Each time you offer a lamb as a burnt offering
Numbers15:6 If you are giving a ram
Numbers15:7 And you must prepare 1?quarts of wine as a drink offering. Its smell will please the Lord.
Numbers15:8 You might prepare a young bull as a burnt offering
Numbers15:9 At that time you must also bring a grain offering with the bull. That grain offering should be 24 cups of fine flour mixed with 2 quarts of olive oil.
Numbers15:10 Also bring 2 quarts of wine as a drink offering as a sweet-smelling gift to the Lord.
Numbers15:11 Each bull or ram
Numbers15:12 Do this for every one of these animals that you give.
Numbers15:13 This is the way every citizen of Israel must give gifts to please the Lord.
Numbers15:14 Foreigners will live among you. If they give gifts to please the Lord
Numbers15:15 The same rules will be for everyone—the Israelites and the foreigners living among you. This law will continue forever. You and the people living among you will be the same before the Lord.
Numbers15:16 This means that you must follow the same laws and the same rules. These laws and rules are for you Israelites and for the other people who are living among you.
Numbers15:17 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers15:18 Tell the Israelites this: I am taking you to another land.
Numbers15:19 When you eat the food that grows in that land
Numbers15:20 You will gather grain and grind it into flour to make dough for bread. You must give the first of that dough as a gift to the Lord. It will be like the grain offering that comes from the threshing floor.
Numbers15:21 This rule will continue forever
Numbers15:22 As you try to obey all the commands that the Lord gave Moses
Numbers15:23 God gave you those commands through Moses
Numbers15:24 If you made this mistake where everyone could see it
Numbers15:25 So the priest will make the whole community of Israel pure
Numbers15:26 Then the whole community of Israel and any foreigners among them will be forgiven for the mistake.
Numbers15:27 But if only one person makes a mistake and sins
Numbers15:28 The priest will make the one who sinned pure and that person will be forgiven.
Numbers15:29 This law is for everyone who makes a mistake and sins. The same law is for the people born in the family of Israel and for the foreigners living among you.
Numbers15:30 If someone sins and knows they are doing wrong
Numbers15:31 They thought the Lord's word was not important

Numbers15:39 You will be able to look at these tassels and remember all the commands that the Lord has given you. Then you will obey the commands. You will not do wrong by forgetting about the commands and doing the things that your own bodies and eyes want.
Numbers15:40 You will remember to obey all my commands. Then you will be God's special people.
Numbers15:41 I am the Lord your God. I am the one who brought you out of Egypt. I did this to be your God. I am the Lord your God.
Numbers16:1 Korah

Numbers16:4 When Moses heard this

Numbers16:6 So Korah

Numbers16:9 You should be happy that the God of Israel chose you and made you special. You are different from the rest of the Israelites. The Lord brought you near to him to do the special work in the Lord's Holy Tent to help the Israelites worship him. Isn't that enough?

Numbers16:11 You and your followers have joined together and turned against the Lord! Did Aaron do anything wrong? No

Numbers16:14 Why should we follow you? You did not bring us into the new land filled with many good things. You did not give us the land God promised. You did not give us the fields or the vineyards. Will you make these men your slaves? No! We will not come.

Numbers16:40 He did this the way the Lord commanded him through Moses. This was a sign to help the Israelites remember that only someone from the family of Aaron should burn incense before the Lord. Any other person who burns incense before the Lord will die like Korah and his followers.

Numbers18:17 But you must not make a payment for the firstborn cow

Numbers18:24 But the Israelites will give one-tenth of everything they have to me
Numbers18:25 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers18:26 Speak to the Levites and tell them: The Israelites will give one-tenth of everything they own to the Lord. That one-tenth will belong to the Levites. But you must give one-tenth of that to the Lord as your offering.
Numbers18:27 That tenth will be your offering to the Lord and will be treated like the grain from the threshing floor and the wine from the winepress.
Numbers18:28 In this way you will also give an offering to the Lord just like the other Israelites do. You will get the one-tenth that the Israelites give to the Lord
