Numbers19:1 The Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron. He said
Numbers19:2 These are the laws from the teachings that the Lord gave to the Israelites. Get a red cow that has nothing wrong with it. That cow must not have any bruises. And it must never have worn a yoke.
Numbers19:3 Give that cow to Eleazar
Numbers19:4 Then Eleazar the priest must put some of its blood on his finger and sprinkle some of the blood toward the Holy Tent. He must do this seven times.
Numbers19:5 Then the whole cow must be burned in front of him; the skin
Numbers19:6 Then the priest must take a cedar stick
Numbers19:7 Then the priest must wash himself and his clothes with water. Then he must come back into the camp. He will be unclean until evening.
Numbers19:8 The man who burns that cow must wash himself and his clothes in water. He will be unclean until evening.
Numbers19:9 Then someone who is clean will collect the ashes from the cow and put them in a clean place outside the camp. These ashes will be used when someone must keep a special ceremony to become clean. These ashes will also be used to remove a person's sins.
Numbers19:10 The man who collected the cow's ashes must wash his clothes. He will be unclean until evening. This rule will continue forever. This rule is for the citizens of Israel and for the foreigners living with you.
Numbers19:11 Those who touch a dead body will be unclean for seven days.
Numbers19:12 They must wash themselves with the special water on the third day and again on the seventh day. If they don't do this
Numbers19:13 Those who touch a dead body are unclean. If they stay unclean and then go to the Holy Tent
Numbers19:14 This is the rule about those who die in their tents: If someone dies in the tent
Numbers19:15 And every jar or pot without a lid becomes unclean.
Numbers19:16 If there is a dead body out in a field
Numbers19:17 If you have become unclean
Numbers19:18 That clean person must take a hyssop branch and dip it into the water. The clean person must sprinkle it over the tent
Numbers19:19 Then that clean person must sprinkle this water on you on the third day and again on the seventh day. On the seventh day you will become clean. You must wash your clothes in water and you will become clean in the evening.
Numbers19:20 Whoever becomes unclean and does not become clean
Numbers19:21 This rule will be for you forever. And whoever sprinkled the special water must wash their clothes because they will be unclean until evening.
Numbers19:22 And whoever an unclean person touches will be unclean until evening.
Numbers20:1 The Israelites arrived at the Zin desert on the first month. The people stayed at Kadesh. Miriam died and was buried there.
Numbers20:2 There was not enough water for the people at that place. So the people met together to complain against Moses and Aaron.
Numbers20:3 The people argued with Moses and said
Numbers20:4 Why did you bring the Lord's people into this desert? Do you want us and our animals to die here?
Numbers20:5 Why did you bring us from Egypt? Why did you bring us to this bad place? There is no grain. There are no figs
Numbers20:6 So Moses and Aaron left the crowd of people and went to the entrance of the Meeting Tent. They bowed down to the ground
Numbers20:7 The Lord spoke to Moses and said
Numbers20:8 Get the special walking stick. Take your brother Aaron and the crowd of people and go to that rock. Speak to the rock in front of the people. Then water will flow from the rock
Numbers20:9 The walking stick was in the Holy Tent
Numbers20:10 Moses and Aaron told the people to meet together in front of the rock. Then Moses said
Numbers20:11 Moses lifted his arm and hit the rock twice. Water began flowing from the rock
Numbers20:12 But the Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Numbers20:13 This place was called the waters of Meribah. This is where the Israelites argued with the Lord and where the Lord showed them that he was holy.
Numbers20:14 While Moses was at Kadesh
Numbers20:15 Many years ago our ancestors went down into Egypt
Numbers20:16 But we asked the Lord for help
Numbers20:17 Please let us travel through your country. We will not travel through any fields or vineyards. We will not drink water from any of your wells. We will travel only along the King's Road. We will not leave that road to the right or to the left. We will stay on the road until we have traveled through your country.
Numbers20:18 But the king of Edom answered
Numbers20:19 The Israelites answered
Numbers20:20 But again the king of Edom answered
Numbers20:21 The king of Edom refused to let the Israelites travel through his country

Numbers20:23 Mount Hor was near the border of Edom. The Lord said to Moses and Aaron

Numbers28:1 Then the Lord spoke to Moses. He said

Numbers28:3 These are gifts that they must give to the Lord. Every day they must give 2 lambs that are one year old. There must be nothing wrong with them.
Numbers28:4 Offer one of the lambs in the morning and the other lamb just before dark.
Numbers28:5 Also give a grain offering of 8 cups of fine flour mixed with 1 quart of olive oil.
Numbers28:6 (They started giving the daily offerings at Mount Sinai as sweet-smelling gifts to the Lord.)
Numbers28:7 The people must also give the drink offerings that go with the gifts. They must give 1 quart of wine with every lamb. Pour that drink offering {on the altar} in the Holy Place. This is a gift to the Lord.
Numbers28:8 Offer the second lamb just before dark. Offer it just as the morning offering. Also give the drink offering that goes with it. This will be a sweet-smelling gift to the Lord.
Numbers28:9 On the Sabbath
Numbers28:10 This is a special offering for the day of rest. This offering is in addition to the regular daily offering and drink offering.

Numbers28:13 Also give a grain offering of 8 cups of fine flour mixed with olive oil with each lamb. This will be a sweet-smelling gift to the Lord.
Numbers28:14 The drink offering will be 2 quarts of wine with each bull
Numbers28:15 In addition to the regular daily burnt offering and drink offering
Numbers28:16 The Lord's Passover will be on the 14 day of the first month.
Numbers28:17 The Festival of Unleavened Bread begins on the 15 day of that month. This festival lasts for seven days. The only bread you can eat is bread made without yeast.

Numbers28:20 You must also give a grain offering of 24 cups of fine flour mixed with olive oil with each bull

Deuteronomy3:11 (Og was the king of Bashan. He was one of the few Rephaites still alive. His bed was made from iron
Deuteronomy3:12 So we took that land to be ours. I gave part of this land to the tribes of Reuben and Gad. I gave them the land from Aroer in the Arnon Valley to the hill country of Gilead with the cities in it. They got half of the hill country of Gilead.
Deuteronomy3:13 I gave the other half of Gilead and the whole area of Bashan to half the tribe of Manasseh. (Bashan was Og's kingdom. Part of Bashan was called Argob. It was also called the Land of Rephaim.
Deuteronomy3:14 Jair
Deuteronomy3:15 I gave Gilead to Makir.
Deuteronomy3:16 And to the tribe of Reuben and the tribe of Gad
Deuteronomy3:17 The Jordan River near the desert is their western border. Lake Galilee is north of this area and the Dead Sea is to the south. It is at the bottom of the cliffs of Pisgah
Deuteronomy3:18 At that time I gave those tribes this command: 'The Lord your God has given you the land on this side of the Jordan River to live in. But now your fighting men must take their weapons and lead the other Israelite tribes across the river.
Deuteronomy3:19 Your wives
Deuteronomy3:20 But you must help your Israelite relatives until they take the land that the Lord is giving them on the other side of the Jordan River. Help them until the Lord gives them peace there
Deuteronomy3:21 Then I told Joshua
Deuteronomy3:22 Don't fear the kings of these lands
Deuteronomy3:23 Then I begged the Lord to do something special for me. I said
Deuteronomy3:24 'Lord God
Deuteronomy3:25 Please let me go across the Jordan River and see the good land on the other side. Let me see the beautiful hill country and Lebanon.'
Deuteronomy3:26 But the Lord was angry with me because of you and refused to listen to me. The Lord said to me
Deuteronomy3:27 Go up to the top of Mount Pisgah. Look to the west
Deuteronomy3:28 You must give instructions to Joshua. Encourage him. Make him strong
Deuteronomy3:29 So we stayed in the valley across from Beth Peor.
Deuteronomy4:1 Now
Deuteronomy4:2 You must not add to what I command you. And you must not take anything away. You must obey the commands of the Lord your God that I have given you.
Deuteronomy4:3 You have seen what the Lord did at Baal Peor. The Lord your God destroyed all your people who followed the false god Baal at that place.
Deuteronomy4:4 But all of you who stayed with the Lord your God are alive today.
Deuteronomy4:5 I taught you the laws and rules that the Lord my God commanded me. I did this so you could obey them in the land you are ready to enter and take for your own.
Deuteronomy4:6 Obey these laws carefully. This will show the people of the other nations that you are wise and sensible. They will hear about these laws and say
Deuteronomy4:7 The Lord our God is near when we ask him to help us. No other nation has a god like that!
Deuteronomy4:8 And no other nation is great enough to have laws and rules as good as the teachings I give you today.
Deuteronomy4:9 But you must be careful! Be sure that as long as you live you never forget what you have seen. You must teach these things to your children and grandchildren.
Deuteronomy4:10 Remember the day you stood before the Lord your God at Mount Horeb. The Lord said to me
Deuteronomy4:11 You came near and stood at the bottom of the mountain. The mountain burned with fire that reached up to the sky. There were thick black clouds and darkness.
Deuteronomy4:12 Then the Lord spoke to you from the fire. You heard the sound of someone speaking
Deuteronomy4:13 The Lord told you about his agreement
Deuteronomy4:14 At that time the Lord also commanded me to teach you the other laws and rules that you must follow in the land you are going to take and live in.
Deuteronomy4:15 On the day the Lord spoke to you from the fire at Mount Horeb
Deuteronomy4:16 So be careful! Don't sin and destroy yourselves by making false gods or statues in the shape of any living thing. Don't make an idol that looks like a man or a woman
Deuteronomy4:17 or like an animal on the earth
Deuteronomy4:18 And don't make an idol that looks like anything that crawls on the ground or like a fish in the sea.
Deuteronomy4:19 And be careful when you look up to the sky and see the sun
Deuteronomy4:20 But the Lord brought you out of Egypt and made you his own special people. It was as if the Lord reached into a hot furnace and pulled you out. And now you are his people.
Deuteronomy4:21 The Lord was angry with me because of you. He swore that I could not go across the Jordan River into the good land that the Lord your God is giving you.
Deuteronomy4:22 So I must die here in this land. I cannot go across the Jordan River
Deuteronomy4:23 You must be careful not to forget the agreement that the Lord your God made with you. You must obey the Lord's command. Don't make any idols in any form
Deuteronomy4:24 because the Lord your God hates for his people to worship other gods. And he can be like a fire that destroys!
Deuteronomy4:25 You will live in the country a long time. You will have children and grandchildren there. You will grow old there. And then you will ruin your lives—you will make all kinds of idols! When you do that
Deuteronomy4:26 So I am warning you now. Heaven and earth are my witnesses! If you do this evil thing
Deuteronomy4:27 The Lord will scatter you among the nations. And only a few of you will be left alive to go to the countries where the Lord will send you.
Deuteronomy4:28 There you will serve gods made by men—things made of wood and stone that cannot see or hear or eat or smell!
Deuteronomy4:29 But there in these other lands you will look for the Lord your God. And if you look for him with all your heart and soul
Deuteronomy4:30 When you are in trouble—when all these things happen to you—then you will come back to the Lord your God and obey him.
Deuteronomy4:31 The Lord your God is a merciful God! He will not leave you there or destroy you completely. He will not forget the agreement that he made with your ancestors.
Deuteronomy4:32 Has anything this great ever happened before? Never! Look at the past. Think about everything that happened before you were born. Go all the way back to the time when God made people on the earth. Look at everything that has happened anywhere in the world. Has anyone ever heard about anything as great as this? No!
Deuteronomy4:33 You people heard God speaking to you from a fire
Deuteronomy4:34 Has any other god ever tried to go and take a people for himself from inside another nation? No! But you yourselves have seen everything that the Lord your God did for you. He showed you his power and strength. You saw the troubles that tested the people. You saw miracles and wonders. You saw war and the terrible things that happened.
Deuteronomy4:35 The Lord showed you all this so that you would know that he is God. There is no other god like him.
Deuteronomy4:36 The Lord let you hear his voice from heaven so that he could teach you a lesson. On earth he let you see his great fire
Deuteronomy4:37 The Lord loved your ancestors. That is why he chose you
Deuteronomy4:38 When you moved forward
Deuteronomy4:39 So today you must remember and accept that the Lord is God. He is God in heaven above and on the earth below. There is no other god!
Deuteronomy4:40 And you must obey his laws and commands that I give you today. Then everything will go well with you and your children who live after you. And you will live a long time in the land the Lord your God is giving you—it will be yours forever.
Deuteronomy4:41 Then Moses chose three cities on the east side of the Jordan River.
Deuteronomy4:42 Any person who killed someone by accident and not out of hate could run away to one of these three cities and not be put to death.
Deuteronomy4:43 The three cities that Moses chose were: Bezer in the high plains for the tribe of Reuben; Ramoth in Gilead for the tribe of Gad; and Golan in Bashan for the tribe of Manasseh.
Deuteronomy4:44 Moses gave God's law to the Israelites.
Deuteronomy4:45 Moses gave these teachings
Deuteronomy4:46 He gave them these laws while they were on the east side of the Jordan River
Deuteronomy4:47 They took Sihon's land to keep. They also took the land of King Og of Bashan. These two Amorite kings lived on the east side of the Jordan River.
Deuteronomy4:48 This land goes from Aroer on the edge of the Arnon Valley all the way to Mount Sirion (Mount Hermon).
Deuteronomy4:49 This land also included the whole Jordan Valley on the east side of the Jordan River. To the south
Deuteronomy5:1 Moses called together all the Israelites and said to them
Deuteronomy5:2 The Lord our God made an agreement with us at Mount Horeb.
Deuteronomy5:3 The Lord did not make this agreement with our ancestors
Deuteronomy5:4 The Lord spoke with you face to face at that mountain. He spoke to you from the fire.
Deuteronomy5:5 But you were afraid of the fire. And you did not go up the mountain. So I stood between the Lord and you to tell you what the Lord said. He said:
Deuteronomy5:6 I am the Lord your God. I led you out of Egypt where you were slaves.
Deuteronomy5:7 You must not worship any other gods except me.
Deuteronomy5:8 You must not make any idols. Don't make any statues or pictures of anything up in the sky or of anything on the earth or of anything down in the water.
Deuteronomy5:9 Don't worship or serve idols of any kind
Deuteronomy5:10 But I will be very kind to people who love me and obey my commands. I will be kind to their families for thousands of generations!
Deuteronomy5:11 You must not use the name of the Lord your God to make empty promises. If you do
Deuteronomy5:12 You must keep the Sabbath a special day like the Lord your God commanded.
Deuteronomy5:13 Work six days a week and do your job
Deuteronomy5:14 but the seventh day is a day of rest in honor of the Lord your God. So on that day no one should work—not you
Deuteronomy5:15 Don't forget that you were slaves in the land of Egypt. The Lord your God brought you out of Egypt with his great power and made you free. That is why the Lord your God commands you to always make the Sabbath a special day.
Deuteronomy5:16 You must honor your father and your mother. The Lord your God has commanded you to do this. If you follow this command
Deuteronomy5:17 You must not murder anyone.
Deuteronomy5:18 You must not commit the sin of adultery.
Deuteronomy5:19 You must not steal.
Deuteronomy5:20 You must not tell lies about other people.
Deuteronomy5:21 You must not want another man's wife. You must not want his house
Deuteronomy5:22 Moses said
Deuteronomy5:23 You heard the voice from the darkness while the mountain was burning with fire. Then all the elders and the other leaders of your tribes came to me.
Deuteronomy5:24 They said
Deuteronomy5:25 But if we hear the Lord our God speak to us again
Deuteronomy5:26 No one has ever heard the living God speak from the fire like we have and still lived!
Deuteronomy5:27 Moses
Deuteronomy5:28 The Lord heard what you said and told me
Deuteronomy5:29 I only wanted to change their way of thinking—I wanted them to respect me and obey all my commands from the heart. Then everything would be fine with them and with their descendants forever.
Deuteronomy5:30 'Go and tell the people to go back to their tents
Deuteronomy5:31 but you stand here near me. I will tell you all the commands
Deuteronomy5:32 So you people must be careful to do everything the Lord commanded you. Do not stop following God!
Deuteronomy5:33 You must live the way the Lord your God commanded you. Then you will continue to live

Deuteronomy6:2 You and your descendants must respect the Lord your God as long as you live. You must obey all his laws and commands that I give you. If you do this

Deuteronomy6:4 Listen
Deuteronomy6:5 You must love the Lord your God with all your heart
Deuteronomy6:6 Always remember these commands that I give you today.
Deuteronomy6:7 Be sure to teach them to your children. Talk about these commands when you sit in your house and when you walk on the road. Talk about them when you lie down and when you get up.
Deuteronomy6:8 Tie them on your hands and wear them on your foreheads to help you remember my teachings.
Deuteronomy6:9 Write them on the doorposts of your houses and on your gates.
Deuteronomy6:10 The Lord your God made a promise to your ancestors
Deuteronomy6:11 He will give you houses full of good things that you did not put there. He will give you wells that you did not dig. He will give you vineyards and olive trees that you did not plant
Deuteronomy6:12 But be careful! Don't forget the Lord. You were slaves in Egypt
Deuteronomy6:13 Respect the Lord your God and serve only him. You must use only his name to make promises.
Deuteronomy6:14 You must not follow other gods. You must not follow the gods of the people who live around you.
Deuteronomy6:15 The Lord your God is always with you
Deuteronomy6:16 You must not test the Lord your God like you tested him at Massah.
Deuteronomy6:17 You must be sure to obey the commands of the Lord your God. You must follow all the teachings and laws he has given you.
Deuteronomy6:18 You must do what is right and good—what pleases the Lord. Then everything will go well for you
Deuteronomy6:19 And you will force out all your enemies

Deuteronomy6:22 The Lord did great and amazing things. We saw him do these things to the Egyptian people
Deuteronomy6:23 And the Lord brought us out of Egypt so that he could give us the land that he promised our ancestors.
Deuteronomy6:24 The Lord commanded us to follow all these teachings. We must respect the Lord our God. Then he will always keep us alive and doing well

Deuteronomy7:2 The Lord your God will put these nations under your power. And you will defeat them. You must destroy them completely. Don't make an agreement with them or show them mercy.
Deuteronomy7:3 Don't marry any of them
Deuteronomy7:4 If you do
Deuteronomy7:5 This is what you must do to those nations: You must smash their altars and break their memorial stones into pieces. Cut down their Asherah poles and burn their statues.
Deuteronomy7:6 Do this because you are the Lord's own people. From all the people on earth
Deuteronomy7:7 Why did the Lord love and choose you? It was not because you are such a large nation. You had the fewest of all people!
Deuteronomy7:8 But the Lord brought you out of Egypt with great power and made you free from slavery. He freed you from the control of Pharaoh
Deuteronomy7:9 So remember that the Lord your God is the only God
Deuteronomy7:10 but the Lord punishes people who hate him. He will destroy them. He will not be slow to punish those who hate him.
Deuteronomy7:11 So you must be careful to obey the commands
Deuteronomy7:12 If you listen to these laws
Deuteronomy7:13 He will love you and bless you. He will make your nation grow. He will bless your children. He will bless your fields with good crops and will give you grain
Deuteronomy7:14 You will be blessed more than all people. Every husband and wife will be able to have children. Your cows will be able to have calves.
Deuteronomy7:15 The Lord will take away all sickness from you and he will not let you catch any of the terrible diseases that you had in Egypt. But he will make your enemies catch those diseases.
Deuteronomy7:16 You must destroy all the people the Lord your God helps you defeat. Don't feel sorry for them
Deuteronomy7:17 Don't say in your heart
Deuteronomy7:18 You must not be afraid of them. You must remember what the Lord your God did to Pharaoh and to all the people of Egypt.
Deuteronomy7:19 You saw the great troubles he gave them and the amazing things he did. You saw the Lord use his great power and strength to bring you out of Egypt. The Lord your God will use that same power against all the people you fear.
Deuteronomy7:20 The Lord your God will send the hornet against them. He will do this until he destroys all the people who escaped and tried to hide.
Deuteronomy7:21 Don't be afraid of them
Deuteronomy7:22 The Lord your God will force those nations to leave your country little by little. You will not destroy them all at once. If you did
Deuteronomy7:23 But the Lord your God will let you defeat those nations. He will confuse them in battle
Deuteronomy7:24 The Lord will help you defeat their kings. You will kill them
Deuteronomy7:25 You must throw the statues of their gods into the fire and burn them. You must not want to keep the silver or the gold that is on those statues for yourselves. It will be like a trap to you—it will ruin your life. The Lord your God hates those idols.
Deuteronomy7:26 You must not bring any of those disgusting idols into your homes

Deuteronomy8:3 He humbled you and let you be hungry. Then he fed you with manna — something you did not know about before. It was something your ancestors had never seen. Why did the Lord do this? Because he wanted you to know that it is not just bread that keeps people alive. People's lives depend on what the Lord says.
Deuteronomy8:4 These past 40 years
Deuteronomy8:5 You must remember that the Lord your God teaches and corrects you as a father teaches and corrects his son.
Deuteronomy8:6 You must obey the commands of the Lord your God. Follow him and respect him.
Deuteronomy8:7 The Lord your God is bringing you into a good land—a land with rivers and pools of water. Water flows out of the ground in the valleys and hills.
Deuteronomy8:8 It is a land with wheat and barley
Deuteronomy8:9 There you will have plenty of food and everything you need. It is a land where the rocks are iron. You can dig copper out of the hills.
Deuteronomy8:10 You will have all you want to eat. Then you will praise the Lord your God for the good land he has given you.

Deuteronomy9:17 So I took the two stone tablets and threw them down. There before your eyes I broke the stones into pieces.
Deuteronomy9:18 Then I bowed down before the Lord with my face to the ground for 40 days and 40 nights

Deuteronomy10:4 And the Lord wrote on the stones the same words he had written before—the Ten Commandments he spoke to you from the fire
Deuteronomy10:5 I came back down from the mountain. I put the stones in the Box I had made. The Lord commanded me to put them there. And the stones are still there in that Box.

Deuteronomy12:1 These are the laws and rules that you must obey in your new land. You must carefully obey them as long as you live in this land. The Lord is the God of your ancestors
Deuteronomy12:2 You will take that land from the nations that live there now. You must completely destroy all the places where the people of these nations worship their gods. These places are on high mountains
Deuteronomy12:3 You must smash their altars and break their memorial stones into pieces. You must burn their Asherah poles and cut down the statues of their gods. Wipe out everything that would remind you of those gods.
Deuteronomy12:4 You must not worship the Lord your God in the same way that these people worship their gods.
Deuteronomy12:5 The Lord your God will choose a special place among your tribes. That will be the home for his name. You must go to that place to worship him.
Deuteronomy12:6 There you must bring your burnt offerings
Deuteronomy12:7 You and your families will eat together at that place
Deuteronomy12:8 You must not continue to worship the way we have been worshiping. Until now each of us has been worshiping God any way we wanted.
Deuteronomy12:9 This is because we have not yet entered the peaceful land that the Lord your God is giving you.
Deuteronomy12:10 But you will go across the Jordan River and live in that land. The Lord is giving that land to you. And he will give you rest from all your enemies. You will be safe.
Deuteronomy12:11 Then the Lord your God will choose a place that will be the home for his name. You must bring everything I command you to that place. Bring your burnt offerings
Deuteronomy12:12 Come to that place with all your people—your children
Deuteronomy12:13 Be sure you don't offer your burnt offerings in just any place you see.
Deuteronomy12:14 The Lord will choose his special place among your tribes. Offer your burnt offerings and do everything else I told you only in that place.
Deuteronomy12:15 Whenever you want to eat meat
Deuteronomy12:16 But you must not eat the blood. You must pour the blood on the ground like water.

Deuteronomy14:5 deer
Deuteronomy14:6 You may eat any animal that has hooves divided into two parts and that chews the cud.
Deuteronomy14:7 But don't eat camels
Deuteronomy14:8 And you must not eat pigs. Their hooves are divided
Deuteronomy14:9 You may eat any kind of fish that has fins and scales.
Deuteronomy14:10 But don't eat anything living in the water that does not have fins and scales. It is not a clean food for you.
Deuteronomy14:11 You may eat any clean bird.
Deuteronomy14:12 But don't eat any of these birds: eagles
Deuteronomy14:13 red kites
Deuteronomy14:14 any kind of raven

Deuteronomy14:16 little owls

Deuteronomy14:18 storks

Deuteronomy16:1 Remember
Deuteronomy16:2 You must go to the place the Lord your God will choose to be the home for his name. There you must offer the Passover sacrifice to honor the Lord. You must offer the cattle and goats.
Deuteronomy16:3 Don't eat bread that has yeast in it with this sacrifice. You must eat unleavened bread for seven days. This bread is called 'Bread of Trouble.' It will help you remember the troubles you had in Egypt. Remember how quickly you had to leave that country. You must remember that day as long as you live.
Deuteronomy16:4 There must be no yeast in anyone's house anywhere in the country for seven days. And all the meat you sacrifice on the evening of the first day must be eaten before morning.
Deuteronomy16:5 You must not sacrifice the Passover animal in any of the towns that the Lord your God gives you.
Deuteronomy16:6 You must sacrifice the Passover animal only at the place that the Lord your God will choose to be the home for his name. There you must sacrifice the Passover animal in the evening when the sun goes down. This is the festival when you remember that God brought you out of Egypt.
Deuteronomy16:7 You must cook the meal and eat it at the place the Lord your God will choose. The next morning you may go back home.
Deuteronomy16:8 You must eat unleavened bread six days. On the seventh day you must not do any work. On this day the people will come together for a special meeting to honor the Lord your God.
Deuteronomy16:9 You must count seven weeks from the time you begin to harvest the grain.
Deuteronomy16:10 Then celebrate the Festival of Weeks for the Lord your God. Do this by bringing him some special gift you want to bring. Decide how much to give by thinking about how much the Lord your God has blessed you.
Deuteronomy16:11 Go to the place the Lord will choose to be the home for his name. You and your people should enjoy yourselves together there with the Lord your God. Take all your people with you—your sons
Deuteronomy16:12 Remember
Deuteronomy16:13 Seven days after you have gathered your harvest in from your threshing floor and from your winepress
Deuteronomy16:14 Enjoy yourselves at this festival—you
Deuteronomy16:15 Celebrate this festival for seven days at the special place the Lord will choose. Do this to honor the Lord your God. The Lord your God blessed your harvest and all the work you did
Deuteronomy16:16 Three times a year all your men must come to meet with the Lord your God at the special place he will choose. They must come for the Festival of Unleavened Bread
Deuteronomy16:17 Each man should give as much as he can. He should decide how much to give by thinking about how much the Lord has given him.
Deuteronomy16:18 Choose men to be judges and officers in every town that the Lord your God gives you. Every tribe must do this. And these men must be fair in judging the people.
Deuteronomy16:19 You must always be fair. You must not favor some people over other people. You must not take money to change your mind in judgment. Money blinds the eyes of wise people and changes what a good person will say.
Deuteronomy16:20 Goodness and Fairness! You must try very hard to be good and fair all the time. Then you will live and keep the land that the Lord your God is giving you.

Deuteronomy16:22 You must not set up special stones for worshiping false gods. The Lord your God hates them.
Deuteronomy17:1 You must not sacrifice to the Lord your God an ox or sheep that has anything wrong with it. The Lord your God would hate that.
Deuteronomy17:2 You might hear about an evil thing that happens in one of the cities that the Lord your God is giving you. You might hear that a man or woman in your group has sinned against the Lord your God. You might hear that they have broken his agreement or
Deuteronomy17:3 that they have worshiped other gods or maybe the sun
Deuteronomy17:4 If you hear bad news like this
Deuteronomy17:5 you must punish the person who did this evil thing. You must take that man or woman out to a public place near the city gates and kill them with stones.
Deuteronomy17:6 But no one should be punished with death if only one witness says that person did the evil thing. But if two or three witnesses say it is true
Deuteronomy17:7 The witnesses must throw the first stones to kill that person. Then the other people should throw stones to finish killing that person. In this way you will remove this evil from your group.
Deuteronomy17:8 There might be some problems that are too hard for your courts to judge. It might be a murder case or an argument between two people. Or it might be a fight in which someone was hurt. When these cases are argued in your towns
Deuteronomy17:9 You must go to the priests who are Levites and to the judge on duty at that time. They will decide what to do about that problem.
Deuteronomy17:10 There at the Lord's special place they will tell you their decision. You must do whatever they say. Be sure to do everything they tell you to do.
Deuteronomy17:11 You must accept their decision and follow their instructions exactly—don't change anything!
Deuteronomy17:12 You must punish anyone who refuses to obey the judge or the priest who is there at that time serving the Lord your God. That person must die. You must remove this evil person from Israel.
Deuteronomy17:13 All the people will hear about this punishment and be afraid. Then they will not be stubborn anymore.

Deuteronomy24:10 When you give someone any kind of loan
Deuteronomy24:11 You must stand outside. Then the person who you gave the loan to will bring out the security to you.

Deuteronomy28:12 The Lord will open his storehouse where he keeps his rich blessings. He will send rain at the right time for your land. He will bless everything you do. You will have money to lend to many nations. And you will not need to borrow anything from them.

Deuteronomy28:23 There will be no clouds in the sky—the sky will look like polished brass. And the ground under you will be hard like iron.
Deuteronomy28:24 The Lord will not send rain—only sand and dust will fall from the sky. It will come down on you until you are destroyed.
Deuteronomy28:25 The Lord will let your enemies defeat you. You will go to fight against your enemies one way
Deuteronomy28:26 Your dead bodies will be food for the wild birds and animals. There will be no one to scare them away from your dead bodies.
Deuteronomy28:27 The Lord will punish you with boils
Deuteronomy28:28 The Lord will punish you by making you crazy. He will make you blind and confused.
Deuteronomy28:29 In daylight you will have to feel your way like a blind man. You will fail in everything you do. Again and again people will hurt you and steal from you. And there will be no one to save you.
Deuteronomy28:30 You will be engaged to a woman
Deuteronomy28:31 People will kill your cattle in front of you
Deuteronomy28:32 Other people will be allowed to take your sons and your daughters. Day after day you will look for your children. You will look for them until your eyes become weak and blind—but you will not find them. And God will not help you.
Deuteronomy28:33 A nation that you don't know will take all your crops and everything you worked for. People will treat you badly and abuse you.
Deuteronomy28:34 The things you see will make you go crazy.
Deuteronomy28:35 The Lord will punish you with sore boils that cannot be healed. These boils will be on your knees and legs. The boils will be on every part of your body—from the bottom of your feet to the top of your head.
Deuteronomy28:36 The Lord will send you and your king away to a nation you don't know. You and your ancestors have never seen that nation. There you will serve false gods made of wood and stone.
Deuteronomy28:37 In the countries where the Lord will send you
Deuteronomy28:38 Your fields will produce plenty of grain. But your harvest will be small
Deuteronomy28:39 You will plant vineyards and work hard in them. But you will not gather the grapes or drink the wine from them
Deuteronomy28:40 You will have olive trees everywhere on your land. But you will not have any of the oil to use
Deuteronomy28:41 You will have sons and daughters. But you will not be able to keep them
Deuteronomy28:42 Locusts will destroy all your trees and the crops in your fields.
Deuteronomy28:43 The foreigners living among you will get more and more power
Deuteronomy28:44 The foreigners will have money to loan you
Deuteronomy28:45 All these curses will come on you. They will keep chasing you and catching you
Deuteronomy28:46 These curses will show people that God judged you and your descendants forever. People will be amazed at the terrible things that happen to you.
Deuteronomy28:47 The Lord your God gave you many blessings. But you did not serve him with joy and a glad heart.
Deuteronomy28:48 So you will serve the enemies the Lord will send against you. You will be hungry
Deuteronomy28:49 The Lord will bring a nation from far away to fight you. You will not understand their language. They will come quickly
Deuteronomy28:50 These people will be cruel. They will not care about old people or show mercy to young children.

Deuteronomy28:54 Even the most kind and gentle man among you will become cruel. He will be cruel to his family
Deuteronomy28:55 He will have nothing left to eat
Deuteronomy28:56 Even the most kind and gentle woman among you will become cruel. She might be a lady so gentle and delicate that she never put her feet on the ground to walk anywhere. But she will become cruel to her husband she loves so much. And she will be cruel to her own son and daughter.
Deuteronomy28:57 She will hide and give birth to a baby. And she will eat the baby and everything that comes out of her body with it. All these bad things will happen when your enemy comes to surround your cities and make you suffer.

Deuteronomy28:61 The Lord will even bring troubles and diseases that are not written in this Book of Teachings. He will continue to do this until you are destroyed.
Deuteronomy28:62 You might have as many people as the stars in the sky. But only a few of you will be left

Deuteronomy28:65 You will not have any peace among these nations. You will have no place to rest. The Lord will fill your mind with worry. Your eyes will feel tired. You will be very upset.

Deuteronomy33:22 Moses said this about Dan: Dan is a lion's cub that jumps out from Bashan.
Deuteronomy33:23 Moses said this about Naphtali: Naphtali
Deuteronomy33:24 Moses said this about Asher: Asher is the most blessed of the sons. Let him be the favorite of his brothers. And let him wash his feet in olive oil.
Deuteronomy33:25 Your gates will have locks made from iron and bronze. You will be strong all your life.
Deuteronomy33:26 There is no one like God
Deuteronomy33:27 God lives forever. He is your place of safety. His power continues forever! He is protecting you. He will force your enemies to leave your land. He will say
Deuteronomy33:28 So Israel will live in safety. Jacob's well belongs to them. They will get a land of grain and wine. And that land will get plenty of rain.
Deuteronomy33:29 Israel
Deuteronomy34:1 Moses climbed Mount Nebo. Moses went from the Jordan Valley in Moab to the top of Mount Pisgah. This was across the Jordan River from Jericho. The Lord showed Moses all the land from Gilead to Dan.
Deuteronomy34:2 The Lord showed him all the land of Naphtali
Deuteronomy34:3 The Lord showed Moses the Negev and the valley that goes from Zoar to Jericho
Deuteronomy34:4 The Lord said to Moses
Deuteronomy34:5 Then Moses
Deuteronomy34:6 The Lord buried Moses in Moab. This was in the valley across from Beth Peor. But even today no one knows exactly where Moses' grave is.
Deuteronomy34:7 Moses was 120 years old when he died. He was as strong as ever
Deuteronomy34:8 The Israelites cried for Moses for 30 days. They stayed in the Jordan Valley in Moab until the time of sadness was finished.
Deuteronomy34:9 Moses had put his hands on Joshua and appointed him to be the new leader. Then Joshua son of Nun was filled with the spirit of wisdom. So the Israelites began to obey Joshua
Deuteronomy34:10 Israel never had another prophet like Moses: The Lord knew Moses face to face.
Deuteronomy34:11 The Lord sent Moses to do powerful miracles in the land of Egypt. Pharaoh
Deuteronomy34:12 No other prophet ever did as many powerful and amazing things as Moses did for the Israelites to see.

Joshua3:15 (During harvest time the Jordan River overflows its banks. So the river was at its fullest.) The priests who were carrying the Box came to the shore of the river. When they stepped into the water
Joshua3:16 immediately the water stopped flowing and filled behind that place like at a dam. The water piled up high a long way up the river—all the way to Adam (a town near Zarethan). And the water flowing down to the sea of Arabah (the Dead Sea) was completely cut off. The people crossed the river near Jericho.

Joshua4:1 After all the people had crossed the Jordan River
Joshua4:2 Choose twelve men
Joshua4:3 Tell them to look in the river where the priests were standing and get twelve rocks from that place. Carry these rocks with you and put them where you stay tonight.
Joshua4:4 So Joshua chose one man from each tribe. Then he called the twelve men together.
Joshua4:5 He said to them
Joshua4:6 These rocks will be a sign for you. In the future
Joshua4:7 You will tell them that the Lord stopped the water from flowing in the Jordan River. When the Holy Box of the Lord's Agreement crossed the river
Joshua4:8 So the Israelites obeyed Joshua. They carried twelve rocks from the middle of the Jordan River. There was one rock for each of the twelve tribes of Israel. They did this the way the Lord commanded Joshua. The men carried the rocks with them. Then they put the rocks at the place where they made their camp.
Joshua4:9 (Joshua also put twelve rocks in the middle of the Jordan River. He put them at the place where the priests had stood while carrying the Lord's Holy Box. These rocks are still there today.)
Joshua4:10 The Lord had commanded Joshua to tell the people what to do. This is what Moses had said Joshua must do. So the priests carrying the Holy Box stood in the middle of the river until everything was done. Meanwhile

The kings of the Amorites living west of the Jordan River and the Canaanites living by the Mediterranean Sea heard about this and became very frightened. Joshua8:1 Then the Lord said to Joshua
Joshua8:2 You will do to Ai and its king the same thing you did to Jericho and its king. Only this time you can take all the wealth and animals and keep it for yourselves. You will share the wealth with your people. Now
Joshua8:3 So Joshua led his whole army toward Ai. Then Joshua chose 30
Joshua8:4 Joshua gave them this command: Listen carefully to what I tell you. You must hide in the area behind the city. Wait for the time to attack. Don't go far from the city. Continue to watch and be ready.
Joshua8:5 I will lead the men with me; we will march toward the city. The men in the city will come out to fight against us. We will turn and run away from them
Joshua8:6 These men will chase us away from the city
Joshua8:7 Then you should come out of your hiding place and take control of the city. The Lord your God will give you the power to win.
Joshua8:8 You must do what the Lord says. Watch me and I will give you the command to attack the city. When you have taken control of the city
Joshua8:9 Then Joshua sent them to their hiding place and waited. They went to a place west of Ai
Joshua8:10 Early the next morning Joshua gathered the men together. Then Joshua and the leaders of Israel led the men to Ai.
Joshua8:11 All of the soldiers who were with Joshua marched to Ai. They stopped in front of the city. The army made its camp north of the city. There was a valley between the army and Ai.
Joshua8:12 Then Joshua chose about 5000 men. He sent these men to hide in the area west of the city
Joshua8:13 So Joshua had prepared his men for the fight. The main camp was north of the city. The other men were hiding to the west. That night Joshua went down into the valley.
Joshua8:14 Later
Joshua8:15 Joshua and all the men of Israons will march in front of the Hback. Joshua and his men began running east toward the desert.
Joshua8:16 The people in the city began to shout and started to chase them. All the people left the city.
Joshua8:17 All the men of Ai and Bethel chased the army of Israel. The city was left open—no one stayed to protect the city.
Joshua8:18 Then the Lord said to Joshua
Joshua8:19 When the Israelites who were hiding saw this
Joshua8:20 The men from Ai looked back and saw their city burning. They saw the smoke rising into the sky. So they lost their strength and courage. They stopped chasing the men of Israel. The Israelites stopped running away. They turned and went to fight the men from Ai. There was no safe place for the men from Ai to run to.
Joshua8:21 When Joshua and his men saw that his army had taken control of the city and that smoke was rising from it
Joshua8:22 Then the men who were hiding came out of the city to help with the fight. The army of Israel was on both sides of the men of Ai—the men of Ai were trapped. Israel defeated them. They fought until none of the men from Ai were left alive— none of the enemy escaped.
Joshua8:23 But the king of Ai was left alive. Joshua's men brought him to Joshua.
Joshua8:24 During the fighting
Joshua8:25 All the people of Ai died that day; there were 12
Joshua8:26 Joshua had held his spear toward Ai as a sign to his people to destroy the city. And he did not stop until all the people of Ai were destroyed.
Joshua8:27 The Israelites kept the animals and other things from the city for themselves. This is what the Lord said they could do when he gave Joshua the commands.
Joshua8:28 Then Joshua burned the city of Ai. That city became an empty pile of rocks. It is still like that today.
Joshua8:29 Joshua hanged the king of Ai on a tree and left him hanging there until evening. At sunset
Joshua8:30 Then Joshua built an altar for the Lord
Joshua8:31 The Lord's servant Moses told the Israelites how to build altars. So Joshua built the altar the way it was explained in the Book of the Law of Moses. The altar was made from stones that were not cut. No tool had ever been used on those stones. They offered burnt offerings to the Lord on that altar. They also gave fellowship offerings.
Joshua8:32 There Joshua copied onto the stones the law Moses had written down. This was for all the people of Israel to see.
Joshua8:33 The elders
Joshua8:34 Then Joshua read all the words from the law. He read the blessings and the curses. He read everything the way it was written in the Book of the Law.
Joshua8:35 All the Israelites were gathered together there. All the women and children and all the foreigners who lived with the Israelites were there. And Joshua read every command that Moses had given.
Joshua9:1 All the kings west of the Jordan River heard about these things. They were the kings of the Hittites
Joshua9:2 All these kings came together and made plans to fight against Joshua and the Israelites.
Joshua9:3 The people from the city of Gibeon heard about the way Joshua had defeated Jericho and Ai.
Joshua9:4 So they decided to try to trick the Israelites. This was their plan: They gathered together old wineskins that were cracked and broken. They put these old wineskins on the backs of their animals. They put old pieces of cloth on their animals to look as if they had traveled from far away.
Joshua9:5 The men put old sandals on their feet and wore old clothes. They found some old bread that was dry and moldy.
Joshua9:6 Then they went to the camp of the Israelites. This camp was near Gilgal. The men went to Joshua and said to him
Joshua9:7 The men of Israel said to these Hivite men
Joshua9:8 The Hivite men said to Joshua
Joshua9:9 The men answered
Joshua9:10 And we heard that he defeated the two kings of the Amorites east of the Jordan River. This was King Sihon of Heshbon and King Og of Bashan in the land of Ashtaroth.
Joshua9:11 So our elders and our people said to us
Joshua9:12 Look at our bread. When we left home
Joshua9:13 Look at our wineskins. When we left home they were new and filled with wine. Now you can see that they are cracked and old. Look at our clothes and sandals. You can see that the long journey has almost destroyed the things we wear.
Joshua9:14 The men of Israel wanted to know if these men were telling the truth. So they tasted the bread—but they did not ask the Lord what they should do.
Joshua9:15 Joshua agreed to make peace with them. He agreed to let them live. The leaders of Israel agreed with this promise of Joshua.
Joshua9:16 Three days later the Israelites learned that these men lived very near their camp.
Joshua9:17 So the Israelites went to the place where they lived. On the third day the Israelites came to the cities of Gibeon
Joshua9:18 But the army of Israel did not try to fight against those cities. They had made a peace agreement with them. They had made a promise to them before the Lord
Joshua9:19 But the leaders answered
Joshua9:20 This is what we must do. We must let them live. We cannot hurt them or God will be angry with us because we broke the promise we made to them.
Joshua9:21 So let them live
Joshua9:22 Joshua called the Gibeonites together. He said
Joshua9:23 Now
Joshua9:24 The Gibeonites answered
Joshua9:25 Now we are your servants. You can do whatever you think is right.
Joshua9:26 So the Gibeonites became slaves
Joshua9:27 He made them slaves of the Israelites. They cut wood and carried water for the Israelites and for the altar of the Lord—wherever the Lord chose it to be. They are still slaves today.
Joshua10:1 At this time Adoni Zedek was the king of Jerusalem. He heard that Joshua had defeated Ai and completely destroyed it. The king learned that Joshua had done the same thing to Jericho and its king. He also learned that the Gibeonites had made a peace agreement with Israel and that they lived very near Jerusalem.
Joshua10:2 So Adoni Zedek and his people were very frightened. Gibeon was not a little town like Ai. It was a very big city—as big as any royal city. And all the men in that city were good fighters
Joshua10:3 King Adoni Zedek of Jerusalem talked with King Hoham of Hebron. He also talked with King Piram of Jarmuth
Joshua10:4 Come with me and help me attack Gibeon. Gibeon has made a peace agreement with Joshua and the Israelites.
Joshua10:5 So these five Amorite kings joined their armies together. (The five kings were the king of Jerusalem

Joshua10:7 So Joshua marched out of Gilgal with his whole army. His best fighting men were with him.
Joshua10:8 The Lord said to Joshua
Joshua10:9 Joshua and his army marched all night to Gibeon
Joshua10:10 The Lord caused those armies to be very confused when Israel attacked. So Israel defeated them and won a great victory. Israel chased the enemy from Gibeon along the road going up to Beth Horon. The army of Israel killed men all the way to Azekah and Makkedah.
Joshua10:11 Then they chased the enemy down the road from Beth Horon to Azekah. While they were chasing the enemy

Joshua10:13 So the sun did not move
Joshua10:14 That had never happened before
Joshua10:15 After this
Joshua10:16 But during the fight

Joshua10:18 He said

Joshua10:22 Joshua said
Joshua10:23 So Joshua's men brought the five kings out of the cave—the kings of Jerusalem
Joshua10:24 When they brought the five kings to Joshua
Joshua10:25 Then Joshua said to his men
Joshua10:26 Then Joshua killed the five kings and hanged their bodies on five trees. He left them hanging in the trees until evening.
Joshua10:27 At sunset Joshua told his men to take the bodies down from the trees. So they threw the bodies into the cave where the kings had been hiding and covered the entrance of the cave with large rocks. Their bodies are still in that cave today.
Joshua10:28 That day Joshua defeated Makkedah. He killed the king and the people in that city. No one was left alive. Joshua did the same thing to the king of Makkedah that he had done to the king of Jericho.
Joshua10:29 Then Joshua and all the Israelites traveled from Makkedah to Libnah and attacked that city.
Joshua10:30 The Lord allowed the Israelites to defeat that city and its king. They killed everyone in the city. No one was left alive. And they did the same thing to that king as they had done to the king of Jericho.
Joshua10:31 Then Joshua and all the Israelites left Libnah and went to Lachish. Joshua and his army camped around that city and attacked it.
Joshua10:32 The Lord allowed them to defeat the city of Lachish. They defeated it on the second day. The Israelites killed everyone in the city
Joshua10:33 King Horam of Gezer came to help Lachish
Joshua10:34 Then Joshua and all the Israelites traveled from Lachish to Eglon. They camped around Eglon and attacked it.
Joshua10:35 That day they captured the city and killed everyone in the city. This was the same thing they had done to Lachish.
Joshua10:36 Then Joshua and all the Israelites traveled from Eglon to Hebron and attacked it.
Joshua10:37 They captured the city and all the little towns near Hebron. The Israelites killed everyone in the city
Joshua10:38 Then Joshua and all the Israelites went back to Debir and attacked it.
Joshua10:39 They captured the city
Joshua10:40 So Joshua defeated all the kings of the cities of the hill country
Joshua10:41 Joshua captured all the cities from Kadesh Barnea to Gaza. He captured all the cities from Goshen to Gibeon.
Joshua10:42 Joshua captured all these cities and their kings on one trip. He did this because the Lord
Joshua10:43 Then Joshua and all the Israelites returned to their camp at Gilgal.
Joshua11:1 King Jabin of Hazor heard about everything that had happened. So he decided to call together the armies of several kings. He sent a message to King Jobab of Madon to the king of Shimron
Joshua11:2 and to the kings of the north in the hill country and in the desert. Jabin sent the message to the kings of the Kinnereth
Joshua11:3 Jabin sent the message to the kings of the Canaanites in the east and in the west. He sent the message to the Amorites
Joshua11:4 So the armies of all these kings came together. There were many fighting men and many horses and chariots. It was a very large army—it looked as if there were as many men as grains of sand on the seashore.
Joshua11:5 All of these kings met together at the small river of Merom. They joined their armies together into one camp and made plans for the battle against Israel.
Joshua11:6 Then the Lord said to Joshua
Joshua11:7 So Joshua and his whole army surprised the enemy and attacked them at the river of Merom.
Joshua11:8 The Lord allowed Israel to defeat them. The army of Israel defeated them and chased them to Greater Sidon
Joshua11:9 Joshua did what the Lord said to do; he cut the legs of their horses and burned their chariots.
Joshua11:10 Then Joshua went back and captured the city of Hazor and killed its king. (Hazor was the leader of all the kingdoms that fought against Israel.)
Joshua11:11 The army of Israel killed everyone in that city and completely destroyed all the people. There was nothing left alive. Then they burned the city.
Joshua11:12 Joshua captured all these cities and killed all their kings. He completely destroyed everything in these cities—just as Moses
Joshua11:13 But the army of Israel did not burn any cities that were built on hills. The only city built on a hill that they burned was Hazor. This is the city Joshua burned.
Joshua11:14 The Israelites kept for themselves all the things and all the animals they found in the cities. But they killed all the people there. They left no one alive.
Joshua11:15 Long ago the Lord commanded his servant Moses to do this. Then Moses commanded Joshua to do this. So Joshua obeyed God. He did everything that the Lord had commanded Moses.
Joshua11:16 So Joshua defeated all the people in that whole area. He had control over the hill country
Joshua11:17 Joshua had control of all the land from Mount Halak near Seir to Baal Gad in the Valley of Lebanon below Mount Hermon. He captured all the kings in that land and killed them.
Joshua11:18 Joshua fought against them for many years.
Joshua11:19 Only one city in all the land made a peace agreement with Israel. That was the Hivite city of Gibeon. All the other cities were defeated in war.
Joshua11:20 The Lord made those people feel brave enough to fight against Israel. This was so that Israel could destroy them completely without mercy just as the Lord had commanded Moses to do.
Joshua11:21 The Anakites lived in the hill country in the area of Hebron

Joshua12:1 The Israelites had taken control of the land east of the Jordan River. They had all the land from Arnon Ravine to Mount Hermon and all the land along the eastern side of the Jordan Valley. These are all the kings the Israelites defeated to take this land:
Joshua12:2 They defeated King Sihon of the Amorites living in the city of Heshbon. He ruled the land from Aroer at the Arnon Ravine to the Jabbok River. His land started in the center of that ravine. This was their border with the Ammonites. Sihon ruled over half of the land of Gilead.
Joshua12:3 He also ruled over the eastern side of the Jordan Valley from Lake Galilee to the Dead Sea (Salt Sea). And he ruled from Beth Jeshimoth to the south to the hills of Pisgah.
Joshua12:4 They also defeated King Og of Bashan. Og was from the Rephaites. He ruled the land in Ashtaroth and Edrei.
Joshua12:5 Og ruled over Mount Hermon
Joshua12:6 The Lord's servant Moses and the Israelites defeated all these kings. And Moses gave that land to the tribe of Reuben
Joshua12:7 These are the kings of the land west of the Jordan River who were defeated by Joshua and the Israelites. This land was in the area west of Baal Gad in the Lebanon Valley as far as Mount Halak that rises toward Seir. Joshua divided it among the tribes.

Joshua13:8 The tribes of Reuben

Joshua13:11 Also the town of Gilead was in that land. And the area where the people of Geshur and Maacah lived was in that land. All of Mount Hermon and all of Bashan as far as Salecah was in that land.
Joshua13:12 All the kingdom of King Og was in that land. King Og ruled in Bashan. In the past he ruled in Ashtaroth and Edrei. Og was from the Rephaites. In the past Moses had defeated those people and had taken their land.
Joshua13:13 The Israelites did not force out the people of Geshur and Maacah. They still live among the Israelites today.

Joshua15:11 Then the border went to the hill north of Ekron. From there the border turned to Shikkeron and went past Mount Baalah. The border continued on to Jabneel and ended at the Mediterranean Sea.

Joshua20:6 You should stay in that city until its court has judged you and until the high priest dies. Then you may go back to your hometown.

Joshua20:9 All Israelites or any foreigners living among them who killed someone by accident were allowed to run away to one of these cities of safety. They could be safe there and not be killed by anyone chasing them. The court in that city would judge them.

Joshua22:1 Then Joshua called a meeting of all the people from the tribes of Reuben
Joshua22:2 Joshua said to them
Joshua22:3 All this time you have supported all the other Israelites. You carefully obeyed all the commands that the Lord your God gave you.
Joshua22:4 The Lord your God promised to give the Israelites peace. And now
Joshua22:5 But remember—continue to obey the law that Moses gave you. You must love the Lord your God and obey his commands. You must continue to follow him and serve him the very best that you can.
Joshua22:6 Then Joshua said goodbye to them

1Samuel6:7 You must build a new wagon and get two cows that just had calves. These must be cows that have never worked in the fields. Tie the cows to the wagon so that they can pull it. Then take the calves back home and put them in their pen. Don't let them follow their mothers.
1Samuel6:8 Put the Lord's Holy Box on the wagon. You must put the golden models in the bag beside the Box. They are your gifts for God to forgive your sins. Send the wagon straight on its way.
1Samuel6:9 Watch the wagon. If the wagon goes toward Beth Shemesh in Israel's own land
1Samuel6:10 The Philistines did what the priests and magicians said. They found two cows that had just had calves and tied the cows to the wagon and put the calves in their pens at home.
1Samuel6:11 Then the Philistines put the Lord's Holy Box on the wagon along with the bag with the golden models of the tumors and mice.
1Samuel6:12 The cows went straight to Beth Shemesh. The cows stayed on the road
1Samuel6:13 The people of Beth Shemesh were harvesting their wheat in the valley. They looked up and saw the Holy Box. They were very happy to see it again. They ran to get it.
1Samuel6:14 The wagon came to the field that belonged to Joshua of Beth Shemesh and stopped there near a large rock. Some Levites took down the Lord's Holy Box and the bag that had the golden models. The Levites put the Lord's Box and the bag that was with it on the large rock. The people of Beth Shemesh cut up the wagon and killed the cows. That day

1Kings4:1 King Solomon ruled over all Israel.
1Kings4:2 These are the names of his leading officials: Azariah son of Zadok was the priest;
1Kings4:3 Elihoreph and Ahijah
1Kings4:4 Benaiah son of Jehoiada was the commander of the army; Zadok and Abiathar were priests;
1Kings4:5 Azariah son of Nathan was in charge of the district governors; Zabud son of Nathan was a priest and an advisor to King Solomon;
1Kings4:6 Ahishar was responsible for everything in the king's palace; Adoniram son of Abda was in charge of the slaves.
1Kings4:7 Israel was divided into twelve districts. Solomon chose governors to rule over each district. These governors were ordered to gather food from their districts and give it to the king and his family. Each of the twelve governors was responsible for giving food to the king one month each year.
1Kings4:8 These are the names of the twelve governors: Ben Hur was governor of the hill country of Ephraim.
1Kings4:9 Ben Deker was governor of Makaz
1Kings4:10 Ben Hesed was governor of Arubboth
1Kings4:11 Ben Abinadab was governor of Naphoth Dor. He was married to Taphath
1Kings4:12 Baana son of Ahilud was governor of Taanach and Megiddo and all of Beth Shean next to Zarethan. This was below Jezreel
1Kings4:13 Ben Geber was governor of Ramoth Gilead. He was governor of all the towns and villages of Jair son of Manasseh in Gilead. He was also governor of the district of Argob in Bashan. In this area there were 60 cities with big walls around them. These cities also had bronze bars on the gates.
1Kings4:14 Ahinadab son of Iddo was governor of Mahanaim.
1Kings4:15 Ahimaaz was governor of Naphtali. He was married to Basemath the daughter of Solomon.
1Kings4:16 Baana son of Hushai was governor of Asher and Aloth.
1Kings4:17 Jehoshaphat son of Paruah was governor of Issachar.
1Kings4:18 Shimei son of Ela was governor of Benjamin.
1Kings4:19 Geber son of Uri was governor of Gilead. There had been two kings in this area
1Kings4:20 In Judah and Israel there were as many people as sand on the seashore. The people were happy and had plenty to eat and drink.
1Kings4:21 Solomon ruled over all the kingdoms from the Euphrates River to the land of the Philistines. His kingdom went as far as the border of Egypt. These countries sent gifts to Solomon
1Kings4:22 This is the amount of food that Solomon needed each day for himself and for everyone who ate at his table: 150 bushels of fine flour
1Kings4:23 ***

1Kings4:27 And each month one of the twelve district governors gave King Solomon everything he needed for all the people who ate at the king's table.
1Kings4:28 The district governors also gave the king enough straw and barley for the chariot horses and the riding horses. Everyone brought this grain to the necessary places.

1Kings4:30 He was wiser than anyone in the East or in Egypt.
1Kings4:31 He was wiser than anyone on earth

1Kings4:34 People from all nations came to listen to his wisdom. Kings sent their wise men to listen to Solomon.

1Kings6:1 So in the month of Ziv
1Kings6:2 The Temple was 60 cubits long
1Kings6:3 The porch of the Temple was 20 cubits long and 10 cubits wide. The porch ran along the front of the main part of the Temple itself. Its length was equal to the width of the Temple.
1Kings6:4 There were narrow windows in the Temple. These windows were smaller on the inside of the wall than on the outside.
1Kings6:5 Then Solomon built a row of rooms around the main part of the Temple. This row of rooms was three stories tall with the rooms built one above the other.
1Kings6:6 The rooms touched the Temple wall
1Kings6:7 The stones were completely finished before they were brought into the Temple area
1Kings6:8 The entrance to these rooms was on bottom floor at the south side of the Temple. Inside there were stairs that went up to the second floor and from there to the third floor.
1Kings6:9 Solomon finished building the main part of the Temple and then covered it inside with cedar boards.
1Kings6:10 Then he finished building the rooms around the Temple. Each story was 5 cubits tall. The cedar beams in these rooms rested on a ledge of the Temple wall.
1Kings6:11 The Lord said to Solomon
1Kings6:12 If you obey all my laws and commands
1Kings6:13 I will live among the children of Israel in this Temple that you are building
1Kings6:14 When Solomon finished the stonework on the Temple
1Kings6:15 the stone walls inside the Temple were covered with cedar boards from floor to ceiling. Then the stone floor was covered with pine boards.
1Kings6:16 They built an inner room 20 cubits long in the back part of the Temple. This room was called the Most Holy Place. They covered the walls in this room with cedar boards
1Kings6:17 In front of the Most Holy Place was the main part of the Temple. This room was 40 cubits long.
1Kings6:18 They covered the walls in this room with cedar boards—none of the stones in the walls could be seen. They carved pictures of flowers and gourds into the cedar.
1Kings6:19 Solomon finished the inner room in the back part of the Temple. This room was for the Lord's Box of the Agreement.
1Kings6:20 This room was 20 cubits long
1Kings6:21 and wrapped gold chains around it.
1Kings6:22 The inside of the Temple was covered with gold
1Kings6:23 The workers made two statues of Cherub angels with wings. They made the statues from olive wood and put them in the Most Holy Place. Each angel was 10 cubits tall.
1Kings6:24 Both Cherub angels were the same size and built the same way. Each one had two wings. Each wing was 5 cubits long. From the end of one wing to the end of the other wing was 10 cubits. And each Cherub angel was 10 cubits tall.
1Kings6:25 ***
1Kings6:26 ***
1Kings6:27 They put the Cherub angels beside one another in the Most Holy Place. Their wings touched each other in the middle of the room. The other two wings touched each side wall.
1Kings6:28 The two Cherub angels were covered with gold.
1Kings6:29 The walls around the main room and the inner room were carved with pictures of Cherub angels
1Kings6:30 The floor of both rooms was covered with gold.
1Kings6:31 The workers made two doors from olive wood. They put these doors at the entrance of the Most Holy Place. The frame around the doors was made with five sides.
1Kings6:32 They made the two doors from olive wood. The workers carved pictures of Cherub angels
1Kings6:33 They also made doors for the entrance to the main room. They used olive wood to make a square doorframe.
1Kings6:34 Then they used fir wood to make the doors. There were two doors. Each door had two parts

1Kings7:1 King Solomon also built a palace for himself. It took 13 years to build Solomon's palace.
1Kings7:2 He also built the building called the Forest of Lebanon. It was 100 cubits long
1Kings7:3 There were cedar beams going across the rows of columns. There were 15 beams for each section of columns. There was a total of 45 beams. On top of these beams there were cedar boards for the ceiling.
1Kings7:4 There were three rows of windows across from each other on the side walls.
1Kings7:5 There were three doors at each end. All the door openings and frames were square.
1Kings7:6 Solomon also built the Porch of Columns. It was 50 cubits long and 30 cubits wide. Along the front of the porch
1Kings7:7 He also built a throne room where he judged people. He called this the Judgment Hall. The room was covered with cedar from floor to ceiling.
1Kings7:8 Behind the Judgment Hall was a courtyard. The palace where Solomon lived was built around that courtyard and looked like the Judgment Hall. He also built the same kind of palace for his wife
1Kings7:9 All of these buildings were made with expensive blocks of stone. The stones were cut to the right size with a saw and then smoothed on front and back. These expensive stones went from the foundation all the way up to the top layer of the wall. Even the wall around the yard was made with expensive blocks of stone.
1Kings7:10 The foundations were made with large
1Kings7:11 On top of these stones there were other expensive stones and cedar beams.
1Kings7:12 There were walls around the palace yard
1Kings7:13 King Solomon sent for a man named Huram who lived in Tyre and brought him to Jerusalem.
1Kings7:14 Huram's mother was an Israelite from the tribe of Naphtali. His dead father was from Tyre. Huram made things from bronze. He was a very skilled and experienced builder. So King Solomon asked him to come
1Kings7:15 Huram made two bronze columns for the porch. Each column was 18 cubits tall and 12 cubits around. The columns were hollow and their metal walls were 3 inches thick.
1Kings7:16 He also made two bronze capitals that were 5 cubits tall. He put these capitals on top of the columns.
1Kings7:17 He made two nets of chain to cover the capitals on top of the two columns.
1Kings7:18 Then he made two rows of bronze pomegranates. He put the bronze pomegranates on the nets of each column to cover the capitals at the top of the columns.
1Kings7:19 The capitals on top of the columns were shaped like flowers.
1Kings7:20 The capitals were on top of the columns
1Kings7:21 Huram put these two bronze columns at the porch of the Temple. One column was put on the south side of the entrance and one was put on the north side of it. The column on the south was named Jakin. The column on the north was named Boaz.
1Kings7:22 They put the flower-shaped capitals on top of the columns
1Kings7:23 Then Huram melted bronze and poured it into a huge mold to make a tank
1Kings7:24 There was a rim around the outer edge of the tank. Under this rim there were two rows of bronze gourds all around the tank. The bronze gourds were made in one piece as part of the tank.
1Kings7:25 The tank rested on the backs of 12 bronze bulls. All 12 of the bulls were looking out
1Kings7:26 The sides of the tank were 3 inches thick. The rim around the tank was like the rim of a cup or like the petals on a flower. The tank held about 11
1Kings7:27 Then Huram made ten bronze carts. Each cart was 4 cubits long
1Kings7:28 The carts were made with square panels set in frames.
1Kings7:29 On the panels and frames were bronze bulls
1Kings7:30 Each cart had four bronze wheels with bronze axles. At the corners there were bronze supports for a large bowl. The supports had designs of flowers hammered into the bronze.
1Kings7:31 There was a frame around the top with an opening for the bowl. The frame was 1 cubit tall
1Kings7:32 There were four wheels under the frame. The wheels were 1?cubits in diameter. The axles between the wheels were made as one piece with the cart.
1Kings7:33 The wheels were like the wheels on a chariot. Everything on the wheels—the axles
1Kings7:34 There were supports at each of the four corners of the carts. They were made as one piece with the cart.
1Kings7:35 There was a strip of bronze around the top of each cart. It was made as one piece with the cart.
1Kings7:36 The sides of the cart and the frames had pictures of Cherub angels
1Kings7:37 Huram made ten carts
1Kings7:38 Huram also made ten bowls. There was one bowl for each of the ten carts. Each bowl was 4 cubits across and could hold about 230 gallons.
1Kings7:39 He put five carts on the south side of the Temple and five carts on the north side. He put the large tank in the southeast corner of the Temple.
1Kings7:40 Huram also made pots

1Kings7:42 ***
1Kings7:43 ***
1Kings7:44 ***
1Kings7:45 ***
1Kings7:46 Solomon never weighed the bronze that was used to make these things. There was too much to weigh. So the total weight of all the bronze was never known. The king ordered these things to be made near the Jordan River between Succoth and Zarethan. They made them by melting the bronze and pouring it into molds in the ground.
1Kings7:47 ***
1Kings7:48 Solomon also commanded that all these things be made from gold for the Temple: the golden altar; the golden table which held the special bread that was offered to God; the lampstands of pure gold (five on the south side and five on the north side in front of the Most Holy Place); the gold flowers

1Kings10:1 The queen of Sheba heard about Solomon
1Kings10:2 She traveled to Jerusalem with a very large group of servants. There were many camels carrying spices

1Kings10:4 The queen of Sheba saw that Solomon was very wise. She also saw the beautiful palace he had built.
1Kings10:5 She saw the food at the king's table. She saw his officials meeting together. She saw the servants in the palace and the good clothes they wore. She saw his parties and the sacrifices that he offered in the Temple. She was so amazed that it took her breath away.
1Kings10:6 Then she said to King Solomon

1Kings10:12 Solomon used this special wood to build supports in the Temple and the palace as well as harps and lyres for the singers. That was the last time such a large shipment of that kind of wood was brought to Israel. There hasn't been any seen around here since then.

1Kings10:16 King Solomon made 200 large shields of hammered gold. Each shield contained about 15 pounds of gold.
1Kings10:17 He also made 300 smaller shields of hammered gold. Each shield contained about 4 pounds of gold. The king put them in the building called the Forest of Lebanon.
1Kings10:18 King Solomon also built a large throne with ivory decorations. It was covered with pure gold.
1Kings10:19 There were six steps leading up to the throne. The back of the throne was round at the top. There were armrests on both sides of the throne
1Kings10:20 There were also two lions on each of the six steps
1Kings10:21 All of Solomon's cups and glasses were made of gold
1Kings10:22 The king also had many cargo ships that he sent out to trade things with other countries. These were Hiram's ships. Every three years the ships would come back with a new load of gold
1Kings10:23 King Solomon became greater in riches and wisdom than any other king on earth.
