2Kings15:3 Azariah did what the Lord said was right
2Kings15:4 But he did not destroy the high places. People still made sacrifices and burned incense in these places of worship.
2Kings15:5 The Lord caused King Azariah to become sick with leprosy. He was a leper until the day he died. Azariah lived in a separate house. His son Jotham was in charge of the king's palace and judged the people.
2Kings15:6 All the great things that Azariah did are written in the book
2Kings15:7 Azariah died and was buried with his ancestors in the City of David. Azariah's son Jotham became the new king after him.
2Kings15:8 Zechariah son of Jeroboam ruled over Samaria in Israel for six months. This was during the 38 year that Azariah was king of Judah.
2Kings15:9 Zechariah did what the Lord said was wrong. He did the same things his ancestors did. He did not stop committing the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat who caused Israel to sin.
2Kings15:10 Shallum son of Jabesh made plans against Zechariah. Shallum killed Zechariah in Ibleam. Shallum became the new king.
2Kings15:11 All the other things that Zechariah did are written in the book
2Kings15:12 In this way the Lord's word came true. The Lord had told Jehu that four generations of his descendants would be kings of Israel.
2Kings15:13 Shallum son of Jabesh became king of Israel during the 39 year that Uzziah was king of Judah. Shallum ruled for one month in Samaria.
2Kings15:14 Menahem son of Gadi came up from Tirzah to Samaria and killed Shallum son of Jabesh. Then Menahem became the new king after him.
2Kings15:15 All the things Shallum did
2Kings15:16 Menahem defeated Tiphsah and the area around it. The people refused to open the city gate for him. So Menahem defeated them and ripped open all the pregnant women in that city.
2Kings15:17 Menahem son of Gadi became king over Israel during the 39 year that Azariah was king of Judah. Menahem ruled ten years in Samaria.
2Kings15:18 Menahem did what the Lord said was wrong. He did not stop committing the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat who caused Israel to sin.
2Kings15:19 King Pul of Assyria came to fight against Israel. Menahem gave Pul 75
2Kings15:20 Menahem raised the money by making all the rich and powerful men pay taxes. He taxed each man 20 ounces of silver and gave the money to the king of Assyria. So the king of Assyria left and did not stay there in Israel.
2Kings15:21 All the great things that Menahem did are written in the book
2Kings15:22 Menahem died and was buried with his ancestors. His son Pekahiah became the new king after him.
2Kings15:23 Pekahiah son of Menahem became king over Israel in Samaria during the 50 year that Azariah was king of Judah. Pekahiah ruled two years.
2Kings15:24 He did what the Lord said was wrong. He did not stop committing the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat that caused Israel to sin.
2Kings15:25 The commander of Pekahiah's army was Pekah son of Remaliah. Pekah killed Pekahiah in Samaria at the king's palace. Pekah had 50 men from Gilead with him when he killed Pekahiah. Then Pekah became the new king after him.
2Kings15:26 All the great things Pekahiah did are written in the book
2Kings15:27 Pekah son of Remaliah began to rule over Israel in Samaria during the 52 year that Azariah was king of Judah. Pekah ruled 20 years.
2Kings15:28 Pekah did what the Lord said was wrong. He did not stop committing the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat who caused Israel to sin.
2Kings15:29 King Tiglath Pileser of Assyria came to fight against Israel while Pekah was king of Israel. Tiglath Pileser captured Ijon
2Kings15:30 Hoshea son of Elah made plans against Pekah son of Remaliah and killed him. Then Hoshea became the new king. This was during the 20 year that Jotham son of Uzziah was king of Judah.
2Kings15:31 All the great things that Pekah did are written in the book
2Kings15:32 Jotham son of Uzziah became king of Judah. This was during the second year that Pekah son of Remaliah was king of Israel.
2Kings15:33 Jotham was 25 years old when he became king. He ruled 16 years in Jerusalem. His mother was named Jerusha
2Kings15:34 Jotham did what the Lord said was right
2Kings15:35 But he did not destroy the high places. The people still made sacrifices and burned incense at those places of worship. Jotham built the upper gate of the Lord's Temple.
2Kings15:36 All the great things that Jotham did are written in the book
2Kings15:37 At that time the Lord sent King Rezin of Aram and Pekah son of Remaliah to fight against Judah.
2Kings15:38 Jotham died and was buried with his ancestors in the City of David
2Kings16:1 Ahaz son of Jotham became king of Judah during the 17 year that Pekah son of Remaliah was king of Israel.
2Kings16:2 Ahaz was 20 years old when he became king. He ruled 16 years in Jerusalem. Unlike his ancestor David
2Kings16:3 He lived like the kings of Israel and even burned his son as a sacrifice. He copied the terrible sins of the nations that the Lord had forced to leave the country when the Israelites came.
2Kings16:4 Ahaz made sacrifices and burned incense at the high places and on the hills and under every green tree.
2Kings16:5 King Rezin of Aram and Pekah son of King Remaliah of Israel came to fight against Jerusalem. Rezin and Pekah surrounded Ahaz
2Kings16:6 At that time King Rezin of Aram took back Elath for Aram. Rezin took all the people of Judah who were living in Elath. The Arameans settled in Elath
2Kings16:7 Ahaz sent messengers to King Tiglath Pileser of Assyria. The message was: I am your servant. I am like a son to you. Come and save me from the king of Aram and the king of Israel. They have come to fight me.
2Kings16:8 Ahaz also took the silver and gold that was in the Temple of the Lord and in the treasuries of the king's palace. Then Ahaz sent a gift to the king of Assyria.
2Kings16:9 The king of Assyria listened to Ahaz and went to fight against Damascus. The king captured that city and took the people from Damascus as prisoners to Kir. He also killed Rezin.
2Kings16:10 King Ahaz went to Damascus to meet King Tiglath Pileser of Assyria. Ahaz saw the altar at Damascus. He sent a model and pattern of this altar to Uriah the priest.
2Kings16:11 Then Uriah the priest built an altar just like the model King Ahaz had sent him from Damascus. Uriah the priest built the altar this way before King Ahaz came back from Damascus.
2Kings16:12 When the king arrived from Damascus
2Kings16:13 On the altar
2Kings16:14 Ahaz took the bronze altar that was before the Lord from the front of the Temple. This bronze altar was between Ahaz's altar and the Temple of the Lord. Ahaz put the bronze altar on the north side of his own altar.
2Kings16:15 He commanded Uriah the priest
2Kings16:16 Uriah the priest did everything that King Ahaz commanded him to do.
2Kings16:17 There were carts with bronze panels and basins for the priests to wash their hands. King Ahaz removed the panels and basins and cut up the carts. He also took the large tank off the bronze bulls that stood under it. He put the large tank on a stone pavement.
2Kings16:18 Workers had built a covered place inside the Temple area for the Sabbath meetings. But Ahaz removed the covered place and the outside entrance for the king. He removed all of these from the Lord's Temple. Ahaz did this because of the king of Assyria.
2Kings16:19 All the great things that Ahaz did are written in the book
2Kings16:20 Ahaz died and was buried with his ancestors in the City of David. Ahaz's son Hezekiah became the new king after him.
2Kings17:1 Hoshea son of Elah began to rule in Samaria over Israel. This was during the 12 year that Ahaz was king of Judah. Hoshea ruled nine years.
2Kings17:2 He did what the Lord said was wrong
2Kings17:3 King Shalmaneser of Assyria came to fight against Hoshea and defeated him. So Hoshea paid tribute to Shalmaneser.
2Kings17:4 Later
2Kings17:5 The king of Assyria attacked many places in Israel. Then he came to Samaria and fought against it for three years.
2Kings17:6 The king of Assyria took Samaria during the ninth year that Hoshea was king of Israel. He captured many Israelites and took them as prisoners to Assyria. He made them live in Halah by the Habor River at Gozan and in other cities of the Medes.
2Kings17:7 These things happened because the Israelites had sinned against the Lord their God. And it was the Lord who brought the Israelites out of the land of Egypt! He saved them from the power of Pharaoh
2Kings17:8 They began doing the same things that other people did. And the Lord had forced those people to leave their land when the Israelites came. The Israelites also chose to be ruled by kings.
2Kings17:9 The Israelites secretly did things against the Lord their God
2Kings17:10 They put up memorial stones and Asherah poles on every high hill and under every green tree.
2Kings17:11 They burned incense there in all those places for worship. They did these things like the nations that the Lord forced out of the land before them. The Israelites did evil things that made the Lord angry.
2Kings17:12 They served idols
2Kings17:13 The Lord used every prophet and every seer to warn Israel and Judah. He said
2Kings17:14 But the people would not listen. They were very stubborn like their ancestors. Their ancestors did not believe the Lord their God.
2Kings17:15 They refused the Lord's laws and the agreement that he made with their ancestors. They refused to listen to the Lord's warnings. They worshiped idols that were worth nothing and they themselves became worth nothing. They lived like the people in the nations around them. They did those bad things. And the Lord had warned the Israelites. The Lord told them not to do those bad things.
2Kings17:16 The people stopped following the commands of the Lord their God. They made two gold statues of calves. They made Asherah poles. They worshiped all the stars of heaven and served Baal.
2Kings17:17 They sacrificed their sons and daughters in the fire. They used magic and witchcraft to try to learn the future. They sold themselves to do what the Lord said was evil. They did this to make the Lord angry.
2Kings17:18 So the Lord became very angry with Israel and removed them from his sight. There were no Israelites left
2Kings17:19 But even the people of Judah did not obey the commands of the Lord their God. They lived just as the Israelites had.
2Kings17:20 The Lord rejected all the Israelites. He brought them many troubles. He let people destroy them. Finally he threw them away and removed them from his sight.
2Kings17:21 The Lord tore them from the family of David
2Kings17:22 So the Israelites sinned in all the ways Jeroboam did. And they did not stop committing these sins
2Kings17:23 until the Lord took Israel away from his sight. And he said this would happen. He sent his prophets to tell the people this would happen. So the Israelites were taken out of their country into Assyria
2Kings17:24 The king of Assyria took the Israelites out of Samaria and brought in other people from Babylon
2Kings17:25 When these people began to live in Samaria
2Kings17:26 Some people said to the king of Assyria
2Kings17:27 So the king of Assyria gave this command: You took some priests from Samaria. Send one of them who I captured back to Samaria. Let that priest go and live there. Then he can teach the people the law of the god of that country.
2Kings17:28 So one of the priests who the Assyrians had carried away from Samaria came to live in Bethel. He taught the people how they should honor the Lord.
2Kings17:29 But all those people made gods of their own. They put them in the temples at the high places that the people of Samaria had made. They did this wherever they lived.
2Kings17:30 The people of Babylon made the false god Succoth Benoth. The people of Cuthah made the false god Nergal. The people of Hamath made the false god Ashima.
2Kings17:31 The people of Avva made the false gods Nibhaz and Tartak. The people from Sepharvaim also burned their children in the fire to honor their false gods
2Kings17:32 But they also worshiped the Lord. They chose priests for the high places from among the people. These priests made sacrifices for the people in the temples at those places of worship.
2Kings17:33 They respected the Lord but also served their own gods
2Kings17:34 Even today they live like they did in the past. They don't honor the Lord. They don't obey the rules and commands of the Israelites. They don't obey the law or the commands that the Lord gave to the children of Jacob.
2Kings17:35 The Lord made an agreement with the Israelites. The Lord commanded them
2Kings17:36 But you must follow the Lord. He is the God who brought you out of Egypt. He used his great power to save you. You must worship the Lord and make sacrifices to him.
2Kings17:37 You must obey the rules
2Kings17:38 You must not forget the agreement that I made with you. You must not respect other gods.
2Kings17:39 No
2Kings17:40 But the Israelites did not listen. They kept on doing the same things they did before.
2Kings17:41 So now those other nations respected the Lord
2Kings18:1 Hezekiah son of Ahaz was king of Judah. Hezekiah began to rule during the third year that Hoshea son of Elah was king of Israel.
2Kings18:2 Hezekiah was 25 years old when he began to rule. He ruled 29 years in Jerusalem. His mother's name was Abi
2Kings18:3 Hezekiah did what the Lord said was right
2Kings18:4 Hezekiah destroyed the high places. He broke the memorial stones and cut down the Asherah poles. At that time the Israelites burned incense to the bronze snake made by Moses. This bronze snake was called Nehushtan. Hezekiah broke this bronze snake into pieces.
2Kings18:5 Hezekiah trusted in the Lord
2Kings18:6 He was very faithful to the Lord and did not stop following him. He obeyed the commands that the Lord had given to Moses.
2Kings18:7 The Lord was with Hezekiah
2Kings18:8 Hezekiah defeated the Philistines all the way to Gaza and the area around it. He defeated all the Philistine cities—from the smallest town to the largest city.

2Kings18:10 At the end of the third year Shalmaneser captured Samaria. He took Samaria during the sixth year that Hezekiah was king of Judah. (This was also the ninth year that Hoshea was king of Israel.)
2Kings18:11 The king of Assyria took the Israelites as prisoners to Assyria. He made them live in Halah
2Kings18:12 This happened because the Israelites did not obey the Lord their God. They broke his agreement and did not obey everything that Moses

2Kings21:5 Manasseh built altars for the stars of heaven in the two courtyards of the Lord's Temple.
2Kings21:6 He sacrificed his own son and burned him on the altar. He used different ways of trying to know the future. He visited mediums and wizards. Manasseh did more and more things that the Lord saw as evil
2Kings21:7 Manasseh made a carved statue of Asherah. He put this statue in the Temple. The Lord had said to David and to David's son Solomon about this Temple: I have chosen Jerusalem from all the cities in Israel. I will put my name in the Temple in Jerusalem forever.
2Kings21:8 I will not cause the Israelites to leave the land that I gave to their ancestors. I will let the people stay in their land if they obey everything I commanded them and all the teachings that my servant Moses gave them.
2Kings21:9 But the people did not listen to God. Manasseh did more evil things than all the nations that lived in Canaan before Israel came. And the Lord destroyed those nations when the Israelites came to take their land.
2Kings21:10 The Lord used his servants the prophets to say this:
2Kings21:11 King Manasseh of Judah has done these hated things and has done more evil than the Amorites before him. He also has caused Judah to sin because of his idols.
2Kings21:12 So the Lord
2Kings21:13 I will stretch the measuring line of Samaria and the plumb line of Ahab's family over Jerusalem. A man wipes a dish
2Kings21:14 There will still be a few of my people left
2Kings21:15 This is because my people did what I said was wrong. They have made me angry with them since the day their ancestors came up out of Egypt.
2Kings21:16 And Manasseh killed many innocent people. He filled Jerusalem from one end to another with blood. And all these sins are in addition to the sins that caused Judah to sin. Manasseh caused Judah to do what the Lord said was wrong.'
2Kings21:17 All the things that Manasseh did
2Kings21:18 Manasseh died and was buried with his ancestors. He was buried in the garden at his house. It was called the Garden of Uzza. His son Amon became the new king after him.
2Kings21:19 Amon was 22 years old when he began to rule. He ruled two years in Jerusalem. His mother's name was Meshullemeth daughter of Haruz from Jotbah.
2Kings21:20 Amon did what the Lord said was wrong
2Kings21:21 Amon lived just as his father had lived. He worshiped and served the same idols his father had worshiped.

2Kings21:25 The other things that Amon did are written in the book
2Kings21:26 Amon was buried in his grave at the Garden of Uzza. His son Josiah became the new king.

2Kings23:1 King Josiah told all the leaders of Judah and Jerusalem to come and meet with him.
2Kings23:2 Then the king went up to the Lord's Temple. All the people of Judah and the people who lived in Jerusalem went with him. The priests
2Kings23:3 The king stood by the column and made an agreement with the Lord. He agreed to follow the Lord and to obey his commands
2Kings23:4 Then the king commanded Hilkiah the high priest
2Kings23:5 The kings of Judah had chosen some ordinary men to serve as priests. These false priests were burning incense at the high places in every city of Judah and all the towns around Jerusalem. They burned incense to honor Baal
2Kings23:6 Josiah removed the Asherah pole from the Lord's Temple. He took the Asherah pole outside the city to the Kidron Valley and burned it there. Then he beat the burned pieces into dust and scattered the dust over the graves of the common people.
2Kings23:7 Then King Josiah broke down the houses of the male prostitutes who were in the Lord's Temple. Women also used these houses and made little tent covers to honor the false goddess Asherah.
2Kings23:8 At that time the priests did not bring the sacrifices to Jerusalem and offer them on the altar in the Temple. The priests lived in cities all over Judah. They burned incense and offered sacrifices at the high places in those cities. The high places were everywhere
2Kings23:9 ***
2Kings23:10 Topheth was a place in the Valley of Hinnom's Son where people killed their children and burned them on an altar to honor the false god Molech. Josiah ruined that place so that no one could use it again.
2Kings23:11 In the past the kings of Judah had put some horses and a chariot near the entrance to the Lord's Temple. This was near the room of an important official named Nathan Melech. The horses and chariot were to honor the sun god. Josiah removed the horses and burned the chariot.
2Kings23:12 In the past the kings of Judah had built altars on the roof of Ahab's building. King Manasseh had also built altars in the two courtyards of the Lord's Temple. Josiah destroyed all the altars and threw the broken pieces into the Kidron Valley.
2Kings23:13 In the past King Solomon built some high places on Destroyer Hill near Jerusalem. The high places were on the south side of that hill. King Solomon built one of these places of worship to honor Ashtoreth
2Kings23:14 He broke all the memorial stones and Asherah poles. Then he scattered dead men's bones over that place.
2Kings23:15 Josiah also broke down the altar and high place at Bethel. Jeroboam son of Nebat had made this altar. Jeroboam caused Israel to sin. Josiah broke down both that altar and the high place. He broke the stones of the altar to pieces. Then he beat it into dust and he burned the Asherah pole.
2Kings23:16 Josiah looked around and saw graves on the mountain. He sent men
2Kings23:17 Josiah said
2Kings23:18 Josiah said
2Kings23:19 Josiah also destroyed all the temples at the high places in the cities of Samaria. The kings of Israel had built those temples
2Kings23:20 Josiah killed all the priests of the high places that were in Samaria. He killed the priests on those altars and burned men's bones on the altars so that they could never be used again. Then he went back to Jerusalem.
2Kings23:21 Then King Josiah gave a command to all the people. He said
2Kings23:22 The people had not celebrated a Passover like this since the days when the judges ruled Israel. None of the kings of Israel or the kings of Judah ever had such a big celebration for Passover.
2Kings23:23 They celebrated this Passover for the Lord in Jerusalem during Josiah's 18 year as king.
2Kings23:24 Josiah destroyed the mediums
2Kings23:25 There had never been a king like Josiah before. Josiah turned to the Lord with all his heart
2Kings23:26 But the Lord did not stop being angry with the people of Judah. He was still angry with them for everything that Manasseh had done.
2Kings23:27 The Lord said
2Kings23:28 All the other things that Josiah did are written in the book
2Kings23:29 While Josiah was king
2Kings23:30 Josiah's officers put his body in a chariot and carried him from Megiddo to Jerusalem. They buried Josiah in his own grave. Then the common people took Josiah's son Jehoahaz and anointed him. They made Jehoahaz the new king.
2Kings23:31 Jehoahaz was 23 years old when he became king. He ruled three months in Jerusalem. His mother's name was Hamutal
2Kings23:32 Jehoahaz did what the Lord said was wrong. He did all the same things that his ancestors had done.
2Kings23:33 Pharaoh Neco put Jehoahaz in prison at Riblah in the land of Hamath. So Jehoahaz could not rule in Jerusalem. Pharaoh Neco forced Judah to pay 7500 pounds of silver and 75 pounds of gold.
2Kings23:34 Pharaoh Neco made Josiah's son Eliakim the new king. Eliakim took the place of Josiah his father. Pharaoh Neco changed Eliakim's name to Jehoiakim. And Pharaoh Neco took Jehoahaz away to Egypt where he died.
2Kings23:35 Jehoiakim gave the silver and the gold to Pharaoh. But Jehoiakim made the common people pay taxes and used that money to give to Pharaoh Neco. So everyone paid their share of silver and gold
2Kings23:36 Jehoiakim was 25 years old when he became king. He ruled eleven years in Jerusalem. His mother's name was Zebidah daughter of Pedaiah from Rumah.
2Kings23:37 Jehoiakim did what the Lord said was wrong. He did all the same things his ancestors had done.
2Kings24:1 In the time of Jehoiakim
2Kings24:2 The Lord sent groups of Babylonians
2Kings24:3 The Lord commanded this to happen to Judah. In this way he would remove them from his sight. He did this because of all the sins that Manasseh committed.
2Kings24:4 He did this because Manasseh killed many innocent people and filled Jerusalem with their blood. The Lord would not forgive these sins.
2Kings24:5 The other things that Jehoiakim did are written in the book
2Kings24:6 Jehoiakim died and was buried with his ancestors. His son Jehoiachin became the new king after him.
2Kings24:7 The king of Babylon captured all the land between the Brook of Egypt and the Euphrates River. This land was previously controlled by Egypt. So the king of Egypt did not leave Egypt anymore.
2Kings24:8 Jehoiachin was 18 years old when he began to rule. He ruled three months in Jerusalem. His mother's name was Nehushta daughter of Elnathan from Jerusalem.
2Kings24:9 Jehoiachin did what the Lord said was wrong. He did all the same things that his father had done.
2Kings24:10 At that time the officers of King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon came to Jerusalem and surrounded it.
2Kings24:11 Then King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon came to the city.
2Kings24:12 King Jehoiachin of Judah went out to meet the king of Babylon. His mother
2Kings24:13 Nebuchadnezzar took from Jerusalem all the treasures in the Lord's Temple and all the treasures in the king's palace. He cut up all the golden dishes that King Solomon of Israel had put in the Lord's Temple. This happened just as the Lord had said.
2Kings24:14 Nebuchadnezzar captured all the people of Jerusalem
2Kings24:15 Nebuchadnezzar took Jehoiachin to Babylon as a prisoner. He also took the king's mother
2Kings24:16 There were 7000 soldiers. Nebuchadnezzar took all of the soldiers and 1000 of the skilled workers and craftsmen. All of these men were trained soldiers
2Kings24:17 The king of Babylon made Mattaniah the new king. Mattaniah was Jehoiachin's uncle. He changed his name to Zedekiah.
2Kings24:18 Zedekiah was 21 years old when he began to rule. He ruled 11 years in Jerusalem. His mother's name was Hamutal daughter of Jeremiah from Libnah.
2Kings24:19 Zedekiah did what the Lord said was wrong. He did all the same things that Jehoiakim did.
2Kings24:20 The Lord became so angry with Jerusalem and Judah that he threw them away. Zedekiah rebelled and refused to obey the king of Babylon.
2Kings25:1 So King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon and all his army came to fight against Jerusalem. This happened on the 10 day of the tenth month of Zedekiah's ninth year as king. Nebuchadnezzar put his army around Jerusalem to stop people from going in and out of the city. Then he built a wall of dirt around the city.
2Kings25:2 His army stayed around Jerusalem until Zedekiah's eleventh year as king of Judah.
2Kings25:3 The famine was getting worse and worse in the city. By the 9 day of the fourth month there was no more food for the common people in the city.
2Kings25:4 Nebuchadnezzar's army finally broke through the city wall. That night King Zedekiah and all his soldiers ran away. They used the secret gate that went through the double walls. It was by the king's garden. The enemy soldiers were all around the city
2Kings25:5 The Babylonian army chased King Zedekiah and caught him near Jericho. All of Zedekiah's soldiers left him and ran away.
2Kings25:6 The Babylonians took King Zedekiah to the king of Babylon at Riblah. The Babylonians decided to punish Zedekiah.
2Kings25:7 They killed Zedekiah's sons in front of him. Then they put out Zedekiah's eyes. They put chains on him and took him to Babylon.
2Kings25:8 Nebuchadnezzar came to Jerusalem on the 7 day of the fifth month of his nineteenth year as king of Babylon. The captain of Nebuchadnezzar's best soldiers was Nebuzaradan.
2Kings25:9 Nebuzaradan burned the Lord's Temple
2Kings25:10 Then the Babylonian army that was with Nebuzaradan pulled down the walls around Jerusalem.
2Kings25:11 Nebuzaradan captured all the people who were still left in the city. He took all the people as prisoners
2Kings25:12 He let only the poorest of the common people stay there. He let them stay so that they could take care of the grapes and other crops.
2Kings25:13 The Babylonian soldiers broke into pieces all the bronze things in the Lord's Temple. They broke the bronze columns
2Kings25:14 The Babylonians also took the pots
2Kings25:15 Nebuzaradan took all the firepans and bowls. He took all the things made of gold for the gold. And he took everything made of silver for the silver.
2Kings25:16 So Nebuzaradan took the large bronze tank and the 2 bronze columns. (Each column was about 31 feet tall. The capitals on the columns were over 5 feet tall. They were made from bronze and had a design like a net and pomegranates. Both columns had the same kind of design.) He also took the carts that Solomon made for the Lord's Temple. The bronze from these things was too heavy to be weighed.
2Kings25:17 ***
2Kings25:18 {From the Temple} Nebuzaradan took Seraiah the high priest
2Kings25:19 From the city Nebuzaradan took one official who was in charge of the army and five of the king's advisors who were still in the city. He took one secretary of the commander of the army who was in charge of counting the common people and choosing some of them to be soldiers and 60 people who just happened to be in the city.
2Kings25:20 Then Nebuzaradan took all these people to the king of Babylon at Riblah in the area of Hamath. The king of Babylon killed them there at Riblah. And the people of Judah were led away as prisoners from their land.
2Kings25:21 ***
2Kings25:22 King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon left some people in the land of Judah. There was a man named Gedaliah son of Ahikam son of Shaphan. Nebuchadnezzar made Gedaliah governor over the people in Judah.
2Kings25:23 The army captains were Ishmael son of Nethaniah
2Kings25:24 Gedaliah made promises to these officers and their men. He said to them
2Kings25:25 Ishmael son of Nethaniah son of Elishama was from the king's family. In the seventh month Ishmael and ten of his men attacked Gedaliah and killed all the men of Judah and Babylonians who were with Gedaliah at Mizpah.
2Kings25:26 Then the army officers and all the people ran away to Egypt. Everyone
2Kings25:27 Later
2Kings25:28 Evil Merodach was kind to Jehoiachin. He gave Jehoiachin a more important place to sit than the other kings who were with him in Babylon.
2Kings25:29 Evil Merodach let Jehoiachin stop wearing prison clothes. And every day for the rest of his life Jehoiachin ate at the same table with the king.
2Kings25:30 And each day

1Chronicles10:2 The Philistines continued chasing Saul and his sons. They caught them and killed them. The Philistines killed Saul's sons Jonathan
1Chronicles10:3 The fighting was heavy around Saul. The archers shot Saul with their arrows and wounded him.
1Chronicles10:4 Then Saul said to his armor bearer
1Chronicles10:5 When the armor bearer saw that Saul was dead
1Chronicles10:6 So Saul and three of his sons died. All of Saul's family died together.
1Chronicles10:7 When all the Israelites living in the valley saw that their own army had run away and that Saul and his sons were dead
1Chronicles10:8 The next day
1Chronicles10:9 The Philistines took things from Saul's body. They took Saul's head and armor. They sent messengers through all their country to tell the news to their false gods and to their people.
1Chronicles10:10 The Philistines put Saul's armor in the temple of their false gods. They hung Saul's head in the temple of Dagon.
1Chronicles10:11 All the people living in the town of Jabesh Gilead heard everything that the Philistines had done to Saul.
1Chronicles10:12 All the brave men from Jabesh Gilead went to get the bodies of Saul and his sons. They brought them back to Jabesh Gilead. They buried the bones of Saul and his sons under the large tree in Jabesh. Then they showed their sadness and fasted for seven days.
1Chronicles10:13 Saul died because he was not faithful to the Lord. He did not obey the Lord's word. Saul also went to a medium and asked her for advice
1Chronicles10:14 instead of asking the Lord. That is why the Lord killed Saul and gave the kingdom to Jesse's son David.

1Chronicles12:1 This is a list of the men who came to David while he was at Ziklag. This was when David was hiding from Saul son of Kish. These men helped David in battle.
1Chronicles12:2 They could shoot arrows from their bows with either their right or left hand. They could also throw stones from their slings with either their right or left hand. They were Saul's relatives from the tribe of Benjamin. They were
1Chronicles12:3 Ahiezer

1Chronicles12:7 and Joelah and Zebadiah
1Chronicles12:8 Part of the tribe of Gad joined David at his fortress in the desert. They were brave soldiers trained for war and skilled with the shield and spear. They looked as fierce as lions
1Chronicles12:9 Ezer was the leader of the army from the tribe of Gad. Obadiah was the second in command. Eliab was the third in command.
1Chronicles12:10 Mishmannah was the fourth in command. Jeremiah was the fifth in command.
1Chronicles12:11 Attai was the sixth in command. Eliel was the seventh in command.
1Chronicles12:12 Johanan was the eighth in command. Elzabad was the ninth in command.
1Chronicles12:13 Jeremiah was the tenth in command. Macbannai was the eleventh in command.
1Chronicles12:14 These men were leaders of the Gadite army. The weakest from that group was worth 100 men
1Chronicles12:15 They were the soldiers who crossed the Jordan River in the first month of the year
1Chronicles12:16 Other men from the tribes of Benjamin and Judah also came to David at the fortress.
1Chronicles12:17 David went out to meet them and said
1Chronicles12:18 Amasai was the leader of the Thirty Heroes. Then the Spirit came on Amasai

1Chronicles12:20 These were the men from Manasseh who joined David when he went to the town of Ziklag: Adnah
1Chronicles12:21 They helped David fight against bad men who were going around the country and stealing things from people. All these men of Manasseh were brave soldiers. They became leaders in David's army.
1Chronicles12:22 More and more men came every day to help David. So he had a large and powerful army.
1Chronicles12:23 These are the numbers of the men who came to David at the town of Hebron. These men were ready for war. They came to give Saul's kingdom to David. That is what the Lord said would happen. This is their number:
1Chronicles12:24 From the tribe of Judah there were 6800 men ready for war. They carried shields and spears.
1Chronicles12:25 From the tribe of Simeon there were 7100 men. They were brave soldiers ready for war.
1Chronicles12:26 From the tribe of Levi there were 4600 men.
1Chronicles12:27 Jehoiada was in that group. He was a leader from Aaron's family. There were 3700 men with Jehoiada.
1Chronicles12:28 Zadok was also in that group. He was a brave young soldier. He came with 22 officers from his family.
1Chronicles12:29 From the tribe of Benjamin there were 3000 men. They were Saul's relatives. Most of them stayed faithful to Saul's family until that time.
1Chronicles12:30 From the tribe of Ephraim there were 20
1Chronicles12:31 From half the tribe of Manasseh there were 18
1Chronicles12:32 From the family of Issachar there were 200 wise leaders. These men understood the right thing for Israel to do at the right time. Their relatives were with them and under their command.

1Chronicles23:2 David gathered all the leaders of Israel and also the priests and Levites.

1Chronicles23:6 David separated the Levites into three groups. They were the tribes of Levi's three sons
1Chronicles23:7 From the tribe of Gershon there were Ladan and Shimei.
1Chronicles23:8 Ladan had three sons. His oldest son was Jehiel. His other sons were Zethan and Joel.
1Chronicles23:9 Shimei's sons were Shelomoth
1Chronicles23:10 Shimei had four sons. They were Jahath
1Chronicles23:11 Jahath was the oldest son and Ziza was the second son. But Jeush and Beriah did not have many children. So Jeush and Beriah were counted like one family.
1Chronicles23:12 Kohath had four sons. They were Amram
1Chronicles23:13 Amram's sons were Aaron and Moses. Aaron was chosen to be very special. Aaron and his descendants were chosen to be special forever. They were chosen to prepare the holy things for the Lord's service. Aaron and his descendants were chosen to burn the incense before the Lord. They were chosen to serve the Lord as priests. They were chosen to use the Lord's name and give blessings to the people forever.
1Chronicles23:14 Moses was the man of God
1Chronicles23:15 Moses' sons were Gershom and Eliezer.
1Chronicles23:16 Gershom's oldest son was Shubael.
1Chronicles23:17 Eliezer's oldest son was Rehabiah. Eliezer had no other sons. But Rehabiah had very many sons.
1Chronicles23:18 Izhar's oldest son was Shelomith.
1Chronicles23:19 Hebron's oldest son was Jeriah. Hebron's second son was Amariah. Jahaziel was the third son
1Chronicles23:20 Uzziel's oldest son was Micah
1Chronicles23:21 Merari's sons were Mahli and Mushi. Mahli's sons were Eleazar and Kish.
1Chronicles23:22 Eleazar died without having sons. He only had daughters. Eleazar's daughters married their own relatives. Their relatives were Kish's sons.
1Chronicles23:23 Mushi's sons were Mahli
1Chronicles23:24 These were Levi's descendants. They were listed by their families. They were the leaders of families. Each person's name was listed. The people who were listed were 20 years old or older. They served in the Lord's Temple.
1Chronicles23:25 David had said
1Chronicles23:26 So the Levites don't need to carry the Holy Tent or any of the things used in its services anymore.
1Chronicles23:27 David's last instructions for the Israelites were to count the descendants from the tribe of Levi. They counted the Levite men who were 20 years old and older.
1Chronicles23:28 The Levites had the job of helping Aaron's descendants in the service of the Lord's Temple. They also cared for the Temple yard and the side rooms in the Temple. They had the job of making all holy things pure. It was their job to serve in God's Temple.
1Chronicles23:29 They were responsible for putting the special bread on the table in the Temple and for the flour

2Chronicles3:1 Solomon began building the Lord's Temple at Jerusalem on Mount Moriah
2Chronicles3:2 Solomon started the work in the second month of his fourth year as king of Israel.
2Chronicles3:3 These are the measurements he used for building the foundation of God's Temple
2Chronicles3:4 The porch in front of the Temple was 20 cubits long and 20 cubits high. He covered the inside of the porch with pure gold.
2Chronicles3:5 He put panels made of cypress wood on the walls of the larger room. Then he put pure gold over the cypress panels and then put pictures of palm trees and chains on the gold.
2Chronicles3:6 He put valuable stones in the Temple for beauty. The gold he used was gold from Parvaim.
2Chronicles3:7 He covered the inside of the Temple with the gold. He put the gold on the ceiling beams
2Chronicles3:8 Then he made the Most Holy Place. This room was 20 cubits long and 20 cubits wide. It was as wide as the Temple was. He put pure gold on the walls of the Most Holy Place. The gold weighed about 22?tons.
2Chronicles3:9 The gold nails weighed 1 pounds. He covered the upper rooms with gold.
2Chronicles3:10 He made two Cherub angels to put in the Most Holy Place. The workers covered the Cherub angels with gold.
2Chronicles3:11 Each wing of the Cherub angels was 5 cubits long. The total length of the wings was 20 cubits. One wing of the first Cherub angel touched the wall on one side of the room. The other wing touched one wing of the second Cherub angel.
2Chronicles3:12 And the other wing of the second Cherub angel touched the other wall on the other side of the room.
2Chronicles3:13 The wings of the Cherub angels covered a total of 20 cubits. The Cherub angels stood facing the Holy Place.
2Chronicles3:14 He made the curtain from blue
2Chronicles3:15 He put two columns in front of the Temple. The columns were 35 cubits tall. The top part of the two columns was 5 cubits long.
2Chronicles3:16 He made chains in a necklace and put them on the tops of the columns. He made 100 pomegranates and put them on the chains.
2Chronicles3:17 Then he put the columns up in front of the Temple. One column stood on the right side. The other column stood on the left side. He named the column on the right side Jakin. And he named the column on the left side Boaz.
2Chronicles4:1 He made a bronze altar that was 20 cubits long
2Chronicles4:2 Then he made a large tank from melted bronze. It was round and it measured 10 cubits across from edge to edge. It was 5 cubits tall and about 30 cubits around.
2Chronicles4:3 There were images of bulls under the lip of the large bronze tank. They were in two rows that went 10 cubits around the tank. The bulls were molded in place when the tank was shaped.
2Chronicles4:4 The large bronze tank was on top of twelve large statues of bulls. Three bulls looked toward the north. Three bulls looked toward the west. Three bulls looked toward the south. Three bulls looked toward the east. The large bronze tank was on top of these bulls. All the bulls stood with their rear ends to each other and to the center.
2Chronicles4:5 The large bronze tank was 3 inches thick. The edge of the large tank was like the edge of a cup. The edge looked like a lily blossom. The tank could hold about 17
2Chronicles4:6 He made ten basins. He put five basins on the right side of the large bronze tank and five basins on the left side. These ten basins were to be used to wash the things offered for the burnt offerings. But the large bronze tank was to be used by the priests for washing before they offered sacrifices.
2Chronicles4:7 He made ten lampstands of gold. He followed the plans made for these lampstands. He put the lampstands in the Temple. There were five lampstands on the right side and five lampstands on the left side.
2Chronicles4:8 He made ten tables and put them in the Temple. Five tables were on the right side and five tables on the left side in the Temple. And he used gold to make 100 basins.
2Chronicles4:9 He also made the Priests' yard
2Chronicles4:10 Then he put the large bronze tank on the right side of the Temple on the southeast side.
2Chronicles4:11 Huram made the pots
2Chronicles4:12 Huram had made the two columns and the large bowls on the top parts of the two columns. He also made the two net decorations to cover the two large bowls on the top parts of the two columns.
2Chronicles4:13 Huram made 400 pomegranates for the two net decorations. There were two rows of pomegranates for each net. The nets covered the large bowls on the top parts on the two columns.
2Chronicles4:14 He also made the stands and the bowls on the stands.
2Chronicles4:15 He made the one large bronze tank and twelve bulls under the tank.
2Chronicles4:16 Huram made the pots
2Chronicles4:17 King Solomon first poured these things in clay molds. The molds were made in the Jordan Valley between the towns of Succoth and Zeredah.
2Chronicles4:18 Solomon made so many of them that no one tried to weigh the bronze used.
2Chronicles4:19 Solomon also made the dishes and things for God's Temple. He made the golden altar and the tables where they put the bread of the Presence.
2Chronicles4:20 He made the lamps and lampstands of pure gold. These were put inside the Holy Place in front of the Most Holy Place.

2Chronicles4:22 He used pure gold to make the trimmers

2Chronicles9:4 She saw the food at the king's table. She saw his officials meeting together. She saw the servants in the palace and the good clothes they wore. She saw his parties and the sacrifices that he offered in the Temple. She was so amazed that it took her breath away!

2Chronicles9:11 King Solomon used this special wood to make steps for the Lord's Temple and the king's palace. Solomon also used the algum wood to make lyres and harps for the singers. No one ever saw such beautiful things like those made from the algum wood in the country of Judah.

2Chronicles9:15 King Solomon made 200 large shields from hammered gold. Each shield contained about 15 pounds of gold.
2Chronicles9:16 He also made 300 small shields of hammered gold. Each shield contained almost 4 pounds of gold. The king put them in the building called the Forest of Lebanon.
2Chronicles9:17 King Solomon also built a large throne with ivory decorations. It was covered with pure gold.
2Chronicles9:18 There were six steps leading up to the throne. The back of the throne was round at the top. There were armrests on both sides of the throne
2Chronicles9:19 There were also two lions on each of the six steps
2Chronicles9:20 All of Solomon's cups and glasses were made of gold. And all of the dishes in the building called the Forest of Lebanon were made from pure gold. Nothing in the palace was made from silver. There was so much gold that in Solomon's time people did not think silver was important!
2Chronicles9:21 The king also had cargo ships that went to Tarshish to trade things with other countries. Hiram's men were on these ships. Every three years the ships would come back with a new load of gold
2Chronicles9:22 King Solomon became greater in riches and wisdom than any other king on earth.

2Chronicles33:1 Manasseh was twelve years old when he became king of Judah. He was king for 55 years in Jerusalem.
2Chronicles33:2 Manasseh did what the Lord said was wrong. He followed the terrible and sinful ways of the nations that the Lord had forced out of the land before the Israelites.
2Chronicles33:3 Manasseh rebuilt the high places that his father Hezekiah had broken down. Manasseh built altars for the Baal gods and made Asherah poles. He bowed down to the constellations and worshiped those groups of stars.
2Chronicles33:4 Manasseh built altars for false gods in the Lord's Temple. The Lord said about the Temple
2Chronicles33:5 He built altars for all the groups of stars in the two yards of the Lord's Temple.
2Chronicles33:6 He also burned his own children for a sacrifice in the Valley of Ben Hinnom. He also used magic by doing soothsaying
2Chronicles33:7 Manasseh also made a statue of an idol and put it in God's Temple—the very same Temple that God had talked about to David and his son Solomon. God had said
2Chronicles33:8 I will not continue to keep the Israelites off the land that I chose to give to their ancestors. But they must obey everything I commanded them. The Israelites must obey all the laws
2Chronicles33:9 Manasseh encouraged the people of Judah and the people living in Jerusalem to do wrong. They were worse than the nations that were in the land before the Israelites—and the Lord destroyed those people.

2Chronicles33:11 So the Lord brought commanders from the king of Assyria's army to attack Judah. These commanders captured Manasseh and made him their prisoner. They put hooks in him and brass chains on his hands and took him to the country of Babylon.

2Chronicles33:22 Amon did evil before the Lord
2Chronicles33:23 Amon did not humble himself in front of the Lord like Manasseh his father humbled himself. But Amon sinned more and more.

2Chronicles34:1 Josiah was eight years old when he became king. He was king for 31 years in Jerusalem.

He broke the idols that were carved and the idols that were made from molds. He beat the idols into powder and sprinkled the powder on the graves of the people who had offered sacrifices to the Baal gods.
2Chronicles34:5 Josiah even burned the bones of the priests who had served the Baal gods on their own altars. This is how he destroyed idols and idol worship from Judah and Jerusalem.

2Chronicles34:7 Josiah broke down the altars and the Asherah poles. He beat the idols into powder. He cut down all the incense altars used for Baal worship in all the country of Israel. Then he went back to Jerusalem.

Ezra4:1 Many people living in the area were against the people of Judah and Benjamin. These enemies heard that the people who had come from captivity were building a temple for the Lord
Ezra4:2 ***
Ezra4:3 But Zerubbabel
Ezra4:4 So the enemies began to discourage them and tried to frighten them in order to stop them from building the Temple.
Ezra4:5 These enemies hired government officials to work against the people of Judah. The officials constantly did things to stop the Jews' plans to build the Temple. This continued the whole time that Cyrus was the king until Darius became the king of Persia.
Ezra4:6 These enemies even wrote letters to the king of Persia trying to stop the Jews. They wrote a letter the year that Xerxes became the king of Persia.
Ezra4:7 Later
Ezra4:8 Then Rehum the commanding officer and Shimshai the secretary wrote a letter against the people of Jerusalem. They wrote the letter to Artaxerxes the king. This is what they wrote:
Ezra4:9 From Rehum the commanding officer and Shimshai the secretary
Ezra4:10 and from the other people who the great and powerful Ashurbanipal moved to the city of Samaria and other places in the country west of the Euphrates River.

Ezra4:12 King Artaxerxes

Ezra4:15 King Artaxerxes
Ezra4:16 King Artaxerxes

Ezra4:19 I gave an order for the writings of the kings before me to be searched. The writings were read
Ezra4:20 Jerusalem has had powerful kings ruling over it and over the whole area west of the Euphrates River. Their kings received taxes
Ezra4:21 Now
Ezra4:22 Be careful not to overlook this matter. We should not let the building of Jerusalem continue. If that work continues
Ezra4:23 So a copy of the letter that King Artaxerxes sent was read to Rehum
Ezra4:24 So the work stopped on God's Temple in Jerusalem. The work did not continue until the second year that Darius was king of Persia.

Ezra6:1 So King Darius gave an order to search the writings of the kings before him. The writings were kept in Babylon in the same place the money was kept.
Ezra6:2 A scroll was found in the fortress of Ecbatana. (Ecbatana is in the province of Media.) This is what was written on that scroll: Official Note:
Ezra6:3 During the first year that Cyrus was king
Ezra6:4 Its wall will be in layers that have three rows of large stones and one row of wooden timbers. The cost of building the Temple must be paid for from the king's treasury.
Ezra6:5 Also
Ezra6:6 Now then
Ezra6:7 Don't bother the workers. Don't try to stop the work on this Temple of God. Let the Jewish governor and the Jewish leaders rebuild it. Let them rebuild God's Temple in the same place it was in the past.
Ezra6:8 Now I give this order. You must do this for the Jewish leaders building God's Temple: The cost of the building must be fully paid from the king's treasury. The money will come from the taxes collected from the provinces in the area west of the Euphrates River. Do these things quickly
Ezra6:9 Give them anything they need. If they need young bulls
Ezra6:10 Give them to the Jewish priests so that they may offer sacrifices that please the God of Heaven. Give these things so that the priests may pray for me and my sons.
Ezra6:11 Also
Ezra6:12 God put his name there in Jerusalem. May God defeat any king or other person who tries to change this order. If anyone tries to destroy this Temple in Jerusalem
Ezra6:13 So Tattenai the governor of the area west of the Euphrates River
Ezra6:14 So the Jewish leaders continued to build. They were successful as Haggai the prophet and Zechariah son of Iddo encouraged them. They finished building the Temple. This was done to obey the command of the God of Israel. It was also done to obey the orders of Cyrus
Ezra6:15 The Temple was finished on the third day of the month of Adar. That was in the sixth year of the rule of King Darius.
Ezra6:16 Then the Israelites celebrated the dedication of God's Temple with much happiness. The priests
Ezra6:17 This is the way they dedicated God's Temple: They offered 100 bulls
Ezra6:18 Then they chose the priests in their groups and the Levites in their groups to serve in God's Temple in Jerusalem. They did these things as it is written in the Book of Moses.
Ezra6:19 On the fourteenth day of the first month
Ezra6:20 The priests and Levites made themselves pure. They all made themselves clean and ready to celebrate the Passover. The Levites killed the Passover lamb for all the Jews who came back from captivity. They did that for their brothers the priests
Ezra6:21 So all the Israelites who came back from captivity ate the Passover meal. Other people washed themselves and made themselves pure from the unclean things of the people living in that country. These pure people also shared in the Passover meal. They did this so that they could go to the Lord
Ezra6:22 They celebrated the Festival of Unleavened Bread with much joy for seven days. The Lord made them very happy because he had changed the attitude of the king of Assyria. So the king of Assyria had helped them do the work on God's Temple.
Ezra7:1 After these things
Ezra7:2 Hilkiah was the son of Shallum. Shallum was the son of Zadok. Zadok was the son of Ahitub.
Ezra7:3 Ahitub was the son of Amariah. Amariah was the son of Azariah. Azariah was the son of Meraioth.
Ezra7:4 Meraioth was the son of Zerahiah. Zerahiah was the son of Uzzi. Uzzi was the son of Bukki.
Ezra7:5 Bukki was the son of Abishua. Abishua was the son of Phinehas. Phinehas was the son of Eleazar. Eleazar was the son of Aaron the high priest.
Ezra7:6 Ezra came to Jerusalem from Babylon. He was a teacher and knew the Law of Moses very well. The Law of Moses was given by the Lord
Ezra7:7 Among the people who came with Ezra were Israelites
Ezra7:8 Ezra arrived in Jerusalem in the fifth month of the seventh year that Artaxerxes was king.
Ezra7:9 Ezra left Babylon on the first day of the first month and arrived in Jerusalem on the first day of the fifth month. The Lord God was with Ezra.
Ezra7:10 Ezra gave all his time and attention to studying and obeying the law of the Lord. He wanted to teach the rules and commandments of the Lord to the Israelites. And he also wanted to help the people follow these laws in Israel.
Ezra7:11 Ezra was a priest and teacher. He knew much about the commands and laws the Lord gave Israel. This is a copy of the letter King Artaxerxes gave to Ezra the teacher:
Ezra7:12 From King Artaxerxes. To Ezra the priest
Ezra7:13 I give this order: Any of the Israelites living in my kingdom
Ezra7:14 I and my seven advisors send you to Judah and Jerusalem. Go and see how your people are doing in obeying the law of your God. You have that law with you.
Ezra7:15 I and my advisors are giving gold and silver to the God of Israel. God lives in Jerusalem. You must take this gold and silver with you.
Ezra7:16 You must also go through all the provinces of Babylonia. Collect the gifts from your people
Ezra7:17 Use this money to buy bulls
Ezra7:18 Then you and the other Jews may spend the silver and gold left over any way you want to. Use it in a way that is pleasing to your God.
Ezra7:19 Take all these things to the God of Jerusalem. They are for the worship in the Temple of your God.
Ezra7:20 And you may get any other things that you need for the Temple of your God. Use the money in the king's treasury to buy anything you need.
Ezra7:21 Now I

Ezra7:23 Anything that the God of Heaven has ordered for Ezra to get

Ezra7:25 Ezra
Ezra7:26 Anyone who does not obey the law of your God
Ezra7:27 Blessed is the Lord
Ezra7:28 The Lord showed his faithful love to me in front of the king

Ezra8:13 from the last of the descendants of Adonikam: Eliphelet
Ezra8:14 from the descendants of Bigvai: Uthai

Ezra8:17 I sent the men to Iddo
Ezra8:18 Because God was with us

Ezra8:21 There near the Ahava River
Ezra8:22 I was embarrassed to ask King Artaxerxes for soldiers and horsemen to protect us as we traveled. There were enemies on the road. The reason I was embarrassed to ask for protection was because of what we had told the king. We had said to King Artaxerxes
Ezra8:23 So we fasted and prayed to our God about our trip. He answered our prayers.
Ezra8:24 Then I chose twelve of the priests who were leaders. I chose Sherebiah
Ezra8:25 I weighed the silver
Ezra8:26 I weighed all these things. There were 25 tons of silver. There were also 7500 pounds of silver dishes and things. There were 3?tons of gold.
Ezra8:27 And I gave them 20 gold bowls. The bowls weighed about 19 pounds. And I gave them two beautiful dishes made from polished bronze that were as valuable as gold.
Ezra8:28 Then I said to the twelve priests: You and these things are holy to the Lord. People gave this silver and gold to the Lord
Ezra8:29 So guard these things carefully. You are responsible for them until you give them to the Temple leaders in Jerusalem. You will give them to the leading Levites and the family leaders of Israel. They will weigh them and put them in the rooms of the Lord's Temple in Jerusalem.
Ezra8:30 So the priests and Levites accepted the silver
Ezra8:31 On the twelfth day of the first month we left the Ahava River and started toward Jerusalem. God was with us
Ezra8:32 Then we arrived in Jerusalem. We rested there for three days.
Ezra8:33 On the fourth day
Ezra8:34 We counted and weighed everything and we wrote down the total weight.

Ezra8:36 Then the people gave the letter from King Artaxerxes to the royal satraps and to the governors of the area west of the Euphrates River. Then the leaders gave their support to the Israelites and to the Temple.

Nehemiah1:1 These are the words of Nehemiah son of Hacaliah: I
Nehemiah1:2 While I was in Susa
Nehemiah1:3 They answered
Nehemiah1:4 When I heard this about the people of Jerusalem and about the wall
Nehemiah1:5 Then I prayed this prayer: Lord
Nehemiah1:6 Please open your eyes and ears and listen to the prayer your servant is praying before you day and night. I am praying for your servants
Nehemiah1:7 We Israelites have been very bad to you. We have not obeyed the commands
Nehemiah1:8 Please remember the teaching you gave your servant Moses. You said to him
Nehemiah1:9 But if you Israelites come back to me and obey my commands
Nehemiah1:10 The Israelites are your servants and your people. You used your great power and rescued them.
Nehemiah1:11 So
Nehemiah2:1 In the month of Nisan in the 20 year of King Artaxerxes
Nehemiah2:2 So the king asked me
Nehemiah2:3 But even though I was afraid
Nehemiah2:4 Then the king said to me
Nehemiah2:5 Then I answered the king
Nehemiah2:6 The king and the queen who was sitting next to him asked me
Nehemiah2:7 I also said to the king
Nehemiah2:8 I also need lumber for the heavy wooden beams for the gates
Nehemiah2:9 So I went to the governors of the area west of the Euphrates River and gave them the letters from the king. The king had also sent army officers and soldiers on horses with me.
Nehemiah2:10 Sanballat from Horon and Tobiah the Ammonite official heard about what I was doing. They were very upset and angry that someone had come to help the Israelites.
Nehemiah2:11 I went to Jerusalem and stayed there three days. Then at night I started out with a few men. I had not said anything to anyone about what God had put on my heart to do for Jerusalem. There were no horses with me except the horse I was riding.
Nehemiah2:12 ***
Nehemiah2:13 While it was dark I went out through the Valley Gate. I rode toward the Dragon Well and the Gate of the Ash Piles. I was inspecting the walls of Jerusalem that had been broken down and the gates in the wall that had been burned with fire.
Nehemiah2:14 Then I rode on toward the Fountain Gate and the King's Pool. As I got close
Nehemiah2:15 So I went up the valley in the dark
Nehemiah2:16 The officials and important Israelites didn't know where I had gone. They didn't know what I was doing. I had not yet said anything to the Jews
Nehemiah2:17 Then I said to them
Nehemiah2:18 I also told them that God had been kind to me. I told them what the king had said to me. Then they answered
Nehemiah2:19 But Sanballat from Horon
Nehemiah2:20 But this is what I said to them: The God of Heaven will help us succeed. We are God's servants and we will rebuild this city. You cannot help us in this work because none of your family lived here in Jerusalem. You don't own any of this land
Nehemiah3:1 The name of the high priest was Eliashib. He and his brothers the priests went to work and built the Sheep Gate. They prayed and made that gate holy to the Lord. They set its doors in place in the wall. The priests worked on the wall of Jerusalem as far as the Tower of the Hundred and the Tower of Hananel. They prayed and made their work holy to the Lord.
Nehemiah3:2 The men from Jericho built the wall next to the priests. And Zaccur son of a man named Imri built the wall next to the men of Jericho.
Nehemiah3:3 The sons of a man named Hassenaah built the Fish Gate. They set the beams in place. They put doors on the building. Then they put the locks and bolts on the doors.
Nehemiah3:4 Meremoth son of Uriah repaired the next section of the wall. (Uriah was the son of Hakkoz.) Meshullam son of Berekiah repaired the next section of the wall. (Berekiah was the son of Meshezabel.) Zadok son of Baana repaired the next section of the wall.
Nehemiah3:5 The men from Tekoa repaired the next section of the wall
Nehemiah3:6 Joiada and Meshullam fixed the Old Gate. Joiada is the son of Paseah and Meshullam is the son of Besodeiah. They set the beams in place. They put the doors on the hinges. Then they put the locks and bolts on the doors.
Nehemiah3:7 The men from Gibeon and Mizpah fixed the next section of the wall. Melatiah from Gibeon and Jadon from Meronoth did the work. Gibeon and Meronoth are places that are controlled by the governors of the area west of the Euphrates River.
Nehemiah3:8 Uzziel son of Harhaiah repaired the next section of the wall. Uzziel was a goldsmith. Hananiah was one of the perfume makers. These men built and repaired Jerusalem as far as the Broad Wall.
Nehemiah3:9 Rephaiah son of Hur repaired the next section of the wall. Rephaiah was the governor of half of Jerusalem.
Nehemiah3:10 Jedaiah son of Harumaph repaired the next section of the wall. Jedaiah repaired the wall next to his own house. Hattush son of Hashabneiah repaired the next section.
Nehemiah3:11 Malkijah son of Harim and Hasshub son of Pahath-Moab repaired the next section. They also repaired Oven Tower.
Nehemiah3:12 Shallum son of Hallohesh repaired the next section of the wall. His daughters helped him. Shallum was the governor of the other half of Jerusalem.
Nehemiah3:13 The Valley Gate was repaired by Hanun and the people who live in the town of Zanoah. They built the Valley Gate. They put the doors on their hinges. Then they put the locks and bolts on the doors. They also repaired 500 yards of the wall. They worked on the wall all the way to the Gate of Ash Piles.
Nehemiah3:14 Malkijah son of Recab repaired the Gate of Ash Piles. Malkijah was the governor of the district of Beth Hakkerem. He repaired the gate. He put the doors on the hinges. Then he put the locks and bolts on the doors.
Nehemiah3:15 Shallun son of Col-Hozeh repaired the Fountain Gate. Shallun was the governor of the district of Mizpah. He repaired the gate and put a roof over it. He put the doors on the hinges. Then he put the locks and bolts on the doors. He also repaired the wall of the Pool of Siloam that is next to the King's Garden. He repaired the wall all the way to the steps that go down from the City of David.
Nehemiah3:16 Nehemiah son of Azbuk repaired the next section. This Nehemiah was the governor of half the district of Beth Zur. He made repairs up to a place that is across from the tombs of David. And he worked as far as the man-made pool and the House of Heroes.
Nehemiah3:17 The men from the tribe of Levi repaired the next section. They worked under Rehum son of Bani. Hashabiah repaired the next section. Hashabiah was governor of half the district of Keilah. He made repairs for his own district.
Nehemiah3:18 Their brothers repaired the next section. They worked under Binnui son of Henadad. Binnui was the governor of the other half of the district of Keilah.
Nehemiah3:19 Ezer son of Jeshua repaired the next section. Ezer was governor of Mizpah. He repaired the section of wall from the room for weapons to the corner of the wall.
Nehemiah3:20 Baruch son of Zabbai repaired the next section. Baruch worked very hard and repaired the section of wall from the corner to the entrance to the house of Eliashib the high priest.
Nehemiah3:21 Meremoth son of Uriah
Nehemiah3:22 The next section of walls was repaired by the priests who lived in that area.
Nehemiah3:23 Benjamin and Hasshub repaired the wall in front of their own house. And Azariah son of Maaseiah
Nehemiah3:24 Binnui son of Henadad repaired the section of wall from Azariah's house to the bend in the wall and then to the corner.
Nehemiah3:25 Palal son of Uzai worked across from the bend in the wall near the tower. This is the tower at the king's upper house. That is near the courtyard of the king's guard. Pedaiah son of Parosh worked next to Palal.
Nehemiah3:26 The Temple servants lived on Ophel Hill. They repaired the next section all the way to the east side of the Water Gate and the tower near it.
Nehemiah3:27 The men from Tekoa repaired the rest of that section from the big tower all the way to the Ophel wall.
Nehemiah3:28 The priests repaired the section over the Horse Gate. Each priest repaired the wall in front of his own house.
Nehemiah3:29 Zadok son of Immer repaired the section in front of his house. Shemaiah son of Shecaniah repaired the next section. Shemaiah was the guard of the East Gate.
Nehemiah3:30 Hananiah son of Shelemiah and Hanun son of Zalaph repaired the rest of that section of wall. (Hanun was Zalaph's sixth son.) Meshullam son of Berekiah repaired the section in front of his house.
Nehemiah3:31 Malkijah repaired the next section of wall all the way to the houses of the Temple servants and the merchants. That is across from the Inspection Gate. Malkijah repaired the section all the way to the room over the corner of the wall. Malkijah was a goldsmith.
Nehemiah3:32 The goldsmiths and the merchants repaired the section of wall between the room over the corner to the Sheep Gate.
Nehemiah4:1 When Sanballat heard that we were building the wall of Jerusalem
Nehemiah4:2 Sanballat talked with his friends and the army at Samaria and said
Nehemiah4:3 Tobiah the Ammonite was with Sanballat. Tobiah said
Nehemiah4:4 Nehemiah prayed and said

Nehemiah9:6 You are God. Lord
Nehemiah9:7 You are the Lord God. You chose Abram. You led him from Ur in Babylonia. You changed his name to Abraham.
Nehemiah9:8 You saw he was true and loyal to you
Nehemiah9:9 You saw our ancestors suffering in Egypt and heard them call for help by the Red Sea.
Nehemiah9:10 You showed the miracles to Pharaoh. You did amazing things to his officials and his people. You knew that the Egyptians thought they were better than our ancestors. But you proved how great you are
Nehemiah9:11 You split the Red Sea in front of them
Nehemiah9:12 With the tall cloud
Nehemiah9:13 Then you came down to Mount Sinai. You spoke to them from heaven. You gave them good laws. You gave them true teachings. You gave them laws and commands that were very good.
Nehemiah9:14 You told them about your special day of rest—the Sabbath. Through your servant Moses
Nehemiah9:15 They were hungry
Nehemiah9:16 But our ancestors became proud and stubborn. They refused to obey your commands.
Nehemiah9:17 They refused to listen. They forgot the amazing things you did with them. They became stubborn. They decided to return to Egypt and become slaves again. But you are a forgiving God! You are kind and full of mercy. You are patient and full of love. So you didn't leave them!
Nehemiah9:18 You didn't leave them even when they made golden calves and said
Nehemiah9:19 You are very kind
Nehemiah9:20 You gave them your good Spirit to make them wise. You gave them manna for food. You gave them water for their thirst.
Nehemiah9:21 You took care of them for 40 years. They had all they needed in the desert. Their clothes didn't wear out
Nehemiah9:22 You gave them kingdoms and nations
Nehemiah9:23 You made their descendants as many as the stars in the sky. You brought them to the land you promised to give their ancestors. They went in and took that land.
Nehemiah9:24 Their children took the land. They defeated the Canaanites living there. You let them defeat those people. You let them do whatever they wanted to those nations
Nehemiah9:25 They defeated powerful cities. They took the fertile land. They got houses filled with good things. They got wells that were already dug. They got vineyards
Nehemiah9:26 And then they turned against you. They threw away your teachings. They killed your prophets. Those prophets warned the people. They tried to bring them back to you. But our ancestors said terrible things against you.
Nehemiah9:27 So you let their enemies have them. The enemy caused them much trouble. When trouble came
Nehemiah9:28 Then as soon as our ancestors were rested
Nehemiah9:29 You warned them. You told them to come back
Nehemiah9:30 You were very patient with our ancestors. You let them mistreat you for many years. You warned them with your Spirit. You sent the prophets to warn them. But our ancestors didn't listen. So you gave them to people in other countries.
Nehemiah9:31 But you are so kind! You didn't completely destroy them. You didn't leave them. You are such a kind and merciful God!
Nehemiah9:32 Our God
Nehemiah9:33 But God
Nehemiah9:34 Our kings
Nehemiah9:35 Our ancestors didn't serve you even when they were living in their own kingdom. They didn't stop doing evil. They enjoyed all the wonderful things you gave them. They enjoyed the rich land and had plenty of room
Nehemiah9:36 And now
Nehemiah9:37 The harvest is big in this land. But we sinned
Nehemiah9:38 Because of all of these things

Esther1:1 This is what happened during the time when Xerxes was king. Xerxes ruled over the 127 provinces from India to Ethiopia.

Esther2:1 Later
Esther2:2 Then the king's personal servants had a suggestion. They said
Esther2:3 Let the king choose leaders in every province of his kingdom. Then let the leaders bring every beautiful young virgin to the capital city of Susa. These young women will be put with the group of the king's women. They will be under the care of Hegai
Esther2:4 Then let the one who is pleasing to the king become the new queen in Vashti's place. The king liked this suggestion
Esther2:5 Now there was a Jew from the tribe of Benjamin named Mordecai. Mordecai was the son of Jair
Esther2:6 Mordecai had been carried into captivity from Jerusalem by King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon. He was with the group that was taken into captivity with King Jehoiachin of Judah.
Esther2:7 Mordecai had a cousin named Hadassah. She didn't have a father or a mother
Esther2:8 When the king's command had been heard
Esther2:9 He liked Esther. She became his favorite
Esther2:10 Esther didn't tell anyone she was a Jew. She didn't tell anyone about her family background
Esther2:11 Every day Mordecai walked back and forth near the area where the king's women lived. He did this because he wanted to find out how Esther was
Esther2:12 Before a young woman could take her turn to go in before King Xerxes
Esther2:13 When her time came to go in to the king
Esther2:14 In the evening the young woman would go to the king's palace. And in the morning she would return to another area where the king's women lived. Then she would be placed under the care of a man named Shaashgaz. He was the king's eunuch in charge of the slave women. She would not go back to the king again unless he was pleased with her. Then he would call her by name to come back to him.
Esther2:15 The time came for Esther to go in to the king. She was the one Mordecai had adopted
Esther2:16 So Esther was taken to King Xerxes in the palace. This happened in the tenth month
Esther2:17 The king loved Esther more than any of the other young women
Esther2:18 And the king gave a big party for Esther. It was for all his important people and leaders. He announced a festival in all the provinces and sent out gifts to people
Esther2:19 Mordecai was sitting next to the king's gate at the time the young women were gathered together the second time.
Esther2:20 Esther had still kept it a secret that she was a Jew. She had not told anyone about her family background. This is what Mordecai had told her to do. She still obeyed Mordecai just as she had done when he was taking care of her.
Esther2:21 During the time Mordecai was sitting next to the king's gate
Esther2:22 But Mordecai learned about these plans and told Queen Esther. Then she told the king. She also told him that Mordecai was the one who had learned about the evil plan.
Esther2:23 Then the report was checked out. It was learned that Mordecai's report was true. The two guards who had planned to kill the king were hanged on a post. All these things were written down in a book of the king's histories in front of the king.

Esther8:8 Now write another command by the authority of the king. Write it to help the Jews in a way that seems best to you. Then seal the order with the king's special ring. No official letter written by the authority of the king and sealed with the king's ring can be canceled.
Esther8:9 Very quickly the king's secretaries were called. This was done on the 23 day of the third month
Esther8:10 Mordecai wrote commands by the authority of King Xerxes. Then he sealed the letters with the king's ring and sent them by messengers on horses. The messengers rode fast horses
Esther8:11 The king's commands in the letters said this: The Jews in every city have the right to gather together to protect themselves. They have the right to ruin
Esther8:12 The day set for the Jews to do this was the 13 day of the twelfth month
Esther8:13 A copy of the letter with the king's command was to be sent out. It became a law in every province. They announced it to all the people of every nation living in the kingdom. They did this so that the Jews would be ready for that special day. They would be allowed to pay their enemies back.

Esther9:1 On the 13 day of the twelfth month (Adar)
Esther9:2 The Jews met together in their cities in all the provinces of King Xerxes so that they would be strong enough to attack the people who wanted to destroy them. No one was strong enough to stand against them. They were afraid of the Jews.
Esther9:3 And all the officials of the provinces
Esther9:4 Mordecai had become a very important man in the king's palace. Everyone in the provinces knew his name and knew how important he was. And Mordecai became more and more powerful.
Esther9:5 The Jews defeated all their enemies. They used swords to kill and destroy them. They did what they wanted to the people who hated them.
Esther9:6 They killed and destroyed 500 men in the capital city of Susa.
Esther9:7 They also killed these men: Parshandatha
Esther9:8 Poratha
Esther9:9 Parmashta
Esther9:10 These men were the ten sons of Haman. Haman son of Hammedatha was the enemy of the Jews. The Jews killed all the men
Esther9:11 That day the king heard how many men had been killed in the capital city of Susa.
Esther9:12 So the king said to Queen Esther
Esther9:13 Esther said
Esther9:14 So the king gave the command that it should be done. So the law was given in Susa
Esther9:15 The Jews in Susa met together on the 14 day of the month of Adar. They killed 300 men in Susa
Esther9:16 At the same time
Esther9:17 This happened on the 13 day of the month Adar. On the 14 day the Jews rested and made that day a happy day of feasting.
Esther9:18 The Jews in Susa had met together on the 13 and 14 days of the month of Adar. And then on the 15 day they rested. So they made the 15 day a happy day of feasting.
Esther9:19 So those who live in the country and small villages celebrate Purim on the 14 day of Adar. They keep the 14 day as a happy day of feasting. On this day they have parties and give presents to each other.
Esther9:20 Mordecai wrote everything down that had happened
Esther9:21 He did this to tell the Jews to celebrate Purim every year on the 14 and 15 days of the month of Adar.
Esther9:22 They were to celebrate those days because on those days the Jews got rid of their enemies. And they were also to celebrate that month as the month when their sadness was turned into joy. It was a month when their crying was changed into a day of celebration. Mordecai wrote letters to all the Jews and told them to celebrate those days as a happy day of feasting. They should have parties
Esther9:23 So the Jews agreed to do what Mordecai had written to them. And they agreed to continue the celebration they had begun.
Esther9:24 Haman son of Hammedatha the Agagite was the enemy of all the Jews. He had made an evil plan against the Jews to destroy them. And Haman had thrown the lot to choose a day to ruin and to destroy the Jews. At that time the lot was called a pur.
Esther9:25 Haman did this
Esther9:26 At this time
Esther9:27 ***
Esther9:28 They do this to help them remember what they had seen happen to them. The Jews and all the people who join them celebrate these two days every year at the right time in just the right way. Every generation and every family remembers these two days. They celebrate this festival in each and every province and in each and every town. And the Jews will never stop celebrating the days of Purim. Their descendants will always remember this festival.
Esther9:29 So Queen Esther daughter of Abihail
Esther9:30 So Mordecai sent letters to all the Jews in the 127 provinces of King Xerxes' kingdom. He told the people that the festival should bring peace and make people trust each other.
Esther9:31 He wrote these letters to tell the people to start celebrating Purim. And he told them when to celebrate this new festival. Mordecai the Jew and Queen Esther had sent out the command for the Jews to establish this two-day festival for themselves and their descendants. They will remember this festivals just as they remember the other festivals when they fast and cry about the bad things that had happened.
Esther9:32 Esther's letter made the rules for Purim official

Esther10:3 Mordecai the Jew was second in importance to King Xerxes. He was the most important man among the Jews. His fellow Jews respected him very much

Job2:13 Then they sat on the ground with Job for seven days and seven nights. They didn't say a word
Job3:1 Then Job opened his mouth and cursed the day he was born.
Job3:2 He said
Job3:3 I wish the day I was born would be lost forever. I wish the night they said
Job3:4 I wish that day had remained dark. I wish God above had forgotten that day and not let any light shine on it.
Job3:5 I wish that bitter day had remained as dark as death
Job3:6 I wish the darkness had carried away that night
Job3:7 I wish that night had produced nothing and no happy shouts had been heard.
Job3:8 Some magicians think they can wake Leviathan. So let them say their curses and curse the day I was born.
Job3:9 Let that day's morning star be dark. Let that night wait for a morning that never comes. I wish it had never seen the first rays of sunlight.
Job3:10 I wish it had stopped me from being born and kept me from seeing all these troubles.
Job3:11 Why didn't I die when I was born? Why didn't I die as I came from my mother's womb?
Job3:12 Why did my mother hold me on her knees? Why did her breasts feed me?
Job3:13 If I had died when I was born
Job3:14 with the kings and their advisors who built palaces that are now in ruins.
Job3:15 I wish I were buried with rulers who filled their graves with gold and silver.
Job3:16 Why wasn't I a child who died at birth and was put in the ground? I wish I had been buried like a baby who never saw the light of day.
Job3:17 There the wicked stop causing trouble
Job3:18 Even prisoners find relief there; they no longer hear their guards shouting at them.
Job3:19 Everyone—from the greatest to the least important—will be there
Job3:20 Why must a suffering person continue to live? Why let anyone live such a bitter life?
Job3:21 Such people want to die
Job3:22 They would be happy to find their grave. They would rejoice to find their tomb.
Job3:23 But God keeps their future a secret and builds a wall around them to protect them.
Job3:24 When it is time to eat
Job3:25 I was afraid something terrible would happen
Job3:26 I cannot calm down or relax. I am too upset to rest!
