Job5:1 Job
Job5:2 A fool's anger will kill him. His jealousy will destroy him.
Job5:3 I saw a fool who thought he was safe
Job5:4 There was no one to help his children. No one defended them in court.
Job5:5 Hungry people ate all his crops
Job5:6 Bad times don't come up from the dirt. Trouble does not grow from the ground.
Job5:7 But people are born to have trouble
Job5:8 If I were you
Job5:9 People cannot understand the wonderful things God does. His miracles are too many to count.
Job5:10 He sends rain all over the earth and waters the fields.
Job5:11 He raises up the humble and makes sad people happy.
Job5:12 He spoils the plans of even the smartest people so that they will not succeed.
Job5:13 He catches those who think they are wise in their own clever traps and brings to an end their evil plans.
Job5:14 Daylight will be like darkness for them. Even at noon they will have to feel their way as in the dark.
Job5:15 God saves the poor from the hurtful words of the wicked. He saves them from those who are powerful.
Job5:16 So the poor have hope; God shuts the mouths of those who would cause them harm.
Job5:17 You are fortunate when God corrects you. So don't complain when God All-Powerful punishes you.
Job5:18 God might injure you
Job5:19 He will save you again and again. No evil will harm you.
Job5:20 God will save you from death when there is famine. He will protect you from the sword when there is war.
Job5:21 People might say bad things about you with their sharp tongues. But God will protect you. You will not be afraid when bad things happen!
Job5:22 You will laugh at destruction and famine. You will not be afraid of wild animals!
Job5:23 It is as if you have a peace treaty with the wild animals and the rocks in the field.
Job5:24 You will live in peace because your tent is safe. You will count your property and find nothing missing.
Job5:25 You will have many children. They will be as many as the blades of grass on the earth.
Job5:26 You will be like the wheat that grows until harvest time. Yes
Job5:27 We have studied this and know it is true. So listen to us
Job6:1 Then Job answered:
Job6:2 I wish my suffering could be weighed and all my trouble be put on the scales.
Job6:3 They would be heavier than all the sand of the sea! That is why my words are so crazy.
Job6:4 God All-Powerful has shot me with his arrows. My spirit feels their poison! God's terrible weapons are lined up against me.
Job6:5 Even a wild donkey does not complain when it has grass to eat. And a cow is quiet when it has food.
Job6:6 Food without salt does not taste good
Job6:7 I refuse to touch that kind of food; it makes me sick!
Job6:8 I wish I could have what I ask for. I wish God would give me what I want.
Job6:9 I wish he would crush me— just go ahead and kill me!
Job6:10 Then I would be comforted by this one thing: even through all this pain I never refused to obey the commands of the Holy One.
Job6:11 With my strength gone
Job6:12 I am not strong like a rock. My body is not made from bronze.
Job6:13 I don't have the power to help myself
Job6:14 Friends should be loyal to you in times of trouble
Job6:15 But I cannot depend on you
Job6:16 In the winter
Job6:17 But when the weather is hot and dry
Job6:18 It twists and turns along the way and then disappears into the desert.
Job6:19 Traders from Tema search for it. Travelers from Sheba hope to find it.
Job6:20 They are sure they can find water
Job6:21 Now
Job6:22 But have I ever asked you to help me? Did I ask you to offer a bribe for me from your wealth?
Job6:23 No
Job6:24 So now
Job6:25 Honest words are powerful
Job6:26 Do you plan to criticize me? Will you speak more tiring words?
Job6:27 Are you the kind of people who would gamble for orphans and sell out your own friends?
Job6:28 Now
Job6:29 You need to start over and stop being so unfair! Think again
Job6:30 I am not lying. I know right from wrong.
Job7:1 People have a hard struggle on earth. Their life is like that of a hired worker.
Job7:2 They are like a slave looking for cool shade or a hired worker waiting for payday.
Job7:3 Month after frustrating month has gone by. I have suffered night after night.
Job7:4 When I lie down
Job7:5 My skin is covered with worms and scabs. It is cracked and covered with sores.
Job7:6 My days pass by faster than a weaver's shuttle
Job7:7 God
Job7:8 Those who see me now will never see me again. You watch me for a while
Job7:9 Just as clouds that come and go
Job7:10 They don't come back to their old homes. The people there would not know them.
Job7:11 So I will not be quiet! I will let my suffering spirit speak! I will let my bitter soul complain!
Job7:12 Am I one of your enemies? Is that why you put a guard over me?
Job7:13 My bed should bring me comfort. My couch should give me rest and relief.
Job7:14 But when I lie down
Job7:15 So I would rather be choked to death than to live like this.
Job7:16 I hate my life—I give up. I don't want to live forever. Leave me alone! My life means nothing.
Job7:17 God
Job7:18 Why do you visit them every morning and test them at every moment?
Job7:19 You never look away from me or leave me alone for a second.
Job7:20 You are always watching us! If I sinned
Job7:21 Why don't you just pardon me for doing wrong? Why don't you just forgive me for my sins? Soon I will die and be in my grave. You will search for me

Job8:2 How long will you talk like that? Your words are nothing but hot air!

Job8:4 If your children sinned against God
Job8:5 But now
Job8:6 If you are pure and good
Job8:7 Then you will have a lot more than you had in the beginning!

Job24:22 By his power God removes the powerful. Even if they have a high position
Job24:23 They might feel safe and secure
Job24:24 They might be successful for a while
Job24:25 I swear these things are true! Who can prove that I lied? Who can show that I am wrong?
Job25:1 Then Bildad from Shuah answered:
Job25:2 God is the ruler. He makes people fear and respect him. He keeps peace in his kingdom above.
Job25:3 No one can count his stars. His sun rises on all people.
Job25:4 How can anyone claim to be right before God? No human being can really be pure.
Job25:5 In God's eyes even the moon is not pure and bright; even the stars are not pure.
Job25:6 People are much less pure. They are like maggots
Job26:1 Then Job answered:
Job26:2 Bildad
Job26:3 You have given such wonderful advice to this foolish man! You have provided so much useful information!
Job26:4 Who helped you say these things? Whose spirit inspired you to speak?
Job26:5 The ghosts and their neighbors in the underworld shake with fear.
Job26:6 But God can see clearly into that place of death. Death is not hidden from God.
Job26:7 God stretched the northern sky over empty space. He hung the earth on nothing.
Job26:8 He fills the thick clouds with water. But he does not let its heavy weight break the clouds open.
Job26:9 He covers the face of the full moon. He spreads his clouds over it and hides it.
Job26:10 He drew the horizon on the ocean
Job26:11 The foundations that hold up the sky shake with fear when God threatens them.
Job26:12 With his own power God calmed the sea. With his wisdom he destroyed Rahab.
Job26:13 His breath made the skies clear. His hand destroyed the snake that tried to get away.
Job26:14 These are only a few of the amazing things God has done. We hear only a small whisper of God's thundering power.
Job27:1 Job continued his answer:
Job27:2 God All-Powerful has been unfair to me; he has made my life bitter. But I swear by his life
Job27:3 as long as I have life in me
Job27:4 I will not be a hypocrite. I will not lie.
Job27:5 You will never hear me say you men are right! Until the day I die I will not be untrue to myself.
Job27:6 I will hold on to my innocence and never let go! My conscience will always be clear!
Job27:7 May my enemies be punished like those who are evil. May those who stood against me end up like all who have done wrong.
Job27:8 What hope do people without God have when it is time to die
Job27:9 When they have troubles and cry out for help
Job27:10 It will be too late for them to enjoy talking with God All-Powerful. They should have prayed to God all the time.
Job27:11 I will teach you about God's power. I will not hide anything about God All-Powerful.
Job27:12 But you have seen it all with your own eyes. So why do you say such useless things?
Job27:13 Here is what God has planned for those who are evil. This is what cruel people will get from God All-Powerful.
Job27:14 They may have many children
Job27:15 All those who are left will die
Job27:16 Evil people collect silver as easily as dirt. They may have so many clothes that they are like piles of clay.
Job27:17 But their piles of clothes will be worn by those who have lived right. All that silver will be given to those who have done no wrong.
Job27:18 Evil people might build houses
Job27:19 They might be rich when they go to bed
Job27:20 Terrible fears will come over them like a flood
Job27:21 The east wind will carry them away
Job27:22 They may try to run away from the power of the storm
Job27:23 It will sound like it is clapping its hands as they run away. It will whistle at them as they run from their homes.
Job28:1 There are mines where people get silver and places where people melt gold to make it pure.
Job28:2 Iron is dug out of the ground
Job28:3 Miners carry lights deep into caves to search for these rocks in the deepest darkness.
Job28:4 Far from where people live
Job28:5 Food grows on the ground above. But underground it is different
Job28:6 In the rocks there are sapphires and grains of pure gold.
Job28:7 Wild birds know nothing about the way to these places. No falcon has ever seen it.
Job28:8 Wild animals have never been there. Lions have not traveled that way.
Job28:9 Miners dig the hardest rocks. They dig away at the mountains and make them bare.
Job28:10 They cut tunnels through the rocks and see all the treasures they hold.
Job28:11 They even find places where rivers begin. They bring to light what once was hidden.
Job28:12 But where can anyone find wisdom? Where can we get understanding?
Job28:13 People don't know where wisdom is. It cannot be found by anyone on earth.
Job28:14 The deep ocean says
Job28:15 You cannot buy wisdom with even the purest gold. There's not enough silver in the world to pay for it.
Job28:16 You cannot buy it with gold from Ophir or with precious onyx or sapphires.
Job28:17 Wisdom is worth more than gold or crystal. It cannot be bought with expensive jewels set in gold.
Job28:18 It is far more valuable than coral and jasper
Job28:19 The topaz from Ethiopia cannot match its value
Job28:20 So where does wisdom come from? Where can we find understanding?
Job28:21 Wisdom is hidden from every living thing on earth. Even birds in the sky cannot see it.
Job28:22 Death and destruction say
Job28:23 Only God knows the way to wisdom. Only he knows where wisdom is.
Job28:24 He can see to the very ends of the earth. He sees everything under the sky.
Job28:25 God gave the wind its power. He decided how big to make the oceans.
Job28:26 He decided where to send the rain and where the thunderstorms should go.
Job28:27 He looked at wisdom and discussed it. He examined it and saw how much it is worth.

Job29:1 Job continued to speak:
Job29:2 I wish my life could be the same as it was a few months ago
Job29:3 God's light shined above me
Job29:4 I wish for the days when I was successful
Job29:5 God All-Powerful was still with me then
Job29:6 Life was so good that I washed my feet in cream and had plenty of the finest oils.
Job29:7 Those were the days when I went to the city gate and sat in the public meeting of the elders.
Job29:8 When the young men saw me coming
Job29:9 The leaders of the people stopped talking and put their hands over their mouths.
Job29:10 Even the most important leaders were quiet
Job29:11 All who heard me said good things about me. Those who saw what I did praised me
Job29:12 because I helped the poor when they cried out. I helped the orphans who had no one to care for them.
Job29:13 People who were dying asked God to bless me. My help brought joy to widows in need.
Job29:14 Right living was my clothing. Fairness was my robe and turban.
Job29:15 I was like eyes for the blind
Job29:16 I was like a father to the poor. I helped people I didn't even know win their case in court.
Job29:17 I stopped evil people from abusing their power and saved innocent people from them.
Job29:18 I always thought I would live a long life
Job29:19 I was like a healthy plant with roots that have plenty of water and branches that are wet with dew.
Job29:20 I thought each new day would bring more honor and be full of new possibilities.
Job29:21 In the past people listened to me. They waited quietly for my advice.
Job29:22 When I finished speaking
Job29:23 They waited for my words as they would for rain. They drank them in like rain in the springtime.
Job29:24 I smiled at them
Job29:25 I was their leader and made decisions about their future. I was like a king among his troops
Job30:1 But now men younger than I make fun of me— men whose fathers were too worthless to put with my sheep dogs.
Job30:2 Their fathers are still too weak to be of any use to me. All their strength is gone.
Job30:3 They are starving with nothing to eat
Job30:4 They pull up salt plants in the desert and eat the roots from the broom tree.
Job30:5 They are forced away from other people
Job30:6 They must live in the dry riverbeds
Job30:7 They howl in the bushes and huddle together under thornbushes.
Job30:8 They are a bunch of worthless people without names
Job30:9 Now their sons sing songs to make fun of me. My name has become a bad word to them.
Job30:10 They hate me and stay far away from me
Job30:11 God has taken the string from my bow and made me weak
Job30:12 They attack me on my right side. They knock my feet out from under me. They build ramps to attack and destroy me like a city.
Job30:13 They guard the road so that I cannot escape. They succeed in destroying me
Job30:14 They break a hole in the wall and come rushing through it
Job30:15 I am shaking with fear. They chased my honor away like dust in the wind. My safety disappears like a cloud.
Job30:16 Now my life is almost gone
Job30:17 All my bones ache at night. Pain never stops chewing on me.
Job30:18 God grabbed the collar of my coat and twisted my clothes out of shape.
Job30:19 He threw me into the mud
Job30:20 God
Job30:21 You have become cruel to me; you use your power to hurt me.

Job30:23 I know you will lead me to my death
Job30:24 Surely no one would attack a man who is already ruined
Job30:25 God
Job30:26 But when I hoped for good
Job30:27 I constantly feel upset. And my suffering has only just begun.
Job30:28 I am always sad and depressed
Job30:29 making sad sounds like the wild dogs
Job30:30 My skin is burned and peeling away. My body is hot with fever.
Job30:31 My harp is tuned to play songs of sorrow. My flute makes sad sounds like someone crying.
Job31:1 I made an agreement with my eyes not to look at a young woman in a way that would make me want her.
Job31:2 What does God above have for us? How does God All-Powerful repay people from his home high in heaven?
Job31:3 He sends trouble to the wicked and disaster to those who do wrong.
Job31:4 God is the one who knows what I do and sees every step I take.
Job31:5 I have not lied to anyone. I never tried to cheat people.
Job31:6 If God would use accurate scales
Job31:7 If I ever stepped off the right path
Job31:8 then let others eat what I planted. Let my crops be pulled up by the roots.
Job31:9 If I have desired another woman or waited at my neighbor's door to sin with his wife
Job31:10 then let my wife serve someone else
Job31:11 To do such a thing would be shameful
Job31:12 Such sin is like a fire that burns until it destroys everything. It would completely ruin my life's work.
Job31:13 If I refused to be fair to my slaves when they had a complaint against me
Job31:14 then what will I do when I must face God? What will I say when he asks me to explain what I did?
Job31:15 The one who made me in my mother's womb also made them. God shaped us all inside our mothers.
Job31:16 I have never refused to help the poor. I always gave widows what they needed.
Job31:17 I have never been selfish with my food. I shared what I had with orphans.
Job31:18 All my life I have been like a father to orphans and have taken care of widows.
Job31:19 Whenever I found people suffering because they didn't have clothes or saw a poor man with no coat
Job31:20 I always gave them something to wear. I used the wool from my own sheep to make them warm. And they thanked me with all their heart.
Job31:21 I never threatened an orphan
Job31:22 If I ever did that
Job31:23 But I didn't do any of these bad things. I fear God's punishment too much. His majesty scares me.
Job31:24 I have never trusted in riches. I never said even to pure gold
Job31:25 I have been wealthy
Job31:26 I have never worshiped the bright sun or the beautiful moon.
Job31:27 I was never foolish enough to worship the sun and the moon.
Job31:28 This is also a sin that must be punished. If I had worshiped them
Job31:29 I have never been happy when my enemies were destroyed. I have never laughed at my enemies when bad things happened to them.
Job31:30 I have never let my mouth sin by cursing my enemies and wishing for them to die.
Job31:31 The people in my house know that I have never let anyone go hungry.
Job31:32 I always invited strangers into my home so they would not have to sleep in the streets.
Job31:33 I have not tried to hide my sins as some people do. I have never hidden my guilt.
Job31:34 I was never so afraid of what people might say or of making enemies that I kept my sins secret and avoided going out.
Job31:35 How I wish someone would listen to me! I will sign my name to all I have said. Now let God All-Powerful answer me. Let him make a list of what he thinks I did wrong.
Job31:36 I would wear it around my neck. I would put it on my head like a crown.
Job31:37 Then I could explain everything I have done. I could come to God with my head held high like a prince.
Job31:38 I did not use my land in a wrong way. I never caused it to suffer.
Job31:39 I always paid the workers for the food I got from the land. I never let any of them starve.
Job31:40 If I ever did any of these bad things

Job37:1 The thunder and lightning frighten me; my heart pounds in my chest.

Job38:1 Then the Lord spoke to Job from a whirlwind and said
Job38:2 Who is this ignorant person saying these foolish things?
Job38:3 Brace yourself and get ready to answer the questions I will ask you.
Job38:4 Where were you when I made the earth? If you are so smart
Job38:5 And who decided how big the earth should be? Who measured it with a measuring line?
Job38:6 What is the earth resting on? Who put the first stone in its place
Job38:7 when the morning stars sang together and the angels shouted with joy?
Job38:8 Who closed the flood gates as the sea gushed from the womb?
Job38:9 Who covered it with clouds and wrapped it in darkness?
Job38:10 I set the limits for the sea and put it behind locked gates.
Job38:11 I said to the sea
Job38:12 Did you ever in your life command the morning to begin or the day to dawn?
Job38:13 Did you ever tell the morning light to grab the earth and shake those who are evil out of their hiding places?
Job38:14 The morning light makes the hills and valleys easy to see. When the daylight comes to the earth
Job38:15 Evil people don't like the daylight. When it shines bright
Job38:16 Have you ever gone to the deepest parts of the sea? Have you ever walked on the ocean bottom?
Job38:17 Has anyone shown you the gates to the world of the dead? Have you ever seen those gates that lead to the dark place of death?
Job38:18 Do you really understand how big the earth is? Tell me
Job38:19 Where does light come from? Where does darkness come from?
Job38:20 Can you take them back to where they belong? Do you know how to get there?
Job38:21 Surely you know these things
Job38:22 Have you ever gone into the storerooms where I keep the snow and the hail?
Job38:23 I save them there for times of trouble
Job38:24 Have you ever gone to the place where the sun comes up
Job38:25 Who dug ditches in the sky for the heavy rain? Who made a path for the thunderstorm?
Job38:26 Who makes it rain even in desert places where no one lives?
Job38:27 The rain gives that dry empty land all the water it needs
Job38:28 Does the rain have a father? Who produces the drops of dew?
Job38:29 Does ice have a mother? Who gives birth to the frost?
Job38:30 That's when the water freezes as hard as a rock. Even the deep sea freezes over!
Job38:31 Can you tie up the Pleiades? Can you unfasten the belt of Orion?
Job38:32 Can you bring out the constellations at the right times? Or can you lead out the Bear with its cubs?
Job38:33 Do you know the laws that control the sky? Can you put each star in its place above the earth?
Job38:34 Can you shout at the clouds and command them to cover you with rain?
Job38:35 Can you give a command to the lightning? Will it come to you and say
Job38:36 Who makes people wise? Who puts wisdom deep inside them?
Job38:37 Who is wise enough to count the clouds and tip them over to pour out their rain?
Job38:38 The rain makes the dust become mud
Job38:39 Do you find food for the lions? Do you feed their hungry babies?
Job38:40 No
Job38:41 Who feeds the ravens when their babies cry out to God and wander around without food?
Job39:1 Do you know when the mountain goats are born? Do you watch when the mother deer gives birth?
Job39:2 Do you know how many months they must carry their babies? Do you know when it is the right time for them to be born?
Job39:3 These animals lie down
Job39:4 Their babies grow strong out in the wild. Then they leave their mothers and never come back.
Job39:5 Who let the wild donkeys go free? Who untied their ropes and let them loose?
Job39:6 I let the wild donkey have the desert for a home. I gave the salt lands to them for a place to live.
Job39:7 They are happy to be away from the noise of the city. They never have to listen to their drivers shouting at them.
Job39:8 They live in the mountains. That is their pasture. That is where they look for food to eat.
Job39:9 Will a wild bull agree to serve you? Will he stay in your barn at night?
Job39:10 Will he let you put ropes on him to plow your fields?
Job39:11 A wild bull is very strong
Job39:12 Can you trust him to gather your grain and bring it to your threshing place?
Job39:13 An ostrich gets excited and flaps its wings
Job39:14 An ostrich lays her eggs on the ground and lets the sand keep them warm.
Job39:15 The ostrich forgets that someone might step on her eggs or that a wild animal might break them.
Job39:16 An ostrich leaves her little babies. She treats them as if they were not her own. If her babies die
Job39:17 That's because I did not give wisdom to the ostrich. She is foolish
Job39:18 But when the ostrich gets up to run
Job39:19 Did you give the horse its strength? Did you put the mane on its neck?
Job39:20 Did you make it able to jump like a locust or snort so loudly that it scares people?
Job39:21 A horse is happy to be so strong. It scratches the ground with its foot and runs into battle.
Job39:22 It laughs at fear; nothing makes it afraid! It does not run away from battle.
Job39:23 The soldier's quiver shakes on the horse's side. The spear and weapons its rider carries shine in the sun.

Job41:26 Swords

Job41:33 No animal on earth is like him. He is an animal made without fear.
Job41:34 He looks down on the proudest of creatures. He is king over all the wild animals.

Psalms72:13 He feels sorry for all who are weak and poor. He protects their lives.
Psalms72:14 He saves them from the cruel people who try to hurt them. Their lives are important to him.

Psalms72:16 May the fields grow plenty of grain and the hills be covered with crops. May the fields be as fertile as Lebanon

Proverbs1:3 They will teach you to develop your mind in the right way. You will learn to do what is right and to be honest and fair.

Proverbs1:19 This is what happens to those who are greedy. Whatever they get destroys them.

Ecclesiastes2:1 I said to myself
Ecclesiastes2:2 It is foolish to laugh all the time. Having fun does not do any good.
Ecclesiastes2:3 So I decided to fill my body with wine while I filled my mind with wisdom. I tried this foolishness because I wanted to find a way to be happy. I wanted to see what was good for people to do during their few days of life.
Ecclesiastes2:4 Then I began doing great things. I built houses
Ecclesiastes2:5 I planted gardens
Ecclesiastes2:6 I made pools of water for myself
Ecclesiastes2:7 I bought men and women slaves
Ecclesiastes2:8 I also gathered silver and gold for myself. I took treasures from kings and their nations. I had men and women singing for me. I had everything anyone could want.
Ecclesiastes2:9 I became very rich and famous. I was greater than anyone who lived in Jerusalem before me. My wisdom was always there to help me.
Ecclesiastes2:10 Anything my eyes saw and wanted
Ecclesiastes2:11 But then I looked at everything I had done and the wealth I had gained. I decided it was all a waste of time! It was like trying to catch the wind. There is nothing to gain from anything we do in this life.
Ecclesiastes2:12 Then I decided to think about what it means to be wise or to be foolish or to do crazy things. And I thought about the one who will be the next king. The new king will do the same as the kings before him.
Ecclesiastes2:13 I saw that wisdom is better than foolishness in the same way that light is better than darkness.
Ecclesiastes2:14 Wise people use their minds like eyes to see where they are going. But for fools
Ecclesiastes2:15 I thought to myself
Ecclesiastes2:16 Whether people are wise or foolish
Ecclesiastes2:17 This made me hate life. It was depressing to think that everything in this life is useless
Ecclesiastes2:18 I began to hate all the hard work I had done
Ecclesiastes2:19 Some other person will control everything I worked and studied for. And I don't know if that person will be wise or foolish. This is also senseless.
Ecclesiastes2:20 So I became sad about all the work I had done.
Ecclesiastes2:21 People can work hard using all their wisdom and knowledge and skill. But they will die and other people will get the things they worked for. They did not do the work

Ecclesiastes7:11 Wisdom is better if you also have property. Wise people will get more than enough wealth.
Ecclesiastes7:12 Wisdom and money can protect you. But knowledge gained through wisdom is even better—it can save your life.
Ecclesiastes7:13 Look at what God has made. You cannot change a thing
Ecclesiastes7:14 When life is good
Ecclesiastes7:15 In my short life
Ecclesiastes7:16 So why ruin your life? Don't be too good or too bad
Ecclesiastes7:17 ***
Ecclesiastes7:18 Try to be a little of this and a little of that. Even God's followers will do some good things and some bad things.
Ecclesiastes7:19 Surely there is no one on earth who always does good and never sins. But wisdom can make one person stronger than ten leaders in a city.
Ecclesiastes7:20 ***
Ecclesiastes7:21 Don't listen to everything people say. You might hear your own servant saying bad things about you.
Ecclesiastes7:22 And you know that many times you too have said bad things about other people.
Ecclesiastes7:23 I used my wisdom and thought about all these things. I wanted to be wise
Ecclesiastes7:24 I cannot understand why things are as they are. It is too hard for anyone to understand.
Ecclesiastes7:25 I studied and I tried very hard to find true wisdom. I tried to find a reason for everything. I did learn that it is foolish to be evil
Ecclesiastes7:26 I also found that some women are dangerous like traps. Their hearts are like nets
Ecclesiastes7:27 The Teacher says
Ecclesiastes7:28 ***
Ecclesiastes7:29 There is one other thing I have learned. God made people good
Ecclesiastes8:1 No one can understand and explain things the way wise people can. Their wisdom makes them happy. It changes a sad face into a happy one.
Ecclesiastes8:2 I say you should always obey the king's command. Do this because you made a promise to God.
Ecclesiastes8:3 Don't be afraid to give suggestions to the king
Ecclesiastes8:4 He has the authority to give commands
Ecclesiastes8:5 People will be safe if they obey his command. But wise people know the right time to do this
Ecclesiastes8:6 There is a right time and a right way to do everything. You must decide what you should do
Ecclesiastes8:7 and you are not sure what will happen. No one can tell you what will happen in the future.
Ecclesiastes8:8 No one has the power to keep their spirit from leaving or to stop their death. During war
Ecclesiastes8:9 I saw all this. I thought very hard about the things that happen in this world. I saw that people always struggle for the power to rule others
Ecclesiastes8:10 I also saw great and beautiful funerals for evil people. While the people were going home after the funeral services
Ecclesiastes8:11 Sometimes people are not immediately punished for the bad things they do. Their punishment is slow to come
Ecclesiastes8:12 A sinner might do a hundred evil things and still live a long time. But I know that it is still better to obey and respect God.
Ecclesiastes8:13 Evil people don't respect God
Ecclesiastes8:14 There is something else that happens on earth that does not seem fair. Bad things should happen to bad people
Ecclesiastes8:15 So I decided it was more important to enjoy life because the best thing people can do in this life is to eat
Ecclesiastes8:16 I carefully studied the things people do in this life. I saw how busy people are. They work day and night
Ecclesiastes8:17 I also saw the many things that God does
Ecclesiastes9:1 I thought about all this very carefully. I saw that God controls what happens to the good and wise people and what they do. People don't know if they will be loved or hated
Ecclesiastes9:2 But
Ecclesiastes9:3 Of all the things that happen in this life
Ecclesiastes9:4 There is hope for those who are still alive—it does not matter who they are. But this saying is true: A living dog is better than a dead lion.
Ecclesiastes9:5 The living know that they will die
Ecclesiastes9:6 After people are dead
Ecclesiastes9:7 So go and eat your food now and enjoy it. Drink your wine and be happy. It is all right with God if you do these things.
Ecclesiastes9:8 Wear nice clothes and make yourself look good.
Ecclesiastes9:9 Enjoy life with the wife you love. Enjoy every day of your short life. God has given you this short life on earth—and it is all you have. So enjoy the work you have to do in this life.
Ecclesiastes9:10 Every time you find work to do
Ecclesiastes9:11 I also saw other things in this life that were not fair. The fastest runner does not always win the race; the strongest soldier does not always win the battle; wise people don't always get the food; smart people don't always get the wealth; educated people don't always get the praise they deserve. When the time comes
Ecclesiastes9:12 You never know when hard times will come. Like fish in a net or birds in a snare
Ecclesiastes9:13 I also saw a person doing a wise thing in this life
Ecclesiastes9:14 There was a small town with a few people in it. A great king fought against that town and put his armies all around it.
Ecclesiastes9:15 But there was a wise man in that town. He was poor
Ecclesiastes9:16 But I still say that wisdom is better than strength. They forgot about the poor man's wisdom
Ecclesiastes9:17 Words spoken by the wise are heard more clearly than those shouted by a leader among fools.
Ecclesiastes9:18 Wisdom is better than weapons of war
Ecclesiastes10:1 A few dead flies will make even the best perfume stink. In the same way
Ecclesiastes10:2 The thoughts of the wise lead them the right way
Ecclesiastes10:3 Fools show how foolish they are
Ecclesiastes10:4 Don't quit your job simply because the boss is angry with you. If you remain calm and helpful
Ecclesiastes10:5 Here is something else that I have seen in this life that isn't fair. It is the kind of mistake that rulers make.
Ecclesiastes10:6 Fools are given important positions
Ecclesiastes10:7 I have seen servants riding on horses
Ecclesiastes10:8 If you dig a hole
Ecclesiastes10:9 If you are moving large stones
Ecclesiastes10:10 But wisdom will make any job easier. It is very hard to cut with a dull knife. But if you sharpen the knife
Ecclesiastes10:11 Someone might know how to control snakes. But that skill is useless if a snake bites when that person is not around.
Ecclesiastes10:12 Words from the wise bring praise
Ecclesiastes10:13 Fools begin by saying something foolish. But in the end
Ecclesiastes10:14 Fools are always talking about what they will do
Ecclesiastes10:15 Fools aren't smart enough to find their way home
Ecclesiastes10:16 It is very bad for a country if the king is like a child. And it is very bad for a country if its rulers use all their time eating.
Ecclesiastes10:17 But it is very good for a country if the king comes from a good family. And it is very good for a country if the rulers control their eating and drinking. They eat and drink to become strong
Ecclesiastes10:18 If someone is too lazy to work
Ecclesiastes10:19 People enjoy eating
Ecclesiastes10:20 Don't say bad things about the king. Don't even think bad things about him. And don't say bad things about rich people
Ecclesiastes11:1 Do good wherever you go. After a while
Ecclesiastes11:2 Invest what you have in several different things. You don't know what bad things might happen on earth.
Ecclesiastes11:3 There are some things you can be sure of. If clouds are full of rain
Ecclesiastes11:4 But there are some things that you cannot be sure of. You must take a chance. If you wait for perfect weather
Ecclesiastes11:5 You don't know where the wind blows. And you don't know how a baby grows in its mother's womb. In the same way
Ecclesiastes11:6 So begin planting early in the morning
Ecclesiastes11:7 It is good to be alive. It is nice to see the light from the sun.
Ecclesiastes11:8 You should enjoy every day of your life
Ecclesiastes11:9 So young people
Ecclesiastes11:10 Don't let your anger control you
Ecclesiastes12:1 Remember your Creator while you are young
Ecclesiastes12:2 Remember your Creator while you are young
Ecclesiastes12:3 At that time your arms will lose their strength. Your legs will become weak and bent. Your teeth will fall out
Ecclesiastes12:4 You will become hard of hearing. You will not hear the noise in the streets. Even the stone grinding your grain will seem quiet to you. You will not be able to hear the women singing. But even the sound of a bird singing will wake you early in the morning because you will not be able to sleep.
Ecclesiastes12:5 You will be afraid of high places. You will be afraid of tripping over every small thing in your path. Your hair will become white like the flowers on an almond tree. You will drag yourself along like a grasshopper when you walk. You will lose your desire
Ecclesiastes12:6 Remember your Creator while you are young
Ecclesiastes12:7 Your body came from the earth. And when you die

Isaiah1:8 Daughter Zion is now like an empty shed left in a vineyard. It is like an old straw hut abandoned in a field of cucumbers or like a city surrounded by enemies.

Isaiah1:24 Because of this

Isaiah2:1 Isaiah son of Amoz saw this message about Judah and Jerusalem.
Isaiah2:2 In the last days the mountain of the Lord's Temple will be on the highest of all mountains. It will be raised higher than the hills. There will be a steady stream of people from all nations going there.
Isaiah2:3 People from many places will go there and say
Isaiah2:4 Then God will act as judge to end arguments between nations. He will decide what is right for people from many lands. They will stop using their weapons for war. They will hammer their swords into plows and use their spears to make tools for harvesting. All fighting between nations will end. They will never again train for war.
Isaiah2:5 Family of Jacob
Isaiah2:6 Family of Jacob
Isaiah2:7 Jacob's land has been filled with silver and gold from other places. There are many treasures there. His land has been filled with horses and many chariots.
Isaiah2:8 His land is full of gods that the people bow down to worship. They made these idols themselves.
Isaiah2:9 The people have become worse and worse. They have become very low
Isaiah2:10 You should be afraid of the Lord! Go hide in the dirt and behind the rocks. Hide from his glorious power!
Isaiah2:11 Proud people will stop being proud. They will bow down to the ground with shame
Isaiah2:12 The Lord All-Powerful has a special day planned when he will punish the proud and boastful people. They will be brought down.
Isaiah2:13 They might be like the tall cedar trees from Lebanon or the great oak trees from Bashan
Isaiah2:14 They might be like the tall mountains and high hills
Isaiah2:15 or like the tall towers and high walls
Isaiah2:16 They might be like great ships from Tarshish
Isaiah2:17 At that time those proud people will fall. They will bow low to the ground
Isaiah2:18 All the idols will be gone.
Isaiah2:19 People will go into the holes in the ground and the cracks in the rocks because they fear the Lord and his great power as he stands to shake the earth.
Isaiah2:20 At that time people will throw away their idols they made from gold and silver. They made these statues to worship
Isaiah2:21 They will go down into cracks in the rocks and boulders because they are afraid of the Lord and his glorious power as he stands to shake the earth.
Isaiah2:22 Stop trusting other people to save you. Do not think too highly of them; they are only humans who have not stopped breathing yet.

Jeremiah6:20 Why do you bring me incense from the country of Sheba? Why do you bring me sweet-smelling cane from a faraway country? Your burnt offerings don't make me happy. Your sacrifices don't please me.
Jeremiah6:21 So this is what the Lord says: I will give the people of Judah problems. They will be like stones that make people fall. Fathers and sons will stumble over them. Friends and neighbors will die.
Jeremiah6:22 This is what the Lord says: An army is coming from the north. A great nation is coming from faraway places on earth.
Jeremiah6:23 The soldiers carry bows and spears. They are cruel; they have no mercy. They are so powerful. They sound like the roaring ocean as they ride their horses. That army is coming

Jeremiah7:2 Jeremiah

Jeremiah7:12 'You people of Judah
Jeremiah7:13 You people of Israel were doing all these evil things. This message is from the Lord! I spoke to you again and again
Jeremiah7:14 So I will destroy the house called by my name in Jerusalem. I will destroy that Temple as I destroyed Shiloh. And that house in Jerusalem that is called by my name is the Temple you trust in. I gave that place to you and to your ancestors.
Jeremiah7:15 I will throw you away from me just as I threw away all your brothers from Ephraim. '
Jeremiah7:16 As for you
Jeremiah7:17 I know you see what they are doing in the towns of Judah. You can see what they are doing in the streets of the city of Jerusalem.
Jeremiah7:18 This is what the people of Judah are doing: The children gather wood. The fathers use the wood to make a fire. The women make the dough and then make cakes of bread to offer to the Queen of Heaven. The people of Judah pour out drink offerings to worship other gods. They do this to make me angry.
Jeremiah7:19 But I am not the one they are really hurting. This message is from the Lord. They are only hurting themselves. They are bringing shame on themselves.
Jeremiah7:20 So this is what the Lord says: I will show my anger against this place. I will punish people and animals. I will punish the trees in the field and the crops that grow in the ground. My anger will be like a hot fire—no one will be able to stop it.

Jeremiah7:22 I brought your ancestors out of Egypt. I spoke to them
Jeremiah7:23 I only gave them this command: 'Obey me and I will be your God
Jeremiah7:24 But your ancestors did not listen to me. They did not pay attention to me. They were stubborn and did what they wanted to do. They did not become good. They became even more evil—they went backward
Jeremiah7:25 From the day that your ancestors left Egypt to this day
Jeremiah7:26 But your ancestors did not listen to me. They did not pay attention to me. They were very stubborn and did evil even worse than their fathers did.
Jeremiah7:27 Jeremiah
Jeremiah7:28 So you must tell them these things: This is the nation that did not obey the Lord its God. These people did not listen to God's teachings. They don't know the true teachings.
Jeremiah7:29 Jeremiah

Jeremiah7:32 So I warn you. The days are coming
Jeremiah7:33 Then the bodies of the dead people will become food for the birds of the sky. Wild animals will eat the bodies of those people. There will be no one left alive to chase the birds or animals away.

Jeremiah34:2 This was the message: This is what the Lord
Jeremiah34:3 Zedekiah
Jeremiah34:4 But listen to the promise of the Lord
Jeremiah34:5 You will die in a peaceful way. People made funeral fires to honor your ancestors
Jeremiah34:6 So Jeremiah gave the message from the Lord to Zedekiah in Jerusalem.
Jeremiah34:7 This was while the army of the king of Babylon was fighting against Jerusalem. The army of Babylon was also fighting against the cities of Judah that had not been captured. These cities were Lachish and Azekah. These were the only fortified cities left in the land of Judah.
Jeremiah34:8 King Zedekiah had made an agreement with all the people in Jerusalem to give freedom to all the Hebrew slaves. A message from the Lord came to Jeremiah after Zedekiah had made that agreement.
Jeremiah34:9 Everyone was supposed to free their Hebrew slaves. All male and female Hebrew slaves were to be set free. No one was supposed to keep another person from the tribe of Judah in slavery.
Jeremiah34:10 So all the leaders of Judah and all the people accepted this agreement. They would free their male and female slaves so that they would no longer serve them. Everyone agreed
Jeremiah34:11 But after that
Jeremiah34:12 Then the message from the Lord came to Jeremiah:
Jeremiah34:13 This is what the Lord
Jeremiah34:14 I said to your ancestors: 'At the end of every seven years
Jeremiah34:15 A short time ago you changed your hearts to do what is right. Everyone set free their fellow Hebrews who were slaves. And you even made an agreement before me in the Temple that is called by my name.
Jeremiah34:16 But now you have changed your minds. You have shown you do not honor my name because each of you has taken back the male and female slaves that you had set free. You have forced them to become slaves again.
Jeremiah34:17 So this is what the Lord says: 'You people have not obeyed me. You have not given freedom to your fellow Hebrews. Because you have not kept the agreement
Jeremiah34:18 I will hand over those who broke my agreement and have not kept the promises they made before me. They cut a calf into two pieces before me and walked between the two pieces.
Jeremiah34:19 These are the people who walked between the two pieces of the calf when they made the agreement before me: the leaders of Judah and Jerusalem
Jeremiah34:20 So I will give them to their enemies and to everyone who wants to kill them. Their bodies will become food for the birds of the air and for the wild animals of the earth.
Jeremiah34:21 I will give King Zedekiah of Judah and his leaders to their enemies and to everyone who wants to kill them. I will give Zedekiah and his people to the army of the king of Babylon
Jeremiah34:22 But I will give the order

Jeremiah52:1 Zedekiah was 21 years old when he became king of Judah. He ruled in Jerusalem for eleven years. His mother's name was Hamutal daughter of Jeremiah. Hamutal's family was from the town of Libnah.
Jeremiah52:2 Zedekiah did evil things
Jeremiah52:3 Terrible things happened to the people of Jerusalem and Judah because the Lord was angry with them. Finally
Jeremiah52:4 So in the ninth year of Zedekiah's rule
Jeremiah52:5 The city of Jerusalem was surrounded by the army of Babylon until the eleventh year that Zedekiah was king.
Jeremiah52:6 By the ninth day of the fourth month of that year
Jeremiah52:7 On that day the army of Babylon broke into Jerusalem. The soldiers of Jerusalem ran away. They left the city at night. They went through the gate between the two walls. That gate was near the king's garden. Even though the army of Babylon had surrounded the city
Jeremiah52:8 But the Babylonian army chased King Zedekiah and caught him on the plains of Jericho. All of Zedekiah's soldiers ran away.

Jeremiah52:10 There at the town of Riblah

Jeremiah52:12 Nebuzaradan was the commander of the king of Babylon's special guard. He was one of the king's most important officials while at Jerusalem. He came to Jerusalem on the tenth day of the fifth month
Jeremiah52:13 Nebuzaradan burned the Lord's Temple
Jeremiah52:14 All the Babylonian soldiers that were with the commander broke down the walls around Jerusalem.

Jeremiah52:16 But Nebuzaradan left some of the poorest people behind in the land. He left them to work in the vineyards and the fields.
Jeremiah52:17 The Babylonian army broke up the bronze columns of the Temple. They also broke up the stands and the bronze tank that were in the Lord's Temple. They carried all that bronze to Babylon.
Jeremiah52:18 The army of Babylon also took these things from the Temple: pots
Jeremiah52:19 The commander of the king's special guards took these things away: basins
Jeremiah52:20 The two pillars
Jeremiah52:21 Each of the bronze pillars was 31 feet tall. Each pillar was almost 21 feet around. Each pillar was hollow. The wall of each pillar was 3 inches thick.
Jeremiah52:22 The bronze capital on top of the first pillar was over 5 feet tall. It was decorated with a net design and bronze pomegranates all around it. The other pillar had pomegranates too. It was like the first pillar.
Jeremiah52:23 There were 96 pomegranates on the sides of the pillars. All together
Jeremiah52:24 The commander of the king's special guards took Seraiah the high priest and Zephaniah the next highest priest as prisoners. The three doorkeepers were also taken as prisoners.
Jeremiah52:25 The commander of the king's special guards also took the officer in charge of the fighting men. He also took seven of the king's advisors as prisoners. They were still there in Jerusalem. He also took the scribe who was in charge of putting people in the army. And he took 60 of the ordinary people who were there in the city.

Lamentations1:3 Judah suffered very much
Lamentations1:4 The roads to Zion are very sad
Lamentations1:5 Jerusalem's enemies have won. Her enemies have been successful. This happened because the Lord punished her. He punished Jerusalem for her many sins. Her children have gone away. Their enemies captured them and took them away.
Lamentations1:6 The beauty of Daughter Zion has gone away. Her princes were like deer that cannot find a meadow to feed in. They walk away without strength from those who chased them.
Lamentations1:7 Jerusalem thinks back. She remembers the time when she was hurt and when she lost her home. She remembers all the nice things that she had in the past. She remembers those nice things that she had in the old days. She remembers when her people were captured by the enemy. She remembers when there was no one to help her. When her enemies saw her

Lamentations1:12 All you who pass by on the road

Lamentations1:14 My sins were tied up like a yoke. My sins were tied up in the Lord's hands. His yoke is on my neck. He has made me weak. He has given me to those who I cannot stand up against.
Lamentations1:15 The Lord rejected all my powerful men who were inside the city. Then he brought a group of people against me. He brought them to kill my young soldiers. It was as if the Lord were trampling on grapes in a winepress

Ezekiel1:9 The wings touched each other. The living beings did not turn when they moved. They went in the direction they were looking.
Ezekiel1:10 Each living being had four faces. {In the front} they each had a man's face. There was a lion's face on the right side and a bull's face on the left side. There was an eagle's face on the back.

Ezekiel4:2 And then pretend you are an army surrounding the city. Build a dirt wall around the city to help you attack it. Build a dirt road leading up to the city wall. Bring battering rams and set up army camps around the city.
Ezekiel4:3 And then take an iron pan and put it between you and the city. It will be like an iron wall separating you and the city. In this way you will show that you are against it. You will surround the city and attack it. This is an example for the family of Israel to show that I will destroy Jerusalem.
Ezekiel4:4 Then you must lie down on your left side. You must do the thing that shows that you are taking the sins of the people of Israel on yourself. You will carry the guilt for as many days as you lie on your left side.
Ezekiel4:5 You must bear the guilt of Israel for 390 days. In this way I am telling you how long Israel will be punished; one day equals one year.
Ezekiel4:6 After that time
Ezekiel4:7 Now
Ezekiel4:8 Now look

Ezekiel4:12 You must make your bread each day. You must get dry human dung and burn it. Then you must cook the bread over this burning dung. You must eat this bread in front of the people.

Ezekiel17:1 Then the word of the Lord came to me. He said
Ezekiel17:2 Son of man
Ezekiel17:3 Say to them: 'A large eagle with big wings came to Lebanon. He had feathers covered with spots.
Ezekiel17:4 He broke the top out of the big cedar tree (Lebanon) and brought it to Canaan. He set the branch down in a city of merchants.
Ezekiel17:5 Then the eagle took some of the seeds from Canaan. He planted them in good soil by a good river.
Ezekiel17:6 The seeds grew and became a grapevine. It was a good vine. The vine was not tall
Ezekiel17:7 Then another eagle with big wings saw the grapevine. The eagle had many feathers. The grapevine wanted this new eagle to care for it. So it stretched its roots and branches toward the eagle. Its branches stretched toward this eagle. The branches grew away from the field where it was planted. The grapevine wanted the new eagle to water it.

Ezekiel44:21 None of the priests may drink wine when they go into the inner courtyard.
Ezekiel44:22 The priests must not marry a widow or a divorced woman. No
Ezekiel44:23 Also
Ezekiel44:24 The priests will be the judges in court. They will follow my laws when they judge people. They will obey my laws and my rules at all my special feasts. They will respect my special days of rest and keep them holy.

Amos1:1 This is the message of Amos
Amos1:2 Amos said: The Lord will shout like a lion in Zion. His loud voice will roar from Jerusalem. The green pastures of the shepherds will turn brown and die. Even Mount Carmel will become dry.
Amos1:3 This is what the Lord says: I will definitely punish the people of Damascus for the many crimes they did. They crushed the people of Gilead with iron threshing tools.
Amos1:4 So I will start a fire at Hazael's house that will destroy the great palaces of Ben-Hadad.
Amos1:5 I will also break open the gates of Damascus and remove the one who sits on the throne in the Valley of Aven. I will remove the symbol of power from Beth- Eden
Amos1:6 This is what the Lord says: I will definitely punish the people of Gaza for the many crimes they did. They took an entire nation of people and sent them as slaves to Edom.
Amos1:7 So I will start a fire at the wall of Gaza that will destroy the high towers in Gaza.
Amos1:8 And I will destroy the one who sits on the throne in Ashdod. I will destroy the king who holds the scepter in Ashkelon. I will punish the people of Ekron. Then the Philistines who are still left alive will die. This is what the Lord God said.
Amos1:9 This is what the Lord says: I will definitely punish the people of Tyre for the many crimes they did. They took an entire nation and sent them as slaves to Edom. They did not remember the agreement they had made with their brothers (Israel).
Amos1:10 So I will start a fire at the walls of Tyre that will destroy the high towers in Tyre.
Amos1:11 This is what the Lord says: I will definitely punish the people of Edom for the many crimes they did. Edom chased his brother (Israel) with the sword. Edom showed no mercy. Edom's anger continued forever—he kept tearing and tearing at Israel like a wild animal.
Amos1:12 So I will start a fire at Teman that will destroy the high towers of Bozrah.
Amos1:13 This is what the Lord says: I will definitely punish the Ammonites for the many crimes they did. They killed the pregnant women in Gilead. The Ammonites did this so that they could take that land and make their country larger.
Amos1:14 So I will start a fire at the wall of Rabbah that will destroy the high towers of Rabbah. Troubles will come to them like a whirlwind into their country.
Amos1:15 Then their kings and leaders will be captured. They will all be taken together. This is what the Lord said.
Amos2:1 This is what the Lord says: I will definitely punish the people of Moab for the many crimes they did. Moab burned the bones of the king of Edom into lime.
Amos2:2 So I will start a fire in Moab that will destroy the high towers of Kerioth. There will be terrible shouting and the sounds of a trumpet
Amos2:3 So I will bring an end to the kings of Moab
Amos2:4 This is what the Lord says: I will definitely punish Judah for the many crimes they did. They refused to obey the Lord's teachings and they did not keep his commands. Their ancestors believed lies
Amos2:5 So I will start a fire in Judah that will destroy the high towers of Jerusalem.
Amos2:6 This is what the Lord says: I will definitely punish Israel for the many crimes they have done. They sold honest people for a little silver. They sold the poor for the price of a pair of sandals.
Amos2:7 They pushed their faces into the ground and walked on them. They stopped listening to suffering people. Fathers and sons had sexual relations with the same woman. They ruined my holy name.
Amos2:8 They took clothes from the poor
Amos2:9 But it was I who destroyed the Amorites before them. They were tall like cedar trees and as strong as oak trees
Amos2:10 I was the one who brought you from the land of Egypt. For 40 years I led you through the desert. I helped you take the Amorites' land.
Amos2:11 I made some of your sons to be prophets and some of your young men to be Nazirites. People of Israel
Amos2:12 But you made the Nazirites drink wine. You told the prophets not to prophesy.
Amos2:13 You are like a heavy weight to me. I am bent low like a wagon loaded with too much straw.
Amos2:14 No one will escape—not even the fastest runner. Strong men will not be strong enough. Soldiers will not be able to save themselves.
Amos2:15 People with bows and arrows will not survive. Fast runners will not escape. People on horses will not escape alive.
Amos2:16 At that time even very brave soldiers will run away. They will not take the time to put their clothes on. This is what the Lord said.

Obadiah1:1 This is the vision of Obadiah. This is what the Lord God says about Edom: We heard a report from the Lord. A messenger was sent to the nations. He said
Obadiah1:2 Edom
Obadiah1:3 Your pride has fooled you. You live in those caves high on the cliff. Your home is high in the hills. So you say to yourself
Obadiah1:4 This is what the Lord says: Even though you fly high like the eagle and put your nest among the stars
Obadiah1:5 You really will be ruined! Thieves will come to you. Robbers will come in the night
Obadiah1:6 But the enemy will search hard for Esau's hidden treasures
Obadiah1:7 All those who are your friends will force you out of the land. Those who were at peace with you will trick you
Obadiah1:8 The Lord says
Obadiah1:9 Teman
Obadiah1:10 You will be covered with shame because you were very cruel to your brother Jacob. So you will be destroyed completely.
Obadiah1:11 You joined the enemies of Israel. Strangers carried Israel's treasures away. Foreigners entered Israel's city gate. They threw lots to decide what part of Jerusalem they would get. And you were right there with them
Obadiah1:12 You should not have laughed at your brother's trouble. You should not have been happy when they destroyed Judah. You should not have bragged at the time of their trouble.
Obadiah1:13 You should not have entered the city gate of my people and laughed at their problems. You should not have taken their treasures in the time of their trouble.
Obadiah1:14 You should have not stood where the roads cross
Obadiah1:15 The Day of the Lord is coming soon to all the nations. And the evil you did to others will happen to you. The same bad things will fall down on your own head.
Obadiah1:16 You spilled blood on my holy mountain
Obadiah1:17 But there will be survivors on Mount Zion. They will be my special people. The nation of Jacob will take back what belongs to it.
Obadiah1:18 The family of Jacob will be like a fire. The nation of Joseph will be like a flame. But the nation of Esau will be like ashes. The people of Judah will burn Edom
Obadiah1:19 Then people from the Negev will live on the mountain of Esau. And people from the foothills will take the Philistine lands. They will live in the land of Ephraim and Samaria. Gilead will belong to Benjamin.
Obadiah1:20 People from Israel were forced to leave their homes
Obadiah1:21 The winners will go up onto Mount Zion to rule the people who live on Esau's mountain. And the kingdom will belong to the Lord.
Jonah1:1 The Lord spoke to Jonah son of Amittai:
Jonah1:2 Nineveh is a big city. I have heard about the many evil things the people are doing there. So go there and tell them to stop doing such evil things.
Jonah1:3 But Jonah tried to run away from the Lord. He went to Joppa and found a boat that was going to the faraway city of Tarshish. Jonah paid money for the trip and went on the boat. He wanted to travel with the people on this boat to Tarshish and run away from the Lord.
Jonah1:4 But the Lord brought a great storm on the sea. The wind made the sea very rough. The storm was very strong
Jonah1:5 The men wanted to make the boat lighter to stop it from sinking
Jonah1:6 The captain of the boat saw Jonah and said
Jonah1:7 Then the men said to each other
Jonah1:8 Then the men said to Jonah

Jonah1:13 Instead
Jonah1:14 So the men cried to the Lord
Jonah1:15 So the men threw Jonah into the sea. The storm stopped

Jonah3:1 Then the Lord spoke to Jonah again and said
Jonah3:2 Go to that big city Nineveh
Jonah3:3 So Jonah obeyed the Lord and went to Nineveh. It was a very large city. A person had to walk for three days to travel through it.
Jonah3:4 Jonah went to the center of the city and began speaking to the people. He said
Jonah3:5 The people of Nineveh believed God. They decided to stop eating for a time to think about their sins. They put on special clothes to show they were sorry. All the people in the city did this
Jonah3:6 When the king of Nineveh heard about this
Jonah3:7 The king wrote a special message and sent it throughout the city: A command from the king and his great rulers: For a short time no person or animal should eat anything. No herd or flock will be allowed in the fields. Nothing living in Nineveh will eat or drink water.
Jonah3:8 But every person and every animal must be covered with a special cloth to show they are sad. People must cry loudly to God. Everyone must change their life and stop doing bad things.
Jonah3:9 Who knows? Maybe God will stop being angry and change his mind
Jonah3:10 God saw what the people did. He saw that they stopped doing evil. So God changed his mind and did not do what he planned. He did not punish the people.

Micah7:1 I am upset because I am like fruit that has been gathered
Micah7:2 By this I mean that all the faithful people are gone. There are no good people left. Everyone is planning to kill someone. Everyone is trying to trap their brother.
Micah7:3 People are good at doing bad things with both hands. Officials ask for bribes. Judges take money to change their decisions in court. Important leaders do whatever they want to do.
Micah7:4 Even the best of them is as crooked as a tangled thornbush. Your prophets said this day would come
Micah7:5 Don't trust your neighbor or trust a friend! Don't even speak freely with your wife.
Micah7:6 Your enemies will be the people in your own house. A son will not honor his father. A daughter will turn against her mother. A daughter-in-law will turn against her mother-in-law.
Micah7:7 So I will look to the Lord for help. I will wait for God to save me. My God will hear me.
Micah7:8 I have fallen
Micah7:9 I sinned against the Lord
Micah7:10 My enemy said to me
Micah7:11 The time will come when your walls will be rebuilt. At that time the country will grow.
Micah7:12 Your people will come back to your land. They will come back from Assyria and from the cities of Egypt. They will come from Egypt and from the other side of the Euphrates River. They will come from the sea in the west and from the mountains in the east.
Micah7:13 The land was ruined by the people who lived there and by what they did.
Micah7:14 So rule your people with your rod. Rule the flock of people who belong to you. That flock lives alone in the woods and up on Mount Carmel. That flock lives in Bashan and Gilead as they did in the past.
Micah7:15 I did many miracles when I took you out of Egypt. I will let you see more miracles like that.
Micah7:16 The nations will see those miracles
Micah7:17 They will crawl in the dust like a snake. They will shake with fear. They will be like insects crawling from their holes in the ground and coming to the Lord our God. God
Micah7:18 There is no God like you. You take away people's guilt. God will forgive his people who survive. He will not stay angry with them forever
Micah7:19 He will come back and comfort us again. He will throw all our sins into the deep sea.
Micah7:20 God
Nahum1:1 This book is the vision of Nahum from Elkosh. This is the sad message about the city of Nineveh.
Nahum1:2 The Lord is a jealous God. The Lord punishes the guilty
Nahum1:3 The Lord is patient
Nahum1:4 He will speak harshly to the sea
Nahum1:5 The Lord will come
Nahum1:6 No one can stand against his great anger. No one can endure his terrible anger. His anger will burn like fire. The rocks will shatter when he comes.
Nahum1:7 The Lord is good. He is a safe place to go to in times of trouble. He takes care of those who trust him.
Nahum1:8 But he will completely destroy his enemies. He will wash them away like a flood and chase them into the darkness.
Nahum1:9 Why are you making plans against the Lord? He will bring complete destruction
Nahum1:10 You will be destroyed completely like thornbushes burning under a pot. You will be destroyed quickly like dry weeds that burn fast.
Nahum1:11 Someone from Nineveh is making evil plans against the Lord. That advisor is a worthless troublemaker.
Nahum1:12 This is what the Lord said: The people of Assyria are at full strength. They have many soldiers
Nahum1:13 Now I will set you free from the power of Assyria. I will take the yoke off your neck and tear away the chains holding you.
Nahum1:14 King of Assyria
Nahum1:15 Judah

Habakkuk1:1 This is the message that was given to Habakkuk the prophet.
Habakkuk1:2 Lord
Habakkuk1:3 People are stealing things and hurting others. They are arguing and fighting. Why do you make me look at these terrible things?
Habakkuk1:4 The law is weak and not fair to people. Evil people win their fights against good people. So the law is no longer fair
Habakkuk1:5 Look at the other nations! Watch them
Habakkuk1:6 I will make the Babylonians a strong nation. They are cruel and powerful fighters. They will march across the earth. They will take houses and cities that don't belong to them.
Habakkuk1:7 The Babylonians will scare the other people. They will do what they want to do and go where they want to go.
Habakkuk1:8 Their horses will be faster than leopards and more dangerous than wolves at sunset. Their horse soldiers will come from faraway places. They will attack their enemies quickly
Habakkuk1:9 The one thing they all want to do is fight. Their armies will march fast like the wind in the desert. And the Babylonian soldiers will take many prisoners—as many as the grains of sand.
Habakkuk1:10 The Babylonian soldiers will laugh at the kings of other nations. Foreign rulers will be like jokes to them. The Babylonian soldiers will laugh at the cities with tall
Habakkuk1:11 Then they will leave like the wind and go on to fight against other places. The only thing the Babylonians worship is their own strength.
Habakkuk1:12 Lord
Habakkuk1:13 Your eyes are too good to look at evil. You cannot stand to see people do wrong. So why do you permit such evil? How can you watch while the wicked destroy people who are so much better?
Habakkuk1:14 You made people like fish in the sea. They are like little sea animals without a leader.
Habakkuk1:15 The enemy catches all of them with hooks and nets. The enemy catches them in his net and drags them in
Habakkuk1:16 His net helps him live like the rich and enjoy the best food. So the enemy worships his net. He makes sacrifices and burns incense to honor his net.
Habakkuk1:17 Will he continue to take riches with his net? Will he continue destroying people without showing mercy?
Habakkuk2:1 I will stand like a guard and watch. I will wait to see what the Lord will say to me. I will wait and learn how he answers my questions.
Habakkuk2:2 The Lord answered me
Habakkuk2:3 This message is about a special time in the future. This message is about the end
Habakkuk2:4 This message cannot help those who refuse to listen to it
Habakkuk2:5 Wine can trick a person. In the same way a strong man's pride can fool him
Habakkuk2:6 But soon enough
Habakkuk2:7 Strong man
Habakkuk2:8 You have stolen things from many nations
Habakkuk2:9 Look at you people! You get rich by cheating people
Habakkuk2:10 You planned shameful things
Habakkuk2:11 The stones of the walls will cry out against you. Even the wooden rafters in your own house will prove that you are wrong.
Habakkuk2:12 Look at them! They kill people to build their city and do wicked things to make their walled city strong.
Habakkuk2:13 But the Lord All-Powerful has decided that a fire will destroy everything that those people worked to build. All their work will be for nothing.
Habakkuk2:14 Then people everywhere will know about the Glory of the Lord. This news will spread just as water spreads out into the sea.
Habakkuk2:15 It will be very bad for those who become angry and make other people suffer. Like an angry drunk
Habakkuk2:16 But they will know the Lord's anger. It will be like a cup of poison in the Lord's right hand. They will taste that anger
Habakkuk2:17 You hurt many people in Lebanon and stole many animals there. So you will be afraid because of the people who died and because of the bad things you did to that country. You will be afraid because of what you did to those cities and to the people who lived there.
Habakkuk2:18 Their false god will not help them
Habakkuk2:19 Look at them! They speak to a wooden statue and tell it
Habakkuk2:20 But the Lord is different. The Lord is in his holy temple
Habakkuk3:1 The Shiggayon prayer of Habakkuk the prophet.
Habakkuk3:2 Lord
Habakkuk3:3 God is coming from Teman. The Holy One is coming from Mount Paran. Selah The Glory of the Lord covers the heavens
Habakkuk3:4 Rays of light shine from his hand
Habakkuk3:5 The sickness went before him
Habakkuk3:6 The Lord stood and judged the earth. He looked at the people of all the nations
Habakkuk3:7 I saw that the cities of Cushan were in trouble and that the houses of Midian trembled with fear.
Habakkuk3:8 Lord
Habakkuk3:9 Even then you showed your rainbow. It was proof of your agreement with the families of the earth. Selah And the dry land split the rivers.
Habakkuk3:10 The mountains saw you and shook. The water flowed off the land. The water from the sea made a loud noise as it lost its power over the land.
Habakkuk3:11 The sun and the moon lost their brightness. They stopped shining when they saw your bright flashes of lightning. That lightning was like spears and arrows shooting through the air.
Habakkuk3:12 In anger you walked on the earth and punished the nations.
Habakkuk3:13 You came to save your people and to lead your chosen king to victory. You killed the leader in every evil family
Habakkuk3:14 You used Moses' walking stick to stop the enemy soldiers. Those soldiers came like a powerful storm to fight against us. They thought they could defeat us easily
Habakkuk3:15 But you marched your horses through the deep water
Habakkuk3:16 My whole body shook when I heard the story. My lips trembled. I felt weak deep down in my bones and stood there shaking. But I will wait patiently for destruction to come to those who attack us.
Habakkuk3:17 Figs might not grow on the fig trees
Habakkuk3:18 But I will still be glad in the Lord and rejoice in God my savior.
Habakkuk3:19 The Lord God gives me my strength. He helps me run fast like a deer. He leads me safely on the mountains. To the music director. On my stringed instruments.

Matthew1:1 This is the family history of Jesus Christ. He came from the family of David and from the family of Abraham.
Matthew1:2 Abraham was the father of Isaac. Isaac was the father of Jacob. Jacob was the father of Judah and his brothers.
Matthew1:3 Judah was the father of Perez and Zerah. (Their mother was Tamar.) Perez was the father of Hezron. Hezron was the father of Ram.
Matthew1:4 Ram was the father of Amminadab. Amminadab was the father of Nahshon. Nahshon was the father of Salmon.
Matthew1:5 Salmon was the father of Boaz. (His mother was Rahab.) Boaz was the father of Obed. (His mother was Ruth.) Obed was the father of Jesse.
Matthew1:6 Jesse was the father of King David. David was the father of Solomon. (His mother had been Uriah's wife.)
Matthew1:7 Solomon was the father of Rehoboam. Rehoboam was the father of Abijah. Abijah was the father of Asa.
Matthew1:8 Asa was the father of Jehoshaphat. Jehoshaphat was the father of Jehoram. Jehoram was the father of Uzziah.
Matthew1:9 Uzziah was the father of Jotham. Jotham was the father of Ahaz. Ahaz was the father of Hezekiah.
Matthew1:10 Hezekiah was the father of Manasseh. Manasseh was the father of Amon. Amon was the father of Josiah.
Matthew1:11 Josiah was the grandfather of Jehoiachin and his brothers
Matthew1:12 After they were taken to Babylon: Jehoiachin was the father of Shealtiel. Shealtiel was the grandfather of Zerubbabel.
Matthew1:13 Zerubbabel was the father of Abiud. Abiud was the father of Eliakim. Eliakim was the father of Azor.
Matthew1:14 Azor was the father of Zadok. Zadok was the father of Achim. Achim was the father of Eliud.
Matthew1:15 Eliud was the father of Eleazar. Eleazar was the father of Matthan. Matthan was the father of Jacob.
Matthew1:16 Jacob was the father of Joseph. Joseph was the husband of Mary
Matthew1:17 So there were fourteen generations from Abraham to David. There were also fourteen generations from David until the people were taken away to Babylon. And there were fourteen more from the time the people were taken to Babylon until Christ was born.
Matthew1:18 This is how the birth of Jesus Christ happened. His mother Mary was engaged to marry Joseph. But before they married
Matthew1:19 Mary's husband
Matthew1:20 But after Joseph thought about this
Matthew1:21 She will give birth to a son. You will name him Jesus. Give him that name because he will save his people from their sins.
Matthew1:22 All this happened to make clear the full meaning of what the Lord said through the prophet:
Matthew1:23 The virgin will be pregnant and will give birth to a son. They will name him Immanuel. (Immanuel means God with us.)
Matthew1:24 When Joseph woke up
Matthew1:25 But Joseph did not have sexual relations with her until her son was born. And he named him Jesus.

Matthew2:23 He went to a town called Nazareth and lived there. This gave full meaning to what God said through the prophets. God said the Christ would be called a Nazarene.

Matthew10:7 When you go

Matthew10:16 Listen! I am sending you
Matthew10:17 Be careful! There are people who will arrest you and take you to be judged. They will whip you in their synagogues.
Matthew10:18 You will be taken to stand before governors and kings. People will do this to you because you follow me. You will tell about me to those kings and governors and to the non-Jewish people.
Matthew10:19 When you are arrested
Matthew10:20 It will not really be you speaking; the Spirit of your Father will be speaking through you.
Matthew10:21 Brothers will turn against their own brothers and hand them over to be killed. Fathers will hand over their own children to be killed. Children will fight against their own parents and will have them killed.
Matthew10:22 Everyone will hate you because you follow me. But the one who remains faithful to the end will be saved.
Matthew10:23 When you are treated badly in one city

Matthew10:31 So don't be afraid. You are worth more than a whole flock of birds.

Matthew10:33 But if you stand before others and say you do not believe in me
Matthew10:34 Do not think that I have come to bring peace to the earth. I did not come to bring peace. I came to bring trouble.

Matthew10:36 Even members of your own family will be your enemies.'
Matthew10:37 Those who love their father or mother more than they love me are not worthy of me. And those who love their son or daughter more than they love me are not worthy of me.
Matthew10:38 Those who will not accept the cross that is given to them when they follow me are not worthy of me.
Matthew10:39 Those who try to keep the life they have will lose it. But those who give up their life for me will find true life.

Matthew13:19 What about the seed that fell by the path? That is like the people who hear the teaching about God's kingdom but do not understand it. The Evil One comes and takes away what was planted in their hearts.
Matthew13:20 And what about the seed that fell on rocky ground? That is like the people who hear the teaching and quickly and gladly accept it.
Matthew13:21 But they do not let the teaching go deep into their lives. They keep it only a short time. As soon as trouble or persecution comes because of the teaching they accepted
Matthew13:22 And what about the seed that fell among the thorny weeds? That is like the people who hear the teaching but let worries about this life and love for money stop it from growing. So it does not produce a crop in their lives.
Matthew13:23 But what about the seed that fell on the good ground? That is like the people who hear the teaching and understand it. They grow and produce a good crop
