Matthew23:1 Then Jesus spoke to the people and to his followers. He said
Matthew23:2 The teachers of the law and the Pharisees have the authority to tell you what the Law of Moses says.
Matthew23:3 So you should obey them. Do everything they tell you to do. But their lives are not good examples for you to follow. They tell you to do things
Matthew23:4 They make strict rules that are hard for people to obey. They try to force others to obey all their rules. But they themselves will not try to follow any of those rules.
Matthew23:5 The only reason they do what they do is for other people to see them. They make the little Scripture boxes they wear bigger and bigger. And they make the tassels on their prayer clothes long enough for people to notice them.
Matthew23:6 These men love to have the places of honor at banquets and the most important seats in the synagogues.
Matthew23:7 They love for people to show respect to them in the marketplaces and to call them 'Teacher.'
Matthew23:8 But you must not be called 'Teacher.' You are all equal as brothers and sisters. You have only one Teacher.
Matthew23:9 And don't call anyone on earth 'Father.' You have one Father. He is in heaven.
Matthew23:10 And you should not be called 'Master.' You have only one Master
Matthew23:11 Whoever serves you like a servant is the greatest among you.
Matthew23:12 People who think they are better than others will be made humble. But people who humble themselves will be made great.
Matthew23:13 It will be bad for you teachers of the law and you Pharisees! You are hypocrites! You close the way for people to enter God's kingdom. You yourselves don't enter
Matthew23:14 ***
Matthew23:15 It will be bad for you teachers of the law and you Pharisees! You are hypocrites. You travel across the seas and across different countries to find one person who will follow your ways. When you find that person
Matthew23:16 It will be bad for you teachers of the law and you Pharisees! You guide the people
Matthew23:17 You are blind fools! Can't you see that the Temple is greater than the gold on it? It's the Temple that makes the gold holy!
Matthew23:18 And you say
Matthew23:19 You are blind! Can't you see that the altar is greater than any gift on it? It's the altar that makes the gift holy!
Matthew23:20 Whoever uses the altar to make a promise is really using the altar and everything on the altar.
Matthew23:21 And anyone who uses the Temple to make a promise is really using the Temple and God
Matthew23:22 Whoever uses heaven to make a promise is using God's throne and the one who is seated on it.
Matthew23:23 It will be bad for you teachers of the law and you Pharisees! You are hypocrites! You give God a tenth of everything you own—even your mint
Matthew23:24 You guide the people
Matthew23:25 It will be bad for you teachers of the law and you Pharisees. You are hypocrites! You wash clean the outside of your cups and dishes. But inside they are full of what you got by cheating others and pleasing yourselves.
Matthew23:26 Pharisees
Matthew23:27 It will be bad for you teachers of the law and you Pharisees! You are hypocrites! You are like tombs that are painted white. Outside they look fine
Matthew23:28 It is the same with you. People look at you and think you are godly. But on the inside you are full of hypocrisy and evil.
Matthew23:29 It will be bad for you teachers of the law and you Pharisees! You are hypocrites! You build tombs for the prophets. And you show honor to the graves of the godly people who were killed.
Matthew23:30 And you say
Matthew23:31 So you give proof that you are descendants of those who killed the prophets.
Matthew23:32 And you will finish the sin that your ancestors started!
Matthew23:33 You are snakes! You are from a family of poisonous snakes! You will not escape God. You will all be judged guilty and go to hell!
Matthew23:34 So I tell you this: I send to you prophets and teachers who are wise and know the Scriptures. You will kill some of them. You will hang some of them on crosses. You will beat some of them in your synagogues. You will chase them from town to town.
Matthew23:35 So you will be guilty for the death of all the good people who have been killed on earth. You will be guilty for the killing of that godly man Abel. And you will be guilty for the killing of Zechariah son of Berachiah. He was killed between the Temple and the altar. You will be guilty for the killing of all the good people who lived between the time of Abel and the time of Zechariah.
Matthew23:36 Believe me when I say that all of these things will happen to you people who are living now.
Matthew23:37 O Jerusalem
Matthew23:38 Now your house will be left completely empty.
Matthew23:39 I tell you

Matthew24:33 In the same way

Matthew24:35 The whole world
Matthew24:36 No one knows when that day or time will be. The Son and the angels in heaven don't know when it will be. Only the Father knows.
Matthew24:37 When the Son of Man comes
Matthew24:38 In those days before the flood

Matthew26:57 The men who arrested Jesus led him to the house of Caiaphas the high priest. The teachers of the law and the older Jewish leaders were gathered there.

Matthew27:1 Early the next morning
Matthew27:2 They tied him
Matthew27:3 Judas saw that they had decided to kill Jesus. He was the one who had handed him over. When he saw what happened
Matthew27:4 Judas said
Matthew27:5 So Judas threw the money into the Temple. Then he went out from there and hanged himself.
Matthew27:6 The leading priests picked up the silver coins in the Temple. They said
Matthew27:7 So they decided to use the money to buy a field called Potter's Field. This field would be a place to bury people who died while visiting in Jerusalem.
Matthew27:8 That is why that field is still called the Field of Blood.
Matthew27:9 This showed the full meaning of what Jeremiah the prophet said
Matthew27:10 They used those 30 silver coins to buy the potter's field
Matthew27:11 Jesus stood before Pilate the governor
Matthew27:12 Then
Matthew27:13 So Pilate said to him
Matthew27:14 But Jesus did not say anything
Matthew27:15 Every year at Passover time the governor would free one prisoner—whichever one the people wanted him to free.
Matthew27:16 At that time there was a man in prison who was known to be very bad. His name was Barabbas.
Matthew27:17 When a crowd gathered
Matthew27:18 Pilate knew that they had handed Jesus over to him because they were jealous of him.
Matthew27:19 While Pilate was sitting there in the place for judging
Matthew27:20 But the leading priests and older Jewish leaders told the people to ask for Barabbas to be set free and for Jesus to be killed.
Matthew27:21 Pilate said
Matthew27:22 Pilate asked
Matthew27:23 Pilate asked
Matthew27:24 Pilate saw that there was nothing he could do to make the people change. In fact
Matthew27:25 The people answered
Matthew27:26 Then Pilate set Barabbas free. And he told some soldiers to beat Jesus with whips. Then he handed him over to the soldiers to be killed on a cross.
Matthew27:27 Then Pilate's soldiers took Jesus into the governor's palace. All the soldiers gathered around him.
Matthew27:28 They took off Jesus' clothes and put a red robe on him.
Matthew27:29 Then they made a crown from thorny branches and put it on his head
Matthew27:30 They spit on him. Then they took his stick and kept hitting him on the head with it.
Matthew27:31 After they finished making fun of him
Matthew27:32 The soldiers were going out of the city with Jesus. They saw a man from Cyrene named Simon
Matthew27:33 They came to the place called Golgotha. (Golgotha means The Place of the Skull.)
Matthew27:34 There the soldiers gave Jesus some wine mixed with gall. But when he tasted it
Matthew27:35 The soldiers nailed Jesus to a cross. Then they threw dice to divide his clothes between them.
Matthew27:36 The soldiers stayed there to guard him.
Matthew27:37 They put a sign above his head with the charge against him written on it: this is jesus
Matthew27:38 Two criminals were nailed to crosses beside Jesus—one on the right and the other on the left.
Matthew27:39 People walked by and shouted insults at Jesus. They shook their heads
Matthew27:40 and said
Matthew27:41 The leading priests
Matthew27:42 They said
Matthew27:43 He trusted God. So let God save him now
Matthew27:44 And in the same way
Matthew27:45 At noon the whole country became dark. The darkness continued for three hours.
Matthew27:46 About three o'clock Jesus cried out loudly
Matthew27:47 Some of the people standing there heard this. They said
Matthew27:48 Quickly
Matthew27:49 But the others said
Matthew27:50 Again Jesus cried out loudly and then died.
Matthew27:51 When Jesus died
Matthew27:52 The graves opened
Matthew27:53 They came out of the graves. And after Jesus was raised from death
Matthew27:54 The army officer and the soldiers guarding Jesus saw this earthquake and everything that happened. They were very afraid and said
Matthew27:55 Many women were standing away from the cross
Matthew27:56 Mary Magdalene
Matthew27:57 That evening a rich man named Joseph came to Jerusalem. He was a follower of Jesus from the town of Arimathea.
Matthew27:58 He went to Pilate and asked to have Jesus' body. Pilate gave orders for the soldiers to give Jesus' body to him.
Matthew27:59 Then Joseph took the body and wrapped it in a new linen cloth.
Matthew27:60 He put Jesus' body in a new tomb that he had dug in a wall of rock. Then he closed the tomb by rolling a very large stone to cover the entrance. After he did this
Matthew27:61 Mary Magdalene and the other woman named Mary were sitting near the tomb.
Matthew27:62 That day was the day called Preparation day. The next day
Matthew27:63 They said
Matthew27:64 So give the order for the tomb to be guarded well for three days. His followers might come and try to steal the body. Then they could tell everyone that he has risen from death. That lie will be even worse than what they said about him before.
Matthew27:65 Pilate said
Matthew27:66 So they all went to the tomb and made it safe from thieves. They did this by sealing the stone in the entrance and putting soldiers there to guard it.
Matthew28:1 The day after the Sabbath day was the first day of the week. That day at dawn Mary Magdalene and the other woman named Mary went to look at the tomb.
Matthew28:2 Suddenly an angel of the Lord came from the sky
Matthew28:3 The angel was shining as bright as lightning. His clothes were as white as snow.
Matthew28:4 The soldiers guarding the tomb were very afraid of the angel. They shook with fear and then became like dead men.

Matthew28:20 Teach them to obey everything that I have told you to do. You can be sure that I will be with you always. I will continue with you until the end of time.
Mark1:1 The Good News about Jesus Christ
Mark1:2 with what the prophet Isaiah said would happen. He wrote: Listen! I will send my messenger ahead of you. He will prepare the way for you.
Mark1:3 There is someone shouting in the desert: 'Prepare the way for the Lord. Make the road straight for him.'
Mark1:4 So John the Baptizer came and was baptizing people in the desert area. He told them to be baptized to show that they wanted to change their lives
Mark1:5 All the people from Judea
Mark1:6 John wore clothes made from camel's hair and a leather belt around his waist. He ate locusts and wild honey.
Mark1:7 This is what John told the people: There is someone coming later who is able to do more than I can. I am not good enough to be the slave who stoops down to untie his sandals.
Mark1:8 I baptize you with water
Mark1:9 About that time Jesus came from the town of Nazareth in Galilee to the place where John was. John baptized Jesus in the Jordan River.
Mark1:10 As Jesus was coming up out of the water
Mark1:11 A voice came from heaven and said
Mark1:12 Then the Spirit sent Jesus into the desert alone.
Mark1:13 He was there for 40 days

Mark1:15 He said

Mark1:17 Jesus said to them

Mark1:20 Their father Zebedee and the men who worked for him were in the boat with the brothers. When Jesus saw the brothers
Mark1:21 Jesus and his followers went to Capernaum. On the Sabbath day
Mark1:22 They were amazed at his teaching. He did not teach like their teachers of the law. He taught like someone with authority.
Mark1:23 While Jesus was in the synagogue
Mark1:24 Jesus of Nazareth! What do you want with us? Did you come to destroy us? I know who you are— God's Holy One!
Mark1:25 Jesus
Mark1:26 The evil spirit made the man shake. Then the spirit made a loud noise and came out of him.
Mark1:27 The people were amazed. They asked each other
Mark1:28 So the news about Jesus spread quickly everywhere in the area of Galilee.
Mark1:29 Jesus and the followers left the synagogue. They all went with James and John to the home of Simon and Andrew.
Mark1:30 Simon's mother-in-law was very sick. She was in bed and had a fever. The people there told Jesus about her.
Mark1:31 So he went to her bed. Jesus held her hand and helped her stand up. The fever left her
Mark1:32 That night
Mark1:33 Everyone in the town gathered at the door of that house.
Mark1:34 Jesus healed many of those who had different kinds of sicknesses. He also forced many demons out of people. But he would not allow the demons to speak
Mark1:35 The next morning Jesus woke up very early. He left the house while it was still dark and went to a place where he could be alone and pray.
Mark1:36 Later
Mark1:37 They found him and said
Mark1:38 Jesus answered
Mark1:39 So Jesus traveled everywhere in Galilee. He spoke in the synagogues
Mark1:40 A man who had leprosy came to Jesus. The man bowed on his knees and begged him
Mark1:41 Jesus felt sorry for the man. So he touched him and said
Mark1:42 Immediately the leprosy disappeared
Mark1:43 Jesus told the man to go
Mark1:44 Don't tell anyone about what I did for you. But go and show yourself to the priest. And offer a gift to God because you have been healed. Offer the gift that Moses commanded. This will show everyone that you are healed.
Mark1:45 The man left there and told everyone he saw that Jesus had healed him. So the news about Jesus spread. And that is why he could not enter a town if people saw him. He stayed in places where people did not live. But people came from all the towns to the places where he was.

Mark3:3 Jesus said to the man with the crippled hand

Mark3:8 Many also came from Judea
Mark3:9 Jesus saw how many people there were
Mark3:10 He had healed many of them

Mark3:25 And a family that is divided will not survive.
Mark3:26 If Satan is against himself and is fighting against his own people
Mark3:27 Whoever wants to enter a strong man's house and steal his things must first tie him up. Then they can steal the things from his house.
Mark3:28 I want you to know that people can be forgiven for all the sinful things they do. They can even be forgiven for the bad things they say against God.
Mark3:29 But anyone who speaks against the Holy Spirit will never be forgiven. They will always be guilty of that sin.
Mark3:30 Jesus said this because the teachers of the law had accused him of having an evil spirit inside him.

Mark4:1 Another time Jesus began teaching by the lake
Mark4:2 Jesus used stories to teach them many things. One of his lessons included this story:
Mark4:3 Listen! A farmer went out to sow seed.
Mark4:4 While he was scattering the seed
Mark4:5 Other seed fell on rocky ground
Mark4:6 But then the sun rose and the plants were burned. They died because they did not have deep roots.
Mark4:7 Some other seed fell among thorny weeds. The weeds grew and stopped the good plants from growing. So they did not make grain.
Mark4:8 But some of the seed fell on good ground. There it began to grow
Mark4:9 Then Jesus said
Mark4:10 Later
Mark4:11 Jesus said
Mark4:12 I do this so that
Mark4:13 Then Jesus said to the followers
Mark4:14 The farmer is like someone who plants God's teaching in people.
Mark4:15 Sometimes the teaching falls on the path. That is like some people who hear the teaching of God. As soon as they hear it
Mark4:16 Other people are like the seed planted on rocky ground. They hear the teaching
Mark4:17 But they don't allow it to go deep into their lives. They keep it only a short time. As soon as trouble or persecution comes because of the teaching they accepted
Mark4:18 Others are like the seed planted among the thorny weeds. They hear the teaching
Mark4:19 but their lives become full of other things: the worries of this life
Mark4:20 And others are like the seed planted on the good ground. They hear the teaching and accept it. Then they grow and produce a good crop—sometimes 30 times more
Mark4:21 Then Jesus said to them
Mark4:22 Everything that is hidden will be made clear. Every secret thing will be made known.
Mark4:23 You people who hear me
Mark4:24 Think carefully about what you are hearing. God will know how much to give you by how much you understand now. But he will give you more than you deserve.
Mark4:25 The people who have some understanding will receive more. But those who do not have much will lose even the small amount they have.
Mark4:26 Then Jesus said
Mark4:27 The seed begins to grow. It grows night and day. It doesn't matter whether the man is sleeping or awake
Mark4:28 Without any help the ground produces grain. First the plant grows
Mark4:29 When the grain is ready
Mark4:30 Then Jesus said
Mark4:31 God's kingdom is like a mustard seed
Mark4:32 But when you plant it
Mark4:33 Jesus used many stories like these to teach the people. He taught them all they could understand.
Mark4:34 He always used stories to teach them. But when he was alone with his followers
Mark4:35 That day
Mark4:36 So they left the crowd behind and went with Jesus in the boat he was already in. There were also other boats that went with them.
Mark4:37 A very bad wind came up on the lake. The waves were coming over the sides and into the boat
Mark4:38 Jesus was inside the boat
Mark4:39 Jesus stood up and gave a command to the wind and the water. He said
Mark4:40 He said to his followers
Mark4:41 They were very afraid and asked each other

Mark5:12 The evil spirits begged Jesus
Mark5:13 So Jesus allowed them to do this. The evil spirits left the man and went into the pigs. Then the herd of pigs ran down the hill and into the lake. They were all drowned. There were about 2000 pigs in that herd.
Mark5:14 The men who had the work of caring for the pigs ran away. They ran to the town and to the farms and told everyone what happened. The people went out to see.
Mark5:15 They came to Jesus

Mark7:2 They saw that some of his followers ate food with hands that were not clean
Mark7:3 The Pharisees and all the other Jews never eat before washing their hands in this special way. They do this to follow the traditions they have from their great leaders who lived long ago.
Mark7:4 And when these Jews buy something in the market
Mark7:5 The Pharisees and teachers of the law said to Jesus
Mark7:6 Jesus answered
Mark7:7 Their worship of me is worthless. The things they teach are only human rules.'
Mark7:8 You have stopped following God's commands
Mark7:9 Then he said
Mark7:10 Moses said
Mark7:11 But you teach that people can say to their father or mother
Mark7:12 You are telling people that they do not have to do anything for their father or mother.
Mark7:13 So you are teaching that it is not important to do what God said. You think it is more important to follow those traditions you have
Mark7:14 Jesus called the people to him again. He said
Mark7:15 There is nothing people can put in their mouth that will make them wrong. People are made wrong by what comes from inside them.

Mark7:22 adultery
Mark7:23 These evil things come from inside a person. And these are the things that make people unacceptable to God.

Mark8:1 Another time there were many people with Jesus. The people had nothing to eat. So he called his followers to him and said
Mark8:2 I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days
Mark8:3 I should not send them home hungry. If they leave without eating
Mark8:4 Jesus' followers answered
Mark8:5 Then Jesus asked them
Mark8:6 Jesus told the people to sit on the ground. Then he took the seven loaves and gave thanks to God. He broke the bread into pieces and gave them to his followers. He told them to give the bread to the people
Mark8:7 The followers also had a few small fish. Jesus gave thanks for the fish and told them to give the fish to the people.
Mark8:8 They all ate until they were full. Then the followers filled seven baskets with the pieces of food that were left.

Luke1:1 Most Honorable Theophilus: Many others have tried to give a report of the things that happened among us to complete God's plan.
Luke1:2 What they have written agrees with what we learned from the people who saw those events from the beginning. They also served God by telling people his message.
Luke1:3 I studied it all carefully from the beginning. Then I decided to write it down for you in an organized way.
Luke1:4 I did this so that you can be sure that what you have been taught is true.
Luke1:5 During the time when Herod ruled Judea
Luke1:6 Zechariah and Elizabeth were both good people who pleased God. They did everything the Lord commanded
Luke1:7 But they had no children. Elizabeth could not have a baby
Luke1:8 Zechariah was serving as a priest before God for his group. It was his group's time to serve.
Luke1:9 The priests always chose one priest to offer the incense
Luke1:10 There was a large crowd outside praying at the time the incense was offered.
Luke1:11 Then
Luke1:12 When he saw the angel
Luke1:13 But the angel said to him
Luke1:14 You will be very happy

Luke3:24 Eli was the son of Matthat. Matthat was the son of Levi. Levi was the son of Melchi. Melchi was the son of Jannai. Jannai was the son of Joseph.
Luke3:25 Joseph was the son of Mattathias. Mattathias was the son of Amos. Amos was the son of Nahum. Nahum was the son of Esli. Esli was the son of Naggai.
Luke3:26 Naggai was the son of Maath. Maath was the son of Mattathias. Mattathias was the son of Semein. Semein was the son of Josech. Josech was the son of Joda.
Luke3:27 Joda was the son of Joanan. Joanan was the son of Rhesa. Rhesa was the son of Zerubbabel. Zerubbabel was the son of Shealtiel. Shealtiel was the son of Neri.
Luke3:28 Neri was the son of Melchi. Melchi was the son of Addi. Addi was the son of Cosam. Cosam was the son of Elmadam. Elmadam was the son of Er.
Luke3:29 Er was the son of Joshua. Joshua was the son of Eliezer. Eliezer was the son of Jorim. Jorim was the son of Matthat. Matthat was the son of Levi.
Luke3:30 Levi was the son of Simeon. Simeon was the son of Judah. Judah was the son of Joseph. Joseph was the son of Jonam. Jonam was the son of Eliakim.
Luke3:31 Eliakim was the son of Melea. Melea was the son of Menna. Menna was the son of Mattatha. Mattatha was the son of Nathan. Nathan was the son of David.
Luke3:32 David was the son of Jesse. Jesse was the son of Obed. Obed was the son of Boaz. Boaz was the son of Salmon. Salmon was the son of Nahshon.
Luke3:33 Nahshon was the son of Amminadab. Amminadab was the son of Admin. Admin was the son of Arni. Arni was the son of Hezron. Hezron was the son of Perez. Perez was the son of Judah.
Luke3:34 Judah was the son of Jacob. Jacob was the son of Isaac. Isaac was the son of Abraham. Abraham was the son of Terah. Terah was the son of Nahor.
Luke3:35 Nahor was the son of Serug. Serug was the son of Reu. Reu was the son of Peleg. Peleg was the son of Eber. Eber was the son of Shelah.
Luke3:36 Shelah was the son of Cainan. Cainan was the son of Arphaxad. Arphaxad was the son of Shem. Shem was the son of Noah. Noah was the son of Lamech.
Luke3:37 Lamech was the son of Methuselah. Methuselah was the son of Enoch. Enoch was the son of Jared. Jared was the son of Mahalaleel. Mahalaleel was the son of Cainan.
Luke3:38 Cainan was the son of Enos. Enos was the son of Seth. Seth was the son of Adam. Adam was the son of God.

John3:18 People who believe in God's Son are not judged guilty. But people who do not believe are already judged
John3:19 They are judged by this fact: The light has come into the world. But they did not want light. They wanted darkness
John3:20 Everyone who does evil hates the light. They will not come to the light
John3:21 But anyone who follows the true way comes to the light. Then the light will show that whatever they have done was done through God.
John3:22 After this
John3:23 John was also baptizing people in Aenon
John3:24 This was before John was put in prison.
John3:25 Some of John's followers had an argument with another Jew about religious washing.
John3:26 Then they came to John and said
John3:27 John answered
John3:28 You yourselves heard me say
John3:29 The bride always belongs to the bridegroom. The friend who helps the bridegroom just waits and listens. He is happy just to hear the bridegroom talk. That's how I feel now. I am so happy that he is here.
John3:30 He must become more and more important
John3:31 The one who comes from above is greater than all others. The one who is from the earth belongs to the earth. He talks about things that are on the earth. But the one who comes from heaven is greater than all others.
John3:32 He tells what he has seen and heard
John3:33 Whoever accepts what he says has given proof that God speaks the truth.
John3:34 God sent him
John3:35 The Father loves the Son and has given him power over everything.
John3:36 Whoever believes in the Son has eternal life. But those who do not obey the Son will never have that life. They cannot get away from God's anger.

John5:4 ***
John5:5 One of the men lying there had been sick for 38 years.

John5:22 Also

John5:24 I assure you
John5:25 Believe me
John5:26 Life comes from the Father himself. So the Father has also allowed the Son to give life.
John5:27 And the Father has given him the power to judge all people because he is the Son of Man.
John5:28 Don't be surprised at this. A time is coming when all people who are dead and in their graves will hear his voice.
John5:29 Then they will come out of their graves. Those who did good in this life will rise and have eternal life. But those who did evil will rise to be judged guilty.

John5:34 I don't need anyone to tell people about me
John5:35 John was like a lamp that burned and gave light
John5:36 But I have a proof about myself that is greater than anything John said. The things I do are my proof. These are what my Father gave me to do. They show that the Father sent me.
John5:37 And the Father who sent me has given proof about me himself. But you have never heard his voice. You have never seen what he looks like.
John5:38 The Father's teaching does not live in you
John5:39 You carefully study the Scriptures. You think that they give you eternal life. These same Scriptures tell about me!
John5:40 But you refuse to come to me to have that life.
John5:41 I don't want praise from you or any other human.
John5:42 But I know you—I know that you have no love for God.
John5:43 I have come from my Father and speak for him
John5:44 You like to have praise from each other. But you never try to get the praise that comes from the only God. So how can you believe?

John6:17 It was dark now

John6:22 The next day came. Some people had stayed on the other side of the lake. They knew that Jesus did not go with his followers in the boat. They knew that the followers had left in the boat alone. And they knew it was the only boat that was there.
John6:23 But then some boats from Tiberias came and landed near the place where the people had eaten the day before. This was where they had eaten the bread after the Lord gave thanks.

John6:35 Then Jesus said
John6:36 I told you before that you have seen me

John6:39 I must not lose anyone God has given me. But I must raise them up on the last day. This is what the one who sent me wants me to do.
John6:40 Everyone who sees the Son and believes in him has eternal life. I will raise them up on the last day. This is what my Father wants.

John6:42 They said
John6:43 But Jesus said

John6:45 It is written in the prophets: 'God will teach them all.' People listen to the Father and learn from him. They are the ones who come to me.
John6:46 I don't mean that there is anyone who has seen the Father. The only one who has ever seen the Father is the one who came from God. He has seen the Father.
John6:47 I can assure you that anyone who believes has eternal life.
John6:48 I am the bread that gives life.
John6:49 Your ancestors ate the manna God gave them in the desert
John6:50 Here is the bread that comes down from heaven. Whoever eats this bread will never die.
John6:51 I am the living bread that came down from heaven. Whoever eats this bread will live forever. This bread is my body. I will give my body so that the people in the world can have life.
John6:52 Then the Jews began to argue among themselves. They said
John6:53 Jesus said
John6:54 Those who eat my body and drink my blood have eternal life. I will raise them up on the last day.
John6:55 My body is true food
John6:56 Those who eat my body and drink my blood live in me
John6:57 The Father sent me. He lives
John6:58 I am not like the bread that your ancestors ate. They ate that bread

John6:61 Jesus already knew that his followers were complaining about this. So he said

John11:51 Caiaphas did not think of this himself. As that year's high priest
John11:52 Yes
John11:53 That day the Jewish leaders began planning to kill Jesus.

John12:1 Six days before the Passover festival

John12:4 Judas Iscariot
John12:5 That perfume was worth a full year's pay. It should have been sold

John12:41 Isaiah said this because he saw Jesus' divine greatness. So he spoke about him.

John12:48 But there is a judge for all those who refuse to believe in me and do not accept what I say. The message I have spoken will judge them on the last day.
John12:49 That is because what I taught was not from myself. The Father who sent me told me what to say and what to teach.
John12:50 And I know that whatever he says to do will bring eternal life. So the things I say are exactly what the Father told me to say.

Acts20:22 But now I must obey the Spirit and go to Jerusalem. I don't know what will happen to me there.
Acts20:23 I know only that in every city the Holy Spirit tells me that troubles and even jail wait for me.
Acts20:24 I don't care about my own life. The most important thing is that I finish my work. I want to finish the work that the Lord Jesus gave me to do—to tell people the Good News about God's grace.
Acts20:25 And now listen to me. I know that none of you will ever see me again. All the time I was with you

Romans1:19 This makes God angry because they have been shown what he is like. Yes

Romans1:21 People knew God

Romans1:26 Because people did those things
Romans1:27 In the same way
Romans1:28 People did not think it was important to have a true knowledge of God. So God left them and allowed them to have their own worthless thinking. And so they do what they should not do.

Romans1:31 they are foolish

Romans2:1 So do you think that you can judge those other people? You are wrong. You too are guilty of sin. You judge them
Romans2:2 God judges all who do such things
Romans2:3 And since you do the same things as those people you judge
Romans2:4 God has been kind to you. He has been very patient
Romans2:5 But you are so stubborn! You refuse to change. So you are making your own punishment greater and greater. You will be punished on the day when God will show his anger. On that day everyone will see how right God is to judge people.
Romans2:6 He will reward or punish everyone for what they have done.
Romans2:7 Some people live for God's glory
Romans2:8 But others are selfish and refuse to follow truth. They follow evil. God will show his anger and punish them.
Romans2:9 He will give trouble and suffering to everyone who does evil—to the Jews first and also to those who are not Jews.
Romans2:10 But he will give glory
Romans2:11 God judges everyone the same. It doesn't matter who they are.
Romans2:12 People who have the law and those who have never heard of the law are all the same when they sin. People who don't have the law and are sinners will be lost. And
Romans2:13 Hearing the law does not make people right with God. They will be right before him only if they always do what the law says.
Romans2:14 Those who are not Jews don't have the law. But when they naturally do what the law commands without even knowing the law
Romans2:15 They show that in their hearts they know what is right and wrong
Romans2:16 All this will happen on the day when God will judge people's secret thoughts through Jesus Christ. This is part of the Good News that I tell everyone.
Romans2:17 What about you? You say you are a Jew. You trust in the law and proudly claim to be close to God.
Romans2:18 You know what God wants you to do. And you know what is important
Romans2:19 You think you are a guide for people who don't know the right way
Romans2:20 You think you can show foolish people what is right. And you think you are a teacher for those who are just beginning to learn. You have the law
Romans2:21 You teach others
Romans2:22 You say they must not commit adultery
Romans2:23 You are so proud that you have God's law
Romans2:24 As the Scriptures say
Romans2:25 If you follow the law
Romans2:26 Those who are not Jews are not circumcised. But if they do what the law says
Romans2:27 You have the written law and circumcision
Romans2:28 You are not a true Jew if you are only a Jew in your physical body. True circumcision is not only on the outside of the body.
Romans2:29 A true Jew is one who is a Jew inside. True circumcision is done in the heart. It is done by the Spirit

1Corinthians12:3 So I tell you that no one who is speaking with the help of God's Spirit says
1Corinthians12:4 There are different kinds of spiritual gifts
1Corinthians12:5 There are different ways to serve
1Corinthians12:6 And there are different ways that God works in people
1Corinthians12:7 Something from the Spirit can be seen in each person. The Spirit gives this to each one to help others.
1Corinthians12:8 The Spirit gives one person the ability to speak with wisdom. And the same Spirit gives another person the ability to speak with knowledge.
1Corinthians12:9 The same Spirit gives faith to one person and to another he gives gifts of healing.
1Corinthians12:10 The Spirit gives to one person the power to do miracles
1Corinthians12:11 One Spirit
1Corinthians12:12 A person has only one body
1Corinthians12:13 Some of us are Jews and some of us are not; some of us are slaves and some of us are free. But we were all baptized to become one body through one Spirit. And we were all given the one Spirit.
1Corinthians12:14 And a person's body has more than one part. It has many parts.
1Corinthians12:15 The foot might say
1Corinthians12:16 The ear might say
1Corinthians12:17 If the whole body were an eye
1Corinthians12:18 If each part of the body were the same part
1Corinthians12:19 ***
1Corinthians12:20 So there are many parts
1Corinthians12:21 The eye cannot say to the hand
1Corinthians12:22 No
1Corinthians12:23 And the parts that we think are not worth very much are the parts we give the most care to. And we give special care to the parts of the body that we don't want to show.
1Corinthians12:24 The more beautiful parts don't need this special care. But God put the body together and gave more honor to the parts that need it.
1Corinthians12:25 God did this so that our body would not be divided. God wanted the different parts to care the same for each other.
1Corinthians12:26 If one part of the body suffers
1Corinthians12:27 All of you together are the body of Christ. Each one of you is a part of that body.
1Corinthians12:28 And in the church God has given a place first to apostles
1Corinthians12:29 Not all are apostles. Not all are prophets. Not all are teachers. Not all do miracles.
1Corinthians12:30 Not all have gifts of healing. Not all speak in different kinds of languages. Not all interpret those languages.
1Corinthians12:31 Continue to give your attention to the spiritual gifts you consider to be the greatest. But I want to point out a way of life that is even greater. And now I will show you the best way of all.

1Corinthians14:2 I will explain why. Those who have the gift of speaking in a different language are not speaking to people. They are speaking to God. No one understands them—they are speaking secret things through the Spirit.
1Corinthians14:3 But those who prophesy are speaking to people. They help people grow stronger in faith
1Corinthians14:4 Those who speak in a different language are helping only themselves. But those who prophesy are helping the whole church.

1Corinthians14:12 That's why you who want spiritual gifts so much should prefer those gifts that help the church grow stronger.

1Corinthians15:8 Last of all

1Corinthians15:10 But
1Corinthians15:11 So then it is not important if I told you God's message or if it was the other apostles who told you—we all tell people the same message

1Corinthians15:18 And those in Christ who have already died are lost.
1Corinthians15:19 If our hope in Christ is only for this life here on earth
1Corinthians15:20 But Christ really has been raised from death—the first one of all those who will be raised.
1Corinthians15:21 Death comes to people because of what one man did. But now there is resurrection from death because of another man.
1Corinthians15:22 I mean that in Adam all of us die. And in the same way
1Corinthians15:23 But everyone will be raised to life in the right order. Christ was first to be raised. Then
1Corinthians15:24 Then the end will come. Christ will destroy all rulers
1Corinthians15:25 Christ must rule until God puts all enemies under his control.
1Corinthians15:26 The last enemy to be destroyed will be death.
1Corinthians15:27 As the Scriptures say
1Corinthians15:28 After everything has been put under Christ
1Corinthians15:29 If no one will ever be raised from death
1Corinthians15:30 And what about us? Why do we put ourselves in danger every hour?
1Corinthians15:31 I face death every day. That is true
1Corinthians15:32 I fought wild animals in Ephesus. If I did that only for human reasons
1Corinthians15:33 Don't be fooled: Bad friends will ruin good habits.
1Corinthians15:34 Come back to your right way of thinking and stop sinning. Some of you don't know God. I say this to shame you.
1Corinthians15:35 But someone may ask
1Corinthians15:36 These are stupid questions. When you plant something
1Corinthians15:37 And when you plant something
1Corinthians15:38 But God gives it the body that he has planned for it
1Corinthians15:39 All things made of flesh are not the same: People have one kind of flesh
1Corinthians15:40 Also there are heavenly bodies and earthly bodies. But the beauty of the heavenly bodies is one kind
1Corinthians15:41 The sun has one kind of beauty
1Corinthians15:42 It will be the same when those who have died are raised to life. The body that is planted in the grave will ruin and decay
1Corinthians15:43 When the body is planted
1Corinthians15:44 The body that is planted is a physical body. When it is raised
1Corinthians15:45 As the Scriptures say
1Corinthians15:46 The spiritual man did not come first. It was the physical man that came first; then came the spiritual.
1Corinthians15:47 The first man came from the dust of the earth. The second man came from heaven.
1Corinthians15:48 All people belong to the earth. They are like that first man of earth. But those who belong to heaven are like that man of heaven.
1Corinthians15:49 We were made like that man of earth
1Corinthians15:50 I tell you this
1Corinthians15:51 But listen
1Corinthians15:52 It will only take the time of a second. We will be changed as quickly as an eye blinks. This will happen when the last trumpet blows. The trumpet will blow and those who have died will be raised to live forever. And we will all be changed.
1Corinthians15:53 This body that ruins must clothe itself with something that will never ruin. And this body that dies must clothe itself with something that will never die.
1Corinthians15:54 So this body that ruins will clothe itself with that which never ruins. And this body that dies will clothe itself with that which never dies. When this happens
1Corinthians15:55 O death
1Corinthians15:56 Death's power to hurt is sin
1Corinthians15:57 But we thank God who gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ!

2Corinthians9:4 If any of those from Macedonia come with me
2Corinthians9:5 So I thought that I should ask these brothers to come there before we do. They will help in getting together the generous gift you promised. Then it will be ready when we come
2Corinthians9:6 Remember this: The one who plants few seeds will have a small harvest. But the one who plants a lot will have a big harvest.
2Corinthians9:7 Each one of you should give what you have decided in your heart to give. You should not give if it makes you unhappy or if you feel forced to give. God loves those who are happy to give.
2Corinthians9:8 And God can give you more blessings than you need
2Corinthians9:9 As the Scriptures say
2Corinthians9:10 God is the one who gives seed to those who plant
2Corinthians9:11 God will make you rich in every way so that you can always give freely. And your giving through us will make people give thanks to God.
2Corinthians9:12 The service you are offering helps God's people with their needs
2Corinthians9:13 This service is a proof of your faith
2Corinthians9:14 And when they pray
2Corinthians9:15 Thanks be to God for his gift that is too wonderful to describe.

2Corinthians10:14 We would be boasting too much only if we had not already come to you. But we have come to you with the Good News of Christ.
2Corinthians10:15 We limit our boasting to the work that is ours. We don't boast about the work other people have done. We hope that your faith will continue to grow. We hope that you will help our work to grow much larger.

2Corinthians10:17 Whoever boasts should boast only about the Lord.
2Corinthians10:18 What people say about themselves means nothing. What counts is whether or not the Lord says they have done well.

2Corinthians11:3 But I am afraid that your minds will be led away from your true and pure following of Christ. This could happen just as Eve was tricked by that snake with his clever lies.
2Corinthians11:4 You seem to be quite patient with anyone who comes to you and tells you about a Jesus that is different from the Jesus we told you about. You seem very willing to accept a spirit or a message that is different from the Spirit and message that you received from us.

Galatians1:1 Greetings from Paul
Galatians1:2 Greetings also from all those in God's family who are with me. To the churches in Galatia:
Galatians1:3 I pray that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be good to you and give you peace.
Galatians1:4 Jesus gave himself for our sins to free us from this evil world we live in. This is what God our Father wanted.
Galatians1:5 The glory belongs to God forever and ever. Amen.
Galatians1:6 A short time ago God chose you to follow him. He chose you through his grace that came through Christ. But now I am amazed that you are already turning away and believing something different from the Good News we told you.
Galatians1:7 There is no other message that is good news
Galatians1:8 We told you the true Good News message. So anyone who tells you a different message should be condemned—even if it's one of us or even an angel from heaven!
Galatians1:9 I said this before. Now I say it again: You have already accepted the Good News. Anyone who tells you another way to be saved should be condemned!
Galatians1:10 Now do you think I am trying to make people accept me? No
Galatians1:11 Brothers and sisters
Galatians1:12 I did not get my message from any other human. The Good News is not something I learned from other people. Jesus Christ himself gave it to me. He showed me the Good News that I should tell people.
Galatians1:13 You have heard about my past life in the Jewish religion. I persecuted the church of God very much. I tried to destroy his people.
Galatians1:14 I was becoming a leader in the Jewish religion. I did better than most other Jews my own age. I tried harder than anyone else to follow the traditions we got from our ancestors.
Galatians1:15 But God had special plans for me even before I was born. So he chose me through his grace. He wanted me
Galatians1:16 to tell the Good News about his Son to the non- Jewish people. So God let me see and learn about his Son. When this happened
Galatians1:17 I did not go to Jerusalem to see those who were apostles before I was. But
Galatians1:18 Three years later I went to Jerusalem to meet Peter. I stayed with him 15 days.
Galatians1:19 I met no other apostles—only James
Galatians1:20 God knows there is nothing untrue in any of this.
Galatians1:21 Later
Galatians1:22 The groups of believers in Judea who belong to Christ had never met me before.
Galatians1:23 They had only heard this about me: This man was persecuting us. But now he is telling people about the same faith that he once tried to destroy.
Galatians1:24 These believers praised God because of me.

Galatians2:2 I went there because God showed me that I should go. I explained to them the message that I tell the non- Jewish people. Galatians2:4 We needed to talk about these problems
Galatians2:5 but we did not agree with anything those false brothers wanted. We wanted the truth of the Good News to continue for you.
Galatians2:6 Those men who were considered to be important did not change the Good News message I tell people. (It doesn't matter to me if they were important or not. To God everyone is the same.)
Galatians2:7 But these leaders saw that God had given me a special work
Galatians2:8 God gave Peter the power to work as an apostle for the Jewish people. God gave me the power to work as an apostle too
Galatians2:9 James

Galatians2:13 So Peter was a hypocrite. The other Jewish believers joined with him

Galatians2:15 We are Jews by birth. We were not born sinners
Galatians2:16 But we know that no one is made right with God by following the law. It is trusting in Jesus Christ that makes a person right with God. So we have put our faith in Christ Jesus
Galatians2:17 We Jews came to Christ to be made right with God
Galatians2:18 But I would be wrong to begin teaching again those things that I gave up.
Galatians2:19 It was the law itself that caused me to end my life under the law. I died to the law so that I could live for God. I have been nailed to the cross with Christ.
Galatians2:20 So I am not the one living now—it is Christ living in me. I still live in my body
Galatians2:21 I am not the one destroying the meaning of God's grace. If following the law is how people are made right with God
Galatians3:1 You people in Galatia are so foolish! Why do I say this? Because I told you very clearly about the death of Jesus Christ on the cross. But now it seems as though you have let someone use their magical powers to make you forget.
Galatians3:2 Tell me this one thing: How did you receive the Spirit? Did you receive the Spirit by following the law? No
Galatians3:3 You began your life in Christ with the Spirit. Now do you try to complete it by your own power? That is foolish.
Galatians3:4 You have experienced many things. Were all those experiences wasted? I hope they were not wasted!
Galatians3:5 Does God give you the Spirit because you follow the law? Does God work miracles among you because you follow the law? No
Galatians3:6 The Scriptures say the same thing about Abraham. Abraham believed God
Galatians3:7 So you should know that the true children of Abraham are those who have faith.
Galatians3:8 The Scriptures told what would happen in the future. These writings said that God would make the non-Jewish people right through their faith. God told this Good News to Abraham before it happened. God said to Abraham
Galatians3:9 Abraham believed this
Galatians3:10 But people who depend on following the law to make them right are under a curse. As the Scriptures say
Galatians3:11 So it is clear that no one can be made right with God by the law. The Scriptures say
Galatians3:12 The law does not depend on faith. No
Galatians3:13 The law says we are under a curse for not always obeying it. But Christ took away that curse. He changed places with us and put himself under that curse. The Scriptures say
Galatians3:14 Because of what Jesus Christ did
Galatians3:15 Brothers and sisters
Galatians3:16 God made promises to Abraham and his Descendant. The Scripture does not say
Galatians3:17 This is what I mean: The agreement that God gave to Abraham was made official long before the law came. The law came 430 years later. So the law could not take away the agreement and change God's promise.
Galatians3:18 Can following the law give us the blessing God promised? If we could receive it by following the law
Galatians3:19 So what was the law for? The law was given to show the wrong things people do. The law would continue until the special Descendant of Abraham came. This is the Descendant mentioned in the promise
Galatians3:20 But when God gave the promise
Galatians3:21 Does this mean that the law works against God's promises? Of course not. The law was never God's way of giving new life to people. If it were
Galatians3:22 But this is not possible. The Scriptures put the whole world in prison under the control of sin
Galatians3:23 Before this faith came
Galatians3:24 I mean the law was the guardian in charge of us until Christ came. After he came
Galatians3:25 Now that the way of faith has come

Galatians4:22 The Scriptures say that Abraham had two sons. The mother of one son was a slave woman
Galatians4:23 Abraham's son from the slave woman was born in the normal human way. But the son from the free woman was born because of the promise God made to Abraham.
Galatians4:24 This true story makes a picture for us. The two women are like the two agreements between God and his people. One agreement is the law that God made on Mount Sinai. The people who are under this agreement are like slaves. The mother named Hagar is like that agreement.
Galatians4:25 So Hagar is like Mount Sinai in Arabia. She is a picture of the earthly Jewish city of Jerusalem. This city is a slave
Galatians4:26 But the heavenly Jerusalem that is above is like the free woman
Galatians4:27 The Scriptures say
Galatians4:28 My brothers and sisters
Galatians4:29 But the other son of Abraham
Galatians4:30 But what do the Scriptures say? Throw out the slave woman and her son! The son of the free woman will receive everything his father has

Ephesians1:4 In Christ
Ephesians1:5 And before the world was made
Ephesians1:6 And this brings praise to God because of his wonderful grace. God gave that grace to us freely. He gave us that grace in Christ
Ephesians1:7 In Christ we are made free by his blood sacrifice. We have forgiveness of sins because of God's rich grace.
Ephesians1:8 God gave us that grace fully and freely. With full wisdom and understanding
Ephesians1:9 he let us know his secret plan. This was what God wanted
Ephesians1:10 God's goal was to finish his plan when the right time came. He planned that all things in heaven and on earth be joined together with Christ as the head.
Ephesians1:11 In Christ we were chosen to be God's people. God had already planned for us to be his people
Ephesians1:12 We Jews were the first to hope in Christ. And we were chosen so that we would bring praise to God in all his glory.
Ephesians1:13 It is the same with you. You heard the true message
Ephesians1:14 The Spirit is the first payment that guarantees we will get all that God has for us. Then we will enjoy complete freedom as people who belong to him. The goal for all of us is the praise of God in all his glory.
Ephesians1:15 That is why I always remember you in my prayers and thank God for you. I have done this ever since I heard about your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all of God's people.
Ephesians1:16 ***
Ephesians1:17 I always pray to the great and glorious Father
Ephesians1:18 I pray that God will open your minds to see his truth. Then you will know the hope that he has chosen us to have. You will know that the blessings God has promised his holy people are rich and glorious.
Ephesians1:19 And you will know that God's power is very great for us who believe. It is the same as the mighty power
Ephesians1:20 he used to raise Christ from death and put him at his right side in the heavenly places.
Ephesians1:21 He put Christ over all rulers
Ephesians1:22 God put everything under Christ's power and made him head over everything for the church.
Ephesians1:23 The church is Christ's body. It is filled with him. He makes everything complete in every way.
Ephesians2:1 In the past you were spiritually dead because of your sins and the things you did against God.
Ephesians2:2 Yes
Ephesians2:3 In the past all of us lived like that
Ephesians2:4 But God is rich in mercy
Ephesians2:5 We were spiritually dead because of all we had done against him. But he gave us new life together with Christ. (You have been saved by God's grace. )
Ephesians2:6 Yes
Ephesians2:7 God did this so that his kindness to us who belong to Christ Jesus would clearly show for all time to come the amazing richness of his grace.
Ephesians2:8 I mean that you have been saved by grace because you believed. You did not save yourselves; it was a gift from God.
Ephesians2:9 You are not saved by the things you have done
Ephesians2:10 God has made us what we are. In Christ Jesus
Ephesians2:11 You were not born as Jews. You are the people the Jews call uncircumcised.  Those Jews who call you uncircumcised call themselves circumcised. (Their circumcision is only something they themselves do to their bodies.)
Ephesians2:12 Remember that in the past you were without Christ. You were not citizens of Israel
Ephesians2:13 Yes
Ephesians2:14 Christ is the reason we are now at peace. He made us Jews and you who are not Jews one people. We were separated by a wall of hate that stood between us
Ephesians2:15 Christ ended the law with its many commands and rules. His purpose was to make the two groups become one in him. By doing this he would make peace.
Ephesians2:16 Through the cross Christ ended the hate between the two groups. And after they became one body
Ephesians2:17 Christ came and brought the message of peace to you non- Jews who were far away from God. And he brought that message of peace to those who were near to God.
Ephesians2:18 Yes
Ephesians2:19 So now you non-Jewish people are not visitors or strangers
Ephesians2:20 You believers are like a building that God owns. That building was built on the foundation that the apostles and prophets prepared. Christ Jesus himself is the most important stone in that building.
Ephesians2:21 The whole building is joined together in Christ
Ephesians2:22 And in Christ you are being built together with his other people. You are being made into a place where God lives through the Spirit.
Ephesians3:1 So I
Ephesians3:2 Surely you know that God gave me this work through his grace to help you.
Ephesians3:3 God let me know his secret plan by showing it to me. I have already written a little about this.
Ephesians3:4 And if you read what I wrote
Ephesians3:5 People who lived in other times were not told that secret truth. But now
Ephesians3:6 And this is the secret truth: that by hearing the Good News
Ephesians3:7 By God's special gift of grace
Ephesians3:8 I am the least important of all God's people. But he gave me this gift—to tell the non- Jewish people the Good News about the riches Christ has. These riches are too great to understand fully.
Ephesians3:9 And God gave me the work of telling all people about the plan for his secret truth. That secret truth has been hidden in him since the beginning of time. He is the one who created everything.
Ephesians3:10 His purpose was that all the rulers and powers in the heavenly places will now know the many different ways he shows his wisdom. They will know this because of the church.
Ephesians3:11 This agrees with the plan God had since the beginning of time. He did what he planned
Ephesians3:12 In Christ we come before God with freedom and without fear. We can do this because of our faith in Christ.
Ephesians3:13 So I ask you not to be discouraged because of what is happening to me. My sufferings are for your benefit—for your honor and glory.
Ephesians3:14 So I bow in prayer before the Father.
Ephesians3:15 Every family in heaven and on earth gets its true name from him.
Ephesians3:16 I ask the Father with his great glory to give you the power to be strong in your spirits. He will give you that strength through his Spirit.
Ephesians3:17 I pray that Christ will live in your hearts because of your faith. I pray that your life will be strong in love and be built on love.
Ephesians3:18 And I pray that you and all God's holy people will have the power to understand the greatness of Christ's love—how wide
Ephesians3:19 Christ's love is greater than anyone can ever know
Ephesians3:20 With God's power working in us
Ephesians3:21 To him be glory in the church and in Christ Jesus for all time
Ephesians4:1 So
Ephesians4:2 Always be humble and gentle. Be patient and accept each other with love.
Ephesians4:3 You are joined together with peace through the Spirit. Do all you can to continue as you are
Ephesians4:4 There is one body and one Spirit
Ephesians4:5 There is one Lord
Ephesians4:6 There is one God and Father of us all
Ephesians4:7 Christ gave each one of us a special gift. Everyone received what he wanted to give them.
Ephesians4:8 That is why the Scriptures say
Ephesians4:9 When it says
Ephesians4:10 So Christ came down
Ephesians4:11 And that same Christ gave these gifts to people: He made some to be apostles
Ephesians4:12 Christ gave these gifts to prepare God's holy people for the work of serving
Ephesians4:13 This work must continue until we are all joined together in what we believe and in what we know about the Son of God. Our goal is to become like a full-grown man—to look just like Christ and have all his perfection.
Ephesians4:14 Then we will no longer be like babies. We will not be people who are always changing like a ship that the waves carry one way and then another. We will not be influenced by every new teaching we hear from people who are trying to deceive us—those who make clever plans and use every kind of trick to fool others into following the wrong way.
Ephesians4:15 No
Ephesians4:16 and the whole body depends on him. All the parts of the body are joined and held together
Ephesians4:17 I have something from the Lord to tell you. I warn you: Don't continue living like those who don't believe. Their thoughts are worth nothing.
Ephesians4:18 They have no understanding
Ephesians4:19 They have lost their feeling of shame and use their lives to do what is morally wrong. More and more they want to do all kinds of evil.
Ephesians4:20 But that way of life is nothing like what you learned when you came to know Christ.
Ephesians4:21 I know that you heard about him
Ephesians4:22 You were taught to leave your old self. This means that you must stop living the evil way you lived before. That old self gets worse and worse
Ephesians4:23 You must be made new in your hearts and in your thinking.
Ephesians4:24 Be that new person who was made to be like God
Ephesians4:25 So you must stop telling lies. You must always speak the truth to each other
Ephesians4:26 When you are angry
Ephesians4:27 Don't give the devil a way to defeat you.
Ephesians4:28 Whoever has been stealing must stop it and start working. They must use their hands for doing something good. Then they will have something to share with those who are poor.
Ephesians4:29 When you talk
Ephesians4:30 And don't make the Holy Spirit sad. God gave you his Spirit as proof that you belong to him and that he will keep you safe until the day he makes you free.
Ephesians4:31 Never be bitter
Ephesians4:32 Be kind and loving to each other. Forgive each other the same as God forgave you through Christ.
Ephesians5:1 You are God's dear children
Ephesians5:2 Live a life of love. Love others just as Christ loved us. He gave himself for us—a sweet-smelling offering and sacrifice to God.
Ephesians5:3 But there must be no sexual sin among you. There must not be any kind of evil or selfishly wanting more and more
Ephesians5:4 Also
Ephesians5:5 You can be sure of this: No one will have a place in the kingdom of Christ and of God if that person commits sexual sins
Ephesians5:6 Don't let anyone fool you with words they don't really believe. God gets very angry when people who don't obey him talk like that.
Ephesians5:7 So don't have anything to do with them.
Ephesians5:8 In the past you were full of darkness
Ephesians5:9 This light produces every kind of goodness
Ephesians5:10 Try to learn what pleases the Lord.
Ephesians5:11 Have no part in the things that people in darkness do
Ephesians5:12 It is really very shameful to even talk about the things those people do in secret.
Ephesians5:13 But the light makes clear how wrong those things are.
Ephesians5:14 Yes
Ephesians5:15 So be very careful how you live. Live wisely
Ephesians5:16 I mean that you should use every opportunity you have for doing good
Ephesians5:17 So don't be foolish with your lives
Ephesians5:18 Don't be drunk with wine
Ephesians5:19 Encourage each other with psalms
Ephesians5:20 Always give thanks to God the Father for everything in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.
Ephesians5:21 Be willing to serve each other out of respect for Christ.

Ephesians5:24 The church serves under Christ
Ephesians5:25 Husbands

Ephesians5:27 Christ died so that he could give the church to himself like a bride in all her beauty. He died so that the church could be holy and without fault
Ephesians5:28 And husbands should love their wives like that. They should love their wives as they love their own bodies. The man who loves his wife loves himself
Ephesians5:29 because no one ever hates his own body
Ephesians5:30 because we are parts of his body.
Ephesians5:31 The Scriptures say
Ephesians5:32 That secret truth is very important—I am talking about Christ and the church.
Ephesians5:33 But each one of you must love his wife as he loves himself. And a wife must respect her husband.

Ephesians6:9 Masters
Ephesians6:10 To end my letter I tell you
Ephesians6:11 Wear the full armor of God. Wear God's armor so that you can fight against the devil's clever tricks.
Ephesians6:12 Our fight is not against people on earth. We are fighting against the rulers and authorities and the powers of this world's darkness. We are fighting against the spiritual powers of evil in the heavenly places.
Ephesians6:13 That is why you need to get God's full armor. Then on the day of evil you will be able to stand strong. And when you have finished the whole fight
Ephesians6:14 So stand strong with the belt of truth tied around your waist
Ephesians6:15 On your feet wear the Good News of peace to help you stand strong.
Ephesians6:16 And also use the shield of faith with which you can stop all the burning arrows that come from the Evil One.
Ephesians6:17 Accept God's salvation as your helmet. And take the sword of the Spirit — that sword is the teaching of God.

Ephesians6:24 God's grace to all of you who love our Lord Jesus Christ with love that never ends.
Philippians1:1 Greetings from Paul and Timothy
Philippians1:2 Grace and peace to you from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
Philippians1:3 I thank God every time I remember you.
Philippians1:4 And I always pray for all of you with joy.
Philippians1:5 I thank God for the help you gave me while I told people the Good News. You helped from the first day you believed until now.
Philippians1:6 I am sure that the good work God began in you will continue until he completes it on the day when Jesus Christ comes again.
Philippians1:7 I know I am right to think like this about all of you because you are so close to my heart. This is because you have all played such an important part in God's grace to me—now
Philippians1:8 God knows that I want very much to see you. I love all of you with the love of Christ Jesus.
Philippians1:9 This is my prayer for you: that your love will grow more and more; that you will have knowledge and understanding with your love;
Philippians1:10 that you will see the difference between what is important and what is not and choose what is important; that you will be pure and blameless for the coming of Christ;
Philippians1:11 that your life will be full of the many good works that are produced by Jesus Christ to bring glory and praise to God.
Philippians1:12 Brothers and sisters
Philippians1:13 All the Roman guards and all the others here know that I am in prison for serving Christ.
Philippians1:14 My being in prison has caused most of the believers to put their trust in the Lord and to show more courage in telling people God's message.
Philippians1:15 Some people are telling the message about Christ because they are jealous and bitter. Others do it because they want to help.
Philippians1:16 They are doing it out of love. They know that God gave me the work of defending the Good News.
Philippians1:17 But those others tell about Christ because of their selfish ambition. Their reason for doing it is wrong. They only do it because they think it will make trouble for me in prison.
Philippians1:18 But that doesn't matter. What is important is that they are telling people about Christ
Philippians1:19 because I know that your prayers and the help the Spirit of Jesus Christ gives me will cause this trouble to result in my freedom.
Philippians1:20 I am full of hope and feel sure I will not have any reason to be ashamed. I am certain I will have now the same boldness to speak freely that I always have. I will let God use my life to bring more honor to Christ. It doesn't matter whether I live or die.
Philippians1:21 To me
Philippians1:22 If I continue living here on earth
Philippians1:23 It would be a hard choice. Sometimes I want to leave this life and be with Christ. That would be much better for me;
Philippians1:24 however
Philippians1:25 I am sure of this
Philippians1:26 When I am there with you again
Philippians1:27 Just be sure you live as God's people in a way that honors the Good News of Christ. Then if I come and visit you or if I am away from you
Philippians1:28 And you will not be afraid of those who are against you. All of this is proof from God that you are being saved and that your enemies will be lost.
Philippians1:29 God gave you the honor of believing in Christ. But that is not all. He also gave you the honor of suffering for Christ. Both of these bring glory to Christ.
Philippians1:30 You saw the difficulties I had to face

Philippians2:2 then do what will make my joy complete: Agree with each other
Philippians2:3 In whatever you do
Philippians2:4 Don't be interested only in your own life
Philippians2:5 In your life together
Philippians2:6 He was like God in every way
Philippians2:7 Instead
Philippians2:8 he humbled himself by being fully obedient to God
Philippians2:9 So God raised him up to the most important place and gave him the name that is greater than any other name.
Philippians2:10 God did this so that every person will bow down to honor the name of Jesus. Everyone in heaven
Philippians2:11 They will all confess
Philippians2:12 My dear friends
Philippians2:13 Yes
Philippians2:14 Do everything without complaining or arguing
Philippians2:15 so that you will be blameless and pure
Philippians2:16 and you offer them the teaching that gives life. So I can be proud of you when Christ comes again. You will show that my work was not wasted—that I ran in the race and won.
Philippians2:17 Your faith makes you give your lives as a sacrifice in serving God. Maybe I will have to offer my own life with your sacrifice. But if that happens
Philippians2:18 You also should be glad and share your joy with me.
Philippians2:19 With the blessing of the Lord Jesus
Philippians2:20 I have no one else like Timothy
Philippians2:21 Others are interested only in their own lives. They don't care about the work of Christ Jesus.
Philippians2:22 You know the kind of person Timothy is. He has served with me in telling the Good News like a son with his father.
Philippians2:23 I plan to send him to you quickly
Philippians2:24 I am sure the Lord will help me come to you soon.
Philippians2:25 For now
Philippians2:26 but now he wants very much to see all of you again. He is worried because you heard that he was sick.
Philippians2:27 He was sick and near death. But God helped him and me too
Philippians2:28 So I want very much to send him to you. When you see him
Philippians2:29 Welcome him in the Lord with much joy. Give honor to people like Epaphroditus.
Philippians2:30 He should be honored because he almost died for the work of Christ. He put his life in danger so that he could help me. This was help that you could not give me.
Philippians3:1 And now
Philippians3:2 Be careful of the dogs—those people whose work does no one any good. They want to cut off everyone who isn't circumcised.
Philippians3:3 But we are the ones who have the true circumcision —we who worship God through his Spirit. We don't trust in ourselves or anything we can do. We take pride only in Christ Jesus.
Philippians3:4 Even if I am able to trust in myself
Philippians3:5 I was circumcised on the eighth day after my birth. I am from the people of Israel and the tribe of Benjamin. I am a true Jew
Philippians3:6 I was such a fanatic that I persecuted the church. And no one could find fault with how I always obeyed the law.
Philippians3:7 At one time all these things were important to me. But because of Christ
Philippians3:8 Not only these things
Philippians3:9 I want to belong to him. In Christ I am right with God
Philippians3:10 All I want is to know Christ and the power that raised him from death. I want to share in his sufferings and be like him even in his death.
Philippians3:11 Then there is hope that I myself will somehow be raised from death.
Philippians3:12 I don't mean that I am exactly what God wants me to be. I have not yet reached that goal. But I continue trying to reach it and make it mine. That's what Christ Jesus wants me to do. It is the reason he made me his.
Philippians3:13 Brothers and sisters
Philippians3:14 I keep running hard toward the finish line to get the prize that is mine because God has called me through Christ Jesus to life up there in heaven.
Philippians3:15 All of us who have grown to be spiritually mature should think this way too. And if there is any of this that you don't agree with
Philippians3:16 But we should continue following the truth we already have.
Philippians3:17 Brothers and sisters
Philippians3:18 There are many who live like enemies of the cross of Christ. I have often told you about them. And it makes me cry to tell you about them now.
Philippians3:19 The way they live is leading them to destruction. They have replaced God with their own desires. They do shameful things
Philippians3:20 But the government that rules us is in heaven. We are waiting for our Savior
Philippians3:21 He will change our humble bodies and make them like his own glorious body. Christ can do this by his power

Philippians4:2 Euodia and Syntyche

Philippians4:4 Always be filled with joy in the Lord. I will say it again. Be filled with joy.
Philippians4:5 Let everyone see that you are gentle and kind. The Lord is coming soon.
Philippians4:6 Don't worry about anything
Philippians4:7 And because you belong to Christ Jesus
Philippians4:8 Brothers and sisters
Philippians4:9 And do what you learned and received from me—what I told you and what you saw me do. And the God who gives peace will be with you.
Philippians4:10 I am so happy
Philippians4:11 I am telling you this
Philippians4:12 I know how to live when I am poor and when I have plenty. I have learned the secret of how to live through any kind of situation—when I have enough to eat or when I am hungry
Philippians4:13 Christ is the one who gives me the strength I need to do whatever I must do.
Philippians4:14 But it was good that you helped me when I needed help.
Philippians4:15 You people in Philippi remember when I first told the Good News there. When I left Macedonia
Philippians4:16 Several times you sent me things I needed when I was in Thessalonica.
Philippians4:17 Really
Philippians4:18 I have everything I need. I have even more than I need. I have all I need because Epaphroditus brought your gift to me. Your gift is like a sweet-smelling sacrifice offered to God. God accepts that sacrifice and it pleases him.
Philippians4:19 My God will use his glorious riches to give you everything you need. He will do this through Christ Jesus.
Philippians4:20 Glory to our God and Father forever and ever. Amen.

Philippians4:22 And greetings to you from all of God's people here
Philippians4:23 The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with you all.
Colossians1:1 Greetings from Paul
Colossians1:2 To the holy and faithful brothers and sisters in Christ who live in Colossae. Grace and peace to you from God our Father.
Colossians1:3 In our prayers we always thank God for you. He is the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
Colossians1:4 We thank him because we have heard about the faith you have in Christ Jesus and the love you have for all of God's people.
Colossians1:5 Your faith and love continue because you know what is waiting for you in heaven—the hope you have had since you first heard the true message
Colossians1:6 that was told to you. Throughout the world
Colossians1:7 You heard it from Epaphras
Colossians1:8 He also told us about the love you have from the Spirit.
Colossians1:9 Since the day we heard these things about you
Colossians1:10 that this will help you live in a way that brings honor to the Lord and pleases him in every way; that your life will produce good works of every kind and that you will grow in your knowledge of God;
Colossians1:11 that God will strengthen you with his own great power
Colossians1:12 and give thanks to the Father. He has made you able to have what he has promised to give all his holy people
Colossians1:13 God made us free from the power of darkness. And he brought us into the kingdom of his dear Son.
Colossians1:14 The Son paid the price to make us free. In him we have forgiveness of our sins.
Colossians1:15 No one can see God
Colossians1:16 Through his power all things were made: things in heaven and on earth
Colossians1:17 He was there before anything was made. And all things continue because of him.
Colossians1:18 Christ is the head of the body
Colossians1:19 God was pleased for all of himself to live in Christ.
Colossians1:20 And through Christ
Colossians1:21 At one time you were separated from God. You were his enemies in your minds
Colossians1:22 But now he has made you his friends again. He did this by the death Christ suffered while he was in his body. He did it so that he could present you to himself as people who are holy
Colossians1:23 And that is what will happen if you continue to believe in the Good News you heard. You must remain strong and sure in your faith. You must not let anything cause you to give up the hope that became yours when you heard the Good News. That same Good News has been told to everyone on earth
Colossians1:24 I am happy in my sufferings for you. There is much that Christ must still suffer through his body
Colossians1:25 I became a servant of the church because God gave me a special work to do. This work helps you. My work is to tell the complete message of God.
Colossians1:26 This message is the secret truth that was hidden since the beginning of time. It was hidden from everyone for ages
Colossians1:27 God decided to let his people know that rich and glorious truth
Colossians1:28 So we continue to tell people about Christ. We use all wisdom to counsel every person and teach every person. We are trying to bring everyone before God as people who have grown to be spiritually mature in Christ.
Colossians1:29 To do this
Colossians2:1 I want you to know that I am trying very hard to help you. And I am trying to help those in Laodicea and others who have never seen me.
Colossians2:2 I want them to be strengthened and joined together with love and to have the full confidence that comes from understanding. I want them to know completely the secret truth that God has made known. That truth is Christ himself.
Colossians2:3 In him all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge are kept safe.
Colossians2:4 I tell you this so that no one can fool you by telling you ideas that seem good
Colossians2:5 Even though I am far away
Colossians2:6 You accepted Christ Jesus as Lord
Colossians2:7 You must depend on Christ only
Colossians2:8 Be sure you are not led away by the teaching of those who have nothing worth saying and only plan to deceive you. That teaching is not from Christ. It is only human tradition and comes from the powers that influence this world.
Colossians2:9 I say this because all of God lives in Christ fully
Colossians2:10 And because you belong to Christ you are complete
Colossians2:11 In Christ you had a different kind of circumcision
Colossians2:12 When you were baptized
Colossians2:13 You were spiritually dead because of your sins and because you were not free from the power of your sinful self. But God gave you new life together with Christ. He forgave all our sins.
Colossians2:14 Because we broke God's laws
Colossians2:15 He defeated the rulers and powers of the spiritual world. With the cross he won the victory over them and led them away
Colossians2:16 So don't let anyone make rules for you about eating and drinking or about Jewish customs (festivals
Colossians2:17 In the past these things were like a shadow that showed what was coming. But the new things that were coming are found in Christ.
Colossians2:18 Some people enjoy acting as if they are humble and love to worship angels. They always talk about the visions they have seen. Don't listen to them when they say you are wrong because you don't do these things. It is so foolish for them to feel such pride
Colossians2:19 They don't keep themselves under the control of the head. Christ is the head
Colossians2:20 You died with Christ and were made free from the powers that influence this world. So why do you act as if you still belong to the world? I mean
Colossians2:21 Don't eat this
Colossians2:22 These rules are talking about earthly things that are gone after they are used. They are only human commands and teachings.
Colossians2:23 These rules may seem to be wise as part of a made-up religion in which people pretend to be humble and punish their bodies. But they don't help people stop doing the evil that the sinful self wants to do.

Colossians3:2 Think only about what is up there

Colossians3:11 In this new life it doesn't matter if you are a Greek or a Jew
Colossians3:12 God has chosen you and made you his holy people. He loves you. So your new life should be like this: Show mercy to others. Be kind
Colossians3:13 Don't be angry with each other
Colossians3:14 Together with these things
Colossians3:15 Let the peace that Christ gives control your thinking. It is for peace that you were chosen to be together in one body. And always be thankful.

Colossians4:2 Never stop praying. Be ready for anything by praying and being thankful.
