1Thessalonians1:2 We always remember you when we pray
1Thessalonians1:3 Every time we pray to God our Father we thank him for all that you have done because of your faith. And we thank him for the work you have done because of your love. And we thank him that you continue to be strong because of your hope in our Lord Jesus Christ.
1Thessalonians1:4 Brothers and sisters
1Thessalonians1:5 When we brought the Good News to you
1Thessalonians1:6 And you became like us and like the Lord. You suffered much
1Thessalonians1:7 You became an example to all the believers in Macedonia and Achaia.
1Thessalonians1:8 The Lord's teaching has spread from you throughout Greece and beyond. In fact
1Thessalonians1:9 People everywhere are already telling the story about the good way you accepted us when we were there with you. They tell about how you stopped worshiping idols and changed to serve the living and true God.
1Thessalonians1:10 And you began waiting for God's Son to come from heaven—the Son God raised from death. He is Jesus

1Thessalonians2:2 Before we came to you

1Thessalonians2:7 When we were with you
1Thessalonians2:8 We loved you very much
1Thessalonians2:9 Brothers and sisters
1Thessalonians2:10 When we were there with you believers
1Thessalonians2:11 You know that we treated each one of you like a father treats his own children.

1Thessalonians2:13 Also

1Thessalonians2:16 And they are trying to stop us from teaching those who are not Jews. They don't want them to be saved. But they are just adding more and more sins to the ones they already have. Now the time has come for them to suffer God's anger.
1Thessalonians2:17 Brothers and sisters
1Thessalonians2:18 Yes
1Thessalonians2:19 You are our hope
1Thessalonians2:20 You bring us honor and joy.

1Thessalonians3:4 Even when we were with you
1Thessalonians3:5 This is why I sent Timothy to you
1Thessalonians3:6 But now Timothy has come back from his visit with you and told us good news about your faith and love. He told us that you always remember us in a good way. He told us that you want very much to see us again. And it is the same with us—we want very much to see you.
1Thessalonians3:7 So
1Thessalonians3:8 Our life is really full if you stand strong in the Lord.
1Thessalonians3:9 We have so much joy before our God because of you! So we thank God for you. But we cannot thank him enough for all the joy we feel.
1Thessalonians3:10 Night and day we continue praying with all our heart that we can come there and see you again. We want to give you everything you need to make your faith strong.
1Thessalonians3:11 We pray that our God and Father and our Lord Jesus will prepare the way for us to come to you.
1Thessalonians3:12 We pray that the Lord will make your love grow. We pray that he will give you more and more love for each other and for all people. We pray that you will love everyone like we love you.
1Thessalonians3:13 This will strengthen your desire to do what is right
1Thessalonians4:1 Brothers and sisters
1Thessalonians4:2 You know all that we told you to do by the authority of the Lord Jesus.
1Thessalonians4:3 God wants you to be holy. He wants you to stay away from sexual sins.
1Thessalonians4:4 God wants each one of you to learn to control your own body. Use your body in a way that is holy and that gives honor to God.
1Thessalonians4:5 Don't let your sexual desires control you like the people who don't know God.
1Thessalonians4:6 Never wrong any of your fellow believers or cheat them in this way. The Lord will punish those who do that. We have already told you this and warned you about it.
1Thessalonians4:7 God chose us to be holy. He does not want us to live in sin.
1Thessalonians4:8 So anyone who refuses to obey this teaching is refusing to obey God
1Thessalonians4:9 We don't need to write to you about having love for your brothers and sisters in Christ. God has already taught you to love each other.
1Thessalonians4:10 In fact
1Thessalonians4:11 Do all you can to live a peaceful life. Mind your own business
1Thessalonians4:12 If you do these things
1Thessalonians4:13 Brothers and sisters
1Thessalonians4:14 We believe that Jesus died
1Thessalonians4:15 What we tell you now is the Lord's own message. Those of us who are still living when the Lord comes again will join him
1Thessalonians4:16 The Lord himself will come down from heaven with a loud command
1Thessalonians4:17 After that
1Thessalonians4:18 So encourage each other with these words.
1Thessalonians5:1 Now
1Thessalonians5:2 You know very well that the day when the Lord comes again will be a surprise
1Thessalonians5:3 People will say
1Thessalonians5:4 But you
1Thessalonians5:5 You are all people who belong to the light. You belong to the day. We don't belong to the night or to darkness.
1Thessalonians5:6 So we should not be like other people. We should not be sleeping. We should be awake and have self-control.
1Thessalonians5:7 People who sleep
1Thessalonians5:8 But we belong to the day
1Thessalonians5:9 God did not choose us to suffer his anger. God chose us to have salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ.
1Thessalonians5:10 Jesus died for us so that we can live together with him. It is not important if we are alive or dead when Jesus comes.
1Thessalonians5:11 So encourage each other and help each other grow stronger in faith
1Thessalonians5:12 Now brothers and sisters
1Thessalonians5:13 Show them the highest respect and love because of the work they do. Live in peace with each other.
1Thessalonians5:14 We ask you
1Thessalonians5:15 Be sure that no one pays back wrong for wrong. But always try to do what is good for each other and for all people.
1Thessalonians5:16 Always be full of joy.
1Thessalonians5:17 Never stop praying.
1Thessalonians5:18 Whatever happens
1Thessalonians5:19 Don't stop the work of the Holy Spirit.
1Thessalonians5:20 Don't treat prophecy like something that is not important.
1Thessalonians5:21 But test everything. Keep what is good
1Thessalonians5:22 and stay away from everything that is evil.
1Thessalonians5:23 We pray that God himself
1Thessalonians5:24 The one who calls you will do that for you. You can trust him.
1Thessalonians5:25 Brothers and sisters
1Thessalonians5:26 Give all the brothers and sisters the special greeting of God's people.
1Thessalonians5:27 I tell you by the authority of the Lord to read this letter to all the brothers and sisters.
1Thessalonians5:28 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
2Thessalonians1:1 Greetings from Paul
2Thessalonians1:2 Grace and peace to you from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
2Thessalonians1:3 We thank God for you always. And that's what we should do
2Thessalonians1:4 So we tell the other churches of God how proud we are of you. We tell them how you patiently continue to be strong and have faith
2Thessalonians1:5 This is proof that God is right in his judgment. He wants you to be worthy of his kingdom. Your suffering is for that kingdom.
2Thessalonians1:6 God will do what is right. He will punish those who are causing you trouble.
2Thessalonians1:7 And he will bring relief to you who are troubled. He will bring it to you and to us when the Lord Jesus comes from heaven for all to see
2Thessalonians1:8 He will come with burning fire to punish those who don't know God—those who refuse to accept the Good News about our Lord Jesus Christ.
2Thessalonians1:9 They will be punished with a destruction that never ends. They will not be allowed to be with the Lord but will be kept away from his great power.
2Thessalonians1:10 This will happen on the day when the Lord Jesus comes to receive honor with his holy people. He will be admired among all who have believed. And this includes you because you believed what we told you.
2Thessalonians1:11 That is why we always pray for you. We ask our God to help you live the good way he wanted when he chose you. The goodness you have makes you want to do good. And the faith you have makes you work. We pray that with his power God will help you do these things more and more.
2Thessalonians1:12 Then the name of our Lord Jesus will be honored because of you
2Thessalonians2:1 Brothers and sisters
2Thessalonians2:2 Don't let yourselves be easily upset or worried if you hear that the day of the Lord has already come. Someone might say that this idea came from us—in something the Spirit told us
2Thessalonians2:3 Don't be fooled by anything they might say. That day of the Lord will not come until the turning away from God happens. And that day will not come until the Man of Evil appears
2Thessalonians2:4 He will stand against and put himself above everything that people worship or think is worthy of worship. He will even go into God's Temple and sit there
2Thessalonians2:5 I told you before that all these things would happen. Remember?
2Thessalonians2:6 And you know what is stopping that Man of Evil now. He is being stopped now so that he will appear at the right time.
2Thessalonians2:7 The secret power of evil is already working in the world now. But there is one who is stopping that secret power of evil. And he will continue to stop it until he is taken out of the way.
2Thessalonians2:8 Then that Man of Evil will appear. But the Lord Jesus will kill him with the breath that comes from his mouth. The Lord will come in a way that everyone will see
2Thessalonians2:9 When that Man of Evil comes
2Thessalonians2:10 The Man of Evil will use every kind of evil to fool those who are lost. They are lost because they refused to love the truth and be saved.
2Thessalonians2:11 So God will send them something powerful that leads them away from the truth and causes them to believe a lie.
2Thessalonians2:12 They will all be condemned because they did not believe the truth and because they enjoyed doing evil.
2Thessalonians2:13 Brothers and sisters
2Thessalonians2:14 God chose you to have that salvation. He chose you by using the Good News that we told you. You were chosen so that you can share in the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.
2Thessalonians2:15 So
2Thessalonians2:16 We pray that the Lord Jesus Christ himself and God our Father will comfort you and strengthen you in every good thing you do and say. God loved us and gave us through his grace a wonderful hope and comfort that has no end.
2Thessalonians3:1 And now
2Thessalonians3:2 And pray that we will be protected from crooked and evil people. Not everyone believes in the Lord
2Thessalonians3:3 But the Lord is faithful. He will give you strength and protect you from the Evil One.
2Thessalonians3:4 The Lord gives us confidence that you are doing what we told you and that you will continue to do it.
2Thessalonians3:5 We pray that the Lord will cause you to feel God's love and remember Christ's patient endurance.
2Thessalonians3:6 Brothers and sisters
2Thessalonians3:7 You yourselves know that you should live like we do. We were not lazy when we were with you.
2Thessalonians3:8 We never accepted food from anyone without paying for it. We worked and worked so that we would not be a burden to any of you. We worked night and day.
2Thessalonians3:9 We had the right to ask you to help us. But we worked to take care of ourselves so that we would be an example for you to follow.
2Thessalonians3:10 When we were with you
2Thessalonians3:11 We hear that some people in your group refuse to work. They are doing nothing except being busy in the lives of others.
2Thessalonians3:12 Our instruction to them is to stop bothering others
2Thessalonians3:13 Brothers and sisters
2Thessalonians3:14 If there are some there who refuse to do what we tell you in this letter
2Thessalonians3:15 But don't treat them as enemies. Counsel them as fellow believers.
2Thessalonians3:16 We pray that the Lord of peace will give you peace at all times and in every way. The Lord be with you all.
2Thessalonians3:17 Here's my greeting in my own handwriting— Paul. I do this in all my letters to show they are from me. This is the way I write.
2Thessalonians3:18 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all.
1Timothy1:1 Greetings from Paul
1Timothy1:2 To Timothy
1Timothy1:3 When I went to Macedonia
1Timothy1:4 Tell them not to give their time to meaningless stories and to long lists of names to prove their family histories. Such things only cause arguments. They don't help God's work
1Timothy1:5 My purpose in telling you to do this is to promote love—the kind of love shown by those whose thoughts are pure
1Timothy1:6 But some have missed this key point in their teaching and have gone off in another direction. Now they talk about things that help no one.
1Timothy1:7 They want to be teachers of the law
1Timothy1:8 We know that the law is good if someone uses it right.
1Timothy1:9 We also know that the law is not made for those who do what is right. It is made for those who are against the law and refuse to follow it. The law is for sinners who are against God and all that is pleasing to him. It is for those who have no interest in spiritual things and for those who kill their fathers or mothers or anyone else.
1Timothy1:10 It is for those who commit sexual sins
1Timothy1:11 That teaching is part of the Good News that our blessed God gave me to tell. In it we see his glory.
1Timothy1:12 I thank Christ Jesus our Lord because he trusted me and gave me this work of serving him. He gives me strength.
1Timothy1:13 In the past I insulted Christ. As a proud and violent man
1Timothy1:14 But our Lord gave me a full measure of his grace. And with that grace came the faith and love that are in Christ Jesus.
1Timothy1:15 Here is a true statement that should be accepted without question: Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners
1Timothy1:16 But I was given mercy so that in me Christ Jesus could show that he has patience without limit. Christ showed his patience with me
1Timothy1:17 Honor and glory to the King who rules forever. He cannot be destroyed and cannot be seen. Honor and glory forever and ever to the only God. Amen.
1Timothy1:18 Timothy
1Timothy1:19 Continue to trust in God and do what you know is right. Some people have not done this
1Timothy1:20 Hymenaeus and Alexander are men like that. I have given them to Satan so that they will learn not to speak against God.
1Timothy2:1 First of all
1Timothy2:2 You should pray for rulers and for all who have authority. Pray for these leaders so that we can live quiet and peaceful lives— lives full of devotion to God and respect for him.
1Timothy2:3 This is good and pleases God our Savior.
1Timothy2:4 God wants everyone to be saved and to fully understand the truth.
1Timothy2:5 There is only one God
1Timothy2:6 gave himself to pay for everyone to be free. This is the message that was given to us at just the right time.
1Timothy2:7 And I was chosen as an apostle to tell people that message. (I am telling the truth. I am not lying.) I was chosen to teach those who are not Jews to believe and understand the truth.
1Timothy2:8 I want the men everywhere to pray. Men who lift their hands in prayer must be devoted to God and pleasing to him. They must be men who keep themselves from getting angry and having arguments.
1Timothy2:9 And I want the women to make themselves attractive in the right way. Their clothes should be sensible and appropriate. They should not draw attention to themselves with fancy hairstyles or gold jewelry or pearls or expensive clothes.
1Timothy2:10 But they should make themselves attractive by the good things they do. That is more appropriate for women who say they are devoted to God.
1Timothy2:11 A woman should learn while listening quietly and being completely willing to obey.
1Timothy2:12 I don't allow a woman to teach a man or tell him what to do. She must listen quietly
1Timothy2:13 because Adam was made first. Eve was made later.
1Timothy2:14 Also
1Timothy2:15 But women will be saved in their work of having children. They will be saved if they continue to live in faith
1Timothy3:1 It is a true statement that anyone whose goal is to serve as an elder has his heart set on a good work.
1Timothy3:2 An elder must be such a good man that no one can rightly criticize him. He must be faithful to his wife. He must have self-control and be wise. He must be respected by others. He must be ready to help people by welcoming them into his home. He must be a good teacher.
1Timothy3:3 He must not drink too much
1Timothy3:4 He must be a good leader of his own family. This means that his children obey him with full respect.
1Timothy3:5 (If a man does not know how to lead his own family
1Timothy3:6 An elder must not be a new believer. It might make him too proud of himself. Then he would be condemned for his pride the same as the devil was.
1Timothy3:7 An elder must also have the respect of people who are not part of the church. Then he will not be criticized by others and be caught in the devil's trap.
1Timothy3:8 In the same way
1Timothy3:9 They must follow the true faith that God has now made known to us and always do what they know is right.
1Timothy3:10 You should test them first. Then
1Timothy3:11 In the same way
1Timothy3:12 The men who are special servants must be faithful in marriage. They must be good leaders of children and their own families.
1Timothy3:13 Those who do well as special servants are making an honorable place for themselves. And they will feel very sure of their faith in Christ Jesus.
1Timothy3:14 I hope I can come to you soon. But I am writing this to you now
1Timothy3:15 so that
1Timothy3:16 Without a doubt
1Timothy4:1 The Spirit clearly says that in the last times some will turn away from what we believe. They will obey spirits that tell lies. And they will follow the teachings of demons.
1Timothy4:2 Those teachings come through people who tell lies and trick others. These evil people cannot see what is right and what is wrong. It is like their conscience has been destroyed with a hot iron.
1Timothy4:3 They say that it is wrong to marry. And they say that there are some foods that people must not eat. But God made these foods
1Timothy4:4 Everything that God made is good. Nothing he made should be refused if it is accepted with thanks to him.
1Timothy4:5 Everything he created is made holy by what he has said and by prayer.
1Timothy4:6 Tell this to the brothers and sisters there. This will show that you are a good servant of Christ Jesus. You will show that you are made strong by the words of faith and good teaching you have followed.
1Timothy4:7 People tell silly stories that don't agree with God's truth. Don't follow what these stories teach. But teach yourself to be devoted to God.
1Timothy4:8 Training your body helps you in some ways. But devotion to God helps you in every way. It brings you blessings in this life and in the future life too.
1Timothy4:9 Here is a true statement that should be accepted without question:
1Timothy4:10 We hope in the living God
1Timothy4:11 Command and teach these things.
1Timothy4:12 You are young
1Timothy4:13 Continue to read the Scriptures to the people
1Timothy4:14 Remember to use the gift you have
1Timothy4:15 Continue to do these things. Give your life to doing them. Then everyone can see that your work is progressing.
1Timothy4:16 Be careful in your life and in your teaching. Continue to live and teach rightly. Then you will save yourself and those who listen to your teaching.
1Timothy5:1 Don't speak angrily to an older man. But talk to him like he was your father. Treat the younger men like brothers.
1Timothy5:2 Treat the older women like mothers. And treat the younger women with respect like sisters.
1Timothy5:3 Take care of widows who really need help.
1Timothy5:4 But if a widow has children or grandchildren
1Timothy5:5 A widow who really needs help is one who has been left all alone. She trusts God to take care of her. She prays all the time
1Timothy5:6 But the widow who uses her life to please herself is really dead while she is still living.
1Timothy5:7 Tell the believers there to take care of their family so that no one can say they are doing wrong.
1Timothy5:8 Everyone should take care of all their own people. Most important
1Timothy5:9 To be added to your list of widows
1Timothy5:10 She must be known for the good she has done: raising children
1Timothy5:11 But don't put younger widows on that list. When their strong physical needs pull them away from their commitment to Christ
1Timothy5:12 Then they will be guilty of not doing what they first promised to do.
1Timothy5:13 Also
1Timothy5:14 So I want the younger widows to marry
1Timothy5:15 But some of the younger widows have already turned away to follow Satan.
1Timothy5:16 If any woman who is a believer has widows in her family
1Timothy5:17 The elders who lead the church in a good way should receive double honor — in particular
1Timothy5:18 As the Scriptures say
1Timothy5:19 Don't listen to someone who accuses an elder. You should listen to them only if there are two or three others who can say what the elder did wrong.
1Timothy5:20 Tell those who sin that they are wrong. Do this in front of the whole church so that the others will have a warning.
1Timothy5:21 Before God and Jesus Christ and the chosen angels
1Timothy5:22 Think carefully before you lay your hands on anyone to make him an elder. Don't share in the sins of others. Keep yourself pure.
1Timothy5:23 Timothy
1Timothy5:24 The sins of some people are easy to see. Their sins show that they will be judged. But the sins of some others are seen only later.
1Timothy5:25 It is the same with the good things people do. Some are easy to see. But even if they are not obvious now
1Timothy6:1 All those who are slaves should show full respect to their masters. Then God's name and our teaching will not be criticized.
1Timothy6:2 Some slaves have masters who are believers
1Timothy6:3 Some people will teach what is false and will not agree with the true teaching of our Lord Jesus Christ. They will not accept the teaching that produces a life of devotion to God.
1Timothy6:4 They are proud of what they know
1Timothy6:5 They are always making trouble
1Timothy6:6 Devotion to God is
1Timothy6:7 When we came into the world
1Timothy6:8 So
1Timothy6:9 People who want to be rich bring temptations to themselves. They are caught in a trap. They begin to want many foolish things that will hurt them. These things ruin and destroy people.
1Timothy6:10 The love of money causes all kinds of evil. Some people have turned away from what we believe because they want to get more and more money. But they have caused themselves a lot of pain and sorrow.
1Timothy6:11 But you belong to God. So you should stay away from all those things. Always try to do what is right
1Timothy6:12 We have to fight to keep our faith. Try as hard as you can to win that fight. Take hold of eternal life. It is the life you were chosen to have when you confessed your faith in Jesus—that wonderful truth that you spoke so openly and that so many people heard.
1Timothy6:13 Before God and Christ Jesus I give you a command. Jesus is the one who confessed that same wonderful truth when he stood before Pontius Pilate. And God is the one who gives life to everything. Now I tell you:
1Timothy6:14 Do what you were commanded to do without fault or blame until the time when our Lord Jesus Christ comes again.
1Timothy6:15 God will make that happen at the right time. God is the blessed and only Ruler. He is the King of all kings and the Lord of all lords.
1Timothy6:16 God is the only one who never dies. He lives in light so bright that people cannot go near it. No one has ever seen him; no one is able to see him. All honor and power belong to him forever. Amen.
1Timothy6:17 Give this command to those who are rich with the things of this world. Tell them not to be proud. Tell them to hope in God
1Timothy6:18 Tell those who are rich to do good—to be rich in good works. And tell them they should be happy to give and ready to share.
1Timothy6:19 By doing this
1Timothy6:20 Timothy
1Timothy6:21 Some people who claim to have that knowledge have gone completely away from what we believe. God's grace be with you all.
2Timothy1:1 Greetings from Paul
2Timothy1:2 To Timothy
2Timothy1:3 I always remember you in my prayers day and night. And in these prayers I thank God for you. He is the God my ancestors served
2Timothy1:4 I remember that you cried for me. I want very much to see you so that I can be filled with joy.
2Timothy1:5 I remember your true faith. That kind of faith first belonged to your grandmother Lois and to your mother Eunice. I know you now have that same faith.
2Timothy1:6 That is why I want you to remember the gift God gave you. God gave you that gift when I laid my hands on you. Now I want you to use that gift and let it grow more and more
2Timothy1:7 The Spirit God gave us does not make us afraid. He is our source of power and love and self-control.
2Timothy1:8 So don't be ashamed to tell people about our Lord Jesus. And don't be ashamed of me—I am in prison for the Lord. But suffer with me for the Good News. God gives us the strength to do that.
2Timothy1:9 God saved us and chose us to be his holy people
2Timothy1:10 And now it has been shown to us in the coming of our Savior Christ Jesus. He destroyed death and showed us the way to have life. Yes
2Timothy1:11 I was chosen to tell people that message as an apostle and teacher.
2Timothy1:12 And I suffer now because of that work. But I am not ashamed. I know the one I have believed
2Timothy1:13 What you heard me teach is an example of what you should teach. Follow that model of right teaching with the faith and love we have in Christ Jesus.
2Timothy1:14 This teaching is a treasure that you have been trusted with. Protect it with the help of the Holy Spirit
2Timothy1:15 You know that everyone in Asia has left me. Even Phygelus and Hermogenes have left me.
2Timothy1:16 I pray that the Lord will show mercy to the family of Onesiphorus. Many times Onesiphorus encouraged me. He was not ashamed that I was in prison.
2Timothy1:17 No
2Timothy1:18 I pray that the Lord Jesus will make sure Onesiphorus receives mercy from the Lord God on that Day. You know how many ways this brother helped me in Ephesus.
2Timothy2:1 Timothy
2Timothy2:2 What you have heard me teach publicly you should teach to others. Share these teachings with people you can trust. Then they will be able to teach others these same things.
2Timothy2:3 As a good soldier of Christ Jesus
2Timothy2:4 A soldier wants to please his commanding officer
2Timothy2:5 Athletes in a race must obey all the rules to win.
2Timothy2:6 It is the farmer who works hard that deserves the first part of the harvest.
2Timothy2:7 Think about what I am saying. The Lord will help you understand it all.
2Timothy2:8 Remember Jesus Christ. He is from the family of David. After Jesus died
2Timothy2:9 And because I tell that message
2Timothy2:10 So I patiently accept all these troubles. I do this to help the people God has chosen so that they can have the salvation that is in Christ Jesus. With this salvation comes glory that never ends.
2Timothy2:11 Here is a true statement: If we died with him
2Timothy2:12 If we remain faithful even in suffering
2Timothy2:13 If we are not faithful
2Timothy2:14 Keep on telling everyone these truths. And warn them before God not to argue about words. Such arguments don't help anyone
2Timothy2:15 Do your best to be the kind of person God will accept
2Timothy2:16 Stay away from people who talk about useless things that are not from God. That kind of talk will lead a person more and more against God.
2Timothy2:17 Their evil teaching will spread like a sickness inside the body. Hymenaeus and Philetus are men like that.
2Timothy2:18 They have left the true teaching. They say that the day when people will be raised from death has already come and gone. And they are destroying the faith of some people.
2Timothy2:19 But God's strong foundation never moves
2Timothy2:20 In a large house there are things made of gold and silver. But there are also things made of wood and clay. Some of these are used for special purposes
2Timothy2:21 The Lord wants to use you for special purposes
2Timothy2:22 Stay away from the evil things a young person like you typically wants to do. Do your best to live right and to have faith
2Timothy2:23 Stay away from foolish and stupid arguments. You know that these arguments grow into bigger arguments.
2Timothy2:24 As a servant of the Lord
2Timothy2:25 You must gently teach those who don't agree with you. Maybe God will let them change their hearts so that they can accept the truth.
2Timothy2:26 The devil has trapped them and now makes them do what he wants. But maybe they can wake up to see what is happening and free themselves from the devil's trap.
2Timothy3:1 Remember this: There are some terrible times coming in the last days.
2Timothy3:2 People will love only themselves and money. They will be proud and boast about themselves. They will abuse others with insults. They will not obey their parents. They will be ungrateful and against all that is pleasing to God.
2Timothy3:3 They will have no love for others and will refuse to forgive anyone. They will talk about others to hurt them and will have no self-control. They will be cruel and hate what is good.
2Timothy3:4 People will turn against their friends. They will do foolish things without thinking and will be so proud of themselves. Instead of loving God
2Timothy3:5 They will go on pretending to be devoted to God
2Timothy3:6 Some of them go into homes and get control over weak women
2Timothy3:7 These women always want to learn something new
2Timothy3:8 Just as Jannes and Jambres
2Timothy3:9 But they will not succeed in what they are trying to do. Everyone will see how foolish they are. That is what happened to Jannes and Jambres.
2Timothy3:10 But you know all about me. You know what I teach and the way I live. You know my goal in life. You know my faith
2Timothy3:11 You know about my persecutions and my sufferings. You know all the things that happened to me in Antioch
2Timothy3:12 Everyone who wants to live showing true devotion to God in Christ Jesus will be persecuted.
2Timothy3:13 People who are evil and cheat others will become worse and worse. They will fool others
2Timothy3:14 But you should continue following the teaching you learned. You know it is true
2Timothy3:15 You have known the Holy Scriptures since you were a child. These Scriptures are able to make you wise. And that wisdom leads to salvation through faith in Christ Jesus.
2Timothy3:16 All Scripture is given by God. And all Scripture is useful for teaching and for showing people what is wrong in their lives. It is useful for correcting faults and teaching the right way to live.
2Timothy3:17 Using the Scriptures
2Timothy4:1 Before God and Jesus Christ I give you a command. Christ Jesus is the one who will judge all people—those who are living and those who have died. He is coming again to rule in his kingdom. So I give you this command:
2Timothy4:2 Tell everyone God's message. Be ready at all times to do whatever is needed. Tell people what they need to do
2Timothy4:3 The time will come when people will not listen to the true teaching. But people will find more and more teachers who please them. They will find teachers who say what they want to hear.
2Timothy4:4 People will stop listening to the truth. They will begin to follow the teaching in false stories.
2Timothy4:5 But you should control yourself at all times. When troubles come
2Timothy4:6 My life is being given as an offering for God. The time has come for me to leave this life here.
2Timothy4:7 I have fought the good fight. I have finished the race. I have served the Lord faithfully.
2Timothy4:8 Now
2Timothy4:9 Do your best to come to me as soon as you can.

Titus1:5 I left you in Crete so that you could finish doing what still needed to be done. And I also left you there so that you could choose men to be elders in every town.
Titus1:6 To be an elder
Titus1:7 An elder has the job of taking care of God's work. So people should not be able to say that he lives in a wrong way. He must not be someone who is proud and selfish or who gets angry quickly. He must not drink too much
Titus1:8 An elder must be ready to help people by welcoming them into his home. He must love what is good. He must be wise. He must live right. He must be devoted to God and pleasing to him. And he must be able to control himself.
Titus1:9 An elder must be faithful to the same true message we teach. Then he will be able to encourage others with teaching that is true and right. And he will be able to show those who are against this teaching that they are wrong.

Hebrews9:1 The first agreement had rules for worship and a place for worship here on earth.
Hebrews9:2 This place was inside a tent. The first area in the tent was called the Holy Place. In the Holy Place were the lamp and the table with the special bread offered to God.
Hebrews9:3 Behind the second curtain was a room called the Most Holy Place.
Hebrews9:4 In the Most Holy Place was a golden altar for burning incense. And also there was the Box of the Agreement. The Box was covered with gold. Inside this Box was a golden jar of manna and Aaron's rod—the rod that once grew leaves. Also in the Box were the flat stones with the Ten Commandments of the old agreement on them.
Hebrews9:5 Above the Box were the Cherub angels that showed God's glory. These Cherub angels were over the place of mercy. But we cannot say everything about this now.
Hebrews9:6 Everything in the tent was made ready in the way I have explained. Then the priests went into the first room every day to do their worship duties.
Hebrews9:7 But only the high priest could go into the second room
Hebrews9:8 The Holy Spirit uses those two separate rooms to teach us that the way into the Most Holy Place was not open while the first room was still there.
Hebrews9:9 This is an example for us today. It shows that the gifts and sacrifices the priests offer to God are not able to make the consciences of the worshipers completely clear.
Hebrews9:10 These gifts and sacrifices are only about food and drink and special washings. They are only rules about the body. God gave them for his people to follow until the time of his new way.
Hebrews9:11 But Christ has already come to be the high priest. He is the high priest of the good things we now have. But Christ does not serve in a place like the tent that those other priests served in. He serves in a better place. Unlike that tent
Hebrews9:12 Christ entered the Most Holy Place only one time—enough for all time. He entered the Most Holy Place by using his own blood
Hebrews9:13 The blood of goats and bulls and the ashes of a cow were sprinkled on those who were no longer pure enough to enter the place of worship. The blood and ashes made them pure again—but only their bodies.
Hebrews9:14 So surely the blood sacrifice of Christ can do much more. Christ offered himself through the eternal Spirit as a perfect sacrifice to God. His blood will make us completely clean from the evil we have done. It will give us clear consciences so that we can worship the living God.
Hebrews9:15 So Christ brings a new agreement from God to his people. He brings this agreement so that those who are chosen by God can have the blessings God promised

Hebrews9:17 A will means nothing while the one who wrote it is still living. It can be used only after that person's death.
Hebrews9:18 That is why blood was needed to begin the first agreement between God and his people.

Hebrews11:1 Faith is what makes real the things we hope for. It is proof of what we cannot see.
Hebrews11:2 God was pleased with the people who lived a long time ago
Hebrews11:3 Faith helps us understand that God created the whole world by his command. This means that the things we see were made by something that cannot be seen.
Hebrews11:4 Cain and Abel both offered sacrifices to God. But Abel offered a better sacrifice to God because he had faith. God said he was pleased with what Abel offered. And so God called him a good man because he had faith. Abel died
Hebrews11:5 Enoch was carried away from this earth
Hebrews11:6 Without faith no one can please God. Whoever comes to God must believe that he is real and that he rewards those who sincerely try to find him.
Hebrews11:7 Noah was warned by God about things that he could not yet see. But he had faith and respect for God
Hebrews11:8 God called Abraham to travel to another place that he promised to give him. Abraham did not know where that other place was. But he obeyed God and started traveling
Hebrews11:9 Abraham lived in the country that God promised to give him. He lived there like a visitor who did not belong. He did this because he had faith. He lived in tents with Isaac and Jacob
Hebrews11:10 Abraham was waiting for the city that has real foundations. He was waiting for the city that is planned and built by God.
Hebrews11:11 Sarah was not able to have children

Hebrews11:15 If they were thinking about the country they had left

2Peter2:3 These false teachers only want your money. So they will use you by telling you things that are not true. But the judgment against these false teachers has been ready for a long time. And they will not escape God who will destroy them.
2Peter2:4 When angels sinned
2Peter2:5 And God punished the evil people who lived long ago. He brought a flood to the world that was full of people who were against God. But he saved Noah and seven other people with him. Noah was a man who told people about living right.
2Peter2:6 God also punished the evil cities of Sodom and Gomorrah. He burned them until there was nothing left but ashes. He used those cities as an example of what will happen to people who are against God.
2Peter2:7 But he saved Lot
2Peter2:8 This good man lived with those evil people every day
2Peter2:9 So you see that the Lord God knows how to save those who are devoted to him. He will save them when troubles come. And the Lord will hold evil people to punish them on the day of judgment.
2Peter2:10 That punishment is for those who are always doing the evil that their sinful selves want to do. It is for those who hate the Lord's authority. These false teachers do whatever they want
2Peter2:11 The angels are much stronger and more powerful than these beings. But even the angels don't accuse them and say bad things about them to the Lord.
2Peter2:12 But these false teachers speak evil against what they don't understand. They are like animals that do things without really thinking—like wild animals that are born to be caught and killed. And
2Peter2:13 They have made many people suffer. So they themselves will suffer. That is their pay for what they have done. They think it is fun to do evil where everyone can see them. They enjoy the evil things that please them. So they are like dirty spots and stains among you— they bring shame to you in the meals you eat together.
2Peter2:14 Every time they look at a woman
2Peter2:15 These false teachers left the right way and went the wrong way. They followed the same way that the prophet Balaam went. He was the son of Beor
2Peter2:16 But a donkey told him that he was doing wrong. A donkey cannot talk
2Peter2:17 These false teachers are like springs that have no water. They are like clouds that are blown by a storm. A place in the deepest darkness has been kept for them.
2Peter2:18 They boast with words that mean nothing. They lead people into the trap of sin. They find people who have just escaped from a wrong way of life and lead them back into sin. They do this by using the evil things people want to do in their human weakness.
2Peter2:19 These false teachers promise those people freedom
2Peter2:20 People can be made free from the evil in the world. They can be made free by knowing our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ. But if they go back into those evil things and are controlled by them
2Peter2:21 Yes
2Peter2:22 What they did is like these true sayings: A dog vomits and goes back to what it threw up. And

2Peter3:8 But don't forget this one thing
2Peter3:9 The Lord is not being slow in doing what he promised—the way some people understand slowness. But God is being patient with you. He doesn't want anyone to be lost. He wants everyone to change their ways and stop sinning.
2Peter3:10 But the day when the Lord comes again will surprise everyone like the coming of a thief. The sky will disappear with a loud noise. Everything in the sky will be destroyed with fire. And the earth and everything in it will be burned up.
2Peter3:11 Everything will be destroyed in this way. So what kind of people should you be? Your lives should be holy and devoted to God.

Revelation5:14 The four living beings said
Revelation6:1 Then I watched as the Lamb opened the first of the seven seals. Then I heard one of the four living beings speak with a voice like thunder. It said
Revelation6:2 I looked
Revelation6:3 The Lamb opened the second seal. Then I heard the second living being say
Revelation6:4 Then another horse came out
Revelation6:5 The Lamb opened the third seal. Then I heard the third living being say
Revelation6:6 Then I heard something that sounded like a voice. The voice came from where the four living beings were. It said
Revelation6:7 The Lamb opened the fourth seal. Then I heard the voice of the fourth living being say
Revelation6:8 I looked
Revelation6:9 The Lamb opened the fifth seal. Then I saw some souls under the altar. They were the souls of those who had been killed because they were faithful to God's message and to the truth they had received.
Revelation6:10 These souls shouted in a loud voice
Revelation6:11 Then each one of them was given a white robe. They were told to wait a short time longer. There were still some of their brothers and sisters in the service of Christ who must be killed as they were. These souls were told to wait until all the killing was finished.
Revelation6:12 Then I watched while the Lamb opened the sixth seal. There was a great earthquake
Revelation6:13 The stars in the sky fell to the earth like a fig tree drops its figs when the wind blows.
Revelation6:14 The sky was split in the middle and both sides rolled up like a scroll. And every mountain and island was moved from its place.
Revelation6:15 Then all the people—the kings of the world
Revelation6:16 They said to the mountains and the rocks
Revelation6:17 The great day for their anger has come. No one can stand against it.
Revelation7:1 After this happened I saw four angels standing at the four corners of the earth. The angels were holding the four winds of the earth. They were stopping the wind from blowing on the land or the sea or on any tree.
Revelation7:2 Then I saw another angel coming from the east. This angel had the seal of the living God. The angel called out in a loud voice to the four angels. These were the four angels that God had given the power to hurt the earth and the sea. The angel said to them
Revelation7:3 Don't harm the land or the sea or the trees before we mark the foreheads of those who serve our God.
Revelation7:4 Then I heard how many people had God's mark on their foreheads. There were 144
Revelation7:5 From the tribe of Judah 12
Revelation7:6 from the tribe of Asher 12
Revelation7:7 from the tribe of Simeon 12
Revelation7:8 from the tribe of Zebulun 12
Revelation7:9 Then I looked
Revelation7:10 They shouted loudly
Revelation7:11 The elders and the four living beings were there. All the angels were standing around them and the throne. The angels bowed down on their faces before the throne and worshiped God.
Revelation7:12 They said
Revelation7:13 Then one of the elders asked me
Revelation7:14 I answered
Revelation7:15 So now these people are before the throne of God. They worship God day and night in his temple. And the one who sits on the throne will protect them.
Revelation7:16 They will never be hungry again. They will never be thirsty again. The sun will not hurt them. No heat will burn them.
Revelation7:17 The Lamb in front of the throne will be their shepherd. He will lead them to springs of water that give life. And God will wipe away every tear from their eyes.
Revelation8:1 The Lamb opened the seventh seal. Then there was silence in heaven for about half an hour.
Revelation8:2 And I saw the seven angels who stand before God. They were given seven trumpets.
Revelation8:3 Another angel came and stood at the altar. This angel had a golden holder for incense. The angel was given much incense to offer with the prayers of all God's holy people. The angel put this offering on the golden altar before the throne.
Revelation8:4 The smoke from the incense went up from the angel's hand to God. The smoke went up with the prayers of God's people.
Revelation8:5 Then the angel filled the incense holder with fire from the altar and threw it down on the earth. Then there were flashes of lightning
Revelation8:6 Then the seven angels with the seven trumpets prepared to blow their trumpets.
Revelation8:7 The first angel blew his trumpet. Then hail and fire mixed with blood was poured down on the earth. And a third of the earth and all the green grass and a third of the trees were burned up.
Revelation8:8 The second angel blew his trumpet. Then something that looked like a big mountain burning with fire was thrown into the sea. And a third of the sea became blood.
Revelation8:9 And a third of the created beings in the sea died
Revelation8:10 The third angel blew his trumpet. Then a large star
Revelation8:11 The name of the star was Bitterness. And a third of all the water became bitter. Many people died from drinking this bitter water.
Revelation8:12 The fourth angel blew his trumpet. Then a third of the sun and a third of the moon and a third of the stars were struck. So a third of them became dark. A third of the day and night was without light.
Revelation8:13 While I watched
Revelation9:1 The fifth angel blew his trumpet. Then I saw a star fall from the sky to the earth. The star was given the key to the deep hole that leads down to the bottomless pit.
Revelation9:2 Then the star opened the hole leading to the pit. Smoke came up from the hole like smoke from a big furnace. The sun and sky became dark because of the smoke from the hole.
Revelation9:3 Then locusts came out of the smoke and went down to the earth. They were given the power to sting like scorpions.
Revelation9:4 They were told not to damage the fields of grass or any plant or tree. They were to hurt only those who did not have God's mark on their foreheads.
Revelation9:5 They were not given the power to kill them but only to cause them pain for five months—pain like a person feels when stung by a scorpion.
Revelation9:6 During those days people will look for a way to die
Revelation9:7 The locusts looked like horses prepared for battle. On their heads they wore something that looked like a gold crown. Their faces looked like human faces.
Revelation9:8 Their hair was like women's hair. Their teeth were like lions' teeth.
Revelation9:9 Their chests looked like iron breastplates. The sound their wings made was like the noise of many horses and chariots hurrying into battle.
Revelation9:10 The locusts had tails with stingers like scorpions. The power they had to give people pain for five months was in their tails.
Revelation9:11 They had a ruler
Revelation9:12 (The first terror is now past. There are still two other terrors to come.)
Revelation9:13 The sixth angel blew his trumpet. Then I heard a voice coming from the horns on the four corners of the golden altar that is before God.
Revelation9:14 It said to the sixth angel who had the trumpet
Revelation9:15 These four angels had been kept ready for this hour and day and month and year. The angels were set free to kill a third of all the people on the earth.
Revelation9:16 I heard how many troops on horses were in their army. There were 200
Revelation9:17 In my vision
Revelation9:18 A third of all the people on earth were killed by these three plagues coming out of the horses' mouths: the fire
Revelation9:19 The horses' power was in their mouths and also in their tails. Their tails were like snakes that have heads to bite and hurt people.
Revelation9:20 The other people on earth were not killed by these plagues. But these people still did not change their hearts and turn away from worshiping the things they had made with their own hands. They did not stop worshiping demons and idols made of gold
Revelation9:21 They did not change their hearts and turn away from killing other people or from their evil magic

Revelation10:2 The angel was holding a small scroll. The scroll was open in his hand. He put his right foot on the sea and his left foot on the land.
Revelation10:3 He shouted loudly like the roaring of a lion. After he shouted
Revelation10:4 The seven thunders spoke
Revelation10:5 Then the angel I saw standing on the sea and on the land raised his right hand to heaven.
Revelation10:6 The angel made a promise by the power of the one who lives forever and ever. He is the one who made the skies and all that is in them. He made the earth and all that is in it
Revelation10:7 In the days when the seventh angel is ready to blow his trumpet
Revelation10:8 Then I heard the same voice from heaven again. It said to me
Revelation10:9 So I went to the angel and asked him to give me the little scroll. He said to me
Revelation10:10 So I took the little scroll from the angel's hand and ate it. In my mouth it tasted sweet like honey

Revelation11:1 Then I was given a measuring rod as long as a walking stick. I was told
Revelation11:2 But don't measure the yard outside the temple. Leave it alone. It has been given to those who are not God's people. They will show their power over the holy city for 42 months.
Revelation11:3 And I will give power to my two witnesses. And they will prophesy for 1260 days. They will be dressed in sackcloth.
Revelation11:4 These two witnesses are the two olive trees and the two lampstands that stand before the Lord of the earth.
Revelation11:5 If anyone tries to hurt the witnesses
Revelation11:6 These witnesses have the power to stop the sky from raining during the time they are prophesying. These witnesses have power to make the water become blood. They have power to send every kind of plague to the earth. They can do this as many times as they want.
Revelation11:7 When the two witnesses have finished telling their message
Revelation11:8 The bodies of the two witnesses will lie in the street of the great city. This city is named Sodom and Egypt. These names for the city have a special meaning. This is the city where the Lord was killed.
Revelation11:9 People from every race of people
Revelation11:10 Everyone on the earth will be happy because these two are dead. They will have parties and send each other gifts. They will do this because these two prophets brought much suffering to the people living on earth.
Revelation11:11 But after three and a half days
Revelation11:12 Then the two prophets heard a loud voice from heaven say
Revelation11:13 At that same time there was a great earthquake. A tenth of the city was destroyed. And 7000 people were killed in the earthquake. Those who did not die were very afraid. They gave glory to the God of heaven.
Revelation11:14 (The second terror is now past. The third terror is coming soon.)
Revelation11:15 The seventh angel blew his trumpet. Then there were loud voices in heaven. The voices said
Revelation11:16 Then the 24 elders bowed down on their faces and worshiped God. These are the elders who sit on their thrones before God.
Revelation11:17 The elders said
Revelation11:18 The people of the world were angry
Revelation11:19 Then God's temple in heaven was opened. The Box of the Agreement that God gave to his people could be seen in his temple. Then there were flashes of lightning
Revelation12:1 And then a great wonder appeared in heaven: There was a woman who was clothed with the sun
Revelation12:2 She was pregnant and cried out with pain because she was about to give birth.
Revelation12:3 Then another wonder appeared in heaven: There was a giant red dragon there. The dragon had seven heads with a crown on each head. It also had ten horns.
Revelation12:4 Its tail swept a third of the stars out of the sky and threw them down to the earth. It stood in front of the woman who was ready to give birth to the baby. It wanted to eat the woman's baby as soon as it was born.
Revelation12:5 The woman gave birth to a son
Revelation12:6 The woman ran away into the desert to a place that God had prepared for her. There she would be taken care of for 1260 days.
Revelation12:7 Then there was a war in heaven. Michael and his angels fought against the dragon. The dragon and its angels fought back
Revelation12:8 but they were not strong enough. The dragon and its angels lost their place in heaven.
Revelation12:9 It was thrown down out of heaven. (This giant dragon is that old snake
Revelation12:10 Then I heard a loud voice in heaven say
Revelation12:11 They defeated him by the blood sacrifice of the Lamb and by the message of God that they told people. They did not love their lives too much. They were not afraid of death.
Revelation12:12 So rejoice you heavens and all who live there! But it will be terrible for the earth and sea
Revelation12:13 The dragon saw that he had been thrown down to the earth. So he chased the woman who had given birth to the child.
Revelation12:14 But the woman was given the two wings of a great eagle. Then she could fly to the place that was prepared for her in the desert. There she would be taken care of for three and a half years. There she would be away from the dragon.
Revelation12:15 Then the dragon poured water out of its mouth like a river. It poured the water toward the woman so that the flood would carry her away.
Revelation12:16 But the earth helped the woman. The earth opened its mouth and swallowed the river that came from the mouth of the dragon.
Revelation12:17 Then the dragon was very angry with the woman. It went away to make war against all her other children. (Her children are those who obey God's commands and have the truth that Jesus taught.)
Revelation12:18 The dragon stood on the seashore.
Revelation13:1 Then I saw a beast coming up out of the sea. It had ten horns and seven heads. There was a crown on each of its horns. It had an evil name written on each head.
Revelation13:2 This beast looked like a leopard
Revelation13:3 One of the heads of the beast looked as if it had been wounded and killed
Revelation13:4 People worshiped the dragon because it had given its power to the beast
Revelation13:5 The beast was allowed to boast and speak insults against God. It was allowed to use its power for 42 months.
Revelation13:6 The beast opened its mouth to insult God—to insult his name
Revelation13:7 It was given power to make war against God's holy people and to defeat them. It was given power over every tribe
Revelation13:8 Everyone living on earth would worship the beast. (These are all the people since the beginning of the world whose names are not written in the Lamb's book of life. The Lamb is the one who was killed.)
Revelation13:9 Anyone who hears these things should listen to this:
Revelation13:10 Whoever is to be a prisoner
Revelation13:11 Then I saw another beast coming up out of the earth. He had two horns like a lamb
Revelation13:12 This beast stood before the first beast and used the same power the first beast had. He used this power to make everyone living on the earth worship the first beast. The first beast was the one that had the death wound that was healed.
Revelation13:13 The second beast did great miracles. He even made fire come down from heaven to earth while people were watching.
Revelation13:14 This second beast fooled the people living on earth by using the miracles that he had been given the power to do for the first beast. He ordered people to make an idol to honor the first beast
Revelation13:15 The second beast was given power to give life to the idol of the first beast. Then the idol could speak and order all those who did not worship it to be killed.
Revelation13:16 The second beast also forced all people
Revelation13:17 No one could buy or sell without this mark. (This mark is the name of the beast or the number of its name.)
Revelation13:18 Anyone who has understanding can find the meaning of the beast's number. This requires wisdom. This number is the number of a man. It is 666.

Revelation14:4 These are the ones who did not do sinful things with women. They kept themselves pure. Now they follow the Lamb wherever he goes. They were bought from among the people of the earth as the first to be offered to God and the Lamb.
Revelation14:5 They are not guilty of telling lies; they are without fault.

Revelation14:11 And the smoke from their burning pain will rise forever and ever. There will be no rest
Revelation14:12 This means that God's holy people must be patient. They must obey God's commands and keep their faith in Jesus.

Revelation16:1 Then I heard a loud voice from the temple. It said to the seven angels
Revelation16:2 The first angel left. He poured out his bowl on the land. Then all those who had the mark of the beast and who worshiped its idol got sores that were ugly and painful.
Revelation16:3 The second angel poured out his bowl on the sea. Then the sea became blood like the blood of someone who has died. And everything living in the sea died.
Revelation16:4 The third angel poured out his bowl on the rivers and the springs of water. The rivers and the springs of water became blood.
Revelation16:5 Then I heard the angel of the waters say to God
Revelation16:6 The people have spilled the blood of your holy people and your prophets. Now you have given those people blood to drink. This is what they deserve.
Revelation16:7 And I heard the altar say
Revelation16:8 The fourth angel poured out his bowl on the sun. The sun was given power to burn the people with fire.
Revelation16:9 The people were burned by the great heat. They cursed the name of God
Revelation16:10 The fifth angel poured out his bowl on the throne of the beast. And darkness covered the beast's kingdom. People bit their tongues because of the pain.
Revelation16:11 They cursed the God of heaven because of their pain and the sores they had. But they refused to change their hearts and turn away from the evil things they did.
Revelation16:12 The sixth angel poured out his bowl on the great river Euphrates. The water in the river was dried up. This prepared the way for the rulers from the east to come.
Revelation16:13 Then I saw three evil spirits that looked like frogs. They came out of the mouth of the dragon
Revelation16:14 (These evil spirits are the spirits of demons. They have power to do miracles. They go out to the rulers of the whole world to gather them for battle on the great day of God All-Powerful.)
Revelation16:15 Listen! I will come at a time you don't expect
Revelation16:16 Then the evil spirits gathered the rulers together to the place that in Hebrew is called Armageddon.

Revelation16:18 Then there were flashes of lightning
Revelation16:19 The great city split into three parts. The cities of the nations were destroyed. And God did not forget to punish Babylon the Great. He gave that city the cup filled with the wine of his terrible anger.
Revelation16:20 Every island disappeared and there were no more mountains.
Revelation16:21 Giant hailstones fell on the people from the sky. These hailstones weighed almost 100 pounds each. People cursed God because of this plague of the hail. It was terrible.

Revelation17:2 The rulers of the earth sinned sexually with her. The people of the earth became drunk from the wine of her sexual sin.
Revelation17:3 Then the angel carried me away by the Spirit to the desert. There I saw a woman sitting on a red beast. The beast was covered with evil names. It had seven heads and ten horns.
Revelation17:4 The woman was dressed in purple and red. She was shining with the gold
Revelation17:5 She had a title written on her forehead. This title has a hidden meaning. This is what was written: the great babylon mother of prostitutes and the evil things of the earth
Revelation17:6 I saw that the woman was drunk. She was drunk with the blood of God's holy people. She was drunk with the blood of those who told about their faith in Jesus. When I saw the woman

Revelation17:9 You need wisdom to understand this. The seven heads on the beast are the seven hills where the woman sits. They are also seven rulers.
Revelation17:10 Five of the rulers have already died. One of the rulers lives now
Revelation17:11 The beast that was once alive but is no longer living is an eighth ruler. This eighth ruler also belongs to the first seven rulers. And he will go away to be destroyed.
Revelation17:12 The ten horns you saw are ten rulers. These ten rulers have not yet received their kingdom
Revelation17:13 All ten of these rulers have the same purpose. And they will give their power and authority to the beast.
Revelation17:14 They will make war against the Lamb. But the Lamb will defeat them
Revelation17:15 Then the angel said to me
Revelation17:16 The beast and the ten horns you saw will hate the prostitute. They will take everything she has and leave her naked. They will eat her body and burn her with fire.
Revelation17:17 God put the idea in their minds to do what would complete his purpose. They agreed to give the beast their power to rule until what God has said is completed.
Revelation17:18 The woman you saw is the great city that rules over the kings of the earth.

Revelation20:1 I saw an angel coming down out of heaven. The angel had the key to the bottomless pit. The angel also held a large chain in his hand.
Revelation20:2 The angel grabbed the dragon
Revelation20:3 Then the angel threw the dragon into the bottomless pit and closed it. The angel locked it over the dragon. The angel did this so that the dragon could not trick the people of the earth until the 1000 years were ended. (After 1000 years the dragon must be made free for a short time.)
Revelation20:4 Then I saw some thrones and people sitting on them. These were the ones who had been given the power to judge. And I saw the souls of those who had been killed because they were faithful to the truth of Jesus and the message from God. They did not worship the beast or its idol. They did not receive the mark of the beast on their foreheads or on their hands. They came back to life and ruled with Christ for 1000 years.
Revelation20:5 (The rest of the dead did not live again until the 1000 years were ended.) This is the first resurrection.
Revelation20:6 What a great blessing there is for those who share in this first resurrection. They are God's holy people. The second death has no power over them. They will be priests for God and for Christ. They will rule with him for 1000 years.
Revelation20:7 When the 1000 years are ended
Revelation20:8 He will go out to trick the nations in all the earth
Revelation20:9 I saw Satan's army march across the earth and gather around the camp of God's people and the city that God loves. But fire came down from heaven and destroyed Satan's army.
Revelation20:10 And he (the one who tricked these people) was thrown into the lake of burning sulfur with the beast and the false prophet. There they would be tortured day and night forever and ever.
Revelation20:11 Then I saw a large white throne. I saw the one who was sitting on the throne. Earth and sky ran away from him and disappeared.
Revelation20:12 And I saw those who had died
Revelation20:13 The sea gave up the dead who were in it. Death and Hades gave up the dead who were in them. Every person was judged by what they had done.
Revelation20:14 And Death and Hades were thrown into the lake of fire. This lake of fire is the second death.
Revelation20:15 And anyone whose name was not found written in the book of life was thrown into the lake of fire.

Revelation21:2 And I saw the holy city
Revelation21:3 I heard a loud voice from the throne. It said
Revelation21:4 He will wipe away every tear from their eyes. There will be no more death
Revelation21:5 The one who was sitting on the throne said
Revelation21:6 The one on the throne said to me
Revelation21:7 All those who win the victory will receive all this. And I will be their God
Revelation21:8 But those who are cowards
Revelation21:9 One of the seven angels came to me. This was one of the angels who had the seven bowls full of the seven last plagues. The angel said
Revelation21:10 The angel carried me away by the Spirit to a very large and high mountain. The angel showed me the holy city
Revelation21:11 The city was shining with the glory of God. It was shining bright like a very expensive jewel
Revelation21:12 The city had a large
Revelation21:13 There were three gates on the east
Revelation21:14 The walls of the city were built on twelve foundation stones. On the stones were written the names of the twelve apostles of the Lamb.
Revelation21:15 The angel who talked with me had a measuring rod made of gold. The angel had this rod to measure the city
Revelation21:16 The city was built in a square. Its length was equal to its width. The angel measured the city with the rod. The city was 12
Revelation21:17 The angel also measured the wall. It was 144 cubits high. (The angel was using the same measurement that people use.)
Revelation21:18 The wall was made of jasper. The city was made of pure gold
Revelation21:19 The foundation stones of the city walls had every kind of expensive jewels in them. The first foundation stone was jasper
Revelation21:20 the fifth was onyx
Revelation21:21 The twelve gates were twelve pearls. Each gate was made from one pearl. The street of the city was made of pure gold
Revelation21:22 I did not see a temple in the city. The Lord God All-Powerful and the Lamb were the city's temple.
Revelation21:23 The city did not need the sun or the moon to shine on it. The glory of God gave the city light. The Lamb was the city's lamp.
Revelation21:24 The peoples of the world will walk by the light given by the Lamb. The rulers of the earth will bring their glory into the city.
Revelation21:25 The city's gates will never close on any day
Revelation21:26 The greatness and the honor of the nations will be brought into the city.
Revelation21:27 Nothing unclean will ever enter the city. No one who does shameful things or tells lies will ever enter the city. Only those whose names are written in the Lamb's book of life will enter the city.
Revelation22:1 The angel showed me the river of the water of life
Revelation22:2 It flows down the middle of the street of the city. The tree of life is on each side of the river
Revelation22:3 Nothing that God judges guilty will be there in that city. The throne of God and the Lamb will be in the city. God's servants will worship him.
Revelation22:4 They will see his face. God's name will be written on their foreheads.
Revelation22:5 There will never be night again. People will not need the light of a lamp or the light of the sun. The Lord God will give them light. And they will rule like kings forever and ever.
Revelation22:6 Then the angel said to me
Revelation22:7 'Listen
Revelation22:8 I am John. I am the one who heard and saw these things. After I heard and saw them
Revelation22:9 But the angel said to me
Revelation22:10 Then the angel told me
Revelation22:11 Let anyone who is doing wrong continue to do wrong. Let anyone who is unclean continue to be unclean. Let anyone who is doing right continue to do right. Let anyone who is holy continue to be holy.
Revelation22:12 Listen
Revelation22:13 I am the Alpha and the Omega
Revelation22:14 What a great blessing there is for those who have washed their robes. They will have the right to eat the food from the tree of life. They can go through the gates into the city.
Revelation22:15 Outside the city are all those who live like dogs—those who do evil magic
Revelation22:16 I
Revelation22:17 The Spirit and the bride say
Revelation22:18 I warn everyone who hears the words of prophecy in this book: If anyone adds anything to these
Revelation22:19 And if anyone takes away from the words of this book of prophecy
Revelation22:20 Jesus is the one who says that all of this is true. Now he says
